title: Sapateiros 44
short_description: Located in the heart of downtown Lisbon, just 200 meters from Terreiro do Paço, Casinha dos Sapateiros is housed in a typical Portuguese building with a peculiar and traditional atmosphere.
full_description:
EXCELLENT LOCATION!
You do not have to Dream when the Reality is here
Located in the heart of downtown Lisbon, just 200 meters from Terreiro do Paço, Casinha dos Sapateiros is housed in a typical Portuguese building with a peculiar and traditional atmosphere.
We are also close to several emblematic places of Lisbon such as Bairro Alto (10 min walk), Rossio square (3 min walk), Castelo de São Jorge (20 min walk) from several viewpoints for the city (15 min walk) ), among others…
With us you can get to know Lisbon in a walking tour with the Marquis of Pombal, Vasco da Gama or even Luís de Camões, go on a Ford of 1931 through the streets of Lisbon, have dinner with live Fado music and enjoy other services that improve your stay. We have several types of studios with different accommodations, but all equipped with Tv and free Wifi. We provide free breakfast at the Palácio das Especiarias which 10min walk. Check-in is also held at the Palácio das Especiarias.
We are surrounded by several restaurants of different environments with the best of Portuguese gastronomy and all kinds of commerce. We appreciate your preference and hope to see you soon.

title: Real Fado
short_description: Authentic Fado concerts in astonishing venues in Lisbon.
full_description:
Real Fado presents authentic Fado concerts in three astonishing venues in Lisbon.
The name honors its mother house – Príncipe Real – a neighborhood that combines romantic gardens with the latest trends in gastronomy and fashion and is a must-see on your visit to Lisbon.
THURSDAYS | Pavilhão Chinês
The concert takes place in the most iconic bar in Lisbon – Pavilhão Chinês. An intimate and welcoming environment decorated with collection pieces welcomes Fado for a memorable late afternoon.
FRIDAYS | Reservatório da Patriarcal
An intimate Fado concert in the mysterious underground of the city of Lisbon – a concert in a stunning cistern. The venue is surprising due to its architectural grandeur and the unique acoustics that this monument provides. The voices of Fado creates a unique experience on these late Friday afternoons.
SUNDAYS | EmbaiXada
A Fado concert in a neo-Arab palace in Príncipe Real. Swap the traditional glass of wine for a gin and enjoy a Fado concert in the central atrium of a mansion that combines Fado tradition with the trendy life of this neighbourhood.
This pre-dinner experience - from 7pm to 8pm - allows you to attend an authentic Fado concert, in the late afternoon, without having to eat dinner.
*
Availability for private/exclusive concerts any day of the week, any time of the day – contact us via email:
realfadoconcerts@gmail.com
.

title: Palace of Belém
short_description: Pretend you’re on a state visit as you tour the Palace of Belém and discover the home of the President of the Portuguese Republic.
full_description:
Feel like the President of the Portuguese Republic for a few hours and make the Palace of Belém your official residence in Lisbon.
In this former property of the count of Aveiras, bought by King João V in 1726, stands the official residence of the president of Portugal.
Nowadays it is the venue for official receptions in its rooms and halls decorated by artists such as Columbano, Malhoa, João Vaz and Leandro Braga.
The entrance hall – Sala dos Bichos – and the ballroom are the most impressive, with chequered marble flooring and painted and bas-relief ceilings.
The palace was the residence of Queen Maria II and, later, that of King Carlos.
Discover the troubled history of the occupants of the palace over the centuries as you walk down the steps in the impressive gardens overlooking the Tagus and through the rooms which have witnessed the pirouettes in Portugal’s recent history.

title: Alcântara Café
short_description: Considered part of Lisbon’s heritage, the vintage space in copper tones is mythical and timeless, with a modern and unique decoration.
full_description:
Located in Alcântara, Step into Alcântara Café, an emblematic venue since 1989
Located in a vintage warehouse, it blends industrial charm with elegance.
Iron beams and large windows create a warm atmosphere adorned with statues, offering a glimpse into 1920s neoclassical industrial architecture.
Red velvet, soft lighting, and steel pillars define its aesthetic, seamlessly blending antique touches with a modern atmosphere.
Today, the former Alcântara Café opens its doors for private events, inviting you to create memorable moments within its storied walls.
It’s not just a space it’s a journey through time

title: Arco da Rua Augusta
short_description: Climb up one of Lisbon’s iconic buildings for a unique view of the city. Open to the public since 9 August 2013, the Arch leaves Lisbon at your feet, literally.
full_description:
This triumphal arch in Rua Augusta has always impressed those roaming Lisbon’s busiest pedestrianised street.
But now you can visit the top of this unique structure for a truly singular and impressive view of Baixa and the river. It symbolises the rebirth of a new Lisbon after the tragedy of the earthquake, fire and tsunami in 1755 that razed what stood before.
And it affirms, in Latin, “The Virtues of the Greatest”: the strength, resilience and achievements of the Portuguese people.
At the top stand the sculptures by Célestin Anatole Calmels of Glory crowning Genius and Valour. You can’t help but be inspired by these values as you admire the 360º view of the best Lisbon has to offer. The remaining statues, by Victor Bastos, represent figures of our history. On the left we find the Marquis of Pombal, the following are Vasco da Gama and Viriato and, on the right, Nuno Álvares Pereira.
Discover the history of this arch in the exhibition in the Clock Room for a sense of what it stands for.

title: Church of Our Lady of the Consolation
short_description: This place of worship, which was founded in 1165, is a landmark of the Christian reconquest of Portugal led by King Afonso Henriques.
full_description: Today, the Church of our Lady of the Consolation is very different from the original church, which was founded in 1165. The portal remains from the Mannerist period and the inside of the church is lined with a set of tile panels from the first half of the 18th Century. The original building was a landmark of the Christian reconquest of Portugal led by King Afonso Henriques. The church, which has a regular longitudinal layout, comprises the church itself, a rectangular bell tower and annexes. The façade is in the plain architectural style. The church comprises a nave, a chancel and sacristy and the walls are lined with blue and white tiles. The chancel, which leads to the sacristy, is separated from the nave by a triumphal arch.

title: Ericeira | World Surfing Reserve
short_description: On the coast is Ericeira, a fishing town that has become a World Surfing Reserve, offering numerous beaches and excellent conditions for surfing.
full_description:
Ericeira is a paradise for the restless.
It is, in fact, the first surfing reserve in Europe.
Windsurfers, kite surfers and bodyboarders will also feel at home here, and will be able to attend some of the main sporting events on these beaches.
Even so, given the length of the beach and the historical setting of this town full of accommodations, restaurants and quality seafood spots, Ericeira is also sought after by many beachgoers.

short_description: Just a few minutes from Amoreiras and Campolide is the Parque Infantil da Serafina. This unique place in Lisbon surrounded by dense woods is the ideal place to take children.
full_description: In the Monsanto Forest Park, just a few minutes from Amoreiras and Campolide, is the Parque Infantil da Serafina. This unique place in Lisbon surrounded by dense woods is the ideal place to take children. Whether for a party, a picnic, a game of football or just to play on the swings and be outside in the fresh air, the Parque Infantil da Serafina is where all parents, aunts and uncles, grandparents and other carers take children with energy to burn. With a restaurant and café, an adventure area, playgrounds for various ages and a children’s driving course, the Parque da Serafina is the ideal place to let the young explore freely and safely. If you bring a blanket and something to eat, you can have a picnic, which will be the best moment of the day for them – and maybe for you too.

title: Artsy Cascais
short_description: Located in Avenida Dom Carlos I in Cascais, with view over the Sea and Bay, 30 minutes from Lisbon Airport and 20 minutes from the Capital Centre, this new icon in Cascais has the best location.
full_description:
For those who prefer the modern, you will have a unique experience of sleeping inside a sculpture, made by the Portuguese artist Vhils, and wake up involved in the light that comes through the art that surrounds the modern wing of the Hotel. To those who prefer the classic, in the historical building you will find rooms with high ceilings and big windows with amazing views to the streets and gardens of Cascais.
In total, ARTSY Cascais has 15 rooms and 2 suites that celebrate Cascais’ history, art and culture with neutral tones and sophisticated design.
Since ARTSY Cascais is an ecofriendly Hotel, all rooms and suites provide Oliófora products.
In our ART Restaurant, the sea products are enhanced, however, we wanted to bet on the multiculture with dishes with 'influence around the world but also, with Portuguese traditions adjusted to our days’ reality'. Our restaurant joins the sustainability cause from the way it reuses all raw material.
ARTSY Cascais has a rooftop, for guests only, that offers a small water layer and sunbeds to enjoy even the windy days.

During your stay at My Suite Lisbon Guest House Príncipe Real, you can walk to Bairro Alto, Chiado, BaixaPombalina, Avenida da Liberdade or descend the streets with colourful facades until you reach the Tagus river in a few minutes.

title: Museum of Lisbon – Pimenta Palace
short_description: Discover the various lives of the city of Lisbon, from the Roman age when it was called Felicitas Julia Olisipo to artefacts from 300, 000 BC.
full_description:
The Museu de Lisboa is one of the places where you can learn more about Lisbon’s many lives down the ages – as well as the history which transformed it into one of the world’s most important and interesting cities.
At Palácio Pimenta, an 18th-century summer house in Campo Grande, you’ll find a collection of paintings, ceramics, engravings, maps, azulejo tiles and archaeological finds that reveal the city’s urban, social, economic and symbolic development.
From ancient artefacts – dating back to 300, 000 BC – to a model of the pre-earthquake city, there are various reasons for discovering the Palácio Pimenta and the city’s history.

Bistrô & Tapas is a restaurant with à la carte service, featuring a menu where the Portuguese and international gastronomies merge in a unique setting. A delicious and full buffet breakfast is served at this restaurant. Bar & Lounge at Meliá Setúbal offers an extensive menu of savouries, snacks, and light meals, served with a variety of beverages and wines by the glass, which are carefully selected.
These spaces are characterised by their modern décor, which make them ideal for an informal, more intimate meeting, a date, or a late evening get-together. They are the perfect meeting point for after work, in the centre of Setúbal.
Meliá Setúbal also provides multiple meeting rooms with the best technology and customised service to hold business events.
The Hotel is pet friendly, against payment of a fee, and an adorable welcome pet kit awaits your pet.

title: Arrábida Fort
short_description: See one of the loveliest beaches in Portugal and the fort by Portinho da Arrábida beach which guards it: Arrábida Fort. It’s a breathtaking sight.
full_description: Discover one of the most interesting – and least known – viewing spots in the Arrábida Nature Park. At Arrábida Fort, which today houses the Park Oceanographic Museum, the defence of the River Sado has long since ceased to be the objective, but the preservation of the stunning view remains unaltered. Built in 1676 after the end of the War of Restoration, it was intended to form a continuous defensive line with the Fort of Santiago de Sesimbra and Fort of Outão along the coast. Dedicated to Our Lady of Arrábida, this fortification is close to one of the most emblematic beaches in the region: Portinho da Arrábida. Park at some distance and venture on foot, visiting the chapel and its stone image of Our Lady, before taking a dip at the beach in the translucent and inviting water.
location: Portinho da Arrábida, São Lourenço, Azeitão, 2925-378, Setúbal

title: Tagus Estuary Natural Reserve
short_description: Discover the south bank of the Tagus. In this large area, decide what you want to find and set off in search of nature, close to Lisbon, in the Reserva Natural do Estuário do Tejo.
full_description:
When you visit Lisbon, you’ll be surprised to find a vast nature reserve on the city’s doorstep.
The Reserva Natural do Estuário do Tejo and Mar da Palha (Straw Sea) represent the largest wetlands in Portugal, with sizeable areas of mud and marshes.
Various migratory birds rest here on their long flights and some species make these areas, which alter with the tides, into their home all year long. Flamingos arrive in the thousands during autumn, in a uniquely beautiful and visually poetic spectacle. But these are not the only amazing guests.
In the salt pans, now almost totally unused, you’ll find common stilts, spotted redshanks, common greenshanks, pied avocets and many others. They shelter, feed and reproduce in this area which once produced salt, or “white gold”.
On the riverside in Alcochete you’ll see birds such as egrets and eagles – and it’s also a lovely place to walk and explore the south bank of the river better. In this large area, decide what you want to find and set off in search of nature, in Lisbon’s back yard.
location: Av. Combatentes da Grande Guerra, n.º 1, 2890-015, Alcochete

title: Hotel Métropole
short_description: On the “Rossio” Square, the heart of historic Lisbon, with unforgettable views over the castle and Alfama and with the best of this fascinating city at your doorstep.
full_description:
Centrally located on the Rossio Square, in the heart of the “Baixa” district, the Métropole is a 1917 classical hotel, in the heart of historic Downtown Lisbon, with the very best of the city at your doorstep.
Since 1917, the Métropole has been a classic hotel, known to those who know: a charming and elegant setting of the 1920’s, with original furnitures and modern conveniences in a warm and intimate atmosphere.
Guestrooms with own character, individually styled and decorated, some with exceptional views over the “Rossio”, the Castle and Alfama.
Excellent starting point for discovering Old Lisbon, as all the public transportation network is at your door, and nearly all attractions are within short walking distance.
Free Wi-fi. Non-smoking hotel.

title: Port of Lisbon Authority
short_description: With an outstanding geographical location, Lisbon is an important shelter for all types of boats, and port of call for cruise ships in the Atlantic, Western Mediterranean, Northern Europe, North Africa as well as for transatlantic cruises.
full_description:
Located on the banks of the Tagus estuary, port of Lisbon has in its history a long tradition in the cruise and yachting markets.
In fact, the port of Lisbon welcomes all kinds of cruise ships. Besides the magnificent natral conditions, the cruise ships that berth in Lisbon have at their disposal four passenger terminals located in the city center, equipped with the modern safety equipment and offering a variety of services.
The mild climate, the diversity of tourist sites, good international air lift and variety of hotel supply are some of the factors that make Lisbon a privileged destination. The Marina de Lisboa hosts approximately 1.000 vessels in its four recreational docks – Alcântara, Santo Amaro, Belém and Bom Sucesso – offering its visitors a wide range of services, which also benefit the existing supply in a big city.
location: Gare Marítima de Alcântara, Alcântara, 1350-355, Lisboa

short_description: Located in the centre of Lisbon, 10 minutes from the airport, with access to many transports network and the main points of interest, Hotel Real Palácio has 147 rooms.

With a total of 147 rooms, 12 of which are situated in the old stately home, Hotel Real Palácio invites you to discover the secrets of reinvented Portuguese cuisine and enjoy your weekend with a delicious family brunch.
Visit our 5 multifunctional rooms with capacity for 230 people, natural light and wi-fi internet free of charge.
Come and discover the charms of the city of Lisbon.

title: Pestana Lisboa Vintage
short_description: The Pestana Lisboa Vintage is located close to the Avenida da Liberdade and to a wide variety of services, shopping and business. Characterised by a contemporary ambience and vintage decor, the hotel boasts spacious rooms.
full_description:
The Pestana Lisboa Vintage is located close to the Avenida da Liberdade and to a wide variety of services, shopping and business. Combining the comfort of a 4-star hotel with the practicality of an apartment in the centre of Lisbon, the hotel is a 3-minute walk from the Praça Marquês de Pombal, where you can find easy access to the whole city.
Characterised by a contemporary ambience and vintage decor, the hotel boasts spacious rooms and junior suites, as well as studios with an equipped kitchenette. Meals can also be enjoyed at the Slang (partner restaurant), located on the ground floor of the hotel with doors opening out right onto the street.
The top-floor lounge provides moments of relaxation and leisure with views of Lisbon.
The privileged location of the Pestana Lisboa Vintage makes it the ideal option for long stays for business or leisure, as a solo traveller or with your family. With the metro station at the Marquês de Pombal, and public car parks a few metres away, exploring the city is a pleasure.
ROOMS
The 121 rooms, 23 of which are junior suites and 32 studios, offer numerous options for a perfect stay. Equipped with free Wi-Fi, LCD TV, USB outlet and air conditioning, the rooms are spacious and comfortable, decorated with vintage touches and featuring a bathroom with shower, guaranteeing all the comforts that make a stay unforgettable. Junior suites and studios are equipped with a kitchenette. Some rooms have a balcony with direct views of the Marquês de Pombal.

title: Galeria 1758
short_description: Galeria 1758 was born from the desire to bring art closer to the public. It is a place of encounter, sharing, and artistic discovery — where creative processes serve as a starting point for dialogue, and inspiration.
full_description:
Located in Ajuda, Lisbon, Galeria 1758 is a space dedicated to promoting contemporary art and creating accessible and inclusive cultural experiences. Curated by Cristina Cabrita, the gallery was born out of her painting studio and is driven by the mission of bringing art closer to the public, fostering an atmosphere of sharing, discovery, and artistic reflection.
One of Galeria 1758’s core purposes is the exploration of creative and technical processes through workshops, offering practical experiences that encourage individual expression and direct engagement with different artistic languages. The gallery thus functions as a living space where creation and learning go hand in hand.
At Galeria 1758, visitors can also purchase small-format works by emerging Portuguese artists, as a way to support and encourage local artistic creation. Additionally, the space hosts workshops in painting, tile art, and other artistic experiences, open to all audiences, whether beginners or experienced.

title: Mário Coelho House Museum
short_description: The house in which the bullfighter Mário Coelho was born is an obligatory stopping off point for aficionados.
full_description:
The house in which the bullfighter Mário Coelho was born is an obligatory stopping off point for aficionados.
The house is filled with memories and all the trophies that this famous son of Vila Franca de Xira acquired throughout his lifetime.
During a career that spanned 40 years, the matador faced more than 3, 000 bulls and took part in 1, 427 runs and 200 benefit festivals.
location: Travessa do Alecrim, 5, 2600-094, Vila Franca de Xira

title: Quinta da Marinha Golf
short_description: This exciting, 18-hole course is designed by the internationally acclaimed golf architect Robert Trent Jones.
full_description:
This exciting, 18-hole course is designed by the internationally acclaimed golf architect Robert Trent Jones. A par 71 with 5,845 metres.
The first nine holes are set amongst some of the most spectacular countryside in the area while the second nine are on the cliff tops, with sensational views over the Atlantic.
True to form, Trent Jones has laid plenty of traps.
The course is studded with water hazards and its 18 undulating greens will challenge players of all levels, from the novice to the scratch golfer.

title: Miradouro de S. Pedro de Alcântara
short_description: Save your breath for one of the best views of Lisbon. Make this miradouro your point of discovery and enchantment with the city.
full_description: The Miradouro de São Pedro de Alcântara is one of the largest and most iconic observation points in Lisbon. It’s right next to the top of the Elevador da Glória and offers a special panorama of the city. From here you can see all of the Avenida da Liberdade, Restauradores and Baixa, besides the Castelo de São Jorge with its imposing walls and photogenic crown of green pine trees. You can also see in full detail the Miradouro da Igreja da Graça, with its imposing church. This viewing point is unusual in that it is part of a beautiful and well cared for garden where you can enjoy a refreshing drink at the kiosk. You’ll also find an original map made of azulejos to identify some of the iconic places you can see. This is undoubtedly one of the places you cannot miss.
location: Rua de São Pedro de Alcântara, Lisboa

title: Champalimaud Centre for the Unknown
short_description: Discover one of the most futuristic and remarkable buildings in Lisbon. Facing the river, the Champalimaud Foundation is a Centre of Research into the Unknown.
full_description:
Discover one of the most futuristic and remarkable buildings in Lisbon, facing right onto the Tagus.
Between the nationalistic tradition of the Torre de Belém and the symbol of seafaring toil that is the Doca de Pedrouços you’ll find the Champalimaud Foundation. Also known as the Centre of Research into the Unknown, the building is as innovative and important as the biomedical research performed inside.
You can visit the gardens and open-air amphitheatre, sit in the magnificent Darwin’s café and appreciate the view as countless scientists and researchers work on neurosciences and cancer research.
The building, designed by the architect Charles Correa, seeks to raise architecture to the level of sculpture, sculpture to the level of beauty and beauty to the level of therapy – in order to honour the centre’s work.
Head off into the unknown at this unique place in Lisbon.

title: No Convento
short_description: Fado No Convento celebrates Portugal’s rich musical and culinary landscape through daily performances and sophisticated French cuisine.
full_description:
Nestled within the timeworn walls of Lisbon’s 17th century Convento das Bernardas,
Fado No Convento celebrates Portugal’s rich musical and culinary landscape through daily performances and sophisticated French cuisine.
Weaving daily fado performances into ancient walls
Inviting the timeless Fado into Convent halls
As part of an ongoing mission to unite past and present, Fado No Convento welcomes fado enthusiasts and curiosos to bathe in a sensorial voyage into culinary and musical reverence through captivating, daily Fado performances.
A remarkable destination for Fado in Lisbon, Fado No Convento stands out through its awed architectural and culinary offering.
Where French gastronomy meets Portuguese terroir
Orchestrated and put in motion by chef João de Oliveira with the support of French chefs Alexis Gielbaum and Maxime Kien, our menu dives into an exploration of Portuguese flavours, punctuated with French gastronomic cuisine.
location: Travessa do Convento das Bernardas, 12, 1200-687, Lisboa

title: Palacete Chafariz D’el Rei
short_description: In the heart of Alfama district this superb example of 19th century aristocratic architecture family house with its lounges and terraces overlooking the Tagus River.
full_description:
The Palacete Chafariz D'El Rei is an eclectic building with a Neo-Moorish style dating from the end of the 19th Century with interiors of the so called Brazilian Art Nouveau Style very much in vogue at the turn of the century.
For three years has undergone thorough a meticulous process of recovery and restoration, that preserved all the original features as well as the decorative and architectural elements that make up the interior and the exterior of the building.
With a Home Boutique Hotel concept , the Palacete offers their guests a homey atmosphere and warm feeling, with all the refinements and perks of a 21th Century house.
The Palacete has six Luxury Suites with unique views both of the river and the city. Its location in the old quarter of Alfama with its typical alleys, lanes and steps, make this Riverfront Mansion unique in the city of Lisbon.
Welcome to the Palacete Chafariz D'El Rei!

title: NAV Portugal
short_description: NAV Portugal's mission is to ensure a SAFE and EFFICIENT provision of Air Navigation Services, assuming a paramount key role in the aviation sector.
full_description:
NAV Portugal carries out its work on mainland Portugal and in the Portuguese autonomous regions of the Azores and Madeira. The company’s head offices are situated next to Lisbon Airport, as well as the
Lisbon Air Traffic Control Center
and the company's
Training Center
. The
Oceanic Control Center
is located on the island of Santa Maria in the Azores Autonomous Region.
In addition to these two important centers, NAV Portugal also has other infrastructures, providing air traffic services in the
control towers of Lisbon, Cascais, Porto, Faro, Funchal, Porto Santo, Santa Maria, Ponta Delgada, Horta and Flores
airports.
To fully carry out its mission,
NAV Portugal has a considerable amount of equipment and many technical installations
(radar, radio and communications stations) in several points of mainland Portugal and the autonomous regions.
The Oceanic Air Traffic Management System on Santa Maria and the phased entry into service of a new generation of air traffic management systems in Lisbon have been of
crucial importance
in keeping NAV Portugal in the forefront of air navigation service providers.
location: Rua D, Edifício 121 - Aeroporto Humberto Delgado, 1700-008, Lisboa

title: House of Corpo Santo - Baroque Museum
short_description: This Baroque building dates back to the 18th Century.
full_description:
The Baroque is evident in every square inch of this 18th Century building, which comprises a chapel lined in gilded work “in the Portuguese style” and two further rooms.
One of the side walls is made up of a stretch of medieval wall. The inside of the building and the stairway are clad in blue and white tiled ashlars.
These form a “welcoming figure” in the entrance courtyard and also show scenes from everyday palace life.
The floors are decorated with single-figure tiles alternating with red brick.
The dome in the Hall of Orders is painted with scenes depicting St. Pedro Gonçalves, known as the Holy Body and protector of fishermen, fishing boat owners and navigators.

title: Adega Cooperativa de Palmela
short_description: Since 1955, Adega de Palmela, a wine brand from the Setúbal Peninsula, has played a meritorious and active role in the region, maintaining an affective and emotional connection in supporting agricultural and wine production.
full_description:
The ADEGA DE PALMELA is one of the main centres of development of the Municipality that is markedly agricultural and where the vineyard and the wine have, for historical reasons, a fairly large importance.
The main wine-growing area lies on the sandy plain that makes up a large part of the Municipality of Palmela.
As a cooperative winery it currently has 300 members that have a combined area of 1000 hectares.
MISSION
To transform the grapes from the associates farms into wine and moscatel, as well as to commercialize it in order to obtain the best benefits for its members and food safety for consumers.
WINE TOURISM VISIT
Would you like to visit us?
We love to receive visitors and tell them more about the Wines of the Costa Azul and the Cooperativa de Palmela. Located just 40 minutes from Lisbon We offer guided tours of the winery and end with a tasting in the wine cellar.
During your visit we will show you the whole winemaking process from when the grapes arrive, how the wines are made and you’ll visit our wine cellar where the oak barrels are stored.
We have space for events.

title: Lisbon’s Azulejos
short_description: Learn more about Portuguese tile-making, an art form rich in history. From antique panels to more modern work, every neighbourhood of Lisbon is replete with it.
full_description:
Discover a remarkable art form in Lisbon.
Portuguese tile work found the ideal setting in Lisbon to showcase its different periods, styles and resistance to the passing of the centuries. And the tiles of Lisbon that have withstood the test of time and catastrophes continue to make their mark as works of art that are unique in the world.
Although you can't see them all, as many are installed in monuments, palaces or inside private homes, there are countless iconic and unmissable façades on the streets of Lisbon.
Explore the history of tiles at the
National Tile Museum
, wander through the streets of the historic districts of Baixa/Chiado, Belém and Parque das Nações to find some unforgettable works from ancient and modern times. Discover some iconic modern works, next to the Águas Livres Aqueduct, by Abel Manta; and on Avenida Infante Santo, by Maria Keil, places marked by tiles, which Lisbon invites you to contemplate and take with you... in your heart, but not in your pocket.
Tiles are a historical heritage with a centuries-old presence in Lisbon, but they have been disappearing from their places of origin due to theft for illegal sale.
Lisbon kindly asks its visitors not to contribute to this situation!
Do not buy or take away part of our history!

title: Museum of Portuguese Music - Verdades de Faria
short_description: A museum of Portuguese music that is well worth stopping off for.
full_description:
The Museum of Portuguese Music is housed in the Casa Verdades de Faria. Raul Lino’s architectural design for this small palace is a perfect example of the influence of 20th Century Romanticism, fine-tuned by a profoundly eclectic revivalism.
The museum's valuable collection of popular musical instruments was amassed by the Corsican musician and ethnologist Michel Giacometti, who spent the greater part of his life studying Portuguese folk music, and by the composer Fernando Lopes Graça.
In addition to hosting exhibitions and concerts, the museum also has an important documentation centre on Portuguese music.

title: Elevador da Bica (Closed temporarly)
short_description: Take one of the most iconic trips in Lisbon on a tram that climbs a few hundred inspiring and very photogenic metres up a steep slope with the Tagus in the background.
full_description:
If you want a unique experience in Lisbon, take the Elevador da Bica.
The most photographed funicular in Lisbon, due to the dramatic contrast between the steep route and the river in the background, this is one of the city’s greatest attractions. It connects Largo do Calhariz to Rua de São Paulo in a heady descent possible on foot but an ascent only suitable for the hardy.
Decorated by artists and witness to the vibrant nightlife in Rua da Bica, the tram seems to share the excitement of the rest of the city during the Festas Populares.
Deemed a national monument since 2002 and in operation for a century, try and photograph the two trams as they cross, with the colourful houses and flowery verandas the perfect backdrop.
One of the most memorable images you’ll take with you.
location: R. da Bica de Duarte Belo, 1200, Lisboa

title: Can the Can Lisboa
short_description: Welcome to the world where tradition and quality are put together to offer the greatest gastronomic treasures of Portuguese CAN`s.
full_description:
CAN THE CAN opened in 2012 with a concept which is unique in the world: to promote the national canning industry, Portuguese tinned food and traditional conservation methods. Our cuisine is typically Atlantic, defined by the use of natural produce and healthy ingredients.
We strive to elevate cans to an exclusive product, through our innovative recipes, with the belief that quality of preparation and presentation can help promote the consumption of tinned fish.
Our cuisine shows that Portuguese tinned fish are an exclusive product, with an enormous tradition and quality, which can be used with a combination of fresh ingredients, highlighting the quality of preparation and presentation.
Portugal’s long coastline, from early days, has led the Portuguese towards the sea in search for new horizons. In this way a maritime odyssey was born creating a culture of seafood, enhanced by the access to excellent fish.
The appropriate conditions – extensive coastline, abundant, high-quality fish, and Portugal’s excellence in the art of fishing – have led to the rapid growth of canning in Portugal, from the 19th century onwards.
The national canning industry was born in Setubal, Portugal, in 1854, to a country with a unique coastal zone, ancestral fishing tradition and unparalleled fish quality. It is also the oldest and most beloved industry in our country.
Can the Can stems from a fascination for this industry, and we aim to dignify it and bring it to a wider audience.
location: Terreiro do Paço, 82/83, Praça do Comércio, 1100-148, Lisboa

title: Feira da Ladra (Flea Market)
short_description: Discover treasures at Lisbon’s most iconic market, the Feira da Ladra, or flea market, where there’s always something original, kitsch or vintage to be found.
full_description:
Come and discover Lisbon’s most original market. The Feira da Ladra, or Flea Market, which is immortalised in the poem by the Portuguese singer songwriter Sérgio Godinho, is held every Tuesday and Saturday in Campo de Santa Clara, behind the monastery of São Vicente de Fora.
Traditionally dedicated to selling used objects, you’ll find real gems amongst indescribable bric-a-brac.
The market dates back to 1272 and was held in various parts of the city before settling permanently here.
From here you can easily visit the Panteão Nacional and the church of São Vicente de Fora, so the best thing to do is save this neighbourhood for a day when the market is on.
Between souvenirs and bargains, you may just take the best memory of your stay in Lisbon from here.

title: National Museum of Ethnology
short_description: The National Museum of Ethnology holds one of the most extraordinary ethnological collections in the world, of over 40.000 objects, representing more than 380 cultures.
full_description:
The National Museum of Ethnology holds the most important national ethnographic heritage.
Its collections, of over 40.000 objects, represent more than 380 cultures, throughout 80 countries and 4 continents, with highlights to the African, Asian and Amerindian cultures, as well as the traditional Portuguese one.
The great diversity of the collections can be discovered not only in the permanent and temporary exhibitions, but also in the museum's storage rooms, accessible if previously booked to visitasguiadas@mnetnologia.dgpc.pt

additional_sections.title: Additional Information

title: Moon & Sun Lisboa
short_description: The Moon & Sun Lisboa hotel aims to provide the best accommodation experience in a refined and welcoming environment, taking advantage of the privileged location of downtown Pombaline.
full_description:
The Moon & Sun Lisboa hotel aims to provide the best accommodation experience in a refined and welcoming environment, taking advantage of the privileged location of downtown Pombaline.
With a service of excellence and comfort as its motto, the restored historical building from the Pombaline period was transformed into a charming space, where it is still possible to find details from the initial construction of the 18th century.
It has 35 rooms, is located on Rua Áurea, close to the Santa Junta Elevator, and just below you discover the large and historic Praça do Comércio with the Rua Augusta Arch, and the Tagus River as far as the eye can see.
Moon & Sun Lisboa is in the heart of the city. Its location allows easy access to the city's most important attractions, whether you want to explore the hills on foot, or easily access public transport.
At Moon & Sun hotels we believe that historic centers are the places that reveal the most genuine and precious secrets of cities. It is in its charms that we lose ourselves and find our origins, our destinies.
On the ground floor you can find the restaurant, where you can enjoy the Italian-inspired piadinas, perfect for a break from the rush of a day exploring the city. You can also take the opportunity to enjoy a refreshing cocktail on the terrace, and watch city life happening.

title: Galeria Subterrânea do Loreto
short_description: Loreto Underground Gallery | Walking along the streets of Lisbon, it’s hard to imagine what’s below your feet. Discover the underground side to the city in the Loreto galleries.
full_description:
Discover one of the best kept secrets in Lisbon: the Galeria Subterrânea do Loreto.
In truth, this gallery is part of the aqueduct (Aqueduto das Águas Livres) that delivered water to Lisbon.
It’s easy to visit, just book beforehand and choose a time in the morning or afternoon to walk down in the depths of the city.
Put 2 hours aside to start the day with a visit at 10 am in the morning; or seek refuge from the heat of the afternoon at 2.30 pm amidst stones that haven’t seen the light of day since 1748.
It was in that year that the gallery was built to connect a reservoir (Reservatório da Patriarcal) built between 1860 and 1864 to the garden and Miradouro de São Pedro de Alcântara.
Those admiring Lisbon from here have no idea what’s going on below their feet. Come and explore the quieter side of the city.

title: Município de Mafra
short_description: The monumentality of its Palace, its natural heritage, including the World Surfing Reserve waves, the unforgettable traditions and gastronomy – Mafra has so much to offer, just 30 minutes away from Lisbon.
full_description: When we speak of Mafra, we cannot fail to mention its Palace-Convent. Its famous library, its imposing carillons and its six organs – the only set in the world designed to be played together – have guaranteed this monument a place among the most emblematic of the country. The natural beauty of the Tapada Nacional de Mafra, the National Hunting Grounds, and the exceptional beaches of Ericeira are features that cannot by missed. In addition to the already renowned success as a beach destination, Ericeira’s exceptional waves led to its recognition as a World Surfing Reserve, the first in Europe and second in the world. All this, combined with an amazing gastronomy, and a diversity of traditions and customs that were not lost despite its proximity to Lisbon, Mafra is a unique destination ... not to be missed!
location: Av. Movimento das Forças Armadas, 28, 2640-495, Mafra

The space consists of several rooms dedicated to different literary genres, as well as specific spaces, which include study areas, a children's room, a multipurpose space, an area dedicated to periodical reading and a area dedicated to comics.
Outside, visitors will be able to enjoy a pleasant garden with a kiosk and direct access to Quinta Pedagógica dos Olivais.
This equipment is not limited to consulting and requesting works, it has a vast program that includes various activities and partnerships with various institutions, with special attention to children and the promotion of reading, including also workshops, book presentations, conferences and other cultural activities.
Visit us!

title: Belém
short_description: The monuments of Lisbon, which personify the age of the Discoveries and the country’s great maritime feats, can be found here. But contemporary, informed and cultured Lisbon provides it with company in this select area. So when you arrive in the city, don’t lose a minute in trying to find the most iconic architecture in the city.
full_description:
The monuments of Lisbon, which personify the age of the Discoveries and the country’s great maritime feats, can be found here. But contemporary, informed and cultured Lisbon provides it with company in this select area. So when you arrive in the city, don’t lose a minute in trying to find the most iconic architecture in the city.
When you arrive in Lisbon, you will find much more than just a city. The history is written into the buildings and streets and the epochs have left their mark on the major edifices. The famous Monastery of Jerónimos and Torre de Belém – internationally recognised as UNESCO world heritage sites – are not the only buildings you find when coming to this part of town. The Padrão dos Descobrimentos and Praça do Império complete the sensation of the grandeur of yesteryear of the Great Portuguese Empire. Walk amongst the monuments that recount the history of the Manueline style in an architectural setting that is unique anywhere in the world.
But these are not the only attractions in Belém, nor do they attempt to freeze frame Portugal at a single moment in history. You’ll also find some of the most contemporary structures that define the life and new architectural profile of the city. Grab a pair of trainers and discover Belém slowly, visiting its squares and gardens, monuments,  cafés, restaurants and discovering its own rhythm.
Add to all of these reasons for visiting Lisbon a riverside with green areas and a unique atmosphere, various marinas and sailing clubs, and a cycle lane in which the fresh air combines with the beauty of the view and you’ll find in Belém the place where you can enjoy Portuguese culture in its purest state. Provided you accompany it with one of the famous and delicious Pastéis de Belém, you’ll be on the right path to heaven. Which in this particular case is called Lisbon.

It is our desire to share with you our culture. We will guide you like a friend during your visit to Lisbon. The city that inspired poets and artists. A place with a special romantic sun light and a place where you can taste excellent gastronomy and wine. For sure we will make your travel a unique experience.

title: Hotel Alvorada
short_description: The Hotel Alvorada, with an excellent location in the centre of Estoril, opposite the Casino, just a few metres from the Congress Centre, the beach and the train station, also offers quick access to Lisbon.

DESIGNED BY RENOWNED ARCHITECTS, THIS 5 STARS RESORT FEATURES AN IMPRESSIVE INTEGRATION OF CONTEMPORARY AND UNIQUELY DESIGNED VILLAS IN THE MAGNIFICENTE LANDSCAPE OF THE ÓBIDOS LAGOON.

title: Monument to the Discoveries
short_description: One of the most recent monuments in Lisbon is an ode to the history of Portugal. Come and visit it and make your own discoveries.
full_description:
One of the most recent monuments in Lisbon is an ode to the history of Portugal.
Come and make your own discoveries at the Padrão dos Descobrimentos, a monumental sculpture beside the Tagus, commemorating the golden age of Portuguese history.
This huge stone caravel was built in 1940 for the Portuguese World Expo by the architect Cottinelli Telmo and the sculptor Leopoldo de Almeida. It was only given permanent status in 1960 to celebrate the fifth centenary of the death of Prince Henry the Navigator.
Try to identify the main navigators, such as Bartholomew Dias – who turned the Cape of Torments into the Cape of Good Hope; Vasco da Gama – who discovered the maritime route to India; Pedro Álvares Cabral – who discovered Brazil; and Magellan – the first European to cross the southern Pacific and circumnavigate the earth.
Visit the Discoveries Cultural Centre; climb to the top to get a unique view of Praça do Império; and be inspired by the way how, in the 15th century, the small caravels set sail from this point in Belém to face the world’s oceans.

title: Residence Inn By Marriott
short_description: The Residence Inn Lisbon offers the perfect balance of comfort and convenience for extended stays.
full_description:
With 71 modern suites equipped with a full kitchen, living area, and workspace, the hotel is ideal for both business and leisure travellers.
It features two meeting rooms and a spacious lounge, perfect for networking.
The design, by Broadway Malyan, combines Portuguese geometric patterns and warm tones, reflecting the authenticity of Lisbon.Among the amenities, highlights include a modern gym, breakfast included, 24-hour market, and a free grocery delivery service.
The Residence Inn Lisboa is located near business, cultural, and shopping centres, making it the best option for those seeking an extended stay in Lisbon with the comfort of a home and the services of a premium hotel.

title: Upon Vila
short_description: Inspired by the traditional and magnificent country estates of Alcochete, Upon Vila is born, at the gates of Lisbon and where the energy of the city combines with countryside tranquility. Here, time slows down.
full_description:
Come in, make yourself at home. Meet us. Find yourself here.
Where city meets nature
We believe that harmony happens in the encounter: between urban energy and the serenity of the countryside, between tradition and invention, between sophistication and rusticity. In the materials, tones and textures that meet our culture.
The Upon Vila is born as its meeting point. It was inspired by the estates of Alcochete, where the magnificent gates and ferrets indicated the way to the properties that, regardless of size, welcomed everyone with familiarity. We have collected this essence of country life and the natural and manorial legacy of the village to decorate our rooms, create genuine environments, and prepare special activities.
Whether you are escaping as a couple, sharing with family or on a work retreat, we want you to find the spirit of simplicity and uncomplicated days where time slows down.

title: Egeac
short_description: EGEAC is responsible for managing some of Lisbon’s key cultural spaces and for organising Festas de Lisboa and other street festivals.
full_description:
For more than 20 years, this municipal company has been promoting cultural activity in Lisbon through its street festivals and management of cultural spaces, including the São Luiz and LU.CA Theatres, various museums and art galleries, and monuments such as the Monument to the Discoveries and São Jorge Castle.
These cultural spaces are visited by more than 2 million people each year, while close to 1 million participate in activities held in the street.
As a key actor in the city, EGEAC provides programming that is multidisciplinary, comprehensive, inclusive and democratic, striving to be an agent that is active and receptive, interacting in both contemporary and popular activities.

title: Café São Bento
short_description: An institution for the true lovers of a good steak! Inspired by the recipe of the old Cafés Marrare, Bife à São Bento has been made in the same way for over 40 years.
full_description:
At number 212, Rua de São Bento, the red colorful awnings stand out in the façade.
Next to the Assembly of the Republic, it maintains its secrets behind closed doors. As soon as the doorbell rings, the mystery is unveiled leading into an intimate setting with a classical style.
Enjoy the comfort while having a good lunch or dinner meal until late hours.
It has two rooms: ground floor and 1st floor, with a privileged view of the São Bento Gardens. It only competes with the sight of the steak just arriving at the table.
AN INSTITUTION
FOR THE TRUE LOVERS OF A GOOD STEAK
We serve it in three different ways: Portuguese style, grilled or, the famous: São Bento style.
Inspired by the recipe of the old Cafés Marrare, Bife à São Bento has been made in the same way for over 40 years. Sirloin cut, tender meat, dipped in a spicy cream sauce, perfect for dipping the crispy home French fries. There are other side dishes to choose from or even a topped fried egg.

title: Escola de Hotelaria e Turismo de Lisboa
short_description: Founded in 1958, the Hotel and Tourism School of Lisbon is a reference in the vocational training of this sector and a space of sharing and development of skills and knowledge for all those who have a passion for Tourism and Hospitality.
full_description:
Founded in 1958, the Hotel and Tourism School of Lisbon is a reference in the vocational training of this sector, being proud of being the first Hotel and Tourism School in Portugal and of offering a space of sharing and development of skills and knowledge for all those who have a passion for Tourism and Hospitality.
Located in the Campo de Ourique district, in the old facilities of the Industrial School Machado de Castro, it has a privileged location in the city of Lisbon and, as a differentiating factor, it has its own hotel - the Hotel da Estrela - where the practical component of the courses is developed in a real work context.
The recognition is the result of the right combination of students, around 500, and an exceptionally talented teaching staff, being a global School as well, with effective course plans, innovative projects of applied knowledge, global partnerships and a strong connection to the business world.
Focused on technological specialization courses, post-secondary education, with the duration of a year and a half, the school offers a wide range of curricular programs, in the areas of Culinary Arts, Pastry Arts Management, Food and Beverage Management, Accommodation Management and Cultural Tourism, taught in Portuguese and English.
With the aim of providing an experience in real context and giving particular relevance to the professional practice, the School invested to improve its infrastructures, having 5 industrial kitchens, 1 pastry area, 1 restaurant and bar, 1 auditorium in the kitchen area, 1 Oenology room, 1 auditorium with 150 seats and 15 classrooms.
The Hotel and Tourism School of Lisbon is also a partner for managers and professionals who looks for personal and professional qualification, and for companies that recognize that their employees’ qualification is crucial to the sustained business growth and competitiveness. We invest in executive training with customizable modular training of short duration.
We also welcome all those interested in these matters and who want to learn and experience the hotel culture, through workshops, team building, seminars, themed dinners and wine tastings - for those who are not indifferent to the world of Gastronomy and Wines.
With a global, structuring, transforming and innovative project, the School maintains its focus on hospitality and has a strong connection to the hotel and tourism sector, contributing daily to the development of new talents and to successful careers in Tourism.

title: Ajuda National Palace
short_description: The Ajuda National Palace was the official royal house in the second half of the 19th century. It presents authentic interiors and important art collections.
full_description:
The Ajuda National Palace is a neoclassical building from the first half of the 19th century.
It became a royal residence on king Luís I accession to the throne and it remained as such until the end of the Monarchy (1910).
It still holds its decorative artworks in place in a lively and truthful to its origins display.
On the ground floor, from the Music Room onwards, the private apartments can be found, while State Rooms occupy the upper floor.
As a museum, it gathers important collections of decorative arts from the 18th and 19th centuries: gold and silver works, textiles, furniture and ceramics as well as paintings, sculptures and photographs.
The most important State ceremonies held by the President of the Republic still take place here.

title: Museum of Fado
short_description: Discover the best fado singers and guitar players. Fascinating performers, sublime guitarists and inspired composers come together at this museum to celebrate Portugal’s traditional music.
full_description:
The Museu do Fado is one of the must-visit places in Lisbon. Besides representing one of the region’s most important cultural legacies, it also has a restaurant and a themed shop where you can spend some time to catch the spirit of saudade (nostalgia).
Totally devoted to fado and the guitar, it has a permanent exhibition and temporary ones, alongside a document centre and an auditorium with regular events and a very interesting programme.
With songs by the greatest Portuguese artists illustrating an art form that Portugal gave to the world, the museum’s artistic quality will surprise you.
The restaurant serves typical Portuguese food and helps to give your visit a traditional flavour.
But technology, in the form of interactive stations documenting fado’s history throughout the museum and audioguides allowing you to listen to dozens of fado songs composed and sung down the decades, lets you delve further into history.
Time and the music fly, in a restored building of national interest right next door to the neighbourhoods where you can hear fado being sung at night.

title: Vandelli Botanical Garden
short_description: Set within the Jardim Botânico da Ajuda — the oldest botanical garden in Portugal, founded in 1768 — this remarkable setting brings together heritage, nature and timeless elegance.
full_description:
Located in the historic district of Ajuda, Vandelli Botanical Garden is one of Lisbon’s most distinctive and enchanting event venues. Set within the Jardim Botânico da Ajuda — the oldest botanical garden in Portugal, founded in 1768 by royal commission and designed by the Italian botanist Domenico Vandelli — this remarkable setting brings together heritage, nature and timeless elegance.
Originally conceived as a centre for scientific research and royal leisure, the garden has preserved its Baroque terraced layout, grand pathways, sculptural fountains and an exceptional collection of native and exotic plant species. With sweeping views over Lisbon and the Tagus River, it offers a serene yet inspiring atmosphere, where history is present in every detail.
Today, Vandelli Botanical Garden stands as an exclusive venue for memorable events.
Featuring versatile indoor and outdoor spaces — including a neoclassical greenhouse and beautifully shaded garden terraces — it is ideally suited for brand activations, corporate dinners, cultural events, private celebrations and refined weddings for up to 300 guests.
Combining extraordinary scenery, historical depth and a strong sense of place, Vandelli Botanical Garden delivers immersive experiences that celebrate Lisbon’s heritage while fostering a genuine connection with nature.

title: Oeiras Seafront Walk
short_description: Enjoy a calm and peaceful part of the Lisbon region. Far from traffic and close to the sea, the seafront walk is a popular place at the weekend in Oeiras.
full_description: The riverside walk in Oeiras, better known as the paredão, is a fantastic place to walk or cycle in Lisbon. It begins by the beach in Paço d’Arcos and ends at Praia da Torre, passing by the dock, saltwater ocean pool and various beaches in the area. It is very popular amongst the local inhabitants for walking, sport and enjoying the excellent weather that it experiences most of the year. Come and discover one of the places with the best quality of life in Lisbon, where the relaxed ambiance and sea air combine in calm and perfect harmony, epitomising your stay in Lisbon.

title: Sociedade Agrícola Félix Rocha
short_description: Quinta da Ribeira is located in Meca, in the medieval council of Alenquer, and is nowadays headquarters of Sociedade Agrícola Félix Rocha.
full_description:
This society began wine production in the mid XIX century, by the strengh of enthusiasm of Mr. Felix Rocha, grandfather of the present managers.
The fourth generation of the family recognized, since 1991, the need for continuous investment in the production. There has been a complete restoration of the vineyards and the cellar.
Nowadays it explores several hectares of vines, distributed between Quinta da Ribeira, Quinta do Casal do Mato, Quinta do Casal Novo, Quinta de São Cristovão, Quinta do Baracho, Quinta das Sete Pedras, Quinta da Amoreira and Quinta do Morais.
The vineyards are planted in clay and limestone soils, in smooth steep hillsides with a solar exposure to the south. In these vineyards you can find national varieties like Touriga Franca, Touriga Nacional, Tinta Roriz (Aragonez), Fernão Pires, Arinto, Alvarinho and Moscatel, and internacional varieties like Merlot, Syrah, Petit Verdot, Alicante Bouschet, Caladoc, Petit Verdot, Cabernet Sauvignon, Chardonnay and Sauvignon Blanc.
The variety of wines is the result of grape varieties, i.e. the different types of grape and the way they are produced. Our red wines are rich, with a good aroma and fine structure, resulting in a very fragrant and elegant product! The white wines are fresh, with good acidity and a mineral and citrus profile. They are very versatile wines with excellent structure and balance.
All the wines have great potential for ageing in the bottle.
We have a global concern about the environment so we work in an integrated protection system.
The Sociedade Agrícola Félix Rocha Cellar went through a major technological remodeling in 2003. In addition to the full modernization of vinification equipment it was also actualized the harvesting, enabling a partial mechanical Harvest of the grapes.
We have, therefore, the best conditions for making high quality wines, in the vinification process as well as in ageing process, for which a special room was created, allowing the wine to age in oak barrels.
location: Quinta da Ribeira, Casais da Ribeira, Meca, 2580-608, Alenquer

title: Zero Zero - Parque das Nações
short_description: A journey to the flavours of Italy! With our eyes open we're by the river, with our eyes closed we're travelling to Italy. With exclusive Italian products, mostly from the Veneto region, ZeroZero brings some of the country's best products to the table, now also in its new space in Parque das Nações. From sight to taste, the experience starts here.
full_description:
PIZZAS
With a balanced mix of 00 flour and other flours that are milled slowly on stone, at Pizzeria ZeroZero pizza dough is prepared using the indirect method, also known as indirect or poolish kneading. We adopt an ancient process that begins with a pre-fermentation at a controlled temperature, followed by a maturation of at least 48 hours.
The end result is an artisan pizza base, thin and crunchy, fragrant like the best freshly baked bread, light and digestible.
MENUS
From classic to speciality pizzas, fresh pastas and creamy risotti, exclusive to ZeroZero Parque das Nações, the menu is very varied and also includes several vegetarian options. All the dishes are written in Italian and offer a real journey through the flavours of Italy.
A meal at ZeroZero can start with some antipasti, such as Carpaccio di Tonno and Panzanella, and is never complete without a coppa di gelati or a dolce, such as the typical tiramisù.
PROSECCHERIA
Inside the restaurant and with a few seats at the counter, the Prosecco bar allows you to take a sensory tour of all of Italy's wine regions and micro-regions, as the menu is made up of more than 80 Italian wines.
There is also room for classic and signature cocktails. It's also ideal not to miss the charcuterie, where you can try countless combinations of Italian cheeses and sausages, bringing together the best of both worlds.
ITALIAN CHARCUTERIE
From a small, distinctive window, you can find some of the best Italian products used in the pizzeria. With a wide selection of PDO and PGI cheeses and sausages, mostly from the Veneto region, it will be hard to choose which combination to take to the table or home.

title: Centro de Meditação Kadampa Deuachen
short_description: Modern Buddhist Kadampa Temple, with a shop, café, gardens and accommodations. Open for visits, meditations, Buddhist teachings and retreats. Everyone is welcome.
full_description: The Kadampa Buddhist Temple in Sintra is a real refuge of peace and a source of inspiration where everyone is welcome. Located in the Sintra mountains, with beautiful views and flower gardens. We also have a café and a shop. Come visit us and consider the option of taking part in guided meditations, Buddhist teachings and even doing a spiritual retreat. The teachings of modern Buddhism present Buddha’s ancient wisdom adapted to the modern way of life, so that we can find peace and meaning in our daily activities. The founder of the Temple and the New Kadampa Tradition is Venerable Geshe Kelsang Gyatso, an internationally renowned master of meditation and Buddhism, author of many books that are available in the Temple’s shop.

Star inn Lisbon is pet-friendly, and an adorable welcome pet kit awaits your pet.

title: Aqueducts and Mãe d'Água
short_description: An essential part of Lisbon to get to know on foot. Drop in at the Arpad Szenes-Vieira da Silva Foundation and its garden nearby for an unforgettable day.
full_description: Visiting the aqueducts and reservoir in Amoreiras is a way of admiring the ingeniousness and care that was put into building these unique structures in Lisbon. The reservoir is located in the Jardim das Amoreiras, one of the spots in the city most appreciated by the locals in summer and on sunny but cool days. Designed in 1745 and completed in 1834, this is the impressive site where the water brought by the aqueduct collected. A look inside is well worth it, where the power of the waterfall and expanse of water is emphasised by the sober architecture. You can also find out more about it and the aqueducts at the Museu da Água (Museum of Water), an interesting and original place to visit.

title: Dom Carlos Liberty
short_description: Close to Av.da Liberdade, the Dom Carlos Liberty its an hotel where its modern interior design is perfectly synchronized with the latest technologies and standards of the nowadays hotels.
full_description:
The Dom Carlos Liberty inaugurated during the 21st century it’s an modern hotel synchronized with the latest technologies and standards of the nowadays hotels.
With its modern and tasteful interior design which combines comfort and functional features without forgetting the classical tradition of hospitality, the Dom Carlos Liberty stands out due to the constant care it proudly takes for the quality of its services and its extremely competitive prices.
Around 50 meters of Av. Da Liberdade, the most important avenue of Lisbon, it is a cosmopolitan and elegant hotel, like few others.
Located in the financial and shopping center of Lisbon, next to Avenida da Liberdade and the Marquês de Pombal metro station, the Dom Carlos Liberty provides the Dom Carlos Lounge, an innovate concept of an integrated space where a good buffet is served at breakfast, while in the afternoon it changes into a pleasant lounge with library, complimentary newspapers, plasma TV, quality background music, Internet wifi access free of charge, drinks and snacks in self-service mode.

title: Art Legacy Hotel
short_description: Art Legacy Hotel is more than just a hotel: it is an architectural and cultural masterpiece. It is a hotel with personality and character, reflected in the modern, bright décor thought out to the last detail.
full_description:
An explosion of striking colors awaits you, promising an unparalleled visual surprise.
Art Legacy’s 53 rooms are true works of art. With a unique décor that perfectly combines contemporary and classic elements, each room has a strong, bold colour, creating an air of energy and personality. We believe that the exclusive décor of our rooms is an extension of the art throughout the hotel, providing guests with an immersive and inspiring aesthetic experience.
At Art Legacy, the culinary experience is also taken to a new level. The kitchen is in a large communal space, making each meal an opportunity to share flavours and stories.
With a privileged location in Baixa district, in the heart of Lisbon, this five-star hotel has been remodeled to become the modern and elegant hotel it is today while paying homage to the centuries-old history and architecture of one of the most beautiful buildings in central Lisbon.

title: Rota dos Petiscos
short_description: Rota dos Petiscos is the perfect place to savour the delicacies of delicious traditional Portuguese cuisine.
full_description:
Discover an incredible gastronomic experience in Lisbon, in a cosy restaurant with the unique atmosphere of the ‘Rota dos Petiscos’.
With a typical Portuguese menu that appeals to all palates, here you can discover the different and appetising specialities we have to offer.
Enjoy the sun in our pleasant outdoor area.

title: Hotel Internacional (Res T. Bastardo)
short_description: Discover a unique view of the centre of Lisbon right in Rossio square. The restaurant at the Hotel Internacional offers a special panoramic vista of the city, especially at dusk.
full_description: Discover a unique view of the centre of Lisbon at the fantastic Bastardo restaurant on the 1st floor of the Hotel Internacional. Though fascinating by day, it is at night that the harmonious beauty of Rossio reveals all of its splendour. Come and discover this unique urban backdrop to make your lunch or dinner even more special. The modern design contrasts with the history of the city that extends before your eyes. Take the chance to rest and drink a cocktail, a glass of wine or even a liqueur after wandering the Baixa area. If you go for the gastronomic option, the pumpkin risotto is one of the most popular dishes on the menu. A visit to Bastardo is worth it to see another view of Portugal’s capital.

title: Martinhal Lisbon Chiado
short_description: Martinhal Lisbon Chiado - Luxury Hotel and Serviced Apartments in the Heart of Lisbon.
full_description:
Martinhal Lisbon Chiado - Luxury Hotel and Serviced Apartments in the Heart of Lisbon
With 37 carefully designed and spacious apartments, Martinhal Chiado is the ideal hub to for your stay in the trendy Chiado area, in the historic centre of Lisbon!
The building, from 1855, has been refurnished offering modern living spaces without compromising the 19th century architecture, with large wooden windows and narrow balconies with stunning views to the city.
Honoring the year of the building, Bar 1855 Gin Garden offers a specially curated cocktail and gin selection as well as delicious tapas and pinchos to enjoy with the creative drinks. The bar also serves breakfast and lunch.
With kids’ clubs and babysitting service, you can enjoy the city as a family but also as a couple – while we look after your children!
The interior design is completed by the artwork of acclaimed Portuguese artists, Jorge Santos, Angela Ferreira aka Kruella D´Enfer, Ana Aragão and Ana Pérez Quiroga.
Bar 1855 Gin Garden
Located in a lovingly restored 19th century building right in the heart of stylish and historic Chiado, in Rua das Flores, the new Bar 1855 Gin Garden brings a new flavor with amazingly fresh and light cocktails and gins to enjoy in the refreshing garden style interiors.
The menu of delicious ‘Low & No’ alcohol cocktails has been specially curated by the award-winning bar owner and former Diageo Whisky Master, Peter O’Connor to make a superb cocktail collection.
Coffee lovers will not want to miss the smooth Expresso Martini mixing Ketel One vodka, coffee liqueur and freshly brewed espresso, our Pink Lady will seduce fruit and sour flavours’ fans and the Seville Negroni is the option for those who want bittersweet notes.
For a refreshing alcohol-free drink, the Virgen Apple Highball is a new favorite.
Customers can also get creative and curate their own G&Ts, before checking out more of what Lisbon has to offer for night owls.
The menu also features tasty “tapas and pinchos” that can be enjoyed alongside the cocktails.

title: Mesa de Frades
short_description: In this old chapel of Dona Rosa Palace, lined with eighteenth-century tile panels, every night Fado takes place. At Mesa de Frades the most unexpected and surprising nights take place.
full_description:
In addition to the prestigious cast that visits this house, there are many musicians and artists who gather over the evenings to sing, play and listen to Fado.
With the company of a good glass of wine, you can taste a dish of typical gastronomy prepared with the best Portuguese products.

title: Cova da Baleia Adventure park
short_description: PASSION, CHALLENGE, ADVENTURE and EMOTION are just some of the feelings that the Whale Cave Adventure Park brings to its customers, with the maximum safety and comfort.
location: Avenida Senhora da Lapa, Rua do Chafariz Grande,, 2640-416, Mafra

title: Church of Nª Srª da Conceição da Lapa
short_description: A simple church in a different part of the city whose images of worship are particularly cherished due to the magnificent Baroque altar.
full_description: If you’re seeking a simple and beautiful representation of faith, visit the church of Nossa Senhora da Conceição da Lapa in Amadora. This small chapel, which was the main parish church for the area until it was later replaced, was built in 1759 in the Ermida da Lapa. Also well known as the chapel of Falagueira, or even chapel of Porcalhota, this church was opened to the faithful in 1760 and until the present day has had a magnificent Baroque altar in its high chapel. With images of veneration of Our Lady of Conception, St. Anthony and St. Sebastian, it’s a chapel that’s well worth a visit for its particular simplicity.

title: Hotel Lisboa Central Park
short_description: Situated in the center of Lisbon, in a pleasant setting of the Eduardo VII Park and the Marquês de Pombal. The prestigious hospitality and service reserves an exclusive environment of class and elegance.
full_description:
Situated opposite the Eduardo VII Park, Lisboa Central Park offers free Wi-Fi and spacious rooms. The lively Avenida da Liberdade with its shops and restaurants is a 10-minute walk away.
The large rooms of Hotel Lisboa Central Park are decorated in neutral tones and warm colours. Each has air conditioning and a private bathroom with bathtub.
The hotel has a 24-hour reception and a laundry service. The Parque Metro Station is a 2-minute walk away and offers easy access to Lisbon’s historic centre in 4 stops.
Avenidas Novas is a great choice for travellers interested in gourmet food, monuments and museums.
This is our guests' favourite part of Lisbon, according to independent reviews.

title: Dolce CampoReal Lisboa Golf
short_description: Lying just 30 minutes north of Lisbon, CampoReal Golf enjoys a wonderful tranquil location.
full_description:
Lying just 30 minutes north of Lisbon, CampoReal Golf enjoys a wonderful tranquil location.
It lies in a beautiful valley overlooking an unspoiled landscape of dramatic rolling hills and ancient vineyards, occupying land formerly used by the Portuguese royal family as hunting ground.
Host to Ladies European Tour Events in 2010 and 2011, the course meanders through the naturally undulating terrain, and provides a challenging and fun test to all standards of players.
The challenging greens are built to USGA specification, but there are relatively few bunkers on the course to trap the errant ball
The modern clubhouse has beautiful panoramic views over the course where you can sit and relax with your post-round refreshment.

title: Hotel NH Sintra Centro
short_description: Located in the centre of the town of Sintra, this 4 star family hotel has a panoramic restaurant and ample sized rooms with mountain and sea views.
full_description:
In the centre of the town of Sintra, this discrete 4 star family hotel in Portugal with private parking offers all the comforts you will need to enjoy the town of Sintra, UNESCO world heritage.
Breakfast in the Monserrate Restaurant will give you insight into the quality of the cuisine at this welcoming family hotel in the centre of Sintra.
The views from the panoramic bar reveal its privileged location between the Sintra Mountains and the Town Palace, with the sea stretching out in the distance.
