title: Casa do Bairro
short_description: Close to the city’s hustle and bustle, Casa do Bairro Boutique Guesthouse is the perfect place for those who seek a tranquil haven with easy access to many points of interest at a walking distance such as Bairro Alto, a Baixa e o Chiado.
full_description: Located in the heart of one of Lisbon’s most traditional areas, Bairro de Santa Catarina, the first Guesthouse of the Shiadu Group brings life to the small and picturesque Beco da Caldeira, surrounded by typical narrow streets, stairways and alleys.

title: MEO Blueticket
short_description: One of the largest ticketing operators in the market, with more than 10M pageviews/year. It sells 3M tickets each year from 350 events of every kind.
full_description:
One of the largest ticketing operators in the market. It sells 3M tickets each year from 350 events of every kind and assures the ticketing in more than 220 venues.
With more than 10M pageviews/year it has agreements with all the major outlets.
MEO Blueticket is a specialist in offering integrated ticketing solutions: event configuration, distribution, access control system, financial and management control.
It ensures an A to Z process to event promoters and organizers, ranging from sales to access control, with management indicators provided in real time.
To the final client to be entertained, MEO Blueticket warrants an easy, safe and versatile ticket purchase and delivery experience.
To further facilitate the access to ticket purchases it has developed its own mobile website, and  content-specific microsites by collaborating with its partners.
location: Rossio dos Olivais, Lote 2.13.01A, Parque das Nações, 1990-231, Lisboa

title: Café Luso
short_description: Café LUSO is world synonym for Fado, song from Lisbon and World Heritage, UNESCO.
full_description: Café Luso (Fado & Food Group) is world synonym for Fado. Doesn´t need special presentations: it´s History speaks for itself, for us, Lisbon citizens, for us, Portuguese. Fado is a common patrimony (recognized by UNESCO in 2011), which is daily spread from Bairro Alto (neighborhood), as an open stage. Professional Fado, singed with class and style, together with the Portuguese Gastronomy. Luso Café, just beside, is a wine bar to travel through Portuguese Regions, also with tidbits. Fado, Tradition without Translation
location: Travessa da Queimada, 10, Bairro Alto, 1200-365, Lisboa

title: No. 24 Tram
short_description: This tram crosses the central hills of Lisbon and goes through historic bairros and taking us until Campolide.
full_description:
The no. 24 tram follows a route through the historic neighbourhoods and the hills at the central Lisbon. It shuttles between Pç. Luís de Camões and Campolide.
It sets off in Pç. Luís de Camões (Bairro Alto/Chiado), crosses Príncipe Real and Rato and climbs up to Campolide.
On its way, highlight to Glória Lift and Príncipe Real and, at Campolide, you are in a walking distance to Torres das Amoreiras, one of the high points on the Lisbon skyline, located at the top of one of the city’s hills, their post-modernist architecture was extremely controversial when built in the 1980s.
location: Largo Luís de Camões ↔ Campolide, Lisboa

title: Culturgest- Fundação Caixa Geral de Depósitos
short_description: Culturgest is dedicated to contemporary creation. Culturgest presents a regular programme of Performing Arts, Music, Visual Arts, Cinema and Contemporary Thought.
full_description:
Culturgest has a significant role in the artistic scene of both city and country. It is aimed at a diverse public, which includes schools, children and young people, invited to enjoy first-rate programmes and to partake in attractive and inspiring cultural activities.
Culturgest follows the career of theatre directors and choreographers, producing new creations and presenting them to an eclectic audience; commissions visual artists to create works and organises solo and collective exhibitions; presents music concerts of many genres and supports and hosts major film festivals.
In addition, Culturgest also offers a programme of lectures and debates, a wide variety of workshops, guided tours, meetings and initiatives for schools and families.

title: Water Museum - Barbadinhos Steam Pumping Station
short_description: The Water Museum gathers a number of monuments and buildings, built between the XVIII and XIX Centuries, which are representative of an important chapter in history of the water supply to the city of Lisbon.
full_description:
Built by the final reservoir of the Alviela Aqueduct, this station was in service between 1880 and 1928 and was responsible for the expansion of domestic distribution of water in Lisbon.
The building preserves the old steam machines, references of the industrial heritage, and hosts the permanent exhibition of the Water Museum that invites the visitor to get to know the water in the areas of history, science, technology and sustainability.
Classified, since 2010, as a Public Interest Set.

title: Emporium Lisbon Suites
short_description: AT HOME...IN LISBON - Emporium Lisbon Suites offers a different concept of accommodation in the city. We plan your arrival so you can feel at home, not in a hotel.
full_description:
AT HOME...IN LISBON
Emporium Lisbon Suites offers a different concept of accommodation in the city. We plan your arrival so you can feel at home, not in a hotel.
We don't have receptionists or impersonal welcoming smiles.
To feel at home, we offer some automated services to fulfil all the requirements during your stay.
Suites
The beautiful and attractive interior design of the Suites welcomes you into an confortable and cosy atmosphere where you can feel at home. The Suites are charming and spacious, from the living and sleeping areas through the kitchenette and bathroom. Let yourself be captivated by the natural light that enters through the windows and by the splendid view over the Tagus River.
All Suites have a modern and fully equipped kitchenette to prepare your daily meals, a pleasant living area with a sofa and Led TV with Cable, king or twin size bed of extreme comfort for a peaceful sleep, and private bathroom with shower or bathtub. They also include free wireless internet, air conditioning, safe deposit and daily cleaning. All Suites have access by elevator and you don’t need a card or a key, just a pin code that will be sent to you by e-mail two days before your arrival. Everything for you to feel at home.
Location
Emporium Lisbon Suites is strategically located in downtown Lisbon, in one of the most alive and exciting area of the city! You can easily walk to Terreiro do Paço and Rua Augusta Arch or to Lisboa Cathedral and get lost in the famous streets of Alfama, one of the most typical quarters of Lisbon.
We are an excellent starting point to explore the city by foot or public transports. On one side you’ll find downtown Lisbon with its museums, churches and historic monuments, on the other side the typical quarters of Lisbon like Alfama, Sé and the medieval Lisbon São Jorge Castle. In front you'll find the new riverside neighborhood that extends along the blue Tagus River.

title: Museum of the Sea | Rei D. Carlos
short_description: In this museum of the whole sea, the various themed displays cover everything from marine biodiversity through to traditional fishing.
full_description:
The King Carlos Museum of the Sea is housed in the former Cascais’ Sporting Club. The club, founded in 1879, was set up by the then prince to promote sports and host social events.
The Museum of the Sea, inaugurated in 1992, is about the sea and the people who live with it.
The museum is organised by theme: Cascais on the Shipwreck Route (underwater archaeology); Biodiversity (fish, mammals and sea birds plus marine shells and fossils); People of the Sea (the lives and habits of the Cascais fishing community and traditional fishing methods); Navy and Navigation (historical and technical focus); Marine Fossils (the museum’s collection of marine fossils and the King Carlos and Oceanography Centre, which reconstructs the main exploits of the king and his team aboard the yacht Amélia).
location: Rua Júlio Pereira de Mello, 2750-407, Cascais

title: Abbey of Alcobaça
short_description: UNESCO’s World Heritage | The Abbey of Alcobaça, is one of Europe’s most important Cistercian abbeys, given its state of conservation and its architecture, symbolic of the order.
full_description:
The Abbey of Alcobaça, a UNESCO World Heritage site, is one of Europe’s most important Cistercian abbeys, given its state of conservation and its architecture, symbolic of the order.
Founded in 1153, following a donation made by King Afonso Henriques to Bernardo de Claraval, building work formally began in 1178.
The monastery was to become the greatest example of early Portuguese Gothic style, subsequently expanded and embellished through successive royal endowments.
In 1382, King Dinis commissioned the Cloister of Silence, and an additional floor was added during the reign of King Manuel, who also ordered construction of the so-called Manueline Sacristy.
Both of these projects were overseen by the architect, João de Castilho.

title: Foodstory
short_description: We are FOODSTORY. Food is our passion and each event has a unique story! Let us tell the story of your event!
full_description:
We are the result of experiences, moments and stories of our events, the shared knowledge between our clients, partners and suppliers and the people who represente us.
We are gastronomy – Food is the genesis of our existence, our soul and reason to exist and what we intend to take to the center stage of our events.
We are innovative, we seek to go further, bring novelty and trends, be the future today, but honoring our tradition and legacy. We are characters in the story of your events.
We are FOODSTORY.
Food is our passion and each event has a unique story! Let us tell the story of your event!
location: Quinta de Monsanto, Estrada da Circunvalação s/n, 1400-061, Lisboa

title: Monte Selvagem
short_description: Just 1h away from Lisbon, in Lavre, a park with wild and domestic animals and many games for the whole family ! Visited by foot and by tractor, is an excellent place to contact with Nature!
full_description:
Just one hour away from Lisbon, in Lavre, municipality of Montemor-o-Novo, district of ÉVORA, a perfect combination between a Biodiversity Reserve, a project of Environmental Education and a Leisure Park! In the beautiful ALENTEJO and committed to animal welfare and environment it provides a unique leisure time in close contact with Nature. Learning about animals and wildlife, jumping on the giant trampoline, resting in the shade of a cork tree or climbing the tree houses, are some of the proposals for an unforgettable day for all ages!
(Walking tour -Tractor Ride - Picnic areas - Snack-bar - Shop; Open from February to March since 10am to 5pm; April since 10am to 6pm; from May to September since 10am to 7pm; October since 10am to 6pm; It closes from November to January and on Mondays)

title: Sintra Tram
short_description: Discover the romanticism of the last century on an obligatory trip from the Sintra hills to Colares, with green vegetation and the smell of the sea air all around.
full_description:
The Sintra tram is an ode to romanticism and a wink to Lisbon’s past. It links Sintra to Colares, via the serra, along a calm route ending at Praia das Maçãs.
The winding and panoramic route compels you to adopt a calm and contemplative mood.
Construction began in 1902 and after an unsettled period is now operating again at the weekends during the season.
The old trolleys are as romantic and elegant as ever and the descent from the hills provides plenty of photo opportunities and unique panoramas.
The rural route and the 190-metre descent forces the journey to be slow, so that you can appreciate this old and original form of travel even more.
location: Vila Alda - Rua General Alves Roçadas, 2 - Estefânia, 2710-527, Sintra

title: Sesimbra Oceanfront Hotel
short_description: Sesimbra Oceanfront Hotel, a panoramic hotel with prevailing view over the Atlantic Ocean, enjoys a privileged location on the beachfront of Sesimbra.
full_description:
Sesimbra Oceanfront Hotel, a panoramic hotel with prevailing view over the Atlantic Ocean, enjoys a privileged location on the beachfront of Califórnia.
With direct access to the beach, it offers 84 rooms and 8 suites of good dimensions.
The balconies facing the sea promise to enchant you with superb views, in different frames, colors, reflections and unique melodies that only the Atlantic can provide. Nearby you will be able to enjoy several interesting touristic spots.
Visit Sesimbra, take advantage of Golf and delight yourself with the landscape and environmental wealth of Sesimbra area and Arrábida Natural Park.
With Sesimbra Oceanfront Hotel, contemplate an ocean of sensations and immerse yourself in a wave of pure evasion, in the poetry of the moment.

title: Feira na Avenida (Fair in the Avenue)
short_description: Find traditional and auteur handicrafts, antiques, design objects, antique books and collector’s items, alongside organic and gourmet food producers that will delight you.
full_description:
The most luxurious and sophisticated avenue in Lisbon is also the stage for one of the most emblematic fairs in the city, with more than 10 years of history.
Every second weekend of the month, the Feira na Avenida occupies a part of the Avenida da Liberdade with its traditional and auteur handicrafts, antiques, design objects, various antique books and collector’s items alongside organic and gourmet food producers that will delight you.
During the months the sun shines most, the fair also takes place on the fourth Saturday of the month and the preceding Thursday.
There are thus many opportunities to shop on the Avenue, even without going into the most exclusive stores. More information about the fair at www.avliberdade.com

title: TAP AIR Portugal
short_description: TAP is Portugal’s leading airline, and member of Star Alliance, the global airline alliance to offer customers worldwide reach, since 2005
full_description: TAP is Portugal’s leading airline, and member of Star Alliance, the global airline alliance to offer customers worldwide reach, since 2005. In operation since 1945, TAP celebrated 70 years on March 14, 2015, and has completed its privatization process in 2015, with the Atlantic Gateway Group now as new private shareholders of its share capital. TAP hub in Lisbon is a key European gateway at the crossroads of Africa, North, Central and South America, where TAP stands out as the international leading carrier in operation to Brazil. The company’s network currently comprises 83 destinations in 35 countries worldwide. TAP currently operates about 2,500 weekly flights in average on a modern fleet of 66 Airbus aircraft and 21 aircraft in TAP Express livery, operating in the company’s regional network, adding up to a 87 aircraft fleet in the whole.
location: Aeroporto de Lisboa, Rua C - ed 25, Aeroporto de Lisboa, 1704-801, Lisboa

title: Anjos Teixeira Museum
short_description: Discover the life and work of two generations of talented sculptors at the Anjos Teixeira Museum. You’ll find the studio and school bequeathed by Master Pedro Anjos Teixeira.
full_description: When visiting Sintra, discover one of the most important exhibitions of Portuguese sculpture of the 20th century. The house where Master Pedro Anjos Teixeira lived and gave classes has been turned into a house museum bringing together his work and that of his father, Master Artur Anjos Teixeira. The works cover the styles of Naturalism, Realism, Late-Naturalism and Neo-Realism. Combining the technical mastery of anatomy, the works found down this small lane in the centre of Sintra show an exemplary devotion to nature and man, with the professions being one of the key themes examined. Come and discover the work of two generations of an incredibly talented family.
location: Azinhaga da Sardinha, Rio do Porto, Volta do Duche, 2710, Sintra

title: D'Bacalhau | Restaurant
short_description: Our restaurant with Tejo river view is specialized in codfish starters and main courses.
full_description: Restaurant with two floors and terrace and a total capacity of 240pax. It open every day to 12h a.m. to 11h p.m. We have an huge variety of codfish dishes but also fresh fish, shrimp in curry sauce, beef in garlic ... and dishes specially designed for kids.
location: Rua da Pimenta, 43/45, Parque das Nações, 1990-254, Lisboa

title: Andaz Lisbon Hotel
short_description: Discover the pinnacle of luxury at Andaz Lisbon! Unwind in our modern, artfully designed rooms and suites, where local charm meets contemporary luxury for a truly unique stay.
full_description:
Exquisitely positioned at Praça do Comércio, at the very heart of Lisbon, Andaz Lisbon captures the essence of the city’s storied past.
More than merely a hotel, Andaz Lisbon serves as a gateway to the essence of Lisbon, inviting guests to discover its hidden tales and dynamic contemporary pulse. Once home to a medley of residences and financial institutions, this architectural gem now unites centuries of history under one roof. Andaz Lisbon beckons you to immerse yourself in the city's labyrinthine streets, vibrant cultural tapestry, and daring gastronomic delights.
Whether it's art, music, history or the local vibe, Andaz Lisbon is a sanctuary for the curious, those in search of depth, connection, and transformative discovery -
So Get Lost and find your Lisbon.

title: Luggage Storage Lisbon
short_description: Luggage Storage Lisbon, the place to stow your bags in Lisbon. We provide secure baggage storage for your long and short term storage needs.
full_description:
If your flight arrives too early on check-in day or leaves too late on check-out day and your accommodation does not have space to keep your luggage in the meanwhile, just leave it with us and enjoy the beautiful city of Lisbon!
We offer you several other services, like, boarding pass printing, transfer to the airport, souvenirs, drinks, snacks and much more.

title: Sophia Natural Italian
short_description: Inspired by my family’s recipe book, filled with three generations of recipes and memories, I began to re-invent Italian cuisine combining the best ingredients with healthy options and the best cooking techniques.
full_description:
Inspired by my family’s recipe book, filled with three generations of recipes and memories, I began to re-invent Italian cuisine combining the best ingredients with healthy options and the best cooking techniques.
My family recipes are respected but updated to reflect my creativity and beliefs.

title: Teatro Nacional D. Maria II
short_description: One of the country’s most important theatres. Discover an important building which ennobles one of Lisbon’s best known squares with an imposing and cultural style.
full_description: The most important theatre in Lisbon, not only for its monumental architecture but also for the demanding and excellent plays it stages. This venue, which has performed the greatest national and international plays, owes its name to the Portuguese queen who was a passionate fan of the theatre and gave the country’s theatre companies so much support. Razed by a horrific fire, it was rebuilt in the second half of the 20th century. Located in one of the most iconic squares in the country, it’s worth taking a guided tour to also get to know what happens on the other side of the curtain – in the changing rooms and costume and prop workshops, amongst others. You’ll also find a bookshop specialising in the theatre and other show arts. Come and see.

title: You and the sea
short_description: YOU AND THE SEA, SEASIDE HOTEL AND APARTMENTS. Here one lives the proximity to the sea, the beach, the nature and culture that surrounds it.
full_description:
You and the sea Is located in praia do Sul in Ericeira.
Has 36 apartments with hotel service, and typologies from studio to our Five Bedroom Apartment, ideal for those looking for a sea-shore experience with all commodities of city life.
The first impression of anyone entering You and the Sea Hotel and Apartments will be the feeling that this is a different world.
A small reception area takes you to an ample space where the Jangada restaurant, a room with a cozy fireplace, overlooks the sea and the seasonal swimming pool.
During the winter, only glass separates Jangada from the garden facing the sea.
Offers a well-equipped gym for those who want to keep fit, even on rest days. The SPA, with massages, steam room and sauna, offers a relaxing space for those who really want to rest.

title: Lx Fábricas
short_description: Where the best events are made!
full_description:
Fábrica XL and Fábrica L are two key event spaces within Lisbon's renowned LX Factory, each offering unique features for different types of events.
Fábrica XL
is a large space covering around 1,400 m², characterised by its high ceilings, exposed industrial elements and flexible layout, making it ideal for large-scale events such as exhibitions, conferences and concerts.
On the other hand,
Fábrica L
is a more intimate space with a vibrant aesthetic. Its blue-painted walls, tall arched windows and abundant natural light create a lively and inviting atmosphere. Fábrica L also has an outdoor balcony, offering spectacular views of the 25 de Abril Bridge, perfect for any event.
location: LX Factory | Rua Rodrigues Faria, 103 Edificio J, 1300-501, Lisboa

title: Pica-Pau
short_description: We are the kind of place that lovers of traditional Portuguese cuisine just can’t resist.
full_description:
We decided that, in our kitchen, we were going to follow the rules of tradition, as we learned them from Maria de Lourdes Modesto's books. Here, there is no place for inventions, creations or deconstructions.
We are from the time of our grandparents and we remain completely given over to
Pastéis de Bacalhau
(Cod Cakes),
Rissóis de Leitão
(Suckling Pig Rissoles),
Açorda
(Panada) and
Pica-Pau
(Spicy Quick-Fry). This everyday food takes us back to our childhoods and to flavours that everyone here knows so well. As in any Portuguese home, the table is always laid and the doors open to whoever comes to visit. We take care of the rest.
We are the kind of place that lovers of traditional Portuguese cuisine just can’t resist.
Our Dishes of the Day, Snacks and Main Courses are a paean to family recipes from north to south and leave those who visit us wondering if we have used the same recipe book they have at home. We serve with tradition, with unique cocktails made of Amarguinha, Ginginha or Moscatel. These pay tribute to our flavours and traditions, which we want to help everyone get to know.
Gather family and friends around the table. At Pica-Pau we have the possibility to book our exclusive room, with a special menu option, for a more intimate experience.
For more information, please contact us by phone or email.

title: SANA Rex
short_description: Located right in the heart of Lisboa, near the renowned Parque Eduardo VII, SANA Rex Hotel takes its inspiration from the typically Portuguese spirit.
full_description:
Located right in the heart of Lisboa, near the renowned Parque Eduardo VII, SANA Rex Hotel takes its inspiration from the typically Portuguese spirit, offering a welcoming and traditional environment.
SANA Rex Hotel offers a fantastic view over the extensive green area of Parque Eduardo VII, the entire historic area of Lisbon and the River Tejo, which can be appreciated from one of the rooms with veranda or the panoramic meeting and events room.
Near Marquês de Pombal, SANA Rex Hotel is just a short walk from the famous Avenida da Liberdade, the historic centre of Lisbon and the well-known shopping centres El Corte Inglés.
The hotel’s privileged location allows quick access to access routes and public transport, with excellent connections to all points of the city of Lisbon.

title: Selo de Mar restaurant
short_description: The Selo de Mar restaurant is located in the city of Setúbal, a place of fishing and tradition, and we offer not only freshness and excellence, but also a deep commitment to the sustainability of the resources that sustain us.
full_description:
The Selo de Mar restaurant is located in the city of Setúbal, opposite the River Sado, a place of fishing and tradition. The bay of Setúbal is part of the Sado estuary, which is within the limits of the National Ecological Reserve. These waters are home to the natural habitat of the bottlenose dolphin, one of the rarest dolphin communities in Europe.
With its roots in fishing and the Portuguese canning industry, this is a region of immense beauty. To the east lie the breathtaking hills of the Serra da Arrábida and the idyllic beaches of Portinho da Arrábida, while to the south of the Sado estuary lie the Roman complex of Tróia and the paradisiacal beaches of the Tróia Peninsula and Comporta.
At the table
The Selo de Mar project is a living tribute to the traditions of our people, a gesture to rescue the ancient arts of preserving fish.
We respect the natural cycles of the raw materials, in a harmonious dance with seasonality. We choose to work hand in hand with local producers and the treasures of our region, because we believe that it is in simple, authentic gestures that true quality lies.
In this way, we offer not only freshness and excellence, but also a deep commitment to the sustainability of the resources that sustain us.

title: Hotel Avenida Park

full_description:
LOCATED IN THE CENTER OF LISBON, NEAR THE EDUARDO VII PARK.
Hotel Avenida Park offers air-conditioned rooms and a buffet breakfast including coffee, juices and jams in its modern breakfast room.
The hotel is located 150 meters from Parque Metro Station and even close to transport links to the city's main and historical points.
All rooms are simply decorated and feature large windows that fill with light every morning.
The Avenida Park Hotel Suite offers a fantastic panoramic view of Eduardo VII Park.
All rooms are soundproofed and equipped with air conditioning and satellite TV.

Specialized in operating customized tourism programs based on the special interest of our clients, CREATIVE STAR is a DMC & MICE providing high personalized services all over Portugal and islands.

title: Hotel Duas Nações
short_description: Located between the Rossio and Praça do Comércio, Duas Nações Hotel combines traditional architecture with stylish furnishings.
full_description:
There are several terraces in front of the hotel where you can enjoy the magnificent Portuguese café and 24-hour reception.
Located on the corner of Rua Augusta with Rua da Vitória, 50 seconds walk from the Metro Chiado, Duas Nações Hotel offers rooms with satellite TV and WI-FI and a magnificent breakfast room and a living room only.
Duas Nações Hotel is located very close to the Castelo de São Jorge, the Alfama district and the Moorish quarter, as well as enjoying the riverside area of ​​Lisbon with its famous nightlife.

short_description: Located just in the city center, few steps from Eduardo VII Park and Marquês de Pombal Square. With exterior swimming pool, SPA and also a terrace with vertical gardens.
full_description:
With 166 rooms, guests can choose between rooms with panoramic views of the city or luxurious and spacious suites. Room Service available 24 hours.
Continental breakfast served in the Luz Restaurant between 07am and 11am. The hotel also offers a delicious variety of unique gastronomic products that we recommend to taste in the restaurant and in the Boalma Bar.
The hotel has 11 meeting rooms, some of which can be converted into 2 or 3 areas, with a max. up to 250 people. Other services include laundry, 24-hour reception and private parking.
At SPA Sensations you will find 3 treatment rooms where guests can enjoy a heated indoor swimming pool, gym, sauna, Turkish bath, sensory and bi-chemical shower and relaxation area.

title: Museum of Lisbon - Santo António
short_description: Take a break as you climb up to the castle to discover a church devoted to the most popular saint in Lisbon. It was built on the site of his place of birth.
full_description:
When visiting Lisbon, it is easy to forget the small and elegant Baroque church of Santo António after glimpsing the solemn and impressive cathedral in the neighbourhood.
But this church, built in 1767, was erected on the site where King João II had already built a chapel in the mid-15th century because it was believed to be the place of birth of Lisbon’s popular patron saint.
The image of St. Anthony survived the earthquake that destroyed almost everything else, along with the Pedro Alexandrino paintings in the nave of the church and the underground corridor lined with azulejo tiles.
You can visit and learn more about the history of St. Anthony at the Museu Antoniano next door.
After all, the patron saint of lovers celebrated today had an eventful life you won’t learn of in the streets.
location: Largo de Santo António da Sé, 24, 1100-499, Lisboa

title: São Miguel de Odrinhas Archaeological Museum
short_description: Visit one of the largest known collections of epigraphs and an exhibition about the “Book of Stone”, covering 2, 000 years of history, very close to Sintra.
full_description: When you visit Sintra, you’ll see clear signs of the Moorish occupation and the Christian reconquest, but seldom indications of the other civilisations that also settled there. In Odrinhas, you can travel back to the 1st century BC and visit the visible walls and compartments of the Roman ruins, as well as the later structure (3rd and 4th century AD) of what could have been a temple or mausoleum. You’ll also find the church of São Miguel here and a medieval necropolis used from the 12th to 16th century with its graves intact. Beside the ruins, to show all the remains collected in the area, is the museum, which besides having a specialised library, auditorium and study and restoration rooms, also shows all the artefacts found at the ruins. The highlight of these is one of the largest known collections of epigraphs and their exhibition is organised around the “Book of Stone”, which covers 2, 000 years of history.
location: Av. Prof. Dr. Fernando de Almeida, 2705-739, São Miguel de Odrinhas | Sintra

title: National Museum of Natural History and Science
short_description: When visiting Lisbon, discover this Natural History and Science Museum with a garden, amphitheatre, chemical laboratory, observatory and an old riding school.
full_description:
Take the chance on your visit to Lisbon to discover this Natural History and Science Museum with a garden, amphitheatre, chemical laboratory, observatory and an old riding school.
This museum which both arouses and satisfies a curiosity in so many subjects is one of Lisbon’s most emblematic attractions.
It has housed educational establishments since the 17th century, holds dozens of exhibitions of its collections and organises a huge array of activities.
Based on the Real Museu de História Natural, the museum continues to establish links between universities and society in a constant search for knowledge.
Come and discover this unique place, with its extraordinary exhibits, right in the heart of Lisbon.

title: Castle Museum
short_description: If you think the Castle of São Jorge is old, wait until you see the objects and remains of the inhabitants of this hill over the more than 25 centuries of settlements.
full_description:
It’s hard forgetting the view from Lisbon castle. It is one of those things that has to be seen. But it’s even more difficult to take your eyes off it and enter the Núcleo Museológico do Castelo, where you’ll discover who lived here and how before the Christian reconquest.
But it’s worth it. Discover the 500 years of Moorish occupation of the capital, mainly in accounts from the 11th and 12th centuries, at the time of the construction of the castle.
Discover the Moorish Alcáçova and its Praça Nova, as well as the various objects that show the daily life of the inhabitants at the time.
Also discover the various inhabitants who have lived here over 25 centuries through their unique and impressive objects, coins and remains.
As unique as the city which extends into the distance.

title: DoubleTree by Hilton Lisbon - Fontana Park
short_description: Linked to Old Town and the airport by metro. Winter garden with waterfall, on-site Portuguese food, fitness center, meeting rooms, and free WiFi.
full_description:
Discover the unique design of the DoubleTree by Hilton Lisbon - Fontana Park. Created from Lisbon’s 20th-century metallurgical factory, our hotel features original details combined with contemporary geometry. The striking design and relaxing waterfall cascade in our outdoor Winter Garden have earned us several accolades in Design and Decoration since 2008. This cosy area has the perfect environment to relax at any time of the day. Enjoy your meal in a setting of trees that will take you to exotic, faraway places.
Modern, innovative and cosmopolitan, the DoubleTree by Hilton Lisbon - Fontana Park offers you a privileged location in the center of Lisbon. The hotel is located in the heart of the city, close to the historical center and the main attractions of the Portuguese capital as well as shopping areas. From the hotel you can take the metro or bus and discover all the facts about Lisbon. Enjoy the city of Light and walk through Avenida da Liberdade where you will find luxury fashion stores and typical Lisbon’s kiosks. This design Hotel is nearby fantastic beaches, golf courses and the Lisbon International Airport. Close to several museums and typical and historical districts Very well served by all means of transportation, it is the perfect point to set out on a discovery of the city.
Regardless of the purpose of your stay, you will always be welcomed with warm Portuguese hospitality and a DoubleTree signature, warm welcome cookie.

short_description: The Lisbon Best Apartments are the perfect place for those who are looking for a quality and exclusive break in Lisbon.
full_description:
Apart from offering privileged locations, we are committed to the quality, comfort and decoration of our apartments so that you feel well, relaxed and happy during your stay in Lisbon.
Lisbon Best Apartments started in 2014 with two buildings - Chiado Trindade Apartments and São Bento Best Apartments.
In 2016 we opened three new units that offer another 32 apartments in Chiado, and in 2018 we inaugurated the newest lodging unit - Marquês Best Apartments.

title: Altis Avenida (rooftop)
short_description: Glamour and class with a view of the streets of the city is what you’ll find on the terrace of this hotel. The bar and restaurant offer a different way of seeing the city.
full_description:
It’s not just the miradouros that offer great views of the city.
Some can be seen from places which swap garden benches for 1940s-inspired designer settees.
Raising the boutique style to the zenith of urban chic, Altis Avenida offers a unique and sophisticated terrace which combines bar and restaurant so you can savour the view in the best possible way.
Sample some of the finest dishes and wines in Portuguese cuisine presented with an original and impressive twist. While observing Avenida da Liberdade and the surrounding buildings, you can decide where to go shopping next or whether you prefer to stay and watch the night fall over the city in the best company you could hope for: the authentic elegance of the avenue.

title: Sintra Myths and Legends Interactive Centre
short_description: Sintra Myths and Legends invites you to explore the mysterious side of Sintra, on a journey of myths and legends that will take you through time and space
full_description:
The
Sintra Myths and Legends Interpretative Centre
invites you on a journey into the mysticism, secrets and romanticism of the town, by means of its history, music and literature.
Through set pieces, multimedia techniques and sensory experiences, reality and fiction intersect at each of the 17 points along the route.
The project team included architects, set designers, scriptwriters, historians and specialists in audiovisual productions, holograms, sensory effects, augmented reality and 3D films.

title: Parque Linear Ribeirinho do Estuário do Tejo
short_description: Parque Linear Ribeirinho do Estuário do Tejo includes a series of hiking and biking trails, with an approximate length of 6 km, and with new leisure areas.
full_description:
Parque Linear Ribeirinho do Estuário do Tejo includes a series of hiking and biking trails, with an approximate length of 6 km, which connect it to the Parque Urbano da Póvoa de Santa Iria, where you can visit the Núcleo Museológico A Póvoa e o Rio and a replica of Casa Avieira (fishermen’s typical house).
Enjoy new leisure areas, such as a playground, an amphitheatre and outdoor sports facilities (such as a volleyball net, skate park and gym) and a multi-purpose area with the Centro de Interpretação do Ambiente e da Paisagem, the Observatório de Aves and a coffee bar, whose structure follows a «beach» concept.

title: Original Lisbon Tours
short_description: LISBON CHILL OUT FREE WALKING TOUR is a great way to get to know Lisbon. Whether staying for a month or for a day, our Free Walking Tour will always fit in your schedule.
full_description:
Our guides love Lisbon and they enrich each and every tour with their unique knowledge of the town and its history.
The free tour concept operates on a donation system. We trust travelers to give a fair and honest value that is within their possibilities. This 2:30-3:30 hours long, English or Spanish, free walking tour goes deeper than just introducing the famous sites: we tell the city’s history, with amazing and interesting stories and legends. We start off in Chiado from where the tour can head anywhere in town!
Our guides are interesting individuals with different knowledge and points of view, and will arrange the best tour WITH you before it starts!
We're an independent organisation. Any recommendation given during our tour is based on our personal tastes and experience. We won't try to sell you anything and we don't receive commissions from places we recommend.

title: Baixa
short_description: This is where you’ll find the city’s most popular and best-known areas. The historic centre – the Baixa Pombalina, with its traditional shops and museums.
full_description:
This is where you’ll find the city’s most popular and best-known areas. The historic centre – the Baixa Pombalina, with its traditional shops and museums.
The Baixa is a must for anyone visiting Lisbon. Besides containing countless tourist attractions, they also feature a vast number of cultural and food spots that are very popular with the locals.
Baixa, a more traditional and authentic neighbourhood, serves up souvenir shops, famous patisseries and adventurous eateries. Take your time to discover the streets and corners and all their charms.

full_description:

The informal rooms are equipped with free Wi-Fi, cable TV and minibar. Enjoy our balconies. Superior rooms include bathtubs, while suites have separate living areas with sofa beds. Room service is available. Take advantage of our meeting rooms perfect for events.
Offers consist of parking and continental breakfast served in a bright restaurant. It also includes a bar, an indoor pool and a seasonal outdoor pool, plus a sports field.

outdoor swimming pool open from may to october

title: Município de Oeiras
short_description: Oeiras is a place of outstanding beauty, history, mysteries, heritage and innovation. Discover the town that embraces the Tagus River and Culture.
full_description:
Oeiras: where the dream takes you! Oeiras is a town bordering Lisbon, Cascais, Sintra, Amadora and the Tagus River.
With approximately 172 000 inhabitants and the largest number of graduates in the country, in Oeiras there is history, gardens and innovation or destinations such as, the Poets' Park, the Palace of the Marquis of Pombal and its gardens, the Royal Estate of Caxias, the Oil Mill, the Casal da Manteiga wine cellar where our awarded wine Villa Oeiras is produced, the Anjos Palace where we can find the Manuel de Brito Art Centre, the Egypt Palace, the Gunpowder factory, the Forts that embrace the Tagus River, the Churches and the Chapels, our business parks and the Yachting Harbour.
Places where History, Art, Culture and Innovation cross and complement themselves.
location: Paços do Concelho - Largo Marquês de Pombal, 2784-501, Oeiras

title: Maritime Museum
short_description: The Maritime Museum is a World of Discoveries. It reflects the importance of the experience and knowledge that the pioneering Portuguese navigators brought to the world.
full_description:
In 1863, King Luís founded a Museum in honor of the Navy which for over 60 years has been installed in the Jerónimos Monastery. After all, it was very close to this place, that in the fifteenth century three Portuguese princes left the Tagus (river) waters to conquer "beyond the sea".
From the great adventure of the Discoveries to the evolution of the commercial and war navies are the motto of the permanent exhibition of the Maritime Museum, which has an impressive collection of over 23,000 pieces.
Knowing the history of the Portuguese in the sea, it is the best way to preserve the deeds of yore. Between boat models, maritime instruments, stunning replicas and real boats is a “World of Discoveries” waiting for you.

title: National Museum of Costume
short_description: The museum’s collection encompasses costumes and accessories from the 17th century to the present day, exhibited to the public both in its permanent exhibition and in temporary exhibitions.
full_description:
The National Museum of Costume opening to the public in 1977.
The museum’s collection encompasses costumes and accessories from the 17th century to the present day, exhibited to the public both in its permanent exhibition and in temporary exhibitions.
The Museum is housed in the Angeja-Palmela Palace, named after the two families who owned the property, including its adjacent Monteiro-Mor Botanical Park.
Its mission is to build a strategy for research, conservation and dissemination of costume and textiles, as well as to set out a strategy for safeguarding the Botanical Park of Monteiro-Mor and promoting the binding of this entire heritage to the community.
Save Green is the concept that integrates the values of responsibility and sustainability, whilst also including the management of the cultural and natural heritage for which the National Museum of Costume is responsible.

full_description:
The Perfumes & Companhia store at Rossio, in the city center, has an architectural structure from the eighteenth century.
The Pombaline archs were built in the reconstruction of Lisbon after the 1755 earthquake, being part of Portugal’s history. Admire our store’s architecture and take a moment to enjoy beauty, from makeup to fragrances and skincare you will find the best brands.
Also find tailor-made service, promotions and up to 17.5% tax-free refund! Welcome!
location: Praça Dom Pedro IV, 45/50, Rossio, 1149-069, Lisboa

title: Penha Longa Resort
short_description: Penha Longa Resort, Luxury Hotel and Golf Resort in Portugal
full_description:
Located within a 545-acre national Park on the southern Sintra Mountains, Penha Longa Resort is a breathtakingly beautiful location which encompasses a luxurious hotel, a nineteenth century palace originally founded as a monastery in the fourteenth century, 27 spectacular golf holes, 8 exceptional dining facilities and a stunning Spa.
Once the retreat for the Royal Family of Portugal, Penha Longa lies just 16 miles (30 minutes drive) from downtown Lisbon, or a ten-minute drive from the beaches of Estoril and Cascais.

title: Quasar
short_description: Quasar has managed to become one of the leading travel agencies in the country with best quality and services reputation.
full_description:
Quasar was founded in 1985 with the sole aim of dealing in inbound tourism for Portugal.
Thanks to the expertise and enthusiasm of its staff, has managed to become one of the leading travel agencies in this country with a reputation for the best quality of services rendered.
Of course Portugal has a rich heritage of hosting, stylish hotels an uncompromisingly healthy and genuine cuisine, impressive monuments, all enhanced by an admiringly mild and sunny climate.
But it takes a deep knowledge of the local product and a sense of innovation allied to competitive rates and stimulating ideas to make of any travel a tailor made event and a fully rewarding one.
Do not hesitate to confront us with your client’s wishes.

title: Water Museum - Patriarchal Reservoir
short_description: Hidden underground the garden of Príncipe Real, you can find the Patriarchal Reservoir, whose construction was completed in 1864. Displays an octagonal shape, to coincide with the lake drawing at the top.
full_description:
Hidden underground the garden of Príncipe Real, you can find the Patriarchal Reservoir, whose construction was completed in 1864.
It was one of the first tanks of the distribution network, designed in 1856 by French engineer Louis-Charles Mary, which enabled the supply of water to the inhabitants of Lisbon.
The Patriarchal Reservoir, initially fuelled by Águas Livres Aqueduct and from 1890, by the waters of the Alviela Aqueduct, displays an octagonal shape, to coincide with the lake drawing at the top.

title: Euro Segway Lisbon
short_description: Discover the highlights of Lisbon on a thrilling Segway ride with a private local guide fully dedicated to provide you the best experiences in the city.
full_description:
Euro Segway has more than 10 years of experience in several European cities and our love for Segways has arrived to Lisbon to provide the most exciting and enjoyable tours, as always with the high standard and dedication our experience can offer.
From teaching how to ride, to showing you around town, you can always expect a quality service with a local guide that can tailor your visit according to your desires, making all this exploring funnier than ever – we're always available to take photos or make videos that will make your experience memorable and your visit to Lisbon unforgettable.
You can find more information at www.eurosegway.pt

full_description:
A space where the city’s urban rhythm meets an international energy, setting the stage for memorable nights.
Best known for its Friday and Saturday programming, Rive Rouge stands out for its carefully curated music, immersive atmosphere and high-quality service, attracting both local and international audiences.
Beyond its regular nightlife programme, the venue also hosts special parties, corporate events, brand activations and private celebrations, positioning itself as a versatile and dynamic space within Lisbon’s nightlife scene.

title: Evolution Valbom Hotel
short_description: Discover the new EVOLUTION Valbom a 4-Star Hotel with a lifestyle & pet-friendly concept in the heart of Lisbon that will win you over as soon as you arrive. Vibrant, urban, and tech-friendly, it stands out for the quieter atmosphere of the cosmopolitan area where it is located.
full_description:
EVOLUTION VALBOM
A LIFESTYLE HOTEL CONCEPT IN LISBON
Discover the new EVOLUTION Valbom a 4-Star Hotel with a lifestyle & pet-friendly concept in the heart of Lisbon that will win you over as soon as you arrive. Vibrant, urban, and tech-friendly, it stands out for the quieter atmosphere of the cosmopolitan area where it is located and for the relaxed attitude that breaks with the routine of a conventional stay.
This Hotel in Lisbon embraces the city and its latest trends, located in the centre of culture and entertainment. Whether you are exploring the elegant Avenidas Novas area, immersing yourself in the nearby cultural centres, or enjoying the enchanting green spaces, you will find yourself surrounded by everything that makes Lisbon so special.
Experience ultimate comfort for both leisure and work, featuring coworking spaces, a fitness room, and a modern GastroBar concept with a buffet and a Grab&Go service open 24/7. To top it off, unwind at the end of the day with music, delectable snacks, and refreshing drinks on the hotel rooftop, while enjoying the panoramic open-air view.
About 15 minutes from the airport, near the underground and with bicycle racks nearby, the EVOLUTION Valbom Hotel is also close to the emblematic Parque Eduardo VII, the Calouste Gulbenkian Museum and the El Corte Inglés Shopping Centre. Perfect for both leisure and business travel.
On arrival at the hotel, after passing through the practical self-check-in kiosks, the modern atmosphere of the EVOLUTION Valbom Hotel invites you to relax.
The open space The Living Room & The Kitchen offers a Gastrobar concept, where you can have a drink and enjoy a delicious meal in the international cuisine restaurant. And of course, there is always the practical Grab&Go service, with sandwiches, snacks and drinks, available 24 hours a day, because hunger has no appointment!
On the rooftop, the most desired space in the evening: The Upper Deck, a trendy rooftop bar with a refreshing water mirror and a fabulous panoramic view over Lisbon, offers entertainment at the end of the day.
To relax, all 111 rooms and 2 suites are distinguished by the elegance of beige and brown tones, creating an atmosphere of comfort. Modern, bright and well-equipped, they offer the essential serenity after a day in the city.
The Hotel also has a well-equipped gym, open 24 hours with everything you need for your workout.
The spacious co-working space and a meeting room with a capacity of up to 12 people offer free Wi-Fi and the best conditions to be able to work alone or in a team.

title: Royal Treasure Museum. Crown Jewels
short_description: A Museum with unique items that is really worth visiting.
full_description:
The permanent collection of the Museu do Tesouro Real/ Joias da Coroa (Royal Treasure Museum/Crown Jewels) displays a great number of the former assets of the Portuguese Royal Family, including artworks that belonged to the Crown, as well as several items that belonged to private collections of various members of the Portuguese Royal Family.
The Museum has eleven exhibition areas.
The visit starts with Ouro e os diamantes do Brasil (Gold and diamonds from Brazil), where raw pieces of rare metals and gems are displayed, followed by a set of Crown coins and medals.
The Jewels on display are from many different sources that belonged either to the Crown or to private collections of different members of the Portuguese Royal Family.
The space dedicated to the Ordens Honoríficas, 4º núcleo (Orders, Decorations and Medals of Portugal, 4th exhibition area), has an unparalleled national collection, a centuries-old testament to the constantly busy international relations of the Portuguese Court.
In the section Insígnias Régias: Objetos Rituais da Monarquia (Royal Insignia: Ritual Objects of the Monarchy), some of the most symbolic and valuable Crown jewels are exhibited, such as the decorations of the Three Military Orders of Portugal – Christ, Avis and St. James – the royal crown, the sceptres and two remaining mantles that were worn by ruling monarchs throughout history.
As for engraved silver objects that were manufactured for civilian use, a set of 16th-century Portuguese silver salver trays and plates are particularly noteworthy. These items were part of the so-called Prata de Aparato da Coroa (The Crown Silverware), and will be displayed to the public for the first time, featuring a set that has been unseen since the late 18th century.
Two Golden Roses (a gold ornament traditionally blessed by Popes of the Catholic Church every year) will be on display in the space dedicated to Ofertas Diplomáticas (Diplomatic Gifts).
The tenth exhibition area is fully dedicated to the Baixela Germain (Germain’s Sterling Silver Serving Set), bearing the name of the famous French silversmith,  François-Thomas Germain, which was commissioned after the Lisbon Earthquake of 1755. It is the only known complete sterling silver serving set in the world.
The visit ends with the Viagens do Tesouro Real (Travels of the Royal Treasure), which highlights the mobility of the Portuguese treasure in all its glory as well as vicissitudes.
The Museum also has a coffee shop, a lounge and store that sells items related to the Museum’s collection.

Tram

title: Pilar 7 Bridge Experience
short_description: An interactive and immersive experience that allows an unique discovery of the Ponte 25 de Abril.
full_description:
A new cultural attraction in Lisbon!
Located in Alcântara (Avenida da Índia), this interactive centre allows all visitors to have an unique experience of the bridge – considered one of the prettiest in the world – via a tour that takes in the exterior areas of this key pillar and the sensory experience of visiting its interior.
It is a trip through the history of its construction and ends with an elevator ride up to a panoramic viewing point that provides an unrivalled view of the city and the river.
Making use of modern multimedia devices, your visit ends with an immersive virtual reality experience of parts of the bridge that are inaccessible and a call at the PhotoBooth so you can record this unforgettable moment.
Additional Information
• SCHEDULES: From 10:00h to 18:00h
* Closed on December 25th.

- Hop on Hop off Travel Circuits, Monumental Belém Circuit.

title: Santa Marta Park
short_description: Make the most of the cafés, bar and restaurant to appreciate the best Ericeira has to offer: the sea right by your side and the cleanest air you’ve ever breathed.
full_description: In the heart of Ericeira, the only surfing reserve in Europe, beside the most famous waves in Portugal, you’ll find a place that’s for those who like an adventure, but wouldn’t dare face the capricious Atlantic that laps the cliffs. The Parque de Santa Marta is in Largo de Santa Marta, beside the chapel built in 1760. With an amazing view over the Furnas area and the Praia do Sul, this wonderful spot allows a calm way to make the most of all the town’s rays of sun and sea air. You’ll find a skating rink, tennis courts, a mini-golf course and a playground, as well as the chance to enjoy the shade and green areas to read and walk. Make the most of the cafés, bar and restaurant to appreciate the best Ericeira has to offer. This incredible spot is just two steps from Mafra and only a few kilometres from Lisbon.
location: Largo de Santa Marta –, 2655-357, Ericeira | Mafra

title: Baixa House
short_description: BAIXA HOUSE is a new and singular type of accommodation in Lisbon: well-appointed serviced apartments with daily cleaning, fresh products for breakfast and permanent support to satisfy further needs travellers may have.
full_description:
Baixa House is the place to stay in Lisbon
Where else to stay in Lisbon? Baixa House
Baixa House is a charismatic five-star apartment building located in the heart of one of Lisbon’s historical districts, Baixa (Downtown), just a 5-minute walk from Praça do Comercio, Praça da Figueira and Rossio, 10 minutes from Chiado and right on the route of tram no. 28.
Baixa House is the perfect place to stay in Lisbon if you want to enjoy the city’s charms to the full.
Following a thorough refurbishment, this 18th-century building, erected after Lisbon’s devastating 1755 earthquake, is now opening its doors to travellers from all over the world looking for a place to stay in Lisbon.

title: Museu da Marioneta (Puppet Museum)
short_description: This very particular world is worth discovering, a place where the art of storytelling and acting, one of the first popular art forms, is told like a tale.
full_description:
The Museu da Marioneta is located in Madragoa, one of the areas of the city you must visit while staying in Lisbon.
Housed in the convent of Bernardas, the museum provides the chance to visit a 17th-century building and the country’s largest collection of traditional Portuguese puppets.
The museum also has an amazing series of African and Asian puppets and masks from the Francisco Capelo collection.
The puppets and the various ways they are used are documented and explained here, from folk representations in fair scenarios to erudite contemporary theatre.
This very particular world is worth discovering, a place where the art of storytelling and acting, one of the first popular art forms, is told like a tale.
location: Convento das Bernardas, R. da Esperança, 146, Madragoa, 1200-660, Lisboa

title: Vila Franca de Xira Municipal Museum
short_description: This museum offers a window into the life of the town of Vila Franca de Xira.
full_description: The Vila Franca de Xira Municipal Museum, which is housed in an 18th Century house, aims to offer visitors an insight into communities past and present and to serve as a repository for local memories. The museum’s main work involves researching, conserving, collecting, communicating and educating. The museum exhibits and disseminates its collections in a cross-disciplinary format. It serves the interests of local populations by building a close relationship with them and investing in the setting up of a network of museum spaces that are located in different parts of the municipality.
location: Rua Serpa Pinto, 65, 2600-263, Vila Franca de Xira

short_description: The hotel Vila Galé Ópera is located by the Tagus River, the Lisbon Congress Centre and 10 minutes from the city center.
full_description:

This hotel in Lisbon, renovated in 2014, has a modern décor inspired by the opera and many of its rooms offer a unique view of the Tagus River.
The hotel offers an area for meetings and banquets, a spa with indoor pool, gym, sauna, Turkish bath and massage rooms, a restaurant and a bar with live opera performances two days a week.

short_description: Make the most of a business trip or weekend getaway in this comfortable and modern Lisbon hotel, away from the hustle and bustle of the center of the capital, but with privileged access to it.
full_description:
The Holiday Inn Express Lisbon Alfragide offers modern air-conditioned rooms with a private bathroom. It features a lounge bar, 2 bright meeting rooms and a 24-hour front desk. Lisbon city center is a 15-minute drive away.
Each stylish, elevator-accessible room features a seating area with a flat-screen TV, a desk, free WiFi and tea/coffe making facilities. The private bathrooms come with a shower and a hairdryer. Some rooms have a sofa bed.
A continental buffet breakfast is served daily in the hotel. Guests can also lay back in the comfortable armchairs of the 24-hour lounge bar, that serves Portuguese drinks and snacks. Local restaurants, cafés and the Alegro Alfragide shopping center are within 10 minutes’ walking distance from the Holiday Inn Express.
Belém Tower, Padrão dos Descobrimentos is within a 15-minute drive away. Lisbon's Humberto Delgado International Airport is within 11,4 km from the Holiday Inn Express Lisbon Alfragide.

title: Valverde Sintra Palácio de Seteais
short_description: The Valverde Sintra Palácio de Seteais is a real XVIII century palace located in the town of Sintra, UNESCO World Heritage and enjoys privileged views of the mountains and sea.
full_description:
This luxurious and romantic palace takes you back to the elegance and style of XVIII century architecture.
On the mountainside of Sintra, UNESCO World Heritage, with spectacular views of the Moorish Castle and Pena Palace, enjoy the magnificent surroundings of lush gardens and spectacular scenery.
The rooms are decorated with furnishing of extraordinary wealth while the ballrooms with paintings, tapestries and frescos of rare beauty make this hotel a unique setting to enjoy an unforgettable experience.
Stroll through maze like lush gardens or admire the view from the Seteais Restaurant.
Rediscover this charming hotel next to the Moorish Castle, remodelled to surprise.

title: Capuchos Convent (Sintra)
short_description: Discover one of the calmest and most contemplative convents in the Sintra hills and Lisbon region. The Convent dos Capuchos awaits you.
full_description:
Discover the most surprising convent in the Lisbon region. In the heart of the Sintra hills, the Franciscan friars took their vows of abnegation and poverty to the extreme and built a convent in the rock decorated with cork, the preeminent example of withdrawal from the world and the comfort associated with life on earth.
This convent was founded in 1560 by D. Álvaro de Castro and given to the Fransican monks of Arrábida.
The extreme austerity of the rooms and the apparent labyrinthine construction will amaze you.
The courtyards and the view of the convent are unique, as well as the surrounding wood, which has been untouched for centuries.
Enjoy this place of contemplation and spirituality to stay for a while and feel the extreme peace that seems to bless this spot.
An unforgettable experience.
location: EN 247-3 - Estrada dos Capuchos, Colares, 2705, Sintra

title: Convent of Capuchos (Almada)
short_description: This 16th Century religious building, erected by Franciscan monks, is now used for cultural activities.
full_description: Although this 16th Century religious building, erected by Franciscan monks, has been fully restored, its original character has been retained. The interior remodelling has resulted in a modern cultural space that is particularly suited to music. There is also a room for temporary exhibitions. The church is noteworthy for its paintings of St. Francis and St. Domingos as well as the 18th Century tile panel. Visitors can also enjoy the beautifully cared for gardens and the viewpoint, which offers spectacular panoramas of Costa de Caparica, Lisbon, the Sintra Mountains, Cape Espichel, St Julian’s Tower, Bugio and Cascais bay.
location: R. Lourenço Pires de Tábua, 2825-014, Caparica | Almada

title: Water Museum - Águas Livres Aqueduct
short_description: The crossing of the Alcântara valley, stands out along its 941 meters long and with arches that reach the 65 meters of height. National monument.
full_description:
Built between 1731 and 1799, the Águas Livres Aqueduct was a vast system of collection and transportation of water, through gravity, which comprises a total of 58 kilometers of existing pipelines between the springs, located 15 kilometers to the northwest of Lisbon and the water standposts in the capital.
In its path, the crossing of the Alcântara valley, stands out along its 941 meters long and with arches that reach the 65 meters of height.
The route is open to visitors between Campolide and the Parque Florestal de Monsanto. It has been classified as a National Monument since 1910.

title: Hotel Sofitel Lisbon Liberdade
short_description: Sofitel Lisbon Liberdade is a luxury hotel where supreme style reigns. Located in downtown Lisbon.
full_description:
Launch yourself upon Lisbon's seven hills and flowing Tagus river at Sofitel Lisbon Liberdade, like an explorer searching for new sensations.
Experience ocean chic, the tast of colors, the excitement of discovery on Lisbon's most glamorous Avenue.
A few minutes from the Airport, Sofitel Lisbon Liberdade is right in the heart of the city, near everything.
Venture out of the Sofitel Lisbon Liberdade on foot and explore the bustling district Bairro Alto or Alfama quarters and stop to relax in the botanical gardens.
From the most sumptuous suite with terrace to the guest room, comfortable space has been created to ensure total serenity throughout your stay.
Suites & rooms
Let your wandering spirit settle awhile amid timeless European elegance in black and gold, and savour views of the vibrant city. Elegant bathrooms with exquisite amenities make revitalisation luxurious. Our Junior Suite is ideal for families, or if you’d like extra space for your Lisbon sojourn.
Restaurants & bars
Take your seat in our cultured dining room or on the shaded terrace. Our menu presents soul-warming Portuguese dishes with inspired flourishes, imagined by our visionary Head Chef. Rare produce comes from natural environments around Portugal. In-room dining is available in your luxe room or suite.

title: Casa Fernando Pessoa
short_description: Discover the home and work of Fernando Pessoa, one of Portugal’s greatest poets. Find out how he lived and where he wrote; and imagine what he saw from his window to feed his inspiration.
full_description:
Casa Fernando Pessoa is the house that was inhabited by the writer during the last 15 years of his life.
It has an exhibition on three floors about the poet's life and work and a library specialized in world poetry.
It is a place of literature that crosses memory, literary creation, and reading.
In 2021, Casa Fernando Pessoa was distinguished by the Portuguese Museology Association with the Best Portuguese Museum Award.
location: Rua Coelho da Rocha, 16/18, Campo de Ourique, 1250-088, Lisboa

title: Pizzaria Zero Zero - Belém
short_description: The pizzeria that brings Italy to Lisbon! To enter ZeroZero - Belém is to forget the city for a brief moment and immerse yourself in the heart of Italy.
full_description:
PIZZAS
With a balanced mix of Flour 00 and other flours that are milled slowly on stone, at Pizzeria ZeroZero the dough for our pizzas is prepared using the indirect method, also known as indirect crusting or poolish. We adopt an ancient process that begins with a pre-fermentation at a controlled temperature, followed by a maturation of at least 48 hours.
The end result is an artisan pizza base, thin and crunchy, fragrant like the best freshly baked bread, light and digestible.
MENUS
From classic to speciality pizzas, fresh pastas and creamy risotti, the menu is very varied and also includes several vegetarian options. All the dishes are written in Italian and offer a real journey through the flavours of Italy.
A meal at ZeroZero can start with some antipasti, such as Carpaccio di Tonno and Panzanella, and is never complete without a coppa di gelati or a dolce, such as the typical tiramisù.
ITALIAN CHARCUTERIE
Also at the entrance, there is a small, distinctive window display with some of the best Italian products used in the pizzeria. Cheeses and sausages, all DOP or IGP, which can be taken home or served on the spot, accompanied by a glass of prosecco.
location: Edifício Espelho d'Água, Avenida Brasília S/N, 1400-038, Lisboa

title: Ah, Amália - Living Experience
short_description: An immersive biographical story dedicated to Amália. A journey of emotions hand in hand with one of the most inspiring women of all time. An experience that crosses all the senses and emotions - the sound, the visual, the virtual, the immersive and the unexpected.
full_description:
The first immersive biographical story dedicated to a national personality.
A journey of emotions hand in hand with one of the most inspiring women of all time. A dynamic and everlasting experience that will remain in the memory, beyond the boundaries of time and space.
An experience that only makes sense to us: sensorial, alive. One that crosses all the senses and emotions - the sound, the visual, the virtual, the immersive and the unexpected.
Over 700m2, in the city's new cultural centre in Marvila, we invite visitors, guided by the fado singer's voice, to embark on a unique journey through the artist's material and immaterial estate, scenographic installations, cutting-edge technology, dynamic and interactive content, combined with a storytelling that combines the physical with the digital.
In the eight rooms of this exhibition, we combine different thematic environments, where you can get to know Amália in her various dimensions - artistic, social, personal and international.
What you'll find
Virtual Reality
Crossing a high-impact scenic installation with a virtual reality experience, the physical and digital dimensions come together to provide a unique experience in which you can live the Amália of yesterday, today and forever.
360º Room
In this room we get to know Amália through an immersive 360º experience, using multimedia and videomapping, allowing the viewer to feel part of this "Strange Way of Life".
Hologram
With a full-scale hologram and scenography that replicates the Olympia stage in Paris, the public will have the unique opportunity to witness a performance by the fado singer.
Ah, Amália - Living Experience brings together SP Entertainment and the Amália Rodrigues Foundation for the voice of a culture.  This exhibition would not be possible without the inexhaustible support of the Amália Rodrigues Foundation, whose mission is to promote and publicise Amália's contribution to the affirmation of national identity.

title: National Cordoaria
short_description: The Vasco da Gama Aquarium, located at the gates of Lisbon, is not only an Aquarium, it is also a Museum. A Museum born of a prince's dream and his ambition to know the depths of the ocean.
full_description:
Visiting the Vasco da Gama Aquarium is to allow yourself to board the Yacht Amelia, belonging to the Portuguese royal family, sailing along the coast of Sesimbra in the company of the then King of Portugal, Carlos I. From the ship's logbook to the Vasco da Gama Aquarium, in a trip between the depths of the sea and the sea slopes where we can contemplate only by sea.
It´s an institution of international interest wich qualifies trainers and taxidermists from across the globe, and strives to continuously improve its important role as a Living Natural History Museum.
It´s possible to observe rare specimens and other biological specimens, mainly molluscs, freshwater fish, aquatic birds, turtles and marine mammals. Has also specimens of other species, especially with regards to native and tropical fauna marine fish and both live and stuffed malacological specimens.
The Vasco da Gama Aquarium is one of the oldest museum aquariums in the world and has been visited by successive generations of families since its opening on May 20th, 1898. It is a historical heritage that promotes Portuguese aquatic life and it has the private King Carlos I Oceanographic Collection.
After renovations, now has a cafeteria with exterior terrace and an auditorium wich functions as a multipurpose space.

title: Fado in Chiado
short_description: Fado in Chiado is a live Fado Show an excellent way to discover culture and the city.
full_description:
Fado is Portuguese and was born in Lisbon. It was adopted by Portuguese culture and is now a symbol of Portugal, with a strong tradition in the historic districts of Lisbon.
The hour-long Fado in Chiado show features talented artists – a singer and two musicians (Portuguese guitar and classical guitar) – performing Fado and the music that makes it the national song.
The live performances are accompanied by images of unique must-visit locations in Lisbon. It is said that Fado combines nostalgia (saudade), Portuguese pride, hope and conformism in a musical style epitomised by Amália Rodrigues.
On account of these unique characteristics, Fado has now been declared part of the Intangible Cultural Heritage of Humanity by UNESCO. Fado in Chiado takes place from Monday to Saturday in the very centre of Lisbon.
The hour-long show is the ideal suggestion for seeing live Fado and then taking advantage of the night to discover more of the city.
Discover Fado in Chiado and you will find the soul of Lisbon

The history of the development of the republic in Portugal is detailed in chronological order. With oil portraits, personal items and various documents illustrating the history and career of each of the presidents of the Portuguese republic, the museum presents all of the heads of state that have marked the country’s 20th century.
Take the chance to get to know the powers of the president and the history of the founding and first few years of the Portuguese republic.
Discover over a century of history and the important figures who have shaped it.

title: Church of São Domingos
short_description: Between Rossio and Praça da Figueira, discover one of Lisbon’s most emblematic churches which combines both beauty and a sinister past.
full_description: Discover one of Lisbon’s most emblematic churches which combines both beauty and a sinister past. Between Rossio and Praça da Figueira, you’ll find the church of São Domingos, one of the prettiest churches in the city. Built in the 13th century by King Sancho II, successive building work, refurbishments, earthquakes and fires have altered its original medieval structure completely. At this church where all national and royal ceremonies were held, from funerals, baptisms and weddings, history also shows its dark side. This is where those condemned for heresy at the neighbouring Palace of Inquisition would be brought out for public execution in Rossio. The architectural and decorative style of the church of São Domingos is very diverse, mainly due to the restoration required after the earthquake in 1755 and the great fire of 1959. But you’ll still find the high chapel designed by the architect Ludovice and the visible effects of the heat in the cracked stone slabs. An amazing and haunted port of call right in downtown Lisbon.

title: Pestana Rua Augusta
short_description: The Pestana Rua Augusta Lisboa is a hotel for a truly memorable stay, located in a restored iconic building in Lisbon’s Baixa district. This is the perfect starting point for unique experiences in the Portuguese capital.
full_description:
A contemporary hotel located in the centre of Lisbon that encapsulates its thrilling vibe!
This hotel in Lisbon’s Baixa district is located next to the Arco da Rua Augusta, a symbol of eighteenth-century Lisbon. It leads to the Praça do Comércio, facing the Tagus River, one of the most magnificent locations in Europe.
The very lively Rua Augusta is the ideal location from which to explore the Baixa district, with its selection of monuments, renowned boutique shops and vibrant culture.
The rooms at the Pestana Rua Augusta Lisboa are welcoming, comfortable and quiet, with a light, relaxing atmosphere and are completely soundproof. Nearly all of them have a balcony overlooking these historic streets, and some even: have a view of the castle on the hill, where Lisbon was founded.
Designed for city explorers, laid-back couples or lively families, the rooms at Pestana Rua Augusta Lisboa all share one thing in common: a strong link to historic Lisbon.
The hotel radiates nature. Each floor has a distinctive plant or floral theme, in harmony with the urban hustle and bustle. The restaurant Olive serves healthy eating options using local, seasonal produce. The bar features a hidden gem: a mural by the renowned Eduardo Nery, a modern master of ceramic tile art. The terrace on Rua Augusta gives you a front-row seat for watching the city spectacle unfold.

Whether you choose the “turn-key” service or a totally independent use of the venue, MEO Arena Corporate Events is the ideal partner for your project. You can put your trust in our 16 years’ experience helping to produce over 1,800 events.

title: Casa-Museu Leal da Câmara
short_description: Discover the house museum of a unique master in the Lisbon region and take the chance to explore the Sintra area which is always worth a visit.
full_description: Discover the most original house-museum in the Sintra area. Located in Rinchoa, it is home to the art collection of Mestre Leal da Câmara, who lived and worked in this house which inspired his work. The house, which previously belonged to the Marquis of Pombal, functioned as a staging post where horses were changed in the journey between Quinta da Granja do Marquês and the Palace of Oeiras. Mestre Leal da Câmara, who was a painter, caricaturist and demagogue, lived here until his death. Explore the vast collection on display, featuring paintings, furniture, caricatures, drawings, fabrics, ceramics and tiles, as well as the archive of his correspondence and documents. With the Sintra hills in the background, this house is an original port of call during your stay in Lisbon.

title: Lisbon - Cascais By Train
short_description: The best way to discover Lisbon and Cascais on the same trip is to catch the train and let your eyes soak up the views of the coast and the sea.
full_description:
Catching the train that travels along the coast between Lisbon and Cascais is one experience you shouldn’t miss when in Lisbon.
It is also the most practical way to reach the beaches and town of Cascais without worrying about traffic or parking. Leaving Cais do Sodré, it usually stops at the many stations leading to the area’s best-known beaches.
The view is fantastic as the route travels parallel to the river and very often right beside it and, further on, the sea. Cascais station, which is in the centre of the town, is within walking distance of the many attractions this elegant and cosmopolitan area has to offer.
The beauty of the landscape, the short journey and the convenience and safety of the form of transport make the train the ideal way to create your own love story with the line.
