title: Arrábida Nature Park
short_description: Dotted by idyllic beaches and secret coves next door to Setúbal, the Parque Natural da Arrábida represents a peaceful, natural and protected encounter between land and sea.
full_description:
Arrábida has been UNESCO-recognized a
Biosphere Reserve
and now joins this international network that aims to preserve biodiversity, cultural and environmental education as well as local community involvement intersect.
The Parque Natural da Arrábida is one of the most beautiful places you can visit during your stay in Lisbon.
Dotted by idyllic beaches and secret coves and just two steps from Setúbal, a fishing town where the grill always smells of the freshest fish, the Parque Natural da Arrábida represents a peaceful, natural and protected encounter between land and sea. If you explore the various roads, you’ll find forts, museums, convents, hermitages, retreats, palaces and unforgettable beaches.
But to venture further along trails, you’ll need to book a visit with the park services, as they are not open to the public.
The Mediterranean vegetation and wildlife consisting of hundreds of species of butterflies, birds and mammals show the efforts made to protect this area. Put on a pair of comfortable shoes, don’t forget your bathing costume and explore the magic of these unique hills, just a few kilometres from Lisbon.

title: Pastéis de Belém
short_description: In 1837 we began making the original Pastéis de Belém, following an ancient recipe from the Mosteiro dos Jerónimos. That secret recipe is recreated every day in our bakery, by hand, using only traditional methods
full_description:
At the beginning of the Nineteenth Century, in Belém, next to Mosteiro dos Jerónimos (the Heironymite Monastery) there was a sugar cane refinery attached to a small general store. As a result of the 1820 liberal revolution, all convents and monasteries in Portugal were shut down in 1834, the clergy and labourers expelled.
In an attempt at survival, someone from the monastery offered sweet pastries for sale in the shop; pastries that rapidly became known as ‘Pastéis de Belém’.
At that period the area of Belém was considered far from the city of Lisbon and mainly accessed by steam-boats. At the same time, the grandeur of the monastery and the Torre de Belém (the Belém Tower) attracted visitors who soon grew used to savouring the delicious pastries originated in the monastery.
In 1837, the baking of the “Pastéis de Belém”began in the buildings attached to the refinery, following the ancient ‘secret recipe` from the monastery. Passed on and known exclusively to the master confectioners who hand-crafted the pastries in the ‘secret room’, this recipe remained unchanged to the present day.
In fact, the only true ‘Pasteis de Belém’ contrive, by means of a scrupulous selection of ingredients, to offer even today the flavour of the time-honoured Portuguese sweetmaking.

Feel like a king or queen in our themed rooms and apartments.
Privacy, dedicated service and spectacular views make Torel Palace Lisbon a peaceful haven in the heart of Lisbon.

At Torel Palace Lisbon, we look for your well-being. Our goal is to make you feel at home, in an environment surrounded by elegance and good taste.
Our 25 rooms are divided into 6 categories:
Standard, Standard with a view, Executive with a view, Master, Premier with a view and Royal with a view.
In addition,
we have a Villa and five new apartments.
Each room has a distinctive and particular decoration inspired by the Kings and Queens of Portugal, creating a warm and sophisticated atmosphere.
The Royal theme that serves as inspiration gave the name of the Portuguese Queens to all the rooms in the pink building; the name of the Portuguese Kings to the rooms of the blue building and the names of houses of the monarchy to the apartments of the white building.
The individual design of each room at Torel Palace Lisbon reflects our commitment to honoring the history of the two mansions that have been carefully restored.
At the same time, we have modern amenities, mixing the past with the present in the most elegant way.
Welcome home!

title: Museum of Illusions
short_description: Experience the impossible! Get ready to be mesmerized in our Lisbon Museum of Illusions!
full_description:
Enter the fascinating world of illusions!
Discover a brilliant collection of perspective-changing rooms, enthralling installations, and spellbinding images. Our exhibits will teach you that nothing is ever quite as it seems, especially in the Lisbon Museum of Illusions.

title: Lisboa Story Centre - Memories of the City
short_description: Located in Terreiro do Paço, this interactive technology-based facility relates the main events in the city from past to present.
full_description:
Built on the hills bathed by the Tagus, and overlooking the Atlantic Ocean, Lisboa is a fascinating mosaic of memories, stories and influences that still mark the urban landscape of this unique city.
The various events that make up its history and some of its protagonists are now presented at Lisboa Story Centre, where all who visit the Portuguese capital are invited to make a trip back in time and discover memories of Lisboa, since its foundation until present day.
In a playful and interactive way whilst respecting the historical accuracy that a city with Lisboa's past deserves, this interpretation centre takes visitors on a journey through space and time with an audio guide system presenting historically accurate events and dramatic scenes of the city's different eras.

NEYA manages its activity by implementing good environmental and social practices using an efficient management model, thus contributing to sustainable tourism. In addition to comfort and well-being, spaces have a personalized and attentive service, providing all amenities.
Services include meeting and event rooms that are part of the "ECO Meetings" initiative to support the planning of sustainable events. To recharge your batteries, you have at your disposal a Day Spa dedicated to health and wellness.
The Lounge Bar is ideal to relax or enjoy a light meal. The Restaurant Viva Lisboa, in the hotel premises, has an Author Portuguese cuisine that reinterprets Portuguese gastronomy using contemporary techniques and a selection of high quality, fresh and seasonal Portuguese ingredients.
Guests have at their disposal bicycles to explore the city of Lisbon in a free, healthy and environmentally friendly manner.

full_description: Located in Chiado, the Cantinho do Avillez restaurant welcomes you with a natural blend of the traditional and the modern. Quality is the priority with chef José Avillez, and so nothing is left to chance. The cuisine, largely Portuguese inspired, is nevertheless influenced by travels abroad. The menu offers a wide choice of entrées, main dishes and desserts, so that the gastronomic experience can be different at each visit. The service, which is pleasant, contributes to the cheerful climate.

title: CAM – Centro de Arte Moderna Gulbenkian
short_description: CAM presents an extensive renovation with new exhibition spaces, new galleries and a vast garden area, designed by Japanese architect Kengo Kuma, in collaboration with landscape architect Vladimir Djurovic.
full_description:
Inspired by the concept of Engawa, an element of traditional Japanese architecture that establishes a harmonious connection between the interior and exterior, Kengo Kuma, one of the most notable architects working today, recreated CAM's original concrete building, increasing its transparency to the south and adding an impressive 100-metre-long canopy with a ceramic roof.
With renovated exhibition spaces, new galleries and a vast garden area, CAM presents a diverse programme, showcasing its collection in various temporary exhibitions and presenting new projects by contemporary artists, including a live arts programme that features national and international names from the artistic production of our time.
The various exhibitions on display will regularly feature CAM's collection, which comprises almost 12,000 artworks in total, including paintings, sculptures, installations, drawings, prints, photographs and videos/films by some of Portugal's most renowned artists, and an important set of works by international artists.
The renovated CAM building also reflects a new level of sustainability, one of the pillars of the Calouste Gulbenkian Foundation, which has joined the Gallery Climate Coalition, the leading international network of arts organisations committed to creating a sustainable sector through effective climate action.

title: Pousada de Lisboa - Praça do Comércio | Small Luxury Hotel
short_description: The best way to know Lisbon is to immerse yourself in the city and forget the common places. Choose Pousada de Lisboa and feel the local community in the most prestigious location in the city!
full_description:
The surprising Pousada de Lisboa honors the spirit of Lisbon and offers a luxurious urban experience.
Lisbon is trendy and to better live it nothing better than staying at Pousada de Lisboa. Part of Pestana Hotel Group and member of Small Luxury Hotels, allows you to enjoy the noblest part of the city with the river on sight.
The rooms are classically elegant and sophisticated. To drink a glass of champagne on the terrace of the Restaurant Lisboeta is a special experience. The Lisboeta stands out as one of the new trendy hotspots of Lisbon for its food and cosmopolitan and casual chic ambience.
Forget the urban agitation at the Magic Spa and enjoy a massage, the sauna or the Turkish bath. A small pool in the top floor and discreet terrace for sunbathing are a hidden oasis in Downton Lisbon.

title: Interpretation Centre of the Battle of Aljubarrota
short_description: The Interpretation Centre of the Battle of Aljubarrota is located in the military camp of St. George, where the August 14, 1385 Portuguese and Castilians fought one of the most important battles of medieval history.
full_description:
The Interpretation Centre of the Battle of Aljubarrota is located in the military camp of St. George, where the August 14, 1385 Portuguese and Castilians fought one of the most important battles in the history of Portugal.
The Spanish army was numerically superior to the Portuguese , but the Constable Nuno Alvares Pereira and King John I rode a tactical system that led Portugal to victory. In the medieval scale, it is considered an event of the utmost importance political, diplomatic and military.
From the military point of view this confrontation is the tactical innovation , where dismounted men were able to overcome the powerful medieval chivalry; in the diplomatic field, it resulted in the alliance between Portugal and England that lasted until the present day.
In the political aspect , Aljubarrota settled the dispute between Portugal and the Kingdom of Castile and Leon, allowing the affirmation of Portugal as independent kingdom and made ​​it even possible that initiated one of the greatest seasons in the history of Portugal : the Discoveries .
location: Avenida Nuno Alvares Pereira, 120, 2480-062, Calvaria de Cima/São Jorge Porto de Mós

title: National Museum of Archaeology
short_description: The National Museum of Archaeology has important collections of the Portuguese archeology, dating from prehistory to medieval times.
full_description:
The National Museum of Archaeology has a vast collection and was designed, by José Leite de Vasconcelos, to gather material elements that would contribute to the knowledge of the Portuguese people.
Throughout its history, it amassed a remarkable collection, encompassing different themes.
Besides, it also boasts some remarkable archaeological and ethnographic “comparative collections”, along with different legacies and donations.
However, the bulk of its holdings includes vast collections of Portuguese archaeology from the pre and protohistoric, Roman, Arab and medieval times.

title: Almaria Edifício da Corte
short_description: For a holiday, to Study to work or simply because you can, Almaria has the perfect apartments to rest for a short or long period…as you wish.
full_description:
For a holiday, to Study to work or simply because you can, Almaria has the perfect apartments to rest for a short or long period…as you wish.
Wifi, Bath and Bed linen, fully furnished and daily maid service.
The Edifício da Corte consists of 15 apartments (Lofts, T1 and T2 apartments), with areas ranging from 50 to 128 m2, which are named after some Portuguese Kings and Queens.
The Edífico da Corte, although equipped according to the best standards of comfort and modernity, was inspired by the history of the Court and the Royal Palaces, to recreate decorative notes that refer to other times. The signatures and coats of the Kings and Queens that give name to the apartments are present in each one of them. The chandeliers that enrich their ceilings, from the moment the imposing chandelier of the Hall - the chandelier of the Court - are contemporary recreations of the fausto of other times.

title: Maria da Mouraria
short_description: There, where ‘saudade’ is acclaimed as the soul of a people, lies one of the oldest and most authentic secrets of Lisboa: the Mystery Of Fado…
full_description:
At the entrance of Rua do Capelão, a sculpture announces Mouraria, the Cradle of Fado.
This is a narrow street, where time stands still, leaving us to wander its curious alleyways, the windows of the houses eager to share their stories.
It is here where we find the old house Casa da Severa. Severa, a Woman who played the guitar and whose singing evoked pain and melancholy.
That’s why people would say that Maria Severa “used to sing Fado”.
Rebuilt and adapted by the architect José Adrião, it contains a restaurant that serves Portuguese delicacies and is the only Fado House in Mouraria. Fado singer Hélder Moutinho, Bela and Carlos Silva welcome visitors to the restaurant, where the sound of the Portuguese Guitar, the Fado Guitar and Voices can be heard every night, from Tuesdays to Sundays.

title: Solar dos Bicos Restaurant
short_description: Located in the Historic Center of Lisbont, with focus on the traditional Portuguese cuisine.
full_description:
The Solar dos Bicos Restaurant is located in the famous ‘Casa das Varandas’ building, at Rua dos Bacalhoeiros 8 A and 8 B.
Until 1859, Rua dos Bacalhoeiros was known as Rua dos Confeiteiros due to the sweet shops that existed there. Rua dos Bacalhoeiros later owed its name to the cod trade that flourished there.
The Casa das Varandas building dates back to the 16th century and was recommended by King Manuel I in a letter dated 1508.
These elements, together with the Portuguese tile panels alluding to Old Lisbon, make this space unique.

Refresh your senses with a swim in our outdoor pool, a pampering visit to the Spa, or keep fit at our gym.

title: Geographia
short_description: Geographia pays tribute to the gastronomy that speaks Portuguese, with influences from Brazil, East-Timor, Cape Verde, Angola, Mozambique, São Tomé e Príncipe, Macao and Goa.
full_description:
GEOGRAPHIA pays tribute to the richness of Portuguese gastronomy, namely its global character.
In fact, Portuguese cuisine was one of the first fusion cuisines, a product of the influences that the Portuguese brought from the “worlds”.
In the same way, the cuisine of the Portuguese-speaking countries’ (Timor-Leste, Goa, Mozambique, Angola, São Tomé and Príncipe, Cape Verde, Guiné-Bissau, Brazil) was also influenced by Portuguese cuisine, with dishes that blend local traditions and ingredients with those the Portuguese left there.

title: The Mitra or Archbishops’ Palace
short_description: The former summer residence of, first, the archbishops and, then, the patriarchs of Lisbon.
full_description: Tomás de Almeida ordered the building of this summer residence for the patriarchs in 1730. The complex, which is arranged around a square, comprises a palace, a church, gardens and a palace-fountain. It also includes an aqueduct that supplies the Baroque fountain. The Italian architect António Canevari was put in charge of the project. He was certainly responsible for the fountain built into the front of the palace-fountain, where the royal family would reside.
location: Praça Monumental, 2660-119, Santo Antão do Tojal | Loures

short_description: A historic 18th-century palace transformed into a 5-star boutique hotel, where past and present meet and every detail is designed for an exclusive stay, with breathtaking views over Lisbon and the Tagus River.
full_description:
Perched atop the Santa Catarina hill, the hotel offers one of the most stunning panoramic views over the Tagus River and the city of Lisbon. It elegantly blends the authenticity of its historical heritage with contemporary comfort and design, creating an exclusive, sophisticated, and welcoming atmosphere.
The palace has been carefully restored to preserve its original features — wooden ceilings, ornate stuccos, and historic tiles — while incorporating noble materials and details inspired by Portuguese tradition. It offers 18 bright and comfortable rooms and suites, thoughtfully designed to provide peace and well-being.
The common areas include SUBA restaurant, featured in the MICHELIN Guide, The Lisbon Club 55’, and an unmissable rooftop bar with an exclusive 360º viewpoint over the city.
With discreet, attentive, and personalized service, Verride Palácio de Santa Catarina is pet-friendly and stands out for its elegance, exclusivity, and privileged location — a true haven of charm in the heart of Lisbon.

title: Macau Scientific and Cultural Centre
short_description: Important collections of Chinese art and the only historical-cultural Museum about Macau outside the People’s Republic of China.
full_description:
The Museum is the most public face of the Macau Scientific and Cultural Centre through its permanent exhibition, temporary exhibitions and its many other activities.
The use of modern and dynamic language, didactic and interactive language is supported with the latest technologies so as to attract the attention of the young as well as the general public.
The Museum aims to inform the public of the essential condition of Macau as Portugal’s and Europe’s meeting point par excellence with China.
It also strives to highlight the importance of the thousand year old heritage of the Chinese civilization and it is one of the most important collections of Chinese art in the Iberian Peninsula and the only historical-cultural Museum about Macau outside the People’s Republic of China.
See also:
The
Asian Circuit
brings together Lisbon's museums with the most significant collections of Asian art, offering visitors a complementary and comprehensive view of objects from the most varied origins, periods and types, on a journey through time and different latitudes.

full_description:
The Nations Park Gondola Lift was inaugurated for the International Exposition of Lisbon EXPO’98. This installation is endowed with 40 closed cabins with capacity for 8.
The course develops on the estuary of the Tagus River, a few meters from the riverfront.
The South terminal is located on Passeio de Neptuno, next to the Oceanarium and the North terminal is located on Passeio das Tágides, next to the old Vasco da Gama Tower.
The trip lasts 8 to 12 minutes on a course of 1230 meters, hovering over the Tagus River at 30 meters high, where we see a breathtaking panorama of the Nations Park.

With a unique and spectacular view of the ocean, Maré is the perfect setting to enjoy the Raw Bar specialties, such as the Marinated meagre with red onion and avocado, and irresistible dishes such as the Scarlet shrimp, crab and clam rice, the Royal seafood platter, the Butterflied scarlet shrimp with Maré Sauce or simply the best charcoal-grilled seafood and fish, among many other options.
José Avillez
Portuguese and a chef, José Avillez’s mission is to promote Portuguese gastronomy and contribute to making Portugal a top gastronomic destination. He’s considered a national reference in the Portuguese gastronomy and has stood out because of his enterprising spirit.

title: Santiago Fortress
short_description: Located in the centre of Sesimbra Bay, Santiago Fort was part of the line of defences protecting the entrances to the Douro, Tejo and Sado rivers.
full_description:
Located right on the shoreline, in the centre of Sesimbra Bay, Santiago Fort was constructed in the 17th Century, on the orders of King João IV. It was an integral part of the line of defences protecting the entrances to the Douro, Tejo and Sado rivers and was one of the most important strongholds along the Arrábida coast.
The military layout inside the fort has been well preserved, particularly the governor’s residence, the garrison outbuildings, the powder store and cisterns, the chapel and the dungeons.
The fort, which has been used as a cultural venue in recent times, is currently being renovated, so that it can house the future museum of the sea and other cultural centres.

The Lutécia Hotel rooms are spread across floors "Disco","Kiss Me" and "White Space" between others and all include modern and designer furniture, with flat-screen interactive TV’s.
All rooms have an espresso machine and most rooms offer panoramic views over the city. The suites have a fantastic living room and a luxurious bathroom with a whirlpool tub. Restaurant InFusion offers à la carte dining in a modern environment.
The menu includes several vegetarian alternatives. Lutécia’s room service allows guests to enjoy their meals in private. In the morning, you can enjoy an extensive buffet breakfast, including hot food, pastries and fresh fruit. A selection of drinks and light snacks are available at the lobby bar, along with several flat-screen TVs.
Lutécia Hotel is located next to the busy and well known Roma Avenue. Rossio square and down town are a short metro ride on the Green Line.

title: Mother Church of Loures
short_description: With an impressive church and pleasant centre, come and discover the historic side of Loures, a place seldom associated with religious buildings.
full_description: Come and discover Loures, in the less explored suburbs of Lisbon. You’ll find here the land given by King Afonso Henriques to the Knights Templar who accompanied him during the Christian reconquest. And on the site of the small 13th-century Templars’ chapel arose a 16th-century church whose various alterations in the 17th and 18th centuries introduced a profusion of styles and details. The doors are inspired by Serlian architecture, while the chancel inside is Manueline and the altarpiece in the high chapel Baroque. The building as a whole is impressive, with its three naves and vaulted ceiling, while the Tuscan columns are decorated with brutesco dating from 1670. Come and admire the historic side of a part of the Lisbon regions seldom visited by tourists.

title: Take Lisboa Free Tours
short_description: Get to know the authentic Lisbon. All of our Take Lisboa tours will get you off the beaten track and show you Lisbon as it really is, never ignoring its real essence.
full_description:
Tourism in Lisbon flourished in the past couple of years and we look at this booming phenomenon as an opportunity but always aware that the city’s authenticity is the reason why people feel compelled to visit us.
Take Lisboa invites everyone that comes to visit to look and feel Lisbon the way we do. Lisbon is over 3.000 years. 3.000 years of stories, culture, legends and traditions. 3.000 years of conquests and defeats, where new worlds were discovered and all kinds of people blended in a melting pot of unparalleled wealth. We at Take Lisboa tours are passionate about the city’s essence and wish not to change it. The real Lisbon is the one that will amaze you.
This is why we invite you to visit Lisbon by the hand of our genuine local guides. Forget the usual touristic tips, Take Lisboa will be your guide in a memorable journey through our streets, spots, sounds and flavours. We will make you feel as a native lisboner.
We want to show you why we’re so proud of our city. It’s Lisbon’s seven hills and it’s narrow streets always in search for the Tagus River. It’s the reflection of light in the traditional pavement and the dazzling secular architecture. It’s the cheerful melancholy of us lisboneers, the welcome naturalness and the farewell struggle.
It’s the Saudade that sticks to you. It’s being overwhelmed with simplicity and an easy going attitude to enjoy life’s little things. It’s the genuineness of a people that recognizes that greatness comes from humility.
Discover the city with true lisboners, the ones that call Lisbon home.

title: Bairro do Avillez
short_description: The most atypical of Lisbon’s neighbourhoods, where you’ll find a surprising Taberna (tavern), a Páteo where fish and seafood are king, and Beco, a cabaret gourmet.
full_description:
The most atypical of Lisbon’s neighbourhoods.
An entire universe dedicated to the best flavours where you’ll find a terrace, a Taberna (tavern), a spectacular Páteo, Mini Bar, where the music takes center stage, and Pizzaria Lisboa.
Right in the hustle and bustle of Chiado, Bairro do Avillez is a surprising, ample and bright place of 1000 m2, signed by José Avillez.
This is a place dedicated to creativity and the best Portuguese flavours.
Here you’ll find a terrace, a surprising Taberna for those who like to eat well, a Páteo where seafood and fish are king, Mini Bar, a restaurant and gastro bar where the music takes center stage, and Pizzaria Lisboa.
location: Rua Nova da Trindade, 18, Chiado, 1200-303, Lisboa

title: Sado Estuary Nature Reserve
short_description: Discover countless species of birds and several dozen dolphins amid marshes, sandbanks, rice paddies and a vast area of woods and reed plantations.
full_description: If you visit Setúbal and Arrábida, you’ll be surprised by the magnitude of the nature and the balance between hills and sea. But to get to know the nature of this region better, mainly the huge area of the Sado estuary, there’s no better way than to explore the huge wetlands separated from the sea by the Tróia peninsula: the Reserva Natural do Estuário do Sado. Here you can find countless species of birds and several dozen dolphins amid marshes, sandbanks, rice paddies and a vast area of woods and reed plantations. This conservation area is also home to several threatened species, such as the black bat and otter. Ideally, join one of the many cruises that visit the estuary from Setúbal to observe nature in its calmest state. But if you only have time to take the ferry across to Tróia, you may still catch sight of the friendly dolphins. Nature is famous for being generous here.

title: Around Lisbon Tours
short_description: Around Lisbon is not your typical Tour Company, as you may have known them. Our goal is to be your local friends who help you enjoy Portugal to the fullest.
full_description:
Around Lisbon is not your typical Tour Company, as you may have known them. In fact, we don’t like to think of Around Lisbon as a business.
It is rather the special life project of two friends, who wanted to develop a friendly local company to cater to like-minded people from all over the world.
Our goal is to be your “local friends” who help you enjoy Portugal to the fullest. There are wonderful sights around Lisbon for you to discover, and you don’t want to leave Lisbon and Portugal without taking a good look at them.
We built our tours having in mind what we would like to see if we were the ones visiting Portugal for the first time.
We want to go off the beaten tracks and show you those secret hidden places only locals know of.
Although we are well informed about the places we take you to visit, we are not history or geography freaks.
Do not expect to be overwhelmed with loads of historic facts or geological information. Do expect to be guided by someone who loves the fresh air and to swim in our oceans.
Expect someone who can help you understand the habits of our people, pointing to you not only the little things we love about our country, but also those we know we need to improve.
We don’t have luxury vans. But we do care to know your name, where you come from and where you’ve been before in your travels.
We want you to spend a great day with us and your tour partners.
That makes our day too.

title: Torres das Amoreiras (Amoreiras Towers)
short_description: Discover one of the high points in Lisbon. Literally. Come and see the towers that shook up Portuguese architecture in the 1980s and one of the best shopping centres in the city.
full_description:
The Torres das Amoreiras are one of the high points on the Lisbon skyline.
Located at the top of one of the city’s hills, their post-modernist architecture was extremely controversial when built in the 1980s.
The shape of their tops, designed to look like medieval helmets, marked a shift in the way of thinking about architecture in a city in which, until then, the word contemporary barely existed. Nowadays, it is unthinkable to land in Lisbon without catching a glimpse of the towers, part of the 1993 Valmor Award-winning complex, standing proudly at one end of the city. The three towers consist of offices and residential units and a shopping mall on its bottom floors.
This is Lisbon’s inner city mall and one of the busiest places in the city.
It’s worth dropping in to shop and to experience the hustle and bustle.

title: Lisbon Bike Tour & Outdoors
short_description: The Lisbon Bike Tour is the best way to get to know Lisbon.
full_description:
Exploring and sightseeing by bicycle is our passion.
That’s the way we love to travel ourselves!
And, after cycling the world, we want to share with you what we know best: our home town - Lisbon, the beautiful Natural Parks around the capital and our beautiful adopted home – Alentejo, only 130 kms from Lisbon and 8 kms from the World Heritage Site of Évora.
Since 1994 we have been producing activity and travel guides to stimulate the discovery of the Alentejo Region.
In 2003, we started organizing cycling holidays.
In 2007, we opened up our guesthouse Monte do Serrado de Baixo, a beautifully restored traditional farmhouse located in a natural paradise.
In all our Tours, you can expect a very personalized relationship, high knowledge of the region’s culture and heritage, and passionate guides.

title: Tapada das Necessidades (Park of Necessidades)
short_description: The Tapada das Necessidades is one of the best places in Lisbon to walk, read a book or have a picnic. You’ll be enticed by the ponds bordered by exotic plants and extensive grassy areas.
full_description: Beside the Palácio das Necessidades, one of the royal residences most cherished by the Braganza dynasty, you’ll find the Tapada das Necessidades. The name was inherited from the former convent founded here by King João V, on the site of an existing hermitage. Queen Maria II chose it as a home and from then on it was permanently occupied. Beside it, the park is one of the best in Lisbon to walk, read a book or have a picnic. The ponds bordered by exotic plants, the English garden and the various pieces of statuary confer the park with a relaxed air, enjoyed by countless kings and queens and now open to everyone, regardless of whether you have a family crest. It’s one of Lisbon’s hipster hotspots, with concerts and sophisticated picnics that are very popular.

title: TOMI
short_description: TOMI is an interactive smart cities solution that provides information and services in the right place at the right time, and aims to bring cities and people together.
full_description:
TOMI is a unique device: the citizens and tourists don’t waste energy and time looking for information, they find it while walking through the city and interacting with TOMI.
TOMI has four main modules: News, Events, Search, Transports and an additional city marketing one: photo and GIF.
In the News, there is urban Information at micro-local level.
In the Events, it’s possible to find everything happening in the city.
In the Search module, TOMI helps to explore every corner in the city: from places to eat, nearby points of interest, where to sleep, activities to do, and so much more.
In the Transports, TOMI promotes mobility, allowing users to search for routes, how much time it will take to arrive, or how much it will cost.
Learn more: tomiworld.com/lisbon

Located next to Marques of Pombal Square and easy access to a vast public transport network, Hotel Florida is the perfect starting point to discover the most emblematic areas of the city such as Baixa, Alfama, Chiado and Bairro Alto.

title: Clueless Wines
short_description: At Clueless Wines, a welcoming wine tasting studio nestled in the lively Campo de Ourique in Lisbon, we've made a home for those who are intrigued by the world of wine but might feel a bit daunted by its complexity.
full_description:
We are Alex and Nadia, the hearts and minds behind Clueless Wines and our love affair with wine began and grew in the diverse wine regions of Spain, and eventually found its home here in Portugal. The charming streets of Lisbon, the warm smiles of the locals, the rich culture and history – and, of course, the exquisite wines.
Our mission is to create an inviting atmosphere where you can explore, learn, and enjoy the enchanting world of wines right alongside us. We're still learning, still exploring, and we wouldn't have it any other way.
While we appreciate the innovation in the world of organic wines, our affection is drawn more toward traditional wine-making methods. There's something beautifully enduring about wines that carry forward age-old techniques, each bottle reflecting the wisdom and heritage of its origins.
We are delighted to share these authentic expressions from the diverse wine regions of Portugal and Spain. We believe that the true beauty of wine shines through in relaxed conversations, shared laughter, and mutual discoveries.
So, whether you're a seasoned wine lover or you're just starting your wine journey, we invite you to join us. Raise a glass to the joy of discovery, the love of community, and the spirit of adventure. Let's be clueless, together!
location: Rua Tenente Ferreira Durão, 62 B, 1350-318, Lisboa

Designers Peter Townsend and European Golf Design were blessed with a beautiful piece of virgin cork oak forest and have created a layout which would grace any European Tour event, with superb oversized greens and a length of 7,370 yards from the back Tees.
Narrow landing areas also play with the mind for long and straight hitters.
Ribagolfe Lakes
has hosted the first stage of the European Tour Qualifying School since 2009.

title: Hotel DAH - Dom Afonso Henriques
short_description: At Hotel Dom Afonso Henriques we guarantee the best value for money, offering a high level of hospitality quality and outstanding services.
full_description:
Recently remodeled, Hotel DAH - D. Afonso Henriques is Lisbon’s hidden gem, conveniently located just 10 minutes away from both Lisbon’s International Airport as well as the city center (Baixa).
Our contemporary style and welcoming environment is ideal to rest and visit the pitoresque Fado capital. Combining safety with convenience, we have private parking available to our guests.
All our rooms are designed with a sober, charming and contemporary feel providing you with comfort, tranquility and a relaxing atmosphere.

title: Passeio Marítimo de Algés
short_description: Enjoy Lisbon and the River Tagus in all their glory in one place. With a view of two stunning buildings and access to the pure sea air, this walk is ideal for... walking.
full_description:
The Passeio Marítimo de Algés is the stage for festivals that bring tens of thousands of people to this spot. It is one of the best places in Lisbon for sport, mainly running, cycling and skateboarding.
With the river by your side, this area offers a unique view of the estuary and city, as well as several remarkable buildings, such as the Port of Lisbon control tower.
If you want to continue walking, you have access to the Jamor area, dedicated to professional and amateur sport, where you can find the high performance athletes centre.
Come and explore this unique part of Lisbon.

title: Casa da Cerca Viewpoint
short_description: One of the best views of Lisbon, the 25 de Abril Bridge and the Tagus Estuary. Mandatory stay until sunset!
full_description:
Resting in the "Chão das Artes" Botanical Garden or strolling along the balcony that runs along the cliff, enjoy the vast landscape, a new dimension of the Tagus River... a different perspective on Lisbon.
Opening hours:
Tuesday to Sunday 10:00 - 17:30 | Closed on public holidays

title: Liberty Portugal DMC
short_description: Today Liberty Internationals’ main expertise is Destination Management, offering and perfecting its services over 30 years.
full_description:
Today Liberty Internationals’ main expertise is Destination Management, and we are known as a worldwide biggest privately owned DMC (Destination Management Company) offering and perfecting its services since 1991, Liberty exists wherever you want your team or your customers to have an experience of a lifetime.
Intimately acquainted with every destination’s special magic, LIBERTY’s management of Incentives always aspires to create such a memorable program that will maximize your people’s loyalty and feelings of recognition.
Meetings & Symposia: Creating the Perfect Environment
"Perfect" is always relative, LIBERTY strives to make every meeting perfect for YOU!
Whether your meeting is exclusive for selected guests or extending to a couple of thousand attendees we are keen to cater to your needs and your budget.

title: America Diamonds Hotel
short_description: The America Diamonds Hotel is located in the city center, near Marques de Pombal and Parque Eduardo VII. It offers comfortable accommodation in a historic building beautifully restored , contrasting with its modern interior.
full_description:
The America Diamonds Hotel in Lisbon is located in the city center, near Marques de Pombal and Parque Eduardo VII.
It offers comfortable accommodation in a historic building beautifully restored in the heart of Lisbon, contrasting with its modern interior equipped with 66 rooms of different typologies.
The America Diamonds Hotel is a few meters from Picoas metro station and other transport networks, which allows quick and easy access to the center of Lisbon and its main tourist attractions: Baixa, Chiado, Alfama, Bairro Alto, Praça do Comércio, Praça dos Restauradores, Castelo de Sao Jorge, Elevador Santa Justa, Elevador da Gloria and many more.
Our team is composed of experienced professionals that will be your best guide to the city of Lisbon.
If you are looking for a Hotel in Lisbon, this is the right place.

title: Sesimbra Castle
short_description: Come and visit Sesimbra and discover there’s more to visit than just the beach. Sesimbra Castle is an imposing and intriguing place that you’ll love discovering.
full_description: In one of the most beautiful places on the outskirts of Lisbon stands Sesimbra Castle. With a view of the town’s incredible bay, it was built at a time that pre-dates the founding of the Portuguese nation, even though the walls you see when you visit date from 1200. Constructed by King Sancho I in the Romanesque style, it made use of the previous Moorish fortifications, from which it gained its name in the region as the “Castle of the Moors”. The view from it of the amazing landscape of the region, and in particular the Tróia peninsula, Tejo and Sado river estuaries and the impressive Cape Espichel, is unique. In an excellent state of preservation and with original elements still standing – including the church of Nossa Senhora do Castelo, built in 1160 – this imposing monument to the history of Portugal is well worth a visit.
location: R. Nossa Sra. do Castelo 11, 2970, 2970, Sesimbra

title: Legendary Lisboa Suites
short_description: Legendary Lisboa Suites: style, professionalism and quality services.
full_description:
Located in the heart of Lisbon, a few minutes from Praça Marquês de Pombal, the Legendary Lisboa Suites hotel invites you to discover one of the cities with the most history and charm of the Atlantic coast.
Like the city itself, Legendary Lisboa Suites is a surprising hotel. Its elegant and modern architecture and its quality services make this accommodation the ideal choice for those who travel for leisure or business.
The hotel puts at your disposal 57 fully equipped apartments in which it includes 9 studios, 39 suites and 9 penthouses. All of them, under a sober, elegant decoration and design furniture, have kitchenette, Wi-Fi, LCD TV, air conditioning, room service and cafeteria service.

title: Ribeira das Naus
short_description: One of Lisbon’s latest must-visit places. This river beach, with its broad steps, attracts locals and tourists in search of hot sun tempered by the breeze of the river.
full_description: Ribeira da Naus is one of the most interesting places in Lisbon. Recently renovated, this old boatyard is today a surprising river beach, with a large grassy area and broad steps extending down into the river. Even though it is forbidden to bathe, this area linking Cais das Colunas, in Praça do Comércio, to Cais do Sodré is inviting for sunbathing and resting one’s feet after a hard day of pounding the streets. To make it even more comfortable, Ribeira das Naus has a kiosk and outdoor seating to make the most of Lisbon’s light and the best sunset in the city. Have a walk around this totally transformed riverside area. It’s a unique veranda onto the Tagus in a contemporary and attractive spot; a veritable river beach in the town centre, whose charms are to be enjoyed.

title: Cit Global
short_description: From there to Portugal! Cit Global will help you discover the best options here in Portugal, the country with the oldest frontiers in Europe, but always looking for the new trends. This is an incredible country. Let us share our passion with you.
full_description:
CIT GLOBAL DMC
From there to Portugal.
Cit Global DMC has a highly motivated team of experts, committed to the sole purpose of providing for the clients every need.
Our multilingual staff and premier network of partners enable us to deliver the most memorable experiences.
Exceptionally experienced in the organization and support of travel related special events, activities and incentives, our team puts all its knowledge, dynamism, resourcefulness, and vast experience at the client service, granting the total fulfilment of all expectations.
With offices in Lisboa and Porto, Cit Global Dmc is acknowledged and valued in the market as a very efficient DMC, specialists in the art of hosting; FIT’s, tailored made experiences, events & congresses, groups and incentive travel.
location: Rua Prof. Manuel Viegas Guerreiro, 8C, 1600-809, Lisboa

title: Quinta da Regaleira
short_description: Quinta da Regaleira is one of the most amazing monuments of the Serra de Sintra. Located at the end of the historical center of the city, it was built between 1898 and 1910.
full_description:
Quinta da Regaleira is one of the most astonishing and unfathomable monuments of Sintra's Cultural Landscape, classified by UNESCO as World Heritage Site.
Set on the elegant promenade connecting Sintra's National Palace and Palácio de Seteais, the state was purchased in 1893 by philanthropist António Augusto Carvalho Monteiro for his family's summerhouse.
Between 1898 and 1910, in the hands of Italian architect and scenographer Luigi Manini, António Augusto Carvalho Monteiro converted his dominion into an ambicious Total Art project, submitted to a complex landscape and architectural program that materalized the imaginary garden concept, much in vogue since the late 17's.
It is nowadays considered the masterpiece of Neo-Manueline Art from the last period of romanticism in Sintra.

additional_sections.title: Additional Information
additional_sections.text: Closed / fechado 1 Jan./Jan. 24, 25 & 31 Dec./Dez.

location: Quinta da Regaleira, Rua Barbosa du Bocage, 2710-567, Sintra

title: Vasco da Gama Tower Babylon 360º
short_description: Enter an experience 145 meters high and get a new and exciting perspective of the city. A must-see turistic viewpoint in Lisbon.
full_description:
The Vasco da Gama Tower viewpoint is open again decades after its official inauguration at Expo98.
Go up to Portugal’s tallest building, overlooking the Tagus River, which pays homage to Vasco da Gama’s discovery of the sea route to India.
This is a must-see for those looking for things to see in Lisbon, a 360º experience of Parque das Nações.
The tour begins with the discovery of each QR code on the dome’s windows, which contain stories, curiosities about Portugal, tourist recommendations and tips on the panoramic view.
location: Rua Cais das Naus 2.21.01, Parque das Nações, 1990 - 173, Lisboa

title: Beaches of Sintra
short_description: Explore the most beautiful Atlantic beaches in the region, where you’re as likely to spend the day in beach shorts in the sun as a fleece in the café.
full_description: Despite their unexpected mists, the beaches of Sintra manage to be truly enchanting. In Praia da Adraga, Praia Grande and Praia das Maçãs, you’ll find resolutely Atlantic beaches with lots of sand and excellent waves. The pure air, beauty of the hills and the train which descends from the centre of Sintra to the beaches are reasons enough to get to know them. Just minutes from Cape Roca is the mythical Ursa beach. Discover the legend and dare to descend, if you have the right footwear. The tranquillity and the view compensate for the effort at this unique beach. And if the sea won’t play along, you can always go for a swim in the pools at Praia Grande or the picturesque town of Azenhas do Mar.

title: Rua Augusta
full_description:
Rua Augusta is a famous street in downtown Lisbon, which starts at the famous triumphal arch and connects Praça do Comércio to Rossio Square. It honors the august figure of King D. José I.
It has high concentration of commerce, since all the street is flanked by several stores, many of them of great international brands. It has been closed to traffic since the late 1980s and is often occupied by street artists, craftsmen and street vendors.

title: Hotel Londres
short_description: Hotel Londres benefits from a central location. Walking, you can go to Tamariz beach (just 400 meters away), the famous Casino Estoril (500 meters away) and the picturesque Cascais village (15 minutes away).
full_description:
Hotel Londres benefits from a central location.
Walking, you can go to Tamariz beach (just 400 meters away), the famous Casino Estoril (500 meters away) and the picturesque Cascais village (15 minutes away).
If you want to travel to Cascais or Lisbon, the Estoril train station is just a five-minute walk away, where you can find trains with a direct connection to the capital of Portugal.

title: Torre da Igreja do Castelo de São Jorge
short_description: In the heart of the Bairro do Castelo neighbourhood, discover one of the most historically significant churches in Lisbon, climb its bell tower, and let yourself be amazed at the city’s most historic scenery.
full_description:
Discover one of the most historically significant churches in Lisbon, the Holy Cross Church of São Jorge Castle.
Having been closed for more than thirty years, the temple hid many secrets, like, for instance, the statue of Saint George, which has been carried atop a white horse since 1570 on the capital’s oldest procession.
In addition to visiting the church itself, it is nowadays possible to climb the fifty steps of the bell tower.
From the top of this, the highest tower in old Lisbon, an incredible view of both city and river unravels.
It’s a one-of-a-kind experience: to enjoy the wonderful landscape of Lisbon and the Tagus river, to sound the 18th century bells, and then to taste a fine wine or replenish your strength with a cold beverage (in summer) or hot chocolate (in winter).
The experience also includes a visit to the permanent exhibit "Há vida no Bairro do Castelo" (“There is life in Bairro do Castelo”), an homage to the residents of this historic neighbourhood, on show in the church’s choir.
Moreover, in the media room, a short immersive film telling the story of the Bairro do Castelo, the church and the mystery of the Holy Cross can be watched.
location: Largo de Santa Cruz do Castelo, 15, 1100-060, Lisboa

title: lx boutique hotel
short_description: Welcome to the lx boutique hotel, with an atmospheric century-old hotel overlooking the River Tagus (Rio Tejo) with elegance and contemporary style to bring you an unique Lisbon experience.
full_description:
Welcome to the lx boutique hotel, a 4-star establishment in the heart of historical Lisbon.
We have renovated an atmospheric century-old hotel overlooking the River Tagus (Rio Tejo in Portuguese) with elegance and contemporary style to bring you an unique Lisbon experience.
The lx boutique hotel is conveniently located in the gentrifying Cais do Sodré quarter, once Lisbon’s red light district and now the rising star of the city.
An unique riverside walk, great restaurants, art galleries, open air music sessions, are just some examples of what we can find two steps away from the hotel.
We are walking distance from the bustling districts of Chiado and Bairro Alto as well as the main train station and local public transport links.
And we are the only hotel in downtown Lisbon offering views over the river estuary as well as the picturesque city, making our location uniquely attractive.

full_description:
We have an event portfolio which has given us the experience and ability to validate any project with accuracy and speed.
For every customer and project we offer consultancy services. In every service we provide we aim to exceed expectations, add value and strengthen our relationship with our customers.
We have a flexible negotiation approach oriented to project feasibility.

short_description: The Vincci Baixa hotel is the Vincci chain's first hotel in Portugal. This is one of the best situated hotels; in Lisbon right in the centre of Lisbon and less than 7 km away from the airport.
full_description: Vincci Baixa is set in a privileged location in the "Baixa" shopping and banking district, one of the city's liveliest areas. Baixa has a wealth of shops, dozens of open-air bars and is popular with musicians and artists, giving it a bohemian air that is very much in keeping with the rest of the city.

short_description: Is a peaceful, familiar campsite, situated in the middle of the nature. The Campsite has 70 bungalows, 4 Mobilehomes, 4 Glampings, 189 equipped bays and a very large area for tents.
full_description:
Is a peaceful, familiar campsite, situated in the middle of the nature. The Campsite has 70 bungalows, 4 Mobilehomes, 4 Glampings, 189 equipped bays and a very large area for tents.
Lisboa Camping & Bungalows is located in Parque Florestal de Monsanto, in the middle of the nature, where you can have a squirrel for neighbor.
You can have access to the swimming pool, tennis court, mini-golf, multisports field and a playgroung for the children. You also have at your disposal a restaurant/snack-bar, a minimarket and a free wi-fi.
Our reception is open from 08:00 until midnight, to assist you if needed.

title: Doc Dmc - Services, Events & Wine
short_description: DOC DMC Services Events & Wine - agency specialized in the creation of one-day wine tourism tours, weekend getaways and thematic tours throughout Portugal.
full_description:
Let us know your ideas we love to be challenged
WHAT WE DO
Group Travel
We create tailormade itineraries according to our client’s needs and wishes, from north to south of Portugal; Our programs can be themed to specific topics such as architecture, culture, food and wine, hiking , biking, golf, surfing or sailing.

Corporate Events

title: Epic Sana
short_description: You’ll find one of the best views of Lisbon here, at Alto das Amoreiras. A terrace with an amazing vista and a good drinks and snacks list is the perfect stopping point.
full_description:
Come and discover one of the best views of Lisbon from one of the city’s most emblematic hills.
At Alto das Amoreiras you’ll find the Epic Sana, a 5-star hotel whose fabulous 9th-floor terrace is open to guests and visitors alike. In the Upscale Bar, the view of the river and the rooftops is phenomenal.
Let your eyes roam the horizon as you recharge your batteries with the snack and drinks menu or even, who knows, as you take a refreshing dip in the infinity pool, after asking permission of course.
Take a break from wandering the city and come and discover a more sophisticated side of Lisbon, with its ever-amazing view.

title: Honorato | Telheiras
short_description: Ready for a delicious and crunchy experience? At Honorato, all our burgers are prepared with the utmost attention to detail to offer you the best experience! When are you coming for a visit?
full_description:
From our kitchen come the best burgers in the country.
And, our specialty goes beyond burgers. Visit us and explore our cocktail menu.
Honorato has the perfect recipe for group dinners: delicious burgers, divine cocktails, and tables where there’s always space for one more.
Or, take the flavors of Honorato wherever you go with our takeaway.

title: NewsMuseum
short_description: An intelligent, interactive and technically advanced entertainment, dedicated to the stories of Portugal and the world as told by the Media. That is how NewsMuseum, located in the Village of Sintra, defines itself.
full_description:
Through a highly interactive approach, using the latest technology, NewsMuseum recalls episodes of the History of Portugal and the world through their journalistic coverage and projects the future of Media.
Visitors are invited to put on the virtual reality glasses, to explore a giant interactive “tablet,” to read the MFA (Armed Forces Movement) statement into the microphone of the radio booth and to travel through the news of the world with the Pyramid of Babel, among other interactive areas.
Visiting this equipment is diving in a Media Age Experience, in which the visitor is encouraged to touch, see, hear and interact with various contents.
Over 16 hours of Media content are shown in the 25 thematic areas, which makes NewsMuseum the greatest interactive infotainment in Europe.

title: Boutique dos Relógios Plus
short_description: In a company with more than 50 years of experience in the watch sector Boutique dos Relógios is the biggest watch retailer in Portugal with 60 points of sale across the country.
full_description:
In Boutique dos Relógios Plus Art, exclusivity rules.
Beginning with unique pieces of art, created exclusively for the new space, alongside special high watchmaking pieces, limited and unique this concept store positions itself at the epitome of luxury.
Boutique dos Relógios Plus Art is located in the luxury shopping street of Lisbon,  Avenida da Liberdade.
In this shop it is possible to find watches from the Top Swiss brands such as Breguet, Blancpain, Breitling, Hublot, IWC, and Longines among others.
In addition it is possible to find jewellery from the famous brands of Bucellatti, Graff and Piaget among others.
Includes exclusive and limited editions only for the Portuguese market. Early tax free refund of 17, 5% available.

Set sail in one of our ships for a cruise along the Tagus or the Portuguese coast in a unique adventure.
After leaving the harbour, time and space become different. To enjoy friends company, to have time for sharing new experiences, to get new emotions, to met new people and to have time for yourself, all this, you can get from this voyage!
On holidays or planning an incentive trip for your co-workers, you can be part of this adventure and participate on the several events and festivals promoted by Veltagus and the portugueses harbours and towns.

title: Sealife Lovers
short_description: The most traditional and trendy boats, give you the coolest and unforgettable trips.
full_description:
Sea Life Lovers is for all those who love the sea, the beach, the nature and above all life. The sun that brings us energy, the sea that refreshes and allows us to discover new worlds, the beach that welcomes us in a hugeness of sand and the Serra of Arrábida, with its unique forest in galleries, typical of the Mediterranean.
Setúbal, a city of fishermen, is considered one of the most beautiful bays of the world, in the southwest of Europe. It is rich for the wines of the region, awarded on an international level, and for its gastronomy: oysters, white fish, shellfish, cuttlefish, a gastronomical panoply of unforgettable tastes, bathed by Sado River.
Lisbon the capital of Portugal, one of the most complete European capitals, River, Sea, Sintra hills, history and culture, bathed by the Tagus River.
The Sado River inserted in one of the Most Beautiful Bays in the World , the immensity of the Tagus River in Lisbon to its border and the different experiences we provide onboard our vessels , are the perfect trilogy to live unique and unforgettable moments.
These are our routes:
Setubal, Troia, Alcácer do Sal, Sesimbra – Atlantic Ocean and Sado River
Lisboa, Oeiras, Estoril, Cascais – Atlantic Ocean and Rio Tejo
Sea Life Lovers offers you the possibility to know the region from one side to the other, taking advantage of its natural maritime richness combined with irresistible tastings, diving baptism, fishing with family, life celebrations, travel and fun with friends and family, culture and tradition.
We offer you the most complete boat trips experiences. You can come aboard of the most charming boats of the regions. Sea life Lovers has several boats with a maximum capacity of 12 people plus the crew, which allows your trips to be personalized and exclusive.
The right choice for those who seek the highest quality.

title: Treasures of the Tagus
short_description: Treasures of the Tagus - an innovative nautical experience! It promotes a new way to explore the Estuary of the Tagus and the adjacent coast.
full_description:
Essência Oceânica is a Lisbon-based company dedicated to creating innovative nautical experiences that combine underwater technology, cultural heritage and environmental awareness.
Through its main brand, Treasures of the Tagus, the company promotes a new way to explore the Estuary of the Tagus and the adjacent coast.

title: VIP Executive Art's Hotel
short_description: One of our top picks in Lisbon. The stylish and modern Vip Executive Art's Hotel has spacious rooms and suites and some offer far-reaching views of the Tagus River and Vasco da Gama Bridge.
full_description:
The stylish and modern Vip Executive Art's Hotel is less than 500 m from the Tagus waterfront and Feira Internacional de Lisboa. It is 3 minutes’ walk from Lisbon Oriente Station.
The spacious rooms and suites are soundproofed and stylishly decorated. Some offer far-reaching views of the Tagus River and Vasco da Gama Bridge. The hotel also features 8 conference rooms and an auditorium with capacity for 165 people.
Each morning guests can enjoy a breakfast buffet in the restaurant. For lunch and dinner appetising Portuguese dishes and international cuisine are served. The VIP Executive Arts Hotel also boasts 1 bar where guests can relax with cocktails and drinks.
Free WiFi is available throughout the hotel and private parking is available.
Portela Airport is just 4 km away from VIP Executive Arts Hotel. It has excellent public transportation links and is within easy walking distance of the Vasco da Gama Shopping Centre and Altice Arena.
Parque das Nações is a great choice for travellers interested in monuments, history and culture.

title: Oceanário de Lisboa
short_description: Considered the best aquarium in the world by Tripadvisor, in 2015, 2017 and 2018, Oceanário de Lisboa creates emotions through the ocean and its 8,000 marine creatures. An experience that everyone wants to live.
full_description:
Considered the best aquarium in the world by Tripadvisor, in 2015, 2017 and 2018, Oceanário de Lisboa (Lisbon Oceanarium) creates emotions through the ocean and its 8,000 marine creatures.
Among these, rays, coral reefs, sharks and sea otters.
An experience that everyone wants to live.
Touring through the permanent exhibition is an exalting experience for the senses. A big central aquarium, with five million litres of saltwater, symbolizes the Global Ocean. Surrounding this big aquarium, four marine habitats create the illusion that there is only one aquarium.
The visit it’s between two levels, at surface and underwater.
The temporary exhibition, “Forests Underwater by Takashi Amano”, presents tropical forests and their aquatic systems through the largest nature aquarium in the world, created by the famous aquascaper, Takashi Amano.
An experience of pure engagement with these environments, where art, beauty and nature are perfectly connected.

title: Parques de Sintra - Monte da Lua
short_description: Parques de Sintra has the responsibility to manage the most important natural and cultural values located in the area of the Cultural Landscape of Sintra and Queluz.
full_description:
Parques de Sintra has the responsibility to manage the most important natural and cultural values located in the area of the Cultural Landscape of Sintra and in Queluz: the Park and Palace of Pena, the Gardens and the Palace of Monserrate, the Moorish Castle, the Convent of the Capuchos, the Garden and Chalet of the Countess of Edla and, since 2012, the National Palaces of Sintra and Queluz and the Portuguese School of Equestrian Art based at the Gardens of Queluz, with performances and training sessions open to the general public at the Henrique Calado Riding Ring in Belém.
The management of these properties involves their restoration, requalification, revitalisation, conservation, research, publicity and operation, opening them to public fruition and enhancing their touristic valour.
Tickets

title: Hotel Fortaleza do Guincho
short_description: Leaning over the Atlantic Ocean, the Fortaleza do Guincho welcomes a charming five star boutique hotel.
full_description:
Leaning over the Atlantic Ocean, the Fortaleza do Guincho welcomes a charming 5 star boutique hotel, with a range of services that focus on the well being and peacefulness of the customers. The Hotel’s restaurant offers an unforgettable dining experience of Portuguese cuisine.
Located just seven kilometers from the town of Cascais and 30 kilometers from Lisbon and the International Airport, the cultural and leisure offer in the region is rich and full of varieties.
Along the cost line you will find Sintra and its magnificent monuments and natural landscape, a UNESCO World Heritage Site. The Hotel is surrounded by the best golf courses, while the Estoril Casino, the largest in Europe, is just a few minutes away.

title: Venuesin

full_description: With a database of hundreds of suppliers, Venuesin is a free service which allows professionals to receive several proposals for venues, hotels and other additional services needed to plan a successful Event.

title: Locke Santa Joana
short_description: Once a 17th century convent, Locke de Santa Joana has a rich, distinctive history. From preserved interior details to artifacts displayed in the cultural gallery, our Lisbon hotel unites the past and present.
full_description:
A fresh and cheerful style flows through the 370 rooms and apartments which, together with vibrant social spaces for coworking, dinning and events make the Locke Santa Joana the new In in Lisbon.
Discover design-led accommodation in the heart of Lisbon.
Locke de Santa Joana, close to Marquês de Pombal and Avenida da Liberdade, offers an innovative work and lifestyle experience.
Our flexible spaces provide an ideal hub for productivity and exploration.
The two restaurants, the bar where once was the convent library or the bar dedicated to whiskey, the outdoor swimming pool, the 24/7 gym or the meeting rooms with capacity up to 80 people is just a few examples of what Locke de Santa Joana has to offer.

title: VIP Executive Éden Aparthotel
short_description: Your favorite view over Lisbon! Strategically located in Lisbon's historical centre, VIP Executive Éden Aparthotel represents a fortunate adaptation of an old national monument, an Art Deco cinema, to a hotel property.
full_description:
Your favorite view over Lisbon
Strategically located in Lisbon's historical centre, in Restauradores Square, VIP Executive Éden Aparthotel represents a fortunate adaptation of an old national monument, an Art Deco cinema, to a hotel property.
Classified as a four star and awarded by the Portuguese Government in 1996 as 'The Best Urban Renovation Project' and 'The Best Tourism Project in Portugal', VIP Executive Éden Aparthotel is very appreciated by the quality of its equipments and architecture.
This 4 star Aparthotel is provided with studios and apartments with separate work areas and wide facilities, ideal for successful stays.
It is also possible to enjoy the sesonal outdoor swimming pool and the terrace bar, with one of the best panoramic views over Lisbon. The Aparthotel has also Wireless Internet access.

The Pestana Cascais is a 4-star hotel located in one of the most luxurious areas of Lisbon's coastal region, Vila de Cascais. It is very close to various beaches, including the surfer's paradise Guincho beach, parks, museums, shopping centres and the Casino do Estoril.

title: Monastery of Odivelas – Convent of St. Dinis
short_description: Discover the impressive history of the monastery of Odivelas, or convent of São Dinis, a national monument right on the edges of Lisbon.
full_description:
If you’re interested in Lisbon’s most important monasteries, don’t miss the convent of São Dinis, better known as the monastery of Odivelas.
This Cistercian monastery was founded in 1295 by King Dinis. Legend has it that it came into being thanks to the divine salvation which saved the king from a bear while hunting in the Alentejo.
But it’s more likely that this place of retreat, built to house Bernadine nuns, was used by the king to ensure the future of his illegitimate daughter, D. Maria Afonso, whose tomb can still be found in the high chapel.
Dinis is also buried in this original Gothic complex, which mixes influences and styles that coincide with the eras when restoration work was done.
Manueline traces can clearly be seen in the cloister, while the font is Renaissance and the chapels, porches and azulejo tiles all Baroque.
Discover the impressive history of the monastery of Odivelas, or convent of São Dinis, a national monument right on the edges of Lisbon.

title: Portuguese Museum of Freemasonry
short_description: Discover one of the most secret organisations in the world at the recently opened Museu Maçónico Português, where the symbols and meanings are unveiled.
full_description: To learn about one of the most secret – or most discreet – organisations in the world, visit the recently opened Museu Maçónico Português. You’ll discover the symbols and meaning of all the spaces and tools used at freemason meetings. At the Grémio Lusitano, the second oldest headquarters in Europe, you’ll find a historic archive recounting the last 150 years of Portuguese freemasonry. Learn about the layout of the rooms and what the pillars and tiles on the floor symbolise. You’ll also get to know about the aprons, books and crockery used. You probably won’t learn about all of the organisation’s secret and complex rituals, but you’ll certainly get a better idea about this very unique group.

title: Miradouro da Senhora do Monte
short_description: A unique spot with an incredible view over Lisbon. Almost mystical in its calmness, this is a perfect place for sitting back and contemplating the city.
full_description: The Miradouro da Senhora do Monte is one of Lisbon’s major landmarks. It is also one of the calmest places to look at the city, despite its stunning panoramic view. Just a few metres from the Miradouro da Graça, it is a marvellous point from which to observe the capital. To its left is the church of Graça and the castle, with houses tumbling down its hillside, standing guard above the city. In the background, the river and the Tagus estuary glow and light up the rooftops in Baixa, the ruins of Carmo and the pastel tones of the Avenidas Novas. All this from the place where the first king of Portugal, Afonso Henriques, assembled his troops to conquer Lisbon. Nine centuries later, it is the Lady of the Mount (Senhora do Monte) and her chapel, founded in 1147, who conquers all before her.
location: Rua da Senhora do Monte à Graça, Lisboa

title: Monastery of Batalha
short_description: UNESCO’s World Heritage | The Monastery of Santa Maria da Vitória (Monastery of Batalha), was built after a vow made by King João I to thank the Virgin Mary for Portugal’s victory over Spain in the battle of Aljubarrota.
full_description:
The Monastery of Santa Maria da Vitória (Monastery of Batalha), was built after a vow made by King João I to thank the Virgin Mary for Portugal’s victory over Spain in the battle of Aljubarrota.
It is one of Europe’s most beautiful monastery complexes from the late Middle Ages.
Building work began in 1386, under the supervision of Master Afonso Domingues.
On completion the monastery was handed over to the Dominican Order.
It subsequently served as a pantheon for the Aviz dynasty and building work was only finally completed in 1517.
As an important testament to late Gothic and Manueline architectural styles, it is graced by a remarkable set of mediaeval stainedglass windows.
The monastery has been on UNESCO’s World Heritage List since 1983.

title: Teatro Nacional de São Carlos
short_description: Catch a show at a unique place in Lisbon. The national opera house, as it is known by many, is an erudite, sophisticated and unique venue in the city.
full_description: The São Carlos, which has existed since 1793, is Lisbon’s grand venue for opera and the production and presentation of choral and symphonic music. A neoclassical building in Chiado of Italian inspiration, it is home to the Portuguese Symphonic Orchestra and the Choir of the Teatro Nacional de São Carlos, the country’s only professional choir. This genuine national house of culture also holds ballet in its Main Hall, various concerts in its Noble Hall and more informal events in its Foyer. It also organises the Festival ao Largo, which brings erudite music outdoors for contact with audiences of all ages. Whether to watch a show or discover the theatre’s extraordinary historic past, your soul will jump with joy.

Our new terrace

Even with the dense vegetation and ferocious creatures on the loose, reflected in the menu or in some more discreet corner.

full_description: This is undoubtedly one of Lisbon’s most beautiful miradouros. Factors which contribute to this are the trams which pass by and sound their bells, the magnificent decorative tiles, the garden and the pond. At the Miradouro de Santa Luzia, you can set your compass by the domes of the surrounding churches. Because you’ll need to get your bearings in the tangle of Alfama’s streets. Discover the dome of Santa Engrácia, the church of Santo Estevão and the two towers of the church of São Miguel. Enjoy the café and the kiosk to observe the panels by António Quaresma, which show what Praça do Comércio was like before the earthquake and the taking of the castle from the Moors. With the Tagus always In the background and a huge desire to keep on wandering.

The museum, which is organised around four sections, deserves special mention for the largest collection of Baroque azulejo tiles under one roof, as well as trails showing how this art form developed – with a compulsory stop at the impressive Galeria das Fábulas (Gallery of Fables).
Come and discover this unique church and its history, which dates back to the reconquest of Lisbon and the centuries that followed right up to today.

title: Centro Nacional de Cultura
short_description: CNC’s main objectives are to promote, defend, disseminate and register Portuguese cultural heritage.
full_description: Centro Nacional de Cultura (CNC) was founded in 1945 as an “intellectuals’ club” in which to exchange ideas. It was the brainchild of a group of monarchists who wished to defend a free culture. Throughout the 50s and 60s it developed to become a democratic forum and by the late seventies, after the 25 April 1974 revolution, it began a new phase under the team leadership of Helena Vaz da Silva. It now included a range of activities addressed to a broad spectrum of the public – Sunday Walks, travel, training courses, international meetings and seminars, exhibitions, publications, literary and artistic competitions, prizes and grants, children’s activities, providing cultural services for schools, corporations and foreign groups visiting Portugal. Currently CNC’s main objectives are to promote, defend, disseminate and register Portuguese cultural heritage, promote “cultural tourism” based on an integrated idea of tourism, environment, heritage and cultural itineraries, and to educate the younger generations on a feeling of global citizenship. Its action can be summarised as a policy of “establishing contacts”, “articulating”, “making things happen”.

title: Pavilhão de Portugal
short_description: Visit the Parque das Nações to admire this unique building by Siza Vieira. Constructed for the 1998 World Expo, its structure is considered an engineering masterpiece.
full_description: The Pavilhão de Portugal is one of the major milestones in contemporary Portuguese architecture and unique in Lisbon. Only here – and only in person – is it possible to appreciate the grandeur of Álvaro Siza Vieira’s epic building. Created to represent the country in the 1998 World Expo that regenerated the Parque das Nações area, the Pavilhão de Portugal is designed to represent a look like a sheet of paper resting on two bricks. The huge concrete roof, weighing 1, 400 tonnes, is an award-winning masterpiece of engineering. If you visit, you’ll be impressed by the pure beauty and strength this building conveys when seen from afar – and the way it envelops you as you approach. Now belonging to the University of Lisbon, this remarkable building will be one of Lisbon’s major cultural hubs.
location: Alameda dos Oceanos, Parque das Nações, 1990, Lisboa

title: São Miguel D'Alfama
short_description: Typical cuisine from north to south of the country, offering its customers a large variety of options. Also, presents daily several female and male voices that dare to continue the tradition of Fado.
full_description:
When we talk about São Miguel DʻAlfama we are undoubtedly referring to a historic house, opened in 1945 and it is one of the oldest in the Alfama District.
It is inserted in the framework of the old Jewish quarter. Architecturally interesting, when crossing its door we come across a floor of particular importance since it is an ancient Roman pavement, its brick arches in sarapantel (basket handle) also date from that time.
The São Miguel D´Alfama is a family home. Its owner Fátima Moura, who inherited it from her mother, intends one day that her children will assume her patronage, thus turning it into an inter-generational house.
Fátima Moura is also the mentor of this space, harmoniously decorated with characteristic symbolism, associated with the extreme good taste of its owner.
Typical cuisine from north to south of the country is one of the strengths of this house. It therefore presents a varied range of dishes that offers its customers a variety of options.
São Miguel D´Alfama decided to bet on the new generation of fado singers for some time, presenting daily female and male voices that dare to continue the tradition of Fado.
This is why it is a real Portuguese house!

full_description:
We were born in Portugal, but we grew up thinking about the world and how it connects through communication and knowledge sharing.
For us, organising conferences is about connecting people around a greater goal or a common dream: accelerating a cure for a disease, bringing more visibility to a campaign, or even creating a movement for real and lasting change.
These special moments of knowledge sharing, innovation and best practices live on well beyond the day of the event. And that legacy is what drives us: building bridges between people and creating unexpected opportunities for collaboration.
WE LISTEN AND DELIVER

This means that today we are trusted by national and international associations and societies to organize their events, something that fills us with that happy feeling of a job well done.
What We Do
Virtual Events, Webinars and Virtual Conferences, Virtual Exhibition Concepts and 3D Stands, Event Technology, In-person Conferences and Scientific Congresses, Association Management, Sponsorship Sales, International Bid Proposal, Tradeshows, Destination Management, Corporate Events.

Alecrim ao Chiado is located in the heart of the Chiado district, less than 5 minutes' walk from the main points of interest in the city of Lisbon. It is also very well served by public transport, with metro, train and buses less than 3 minutes walk from the Hotel.

It is a very comfortable space, where each guest can enjoy a drink or snack. All the space is equipped with wifi and AC.

title: 1908 Lisboa Hotel
short_description: Rooms with a contemporary design in a classic Art Nouveau style building. A new concept in the city. Relax and start your journey with the 1908 Lisbon Hotel.
full_description:
The award‐winning 1908 Lisboa Hotel is a Lifestyle Boutique Hotel located in a typical zone of Lisbon, where you can find everything you need, as well as cafés, bars with relaxed atmospheres, where you can dance to the sound of indie music, local products and souvenirs to take to friends and family. The district of the Intendente has all of this and much more.
This area is a mixture of different cultures, culinary arts, colours and flavours and is usually frequented by the "cool‐kids" of Lisbon. More than a hotel, the 1908 is also a pub, a restaurant, and a place where you can experience the authentic portuguese culture.
It is impossible to talk about this Hotel without mentioning the astonishing building that houses it.
A building with history, honoured in 1908 with one of the most important architecture awards: the Valmor prize.
