short_description: The main objective of the Associação Comercial e Empresarial do Bairro Alto is to defend the rights of its members and traders and to promote the Bairro Alto.

title: The Lift Boutique Hotel
short_description: The Lift Boutique Hotel welcomes its guests in the Historic Center of Lisbon, in an old building of Lisbon architecture in Pombalino style, interiors are decorated in a contemporary and elegant environment,
full_description:
Wearing blue the first block as we advance from Rossio to the river, THE LIFT BOUTIQUE HOTEL welcomes its guests in the Historic Center of Lisbon, in an old building of Lisbon architecture in Pombalino style, which participated in the reconstruction of Lisbon after the earthquake of 1755.
From the ceilings of the building's top floor accommodations – the attic – you can still see the original woodwork of the roof, where you can peek at the Castle of São Jorge or the Elevador de Santa Justa from a typical Lisbon trapeira, which so often illustrates postcards from the capital.
The interiors were decorated in a contemporary and elegant environment, inspired by the neo-Gothic lines of the neighboring monument - the Elevador de Santa Justa.

title: Hotel Borges Chiado
short_description: In the heart of one of the most charming areas of Lisbon, the Borges Chiado Hotel is sometimes a unique refuge, owner of a unique charisma and the perfect combination between tradition and contemporaneity.
full_description:
A few metres from Bairro Alto, Rossio, Príncipe Real and even Mercado da Ribeira, this hotel in Lisbon stands out for its quality and elegance, the strong point of which is its location: Borges Chiado Hotel is located 30 minutes away from Lisbon airport, by car or metro, and is one step away from Baixa-Chiado station.
For those who want to know and explore the real Lisbon, full of charms and well kept secrets, the Borges Chiado Hotel is the starting point, where you can enjoy a sublime view over the Largo do Chiado, where the frenetic energy of people moving in shuttles immediately contagious.
Built in a secular building during the vibrant year of 1884, Hotel Borges Chiado is distinguished by being one of the oldest in Lisbon. Located in an eclectic and modernist building on Rua Garrett - Chiado, it is characterized by its structure with balconies throughout its wall.
With more than a century of existence, the Hotel Borges Chiado has hosted illustrious figures of Portuguese and international society and has for decades been the stage of the most varied odyssey that inspired many of the stories of our great poets like Fernando Pessoa or Eça de Queirós.
The Hotel Borges was opened in the year 1884. It had the name "Grande Hotel Borges", it was one of the main Hotels in Lisbon of the century XIX and is currently the oldest hotel in Lisbon still in operation.
A curious note, even at the time when telephones had only three digits, indicates that the Grande Hotel Borges had car and car services, in a clear reference to the evolution registered in society over the last century.
The hotel faced several adversities throughout its existence, one of the most striking being the great fire in Chiado, dated 25 August 1988, which destroyed a large part of the area where the hotel is located, having burned much of the heritage from of the century. XIX and beginning of the century XX.
Our rooms have been designed to guarantee the best comfort to our guests, interiors masterfully combine luxury, elegance and modernity of our times.

title: Setúbal Alive
short_description: We are specialized in Private Boat Trips, Dolphin Watching, Birthday Parties, Bachelorette Parties, Sunset Party, Corporate Events, Boat rental without crew and Jetski rental.
full_description:
It all started more than 20 years ago with the beginning of a relationship between two young people, Vanda and André, from Setubal, in love with their region and, of course, in love with their blue river, the “Rio Sado”.
Both are divers, with ocean skipper license, experienced nautical instructors with an enormous knowledge of the sea, the region and the Sado River.
To pass on the vast knowledge of this fantastic region, in 2015 Vanda and Andre founded Setúbal Alive. Since then, Setúbal Alive offers private boat tours, either on small boats, or bigger, such as an exclusive yacht or a traditional motor sailer.
Vanda and Andre accompany their guests on these boat tours, showing them the most spectacular places, stop for swimming at beautiful, hidden and very different places and, with luck, are able to show their guests the family of dolphins which is living in the Sado estuary.
All tours can be planned individually and absolutely according to the wishes of the guests to make them unique and unforgettable. Even meals can be ordered.
But, there is also the possibility of renting a boat or jet ski without crew. Like this, you can be your own captain on “your own boat” and give the family or friends unforgettable and unique experiences.
THERE ARE SEVERAL TYPES OF BOATS IN ORDER TO MEET THE MOST DIVERSE NEEDS.
Even different tours for different occasions such as: birthday parties, sunset tours, bachelor parties or corporate events can be planned.
location: Av. Baía de Setúbal - Doca das Fontainhas , 2910-082, Setúbal

title: Casa-Museu Dr. Anastácio Gonçalves
short_description: The art collection includes exceptional museum pieces, acclaimed both nationally and internationally, such as the Chinese ceramic collection.
full_description:
This house, winner of the 1905 Valmor Architecture Award and the former Studio of the painter José Malhoa, was purchased in 1932 by the ophthalmologist and art collector Dr António Anastácio Gonçalves (1888-1965), who used the house to display his impressive collection, which he wished to bestow to the State.
The collection includes a wide variety of objects, in particular Portuguese 19th century naturalist paintings and decorative art pieces, highlighted by an exquisite collection of Chinese porcelain.
Visitors to this house-museum can view the collections in the welcoming and day-to-day ambience of a house that is also a museum.
Besides the display, study and preservation of the permanent collection, the house-museum offers guided thematic visits and artistic expression workshops to foster life-long art learning experiences.
The house-museum also features free concerts, colloquiums and conferences.

title: Lisb-on Festival
short_description: Lisb-on is a prominent electronic music festival, located in the Keil do Amaral garden in Lisbon.
full_description:
Famous for its magical atmosphere, it attracts an international audience with a variety of electronic styles, including disco, house and techno.
The festival stands out for its focus on sustainability and cultural diversity.
It is a unique celebration that combines electronic music with lisbon's urban culture.

title: Casa Cadaval
short_description: Visiting Casa Cadaval Domaine is discovering a place where tradition and modernity are combined in a harmonious relationship between man and nature.
full_description: Located 80 kilometres, north of Lisbon, on the left bank of the Tejo river, the Herdade de Muge Casa Cadaval, has an inheritance of four centuries of history. Around the large Dam and its natural beauties, the mares of Puro Sangue Lusitano, graze the fields of Casa Cadaval, which are cohabited by many ornithologyc species; the vineyards; the century-old cork trees forests and the “Experience Camp, called “Sobreiro do Neto”, a leisure area with a privileged location where every kind of event including Team Building workshops take place. Visiting Casa Cadaval Domaine is discovering a place where tradition and modernity are combined in a harmonious relationship between man and nature.
location: Rua Vasco da Gama, Muge, 2125-317, Salvaterra de Magos

title: Estórias na Casa da Comida
short_description: Come with us in a discovery of the most authentic Portuguese cuisine … we offer authentic experiences of flavours!
full_description:
Our menu is signed by Chef João Pereira and it serves up all the flavours of traditional portuguese cuisine with a contemporary touch, whilst highlighting nationally sourced ingredients.
Inspired by his love to the theatre arts and as a reference to the Casa da Comédia, Jorge Vale created, 40 years ago, the Casa da Comida.
Winner of one of the firsts Michelin stars in Portugal, the restaurant kept the same concept over the years: sophisticated, with great refinement and personalised service.
The Estórias in Casa da Comida is an homage to the Casa da Comida heritage, in which the tradition of our cuisine is respected but where a contemporary approach to Portuguese flavours is sought. Being aware of new trends in gastronomy, we offer authentic experiences of flavours.

title: Lisbon by Segway
short_description: The Segway experience can be considered the coolest way to move around!
full_description:
The Segway experience can be considered the coolest way to move around!
This out of the box means of transportation is as green as it can be, and for sure, an experience that sticks in the memory. After a one-on-one practice session, our monitor will soon have you gliding effortlessly through the city, offering fantastic photo opportunities.
Book a tour with us and get a 15% discount on any of our other activities: a Live Escape Game, a GoCar Tour, an Eco Tuk Tour, an E-Bike tour or an awesome private tour on a convertible Vintage Beetle.
More information at www.lisbonbysegway.com

title: Silk Club
short_description: Exclusive, special and original, the Silk Club is a different way to enjoy the atmosphere of Lisbon’s nightlife. Come and discover this unique place.
full_description:
Visit one of the most exclusive clubs in Lisbon.
The Silk Club, on the top floor of a building in Rua da Misericórdia, is a member of the World’s Finest Clubs and offers a very exclusive ambiance.
To add a touch of total sophistication to your stay in the city, this is the ideal destination.
Dress in style and climb up to this bar with its fabulous and stunning view of the whole city.
Wherever you look, you’ll see something new and worthy of comment.
With its minimalist decor, to better emphasise the unique view, you can try various original cocktails, as well as genuine asian and Mediterranean cuisine.
This is a unique experience in Lisbon.
Visit the Silk Club and discover a more cosmopolitan and reserved side of Lisbon.

title: Anjos70 Art & Fleamarket
short_description: A unique and multicultural market! You’ll find a lot of original stuff to buy.
full_description:
The most multicultural market in Lisbon takes place here.
This local market brings together various food options, organic produce, crafts people and also sellers of second-hand objects in a unique and lively atmosphere. It takes place in Marvila and Alvalade.
This is an original and free market where everyone is welcome and can find unique products and items that are genuinely one of a kind.
Come and explore these neighborhoods and get to know Anjos70 Art & Fleamarket in the best possible way.

title: Ultra Serviços
short_description: Ultra Serviços is a company that offers currency exchange and money transfer services, a wide range of travel solutions, visas, insurance, tax free and services, tax free and corporate services specialized in various areas.
location: Rua Fernando Curado Ribeiro, 4D-3º Esq., 1600-449, Lisboa

title: Carris Museum (Public Transport Museum)
short_description: The Museum da Carris takes you on a trip into the history of public transport in Lisbon, the key to understanding the development of the city.
full_description: The Museum da Carris takes you on a trip into the history of public transport in Lisbon. Essential for understanding the development of the city and, as the name indicates, the predominance of rails as a distinguishing feature, the museum takes a look at previous centuries. The exhibition of transport vehicles can be seen in a huge shed of workshops where you can travel back through Lisbon’s past from the horse-drawn trolleys to the buses of the second half of the 20th century. Interestingly, all of the vehicles shown are still roadworthy and can be driven around the city, except for those in section III which are still awaiting restoration. The museum’s galleries also hold temporary exhibitions and events, due to the size of this original and different museum in Alcântara.

title: Independente Príncipe Real
short_description: The Independente is the product of a fresh hospitality vision providing the background for travellers as well as locals to come together in a multi-cultural environment in the bohemian heart of Lisbon.
full_description:
Born of the vision and audacity of 3 brothers, The Independente lives in the core of two palaces erected in the late XIX century, planted where 3 of the most iconic Lisbon neighborhoods meet.
Like a carefully chiseled terrace over the secular urban landscape, the Miradouro (viewpoint) de São Pedro de Alcântara provides the perfect foreground for a hostel that expands and transforms the very definition of hostel as we once knew it.
Two restaurants and respective bars with weatherproof terraces and open to the public, 33 rooms ranging from the large 12 bed dormitory to the suite deluxe, The Independente has become one of the symbols of the new multiculturalism in town, welcoming in its bosom: natives and foreigners, locals or travellers, of all ages and provenances.
location: Rua de São Pedro de Alcântara, 81 e 83, 1250-238, Lisboa

title: VIP Executive Zurique Hotel
short_description: Successful stays in Lisbon! Strategically located near the financial and business centre of Lisbon, and only a few minutes away from the historical centre and from Lisbon's International Airport.
full_description:
Successful stays in Lisbon!
Strategically located near the financial and business centre of Lisbon, and only a few minutes away from the historical centre and from Lisbon's International Airport, VIP Executive Zurique Hotel has 252 rooms that represent the essence of convenience and comfort.
This 3 star Hotel has leisure areas, like a cosy and comfortable bar, an exterior swimming pool (seasonal from May to September) and also the “O Infante” restaurant, with natural light and capacity for 120 people, where typical Portuguese and international meals are served.
VIP Executive Zurique Hotell has a private parking and Internet Wireless service.
The Hotel also has 10 conference rooms suitable for congresses, conventions, meetings or banquets, with capacityfor up to 300 people, predominantly with natural light.

EVOLUTION Lisboa Hotel is located in Duque de Saldanha Square, at the heart of Lisbon, only a 10-minute drive away from Lisbon Airport and a 10 minute underground ride to the city's historical centre.
A vibrant and bustling urban area where shopping, entertainment, history, culture and business are constantly present.
You’ll find yourself in the middle of a multicultural meeting point, in one of Europe’s coolest cities, where hundreds of years of history blend perfectly with the most recent trends.

title: Casa de São Mamede Hotel
short_description: Welcome to “Casa de São Mamede” ... a small, family kind hotel type establishment with 28 rooms, in a beautiful building of XVIII century, adapted to a hotel unit in 1948.
full_description:
The “Casa de São Mamede” is a small, family kind hotel type establishment with 28 rooms, that functions in a beautiful building of XVIII century.
It was in 1758 that the property was constructed for residence of a magistrate, the Dr. Manuel Jose de Faria and Sousa, curiously, is one of the first buildings to be constructed in the traditional system of “birdcage” – heartquake- proof construction used after earthquake of 1755.
Having been adapted to a hotel unit in 1948, it knew how to keep throught the years, the treatment, the tradition and the atmosphere of a true family house.
Its position in the geography of Lisbon forming a plaza with the Church of the same name and respective garden, constitutes in the words of an illustrious portuguese professor the “
better localization of this kind of establishments, since that, is placed in full heart of the city, confers it something of the bucolic beauty of a village
“*;
Always trying to preserve the past we did not relinquish, however, the comfort requirements that the contemporary life demands, so, all the rooms have the same infrastructures, namely: all have privative bathroom, telephone, digital safe, satellite television, double glasses and air-conditioned with individual command.
The fact that we’re dealing with a small hotel type unit, allow us to grant a more intent and personalized treatment to each customer.
*Professor Augusto França in “Monte Olivete Minha Aldeia”

title: Rotas do Sal
short_description: Dolphin watching in the Setúbal bay, one of the most beautiful bay in the World. The Tróia peninsula, the coast of Arrábida and its beaches, the Fortress of Outão and the Palace of Commenda, are some of the most common passing places.
full_description:
In the Sado estuary, there is a community of approximately 30 dolphins, who live here all the year long, and are a major attraction for the local population and also the tourists.
Join us to look for them in their natural habitat, in the fullness of all their freedom, and feel, quite closely, the magic of their presence and the joy of their games and performances of aquatic choreography.
All existing bottle-nose dolphins in the Sado are identified by their name, thanks to the individual knowledge of the shapes and dimensions of their dorsal fins. Dolphins, like we humans, have their moods and can be very playful and even interactive with people who observe them.
It is very high (98,6% in 2017) the probability of finding our community of dolphins of Sado, swimming, jumping and doing all sorts of games with each other.
location: Setúbal | Doca das Fontainhas • Tróia | Marina - Pontão de abastecimento, Tróia, Setúbal, Álcacer do Sal, 2900, Setúbal

title: The Beautique Hotels Figueira
short_description: Centrally in Lisbon, the Beautique Hotels Figueira offers unique experiences and features a rooftop Spa overlooking the São Jorge Castle.
full_description:
At Figueira Square, seconds from the historic Augusta Street, there is a story to tell: a planted tree on the sidewalk, guide us on a journey to Indoor nature.
Away from the canons of conventional hospitality, The Beautique Hotels Figueira is a place of unique experiences, the first unit of a unique collection of boutique hotels.
It has the body of a tree and the scent of ripe fruit: the fig tree personified in the interior signed by Nini de Andrade e Silva, winning Portuguese interior designer, invites you to dive into a narrative magical and intimate full of unexpected details.
On reception floor, the restaurant and the bar give the welcome starting travel under the sign of traditional cuisine. The The Beautique Hotels Figueira offers 50 rooms divided into 4 sophisticated types.
The top floor is dedicated to the Spa, a supreme shelter in the fig tree canopy, open to the public through access fee. Here you can enjoy a great variety of organic based treatments, sauna and Turkish bath. As a complement, you´ve a small fitness room.
Away from the urban frenzy, this is the perfect setting for body and mind rebalancing, in a cozy nest overlooking the sky.

short_description: The Altis Belém Hotel & Spa is a 5 star design Hotel located just a few steps from the Tagus River – In Doca do Bom Sucesso, in the cultural and historic quarter of Belém.
full_description:
As the surrounding area is full of the landmarks of the Portuguese achievements in the XV and XVI Century, the Hotel is all about the Discoveries with a modern and design approach.
The 45 rooms and 5 suites offer all the comforts and high tech equipment.
The 1000 sqm of the Bspa by Karin Herzog with a interior swimming pool, Gym, treatments such as Haman baths, Vichy showers, rituals, massages, essential oils and ayurvedic therapy’s.
There’s also a Sundeck on the top roof with pool and Tagus River view.
Feitoria Restaurant & Wine Bar with Michelin Star is a signature cuisine restaurant, that offers Portuguese gastronomy combining the old and the new with a twist of the flavours brought by the 4 continents knowledge.
For a more light meal the Cafeteria Mensagem is an excellent choice and also to enjoy the Oyster & Sushi Bar with the dock view. The Bar 38º41’ with an inviting atmosphere is also a hit.
The hotel offers 3 multifunctional meeting rooms.

full_description:
With more than 45 years of experience in the sector and extensive knowledge of the destinations in which we operate, we can count on a team of people who are specially trained and ready to guarantee a perfect and unforgettable experience to all our clients.
Our commitment and hard work, as well as our constant evolution and commitment to technology, allow us to serve more than 2,400,000 clients a year and to represent the main and most renowned tour operators in the international sector.

title: Fertagus
short_description: FERTAGUS is a first private operator that ensures the management and commercial operation of a railway line, from Setúbal to Lisboa (Roma-Areeiro station).
full_description:
Along the way, FERTAGUS currently manages 14 stations on a line extension of approximately 54 km.
On the Lisbon South Bank: from Setúbal, Palmela, Venda do Alcaide, Pinhal Novo, Penalva, Coina, Fogueteiro, Foros de Amora, Corroios and Pragal, and four on the North Bank, already in Lisbon : Campolide, Sete Rios, Entrecampos and Roma-Areeiro.
The southern stations managed by Fertagus, have a large number of commercial spaces, parking lots and connections to other modes of transport.
The total travel time between Setúbal and Roma-Areeiro is 57 minutes.

title: Hotel Lezíria Parque
short_description: With a modern architecture and décor and a prime location next to the main access roads to Lisbon, Porto, Algarve and Spain and 15 minutes from Lisbon International Airport.
full_description:
Hotel
The Lezíria Parque has 103 rooms fully equipped to allow a comfortable stay.
With a modern architecture and décor, a prime location on National Road 1 (Estrada Nacional 1) next to the main access roads to Lisbon, Porto, Algarve and Spain and 15 minutes from Lisbon International Airport and Parque das Nações, this hotel is set in magnificent landscapes of Ribatejo and Tagus River. The Lezíria Parque Hotel is the ideal place for those who want to relax, socialize or work.
The Aquarius Restaurant has typical specialties of our area for your meals and the Tagus Bar awaits for a well-spent break.
We hold all kinds of social and scientific events.
Bedrooms
Rooms with Tagus River view, TV equipment, air conditioning, hair dryer, electric coffee maker, minibar, safe-box, direct dial telephone and free wi-fi.
Facilities

title: Miradouro do Parque Eduardo VII
short_description: A compulsory port of call, the impressive Miradouro do Parque Eduardo VII provides an unobstructed view of the valley, leading down to the centre of Lisbon. Come and see.
full_description: The Parque Eduardo VII has the most magnificent viewing point in Lisbon. With a stunning vista of the park, imposing statue of the Marquis of Pombal and the Avenida da Liberdade, you can see the Tagus, the south bank of the river and, on a good day, the hills of Arrábida. This is also where you’ll find the 25th April Memorial created by the sculptor João Cutileiro, and an impressive Portuguese flag. Because of its view and location, the miradouro is different. You can see all the surrounding hills and realise the extent of Lisbon outside the historic quarters. Designed by the architect Keil do Amaral in 1945, it’s a nice place to stroll and admire the two cobbled pavements in calçada portuguesa where the Lisbon Book Fair is held every year. It’ll make you feel a little bit Portuguese.

title: Lat.a
short_description: A tribute to Latin American gastronomy. A journey of flavors in a tropical environment with musical programming.
full_description:
Samba, salsa and merengue sound set the rhythm in a tribute to the traditional music and joy of Latin American countries. But it is in the Menu that this inspiration is most evident. The gastronomic influences of LAT.A offer typical dishes from Latin America, guiding each visitor on an authentic exotic journey.
From the popular Peruvian ceviche to Mexican tacos, passing through Brazilian classics, such as cheese bread, the famous picanha or pastries, LAT.A serves dishes full of history and culture.
Located in Doca de Santo Amaro in Lisbon, to enter the LAT.A you just have to cross the tropical forest of the Doca de Santo restaurant. The vibrant colors and extravagant decoration will not leave anyone indifferent.
location: Armazém CP – Doca de Santo Amaro, 1350-353 , Lisboa

title: Hotel Jerónimos 8
short_description: Design and excellent location in Belém for leisure and business, close to Jerónimos Monastery, Lisbon Congress Center and Belém Cultural Center.
full_description:
Just steps from the Tagus waterfront, Jerónimos 8 is a brilliant merging of the best of Portugal past and present.
Located in Lisbon historic Belém district, the 1940s building has been reinvigorated by locally-based architects Capinha Lopes and Associates.
The revamp allows guests to experience the ornate gables, statues and reliefs of the nearby 16th-century Jerónimos Monastery while basking in a clean, urban-contemporary vibe.
Inside, corridors carpeted in stripes of red, cream and brown lead to the 61 rooms and four suites, each individually designed, each boasting profuse natural light, clean white walls, gently-coloured finishings and lavish marble bathrooms with frosted glass panels.
Free superspeed internet wireless access.

title: Portas de Benfica (Benfica Toll Booth)
short_description: Discover the Portas de Benfica, an interesting set of 19th-century buildings once occupied by the customs police. It demarcated the city’s tax limits and is today a curiosity.
full_description: In the middle of a lively residential working class neighbourhood in Lisbon can be found the Portas de Benfica. Located between Benfica and Amadora, at the outer edges of the city, it has existed since the 19th century. There were once 26 official entrance points into the city and this was one, occupied by the customs authority and marking the capital’s tax boundaries. A toll was levied here on people and goods coming into the city. The original building, with eight turrets, was rebuilt in 1996. Come and discover a less well-known aspect of this area’s history, which was formerly occupied by farms. The Estrada de Benfica, which is almost 4 km long, is also interesting with lots of traditional shops and dotted with typical patisseries.

title: Zero Zero - Príncipe Real
short_description: The pizzeria that brings Italy to Lisbon! To enter ZeroZero is to forget the city for a brief moment and immerse yourself in the heart of Italy. All the products are of Italian origin, especially from the Veneto region. Príncipe Real has a new 100% Italian destination.
full_description:
PIZZAS
With a balanced mix of Flour 00 and other flours that are milled slowly on stone, at Pizzeria ZeroZero the dough for our pizzas is prepared using the indirect method, also known as indirect crusting or poolish. We adopt an ancient process that begins with a pre-fermentation at a controlled temperature, followed by a maturation of at least 48 hours.
The end result is an artisan pizza base, thin and crunchy, fragrant like the best freshly baked bread, light and digestible.
MENUS
From classic to speciality pizzas, fresh pastas and creamy risotti, exclusive to ZeroZero Príncipe Real, the menu is very varied and also includes several vegetarian options. All the dishes are written in Italian and offer a real journey through the flavours of Italy.
A meal at ZeroZero can start with some antipasti, such as Carpaccio di Tonno and Panzanella, and is never complete without a coppa di gelati or a dolce, such as the typical tiramisù.
PROSECCHERIA
The Prosecco bar, right at the entrance, has an exclusive Italian wine list with over 80 references from various wine regions and micro-regions in Italy. It also includes classic and signature cocktails.
Behind the bar you can admire the mural created by illustrator Ana Gil, with details of the different ingredients so typical of Italian gastronomy and indicating the region from which each product comes.
ITALIAN CHARCUTERIE
Also at the entrance, there is a small, distinctive window display with some of the best Italian products used in the pizzeria. Cheeses and sausages, all DOP or IGP, which can be taken home or served on the spot, accompanied by a glass of prosecco.

title: Museum of Aljube – Resistance and Freedom
short_description: Discover the Museu do Aljube – Resistência e Liberdade whose name belies its aim: to remember and document the struggle against Portugal’s dictatorship.
full_description:
As its name implies, the Museu do Aljube – Resistência e Liberdade is an activist museum with a clear purpose: to remember and document the struggle against the dictatorship which governed Portugal from 1926 to 1974.
Represented here is resistance in the name of freedom and democracy, a tribute to all those who fought and risked their lives during the years of continuous struggle and for which they were often persecuted and denounced.
Housed in the Aljube building, which dates back to the Moorish era, its name means “dry well”, “cistern”, “dungeon” and “prison” – a role which it almost always fulfilled.
It was a political prison from 1928 to 1965 and has now seen its purpose reversed, examining in an informed and informative manner the ways in which fascism was resisted and fought against.
It’s worth learning more about this period in Portugal’s recent history.

title: Lisboat
short_description: “LISBOAT – a new way to love Lisbon”. LISBOAT river cruise is a Hop On Hop Off sightseeing cruise, allowing embark and disembark at any pier of the tour during the ticket validity.
full_description:
It's very easy to love Lisbon. It was from their waters that we discovered the world and it is on them that LISBOAT invites everyone to see Lisbon with different eyes, in a unique perspective of the city. LISBOAT Tagus cruises it's a Hop On Hop Off sightseeing tour, allowing embark and disembark at any pier of the tour during the ticket validity.
The cruises take place between Belém and Cais do Sodré, and / or vice versa, passing through Terreiro do Paço and other monuments and points of interest in the Portuguese capital.
The cruise offers breathtaking views, full of stories to tell, as well as moments of relaxation, packed by the waters of the Tagus.
location: Rua dos Remolares, 14 – 1º andar – Sala 101, 1200-371, Lisboa

title: Tropical Botanical Garden
short_description: Visit the Jardim Botânico Tropical, also known as the Colonial Garden, created in 1906 and totally designed for the purposes of education and scientific knowledge.
full_description: It’s had many names, but the plants that live in Jardim Botânico Tropical, in Lisbon’s most select area, have more difficult ones to pronounce. The over 500 perennial species, of tropical or subtropical origin, are examples of species which have even died out in their natural habitats, such as Ginkgo Biloba. The Jardim Botânico Tropical, also known as the Colonial Garden, was created as the Tropical Agriculture Museum Garden, as both its scientific and educational purpose came under the colonial agriculture department. Part of the Instituto Superior de Agronomia for decades, nowadays its job is to showcase the work of the Instituto de Investigação Científica Tropical and venue for appropriate themed exhibitions. Take a rest from your roaming amid the imperial monuments at the Jardim Tropical and feel like you’ve travelled to distant lands.

title: Pavilion of Knowledge
short_description: The Pavilion of Knowledge - Ciência Viva is an interactive science and technology museum, aims to stimulate scientific knowledge and promote scientific culture among all citizens.
full_description:
The Pavilion of Knowledge - Ciência Viva is an interactive science and technology museum that has been open to the public since July 25th 1999. It is an integral part of the Ciência Viva Centres Network and serves as both the driving force and resource centre for this network.
The main objective is to stimulate scientific knowledge and promote scientific and technological culture among all citizens. The exhibitions and activities proposed allow visitors to explore many wide-ranging themes with interactive Exhibits, relaxing and having fun at the same time.
Besides the major theme exhibitions, the Pavilion of Knowledge - Ciência Viva promotes various scientific and educational initiatives.

short_description: In the historic Condes da Ribeira Grande Palace and in a modern extension, the MACAM Hotel has 64 rooms where guests can enjoy the elegance of a 18th century building combined with the comfort and design of contemporary architecture.
full_description:
Art everywhere
What sets this hotel apart is its perfect fusion with the Armando Martins Museum of Contemporary Art (MACAM).
On the walls, on the terraces and in all the hotel's rooms, guests will be able to appreciate a careful selection of works from the MACAM Collection. A unique opportunity to experience the world of art.
The MACAM Hotel's rooms are home to works from the Collection and will offer guests a unique experience and a more private interaction with art. All the elegantly styled rooms will feature works by Portuguese and foreign artists from various generations of the contemporary era.

Uma das suas principais missões é a criação de conteúdos culturais para a valorização do destino Portugal. Desde 2007, data da sua formação, a EIN tem atraído milhões de pessoas às salas de espetáculos portuguesas, com a promoção de concertos de artistas como Coldplay, Bon Jovi, Beyoncé, Madonna, Pearl Jam, Leonard Cohen, entre muito outros.
A promotora é ainda responsável pelo NOS Alive, um dos maiores festivais europeus e o mais importante festival nacional, bem como pela exposição individual temporária mais visitada de sempre em Portugal, "Joana Vasconcelos no Palácio Nacional da Ajuda", e pelas exposições de sucesso “Prado em Lisboa e “Os Saboias. The
Luster Hotel

All the rooms have a desk and high-speed Wi-Fi. But what matters most is that from luxurious amenities to decorative elements, everything has been designed in detail to offer you a unique, friendly welcome.

After a walk around the city or a day’s work, the only thing you want to do is relax. Our double or twin rooms were designed for this purpose. Contemporary decoration, with every detail in terms of comfort inviting you to relax until the hustle and bustle of the city calls you back.
SUITES
Our suites make you feel that the privilege of having a luxury apartment in Lisbon has been reserved especially for you. Find more space for your privacy and to accommodate all the family. Spacious, abounding in light and comfort, endowed with a modern, sophisticated atmosphere they are ideal for your to enjoy an absolute sense of peace of mind, on an exclusive basis.
SPA
We know what it means to stroll through the streets of a city which is made for walking, and we know how invigorating it is to release the energy accumulated after a long day's work.

title: Graça
short_description: Discover one of the best views of Lisbon. Sitting at the top of one of the city’s famous hills, the view lets you write the history of Lisbon through the naked eye.
full_description: The ideal place to combine two of the things you can’t miss when in Lisbon: riding on the no. 28 tram to Graça and enjoying the view at the Miradouro da Graça. The shade of the church and its pine trees give it a certain air of spiritual refuge for those who take a rest at the café or on the walls which surround it. Located on the hill next to that of the castle, the view of Lisbon is quite simply stunning. The city falls away at your feet, the daily life of the historic neighbourhoods goes about its business, the Tagus flows in the background and the sound seems to get lost at this tranquil spot so popular with those who like to discover Lisbon on foot.

title: Miradouro de Monte Agudo
short_description: Discover one of the calmest viewing points (miradouros) in Lisbon. Unknown by the vast majority of visitors, it has a unique view of a part of the city that is rarely explored.
full_description: The Miradouro do Monte Agudo is one of Lisbon’s best-kept secrets. Its location between Graça and Anjos is hidden by the Dona Luísa de Gusmão secondary school. It has a fabulous view to the north and entire western part of the city. Built in the 1950s, it provides shade, peace and tranquillity for enjoyment with time. Let your eyes roam as you try to identify the various points of the city. Or take the chance to rest as you cross Rua Heliodoro Salgado towards Rua Ilha do Príncipe. The pergola provides excellent shade in summer, as well as the kiosk where you can relax and enjoy a refreshing drink. With a view of Lisbon.

title: Residencial Horizonte
short_description: Located in the city center, facing the underground station (Parque), the Residencial Horizonte Hotel offers 53 rooms.
full_description:
Hotel in Lisbon with Underground station across the street. Its prime location in the city center, along with pleasant Parque Eduardo VII gardens, the shopping area and facing the Underground Station (Parque) provides a quick and convenient access to major points of interest.
On the one hand the vicinity, the other a vast network of transport available to those visiting the capital, allowing easy access to places such as Historical Downtown, Belém (discoveries heritage), the docks in Alcantara, Park of Nations (formerly Expo 98), Lisbon International Airport or even Lisbon surroundings ( Cascais, Estoril, Sintra).
Horizonte Residence is a Tourist Accommodation that offers a calm and welcoming environment with practical and comfortable facilities. The family-like atmosphere of this lodging is characterized by quality and warmth, aiming to ensure our guests feel at home.
We offer a variety of rooms with all the comfort for a carefree stay. Explore our welcoming facilities.

title: Open Brasserie Mediterrânica
short_description: Open is a gluten free certified restaurant with a mediterranean inspired cuisine.
full_description:
Open Brasserie Mediterrânica is settled in Inspira Santa Marta Hotel and its cuisine highlights the best mediterranean influences combined with the most traditional elements of the portuguese cuisine.
With a premium quality service, this restaurant shares the main principles of sustainability, transversals to the entire hotel.
The menu respects the rhythm of the seasons and pays special tribute to local products and organic, if possible.
This menu includes options for those who, for medical conditions or even for preference, choose a particular food diet and is proud to offer its clients several vegetarian, vegan or lactose-free dishes.
Actually, this is also a gluten-free certified restaurant by Portuguese Association for Celiac.

title: Olga Cadaval Cultural Centre
short_description: With an eclectic programme of concerts, film, dance, theatre and more, this modern venue dedicated to timeless art is worth discovering in the heart of romantic Sintra.
full_description: One of the most dynamic and important cultural venues in Lisbon is actually in Sintra. In the former Cine-Teatro Carlos Manuel, totally restored for the Sintra Festival, a cultural centre has been created. The Marquise of Cadaval, born in Turin, came to Colares where she married and rapidly became the patron of countless artists and a promoter of a wide variety of activities linked to music. In her honour, Sintra named this cultural centre after her, where you can find an eclectic programme of concerts, film, dance, theatre and much more. Discover a modern venue dedicated to timeless art in the heart of romantic Sintra.
location: Praça Dr. Francisco Sá Carneiro, 2710-720, Sintra

With more than a thousand events created for the most important companies in Portugal and around the world, Seaventy has the right experience to satisfy the most demanding customers.

title: SIBS
short_description: Founded in 1983, SIBS provides financial, modern, reliable and secure services, namely in the payments’ area, to more than 300 million users, from three different Continents, processing more than 4 billion transactions annually.
full_description:
We offer a global and diversified portfolio of payment solutions.
Alongside being a major payment processor in Europe, SIBS is a benchmark in Security and Anti-Fraud solutions and services in Business Process Outsourcing and the Iberian leader in Card Production and Personalization.
SIBS grew innovating and it will continue its mission. SIBS takes technology as its driver and through it reinvented existing payment methods and created MB WAY, Portugal’s most modern and complete mobile payments service, with more than 2,6 million users that make purchases, instant transfers and withdrawals using only the smartphone. It’s also responsible for the management of ATM Express and MULTIBANCO networks, to which SIBS added dozens of features, many of them pioneer worldwide, turning the largest ATM network in Portugal also one of the most complete in the world. In parallel, SIBS manages multiple digital payment channels, from Automatic Payment Terminals to online channels or mobile phones.
SIBS established itself as an important international player, present in several markets, namely in Europe, Asia and Africa, where SIBS solutions operate in more than 350 thousand terminals that process around 2 billion transactions. Noteworthy SIBS activity in Romania, where it has acquired the leading company in card payment processing – Romcard / Supercard – that now operate under SIBS Romania – and in Poland, where SIBS owns Paytel, one of the most dynamic payment terminal operators in that market. SIBS has a relevant position in these major markets in Central and Easter Europe, contributing with Portuguese technology and expertise to the development and digitalization of these countries.
Committed in its daily work, SIBS is constantly pursuing its mission of being the reference partner of public and private entities, creating value for society through the development and management of payment solutions, processes and related services based on technology that combines safety, convenience, and innovation, respecting the good behavioral principles and the sustainability conditions.
location: Rua Soeiro Pereira Gomes, Lote 1, 1649-031, Lisboa

title: Lisbon Cycle Paths
short_description: Discover Lisbon on two wheels, exploring another way of seeing the city. The cycle paths are not inter-connected, but they are a great way to explore certain parts of the capital.
full_description:
Lisbon’s cycle paths are one of the best ways to see the city. The star is the riverside route which accompanies the Tagus for more than 20 km between the port, the various docks and the city’s most important historic zones.
You just need to watch out for tourists and joggers using the path as a panoramic pavement. But there are many other cycle paths to discover Lisbon: the urban and elegant Avenida Duque d’Ávila, the route through the gardens of Campo Grande towards the Quinta das Conchas, and the one through Jardim Amália Rodrigues to Parque Eduardo VII.
They are calm and safe and make pedalling a pleasure. In Parque das Nações, you’ll also find silent and calm routes.
In general, drivers respect cyclists where they have to cross the road since the cycle paths are not all interlinked.

title: Navy Planetarium
short_description: The Navy Planetarium was inaugurated on the 20th of july 1965 by Head of State, Admiral Américo Thomaz, and Minister for Public Works, Azeredo Perdigão.
full_description:
Come to the Navy Planetarium and get closer to the sky, with a 25 meters (82ft) dome, feel like an astronaut among the stars. In what is one of the largest planetariums in the world, we have several sessions that allow us to understand what goes on beyond what eyes reach.
In addition to the Auditorium, it also has a Gallery with several themed exhibitions, an Astronomical Observatory (in recovery), and a Library holding approximately 1000 books.
It was inaugurated on July 20th, 1965, by the occasion of the celebration of the 10th anniversary of the death of Calouste Gulbenkian. The Planetarium bears the flag of a Live Science center and is an active part of several national and international astronomy projects.
Here we can understand the difficulties that sailors of yore had when navigating the unknown oceans, and also, we study the challenges that today's sailors face, trying to know the infinite space.
There are sessions to the various age groups and in diverse languages. There is also available special exhibitions for teachers, younger audiences and activities for school groups.

title: SANA Malhoa Hotel
short_description: SANA Malhoa Hotel has a central location in Lisbon, between Sete Rios and Praça de Espanha and 5 minutes from Marquês de Pombal and Baixa, and stands out for its contemporary decoration and comfortable atmosphere.
full_description:
Located in the centre of Lisbon, in the heart of Avenida José Malhoa - in Sete Rios district -, SANA Malhoa Hotel stands out for its contemporary decoration and comfortable atmosphere.
Everything for you to have a relaxed and healthy accommodation in Lisbon.
A few minutes walk from Praça de Espanha and El Corte Inglés, near various access routes and public transport connections and with a private car park.
SANA Malhoa Hotel also offers added facilities for your business or pleasure trips to Lisbon: Health Club with gym, sauna, Turkish bath and Jacuzzi and 7 meeting and events rooms with a capacity for 700 people.

title: UACS
short_description: Non-profitable employers body of associations for the Lisbon region, congregating 14 associations from different kinds of activities, focused on commerce and services.
full_description:
The União de Associações do Comércio e Serviços (UACS) was established in 1870, and it is a private, non-profitable body of associations with its services being considered as public utility.
Through its 14 associations, its services and technical expertise, we aim to answer the entrepreneur’s questions, always keeping in mind the development of the sectors of commerce and services.
These sectors develop a major role in employment, generating business and contribute deeply to Portugal’s gross domestic product.
New brands, new concepts and entrepreneurship are our fighting flags in a sector that is able of continuous renovation, while working to keep the tradition, the charm and the sense of closeness.

Assuring that all clients expectations are reached by creating unforgettable experiences.
Always with a smile!

title: Carmo Archaeological Museum
short_description: During the 19th and 20th centuries, MAC incorporated a series of pieces of historical, archaeological and artistic importance from Pre-History to the present day.
full_description:
In 1863, royal architect, Joaquim Passidónio da Silva, founded the Portuguese Civil Architects Association, from which the Association of Portuguese Archaeologists was created.
About one year later, in 1864, the Carmo Archaeological Museum was installed there for the storage and display of important sculptures from old ruined buildings (particularly from the monastic houses which were closed in 1834), and items which were part of the temple itself and were found among the rubble.
During the 19th and 20th centuries, MAC incorporated a series of pieces of historical, archaeological and artistic importance from many different chronological ages, with artefacts and works, which date from Pre-History to the present day and show the way people have thought and felt in different areas of culture through the ages.

title: José Saramago Foundation
short_description: A cultural institution located at Casa dos Bicos
full_description: The José Saramago Foundation is a cultural institution located at Casa dos Bicos, in Lisbon, which carries the name of the Nobel Prize of Literature 1998.

title: Countess of Edla Chalet and Gardens
short_description: This romantic pavilion in Pena Park was inspired by Swiss mountains houses. However, most of the materials used, including the cork and the masonry, are from Portugal.
full_description:
Lying within the Pena Park, the Chalet of the Countess of Edla was built in 1870, on the orders of the Prince Consort Fernando II.
This fascinatingly romantic and highly scenic pavilion was inspired by the houses found in the Swiss mountains.
However, most of the materials used in its construction were sourced locally, including all the cork and masonry.
The surrounding garden and park, with their leafy vegetation, are populated with botanical species that were brought in from all over the world.
Of particular interest are the arboreal ferns from Australia and New Zealand.

Located between the historical area and the business centre of Lisbon, in VIP Grand Lisboa Hotel & SPA you will find the widest range of services, at the highest level.
At this Hotel, with a total of 295 rooms, including 31 Suites, you can enjoy the relaxing ambience, surrounded by state-of-the-art equipments and panoramic views over the city of Lisbon.
Decorated in a cosy and modern way, in this Hotel you will be able to enjoy all the comfort and services of a 5 star hotel, namely: the Cenário restaurant, the Press Club bar, and an outdoor swimming pool.
The Hotel has also private parking and Wireless Internet access. It is also possible to enjoy the SPA & Wellness Centre that will make you feel renewed.

title: Rossio
full_description:
Rossio Square is the popular name of the Pedro IV Square (Portuguese: Praça de D. Pedro IV) in the city of Lisboa. It is located in the Pombaline Downtown of Lisbon and has been one of its main squares since the Middle Ages.
The Rossio has been a meeting place for people of Lisbon for centuries. Some of the cafés and shops of the square date from the 18th century. The building of the Maria II Theatre and the Public Gardens to the north of the square only made the area more attended by Lisbon high society in the 19th century.

title: GN Cellar
short_description: With more than 80 years of history Garrafeira Nacional has a vast and valuable wine and spirits collection
full_description:
GARRAFEIRA NACIONAL was founded in 1927.
With eight decades of trade history, this family-run merchant has been supplying exclusive fine wines to clients throughout the country from its Lisbon base, featuring a sumptuous assortment of unique, flawlessly-crafted premium vintage wines and liquors carefully sourced from a variety of established growers, passionate winemakers and private cellars.
Our wine collection is one of the most coveted in the country, encompassing some of the rarest Ports, Madeiras, whiskeys, cognacs, champagnes and exquisite beverages, hand-picked with utmost attention to authenticity and origin.
The shop is staffed by more than 20 dedicated professionals under the expert guidance of Jaime Vaz.
Together, they pursue a deeply rooted passion for wine-making, firmly embodied in Garrafeira Nacional's core values: discovering, treasuring and sharing the ageless delight to be found in the finest wines.

title: National Museum of Theatre and Dance
short_description: The National Museum of Theatre and Dance is the main archive preserving the memories and history of performing arts in Portugal: Theatre, Dance and Opera.
full_description:
[ closed for renovations ]
The National Museum of Theatre and Dance endeavours to further knowledge about the history and current situation of performing arts.
It also preserves, organises, investigates, documents and disseminates its collections. The museum is housed at the Monteiro-Mor Palace, an 18th century building, restored and adapted for this purpose.
Its current collection, which started in 1979, features over 300,000 pieces that include costumes and stage props, scenes, figurines, billboards, programmes, records and music scores and about 120,000 photographs.
The museum also has a specialised library containing 35,000 volumes.
The museum periodically holds exhibitions dedicated to celebrities who were involved in performing arts and also to less known aspects of theatre and dance, as well as activities related to performing arts in general.

additional_sections.title: Additional Information

title: Museum of Portuguese Decorative Arts
short_description: The Foundation fulfills its cultural mission through the Museum of Portuguese Decorative Arts, the Arts and Crafts Workshops and completes its project with the schools, organizing visits and activities for different kinds of public.
full_description:
The Ricardo do Espírito Santo Silva Foundation, created in 1953 by Ricardo do Espírito Santo Silva, is a Portuguese institution of private law and public utility, recognized nationally and internationally for its technical and scientific skills in the area of decorative arts, traditional crafts and conservation and restoration.
Its mission is the protection, promotion, dissemination and transmission of the art of know-how in decorative arts. Reaffirming its role and importance in the cultural context, FRESS integrates:
The Museum of Portuguese Decorative Arts
– the conservation, research and exhibition of works bequeathed by the founder are in line with the importance given to the traditional Portuguese arts and crafts. A guided tour of the Museum can be combined with a guided tour of the Workshops.
FRESSforma is our school
that has as its vocation the teaching and preservation of a remarkable know-how. The training is provided by highly qualified masters and trainers.
18 Traditional Arts and Crafts Workshops
that produce new works of rare technical and artistic quality and integrate traditional methods with more contemporary language.
The Department of Conservation and Restoration
ensures the conservation and restoration of furniture and immovable heritage, both in laboratory and in shipyard.
Our organic model benefits from the articulation between the Museum of Portuguese Decorative Arts, FRESSforma, 18 Workshops and the Department of Conservation and Restoration.
After several decades, the Foundation continues to be a prestigious reference in the dissemination and preservation of the know-how of Portuguese decorative arts.
location: Largo das Portas do Sol, 2, 1100-411, Lisboa

title: Olivia Lisboa Hotel
short_description: Located in one of the most emblematic areas of Lisbon, our hotel offers an ideal accommodation experience for both business travelers and those looking to explore the magic of the Portuguese capital.
full_description:
In the most sophisticated heart of Lisbon, our five-star hotel stands on the distinguished corner of Rua Castilho with Rua Alexandre Herculano. Just a few steps from the iconic Avenida da Liberdade, you will enjoy a privileged location, surrounded by exclusive boutiques, art galleries, and the best of haute cuisine. A unique enclave that combines the elegance of a residential neighborhood with immediate access to the most vibrant center of the city.
And the best part: you will be just a 10-minute walk from the trendiest neighborhood in Lisbon, Príncipe Real. From there, a pleasant stroll will take you to the lively Bairro Alto and the sophisticated Chiado, which connects directly to the historic Baixa Pombalina, where you will find Plaza D. Pedro IV (better known as Rossio) and the emblematic Praça do Comércio, right on the banks of the majestic Tagus River.
Discover Lisbon from an unbeatable location, designed for those seeking exclusivity, comfort, and authenticity.
ROOMS
Our hotel has a total of 143 rooms designed to provide comfort and style. These are distributed as follows:
96 rooms between 22 and 25 m², perfect for individual or couple stays.
7 suites, exclusive spaces for those seeking a superior level of luxury and spaciousness.
The remaining 40 rooms, with surfaces ranging from 25 to 30 m², ideal for those who want a little more space during their stay.
Each room is equipped with modern furniture, high-speed Wi-Fi connection, state-of-the-art TVs, and all the necessary amenities to make your experience unforgettable.

title: Casa de Linhares
short_description: CASA DE LINHARES is today what remains of a Renaissance building that collapsed in the 1755 earthquake, and today it is a house where fado takes place every night with some of fado's most emblematic names.
full_description:
CASA DE LINHARES is today what remains of a Renaissance building that collapsed in the 1755 earthquake. This was the home of the Counts of Linhares, one of Portugal's noble families. Built on the banks of the Tagus in the 16th century, the Casa de Linhares still retains its 19th century charm, with its turret and coat of arms.
Fado emerged in the 19th century in the city's historic neighbourhoods and in popular social contexts. It was sung spontaneously and the lyrics portrayed people's experiences, using slang. However, the presence of aristocratic figures in this bohemian life dictated a different future for the history of Fado.
Played and sung by professionals, Fado was established in houses of its own, where traditional Portuguese dishes and snacks could also be savoured. Other houses also sprang up, where you could hear Fado vadio, sung by amateurs and à desgarrada. This ritual has continued to this day.
Fado's importance was recognised when it was declared Intangible Cultural Heritage of Humanity by UNESCO in November 2011.
location: Beco dos Armazéns de Linho, 2, 1100-037, Lisboa

title: Lisbon Sports Club
short_description: Located in Serra da Carregueira, Belas, about 20 km from Lisbon. The 18 hole circuit is one of the most original in the whole country: it is nested in a valley, making the most of the plains situated on the north side of the mountains.
full_description:
Since 1992 the LSC it prides itself on 18 holes, PAR 69, over 5,261 meters, with an extremely rich and beautiful forest biodiversity and a striking brook which runs throughout the entire course.
The itinerary's layout is that of a typical Scottish golf course. Inaugurated in 1964, with nine holes designed by Hawtree & Sons, it was initially extended to 14 and finally to 18.
The holes are characterized by an assortment of different designs, which implies a range of different and interesting game possibilities. Some holes encompass criss-crossing paths, allowing golfers to intermingle along the course. The highlights of the LSC course are definitely holes number 1, 3 and 18, which stand out thanks to their natural beauty, unique layouts and relative difficulty.
The first hole is a PAR 3 and it is 169 meters long. It showcases the whole LSC course, with tees about 10 metres higher than the green, which in turn is surrounded by four bunkers. The entire right side of the hole is escorted by a brook.
Hole 3 is also a PAR 3, 176 meters long, and considered by many one of the most beautiful golf course holes in Portugal. It also features elevated tees and a brook in the middle of the fairway which follows the right side of the green.
The vegetation is stunning, generating a unique layout. Hole number 18 is the biggest challenge for the players, not only for its length but also because it lays claim to a brook that meanders along the route to the green, this latter protected by two bunkers.
Thanks to the existence of large amounts of water and an automatic irrigation system in all holes on the course, the quality of the fairways and greens of the LSC golf course is rather high all year round. This places the Lisbon Sports Club as one of the best in the whole Greater Lisbon area.
The course in enveloped by the Serra da Carregueira, displaying a wide variety of vegetation and truly breathtaking scenery. One can often enjoy the sightings of animals such as rabbits and partridges, as well as a wide variety of birds, which presence and singing turn the time spent on the golf course even more enjoyable and relaxing.
The inexistence of houses around the course, as well as the fact that the LSC is not integrated into any real estate development, has allowed the club to remain an extremely calm and resting facility, unique situation amongst golf clubs in the Greater Lisbon area.

title: Geological Museum
short_description: If you want to take a real journey back in time while in Lisbon, discover the Museu Geológico in the former convent of Jesus. It’s stood there for over 150 years.
full_description: If you want to take a real journey back in time while in Lisbon, discover the Museu Geológico in the former convent of Jesus. Built over 150 years ago, its 18th-century character and exhibition structure have remained unchanged over the decades and is thus considered the “museum of museums”. Besides its very rich geological and archaeological exhibits, which recount the research and discoveries made in Portugal over the years, the museum itself is a journey into the past. The permanent exhibition consists of four rooms: palaeontology and stratigraphy; the palaeontology of vertebrates; pre-historical archaeology; and mineralogy. Enter modern Bairro Alto and end up examining the composition of Earth millions of years ago.
location: R. da Academia das Ciências, 19, 1200-003, Lisboa

title: Jardim Botânico da Faculdade de Ciências de Lisboa
short_description: Learn more about the species in the Lisbon botanical and scientific garden. This is the perfect place for a few hours of wandering amid the tranquillity and variety of plants.
full_description:
Between the neighbourhoods of Rato and Princípe Real and amid museums and unique boutiques, you’ll find one of the best scientific gardens in Europe.
It is the perfect place for a break while exploring the city on foot. Inviting, tranquil and complete, the Botanical Garden of the Lisbon Faculty of Sciences was created for the use of the polytechnic in 1837.
Thanks to Edmund Goeze and Jules Daveau, the two gardeners most responsible for its construction, it brought together a huge variety of plants from the furthest reaches of the world. Hothouses, irrigation systems and a lovely arboretum were all designed.
It has notable rare species and a unique butterfly house, ideal for adding romance to your cosmopolitan stroll around Princípe Real.

additional_sections.title: Additional Information
additional_sections.text: Closed for Renovation

title: Casa Roque Gameiro
short_description: Off the beaten track, this is a unique house that shows a different side of the city. Visit one of the exhibitions and let the house cast its spell over you.
full_description: The Casa Roque Gameiro is one of the oldest and most picturesque houses to be found in the municipality of Amadora. Turned into a cultural centre, this house designed by the famous Portuguese painter Roque Gameiro who specialised in watercolours, today holds various local exhibitions. The artist was clearly inspired by nationalistic ideals when building the house. It is typically Portuguese, combining the architectural characteristics of various parts of the country. In a style very much in vogue in the late 19th century, the décor includes naturalist, revivalist and folk elements. This dynamic and interesting cultural centre is well worth visiting, in an area that is emerging as representative of alternative culture in present day Lisbon.

full_description: We work daily to deserve your preference, we bring the Sea to the table with excellent service and the freshest fish and seafood.

title: AHP - Portuguese Hotels Association
short_description: AHP is the largest and most representative hotel association in Portugal, with more than a century of experience at the service of Portuguese hospitality.
full_description:
AHP - Associação de Hotelaria de Portugal is the largest hotel association in Portugal, with more than 900 members which, between independent units and large chains, represent more than two-thirds of the hotel contribution to the national economy.
It is a private, non-profit association with centenary roots, recognized as a Public Utility Institution in 2013.
As an employer’s association, AHP focuses its efforts on creating the best business conditions for hotel activity in Portugal, providing various consulting services, information sharing and knowledge creation; negotiation of contracts and partnerships and intervening systematically in the political, economic and social spheres, in the defence and recognition of Tourism and Hospitality as a fundamental sector for the sustainable and responsible growth of the country's economy.

title: Prazeres Cemetery and Museum
short_description: Cemeteries can be museums, chiefly when their occupants are interested in the arts and their last resting places. At Prazeres, there are magnificent works of art to see.
full_description: If you want to see several unique works of art, in the only open-air museum in Lisbon, then the Prazeres Cemetery and its museological pieces is the place to go. At the end of the no. 28 tram line, you’ll find a place housing the largest and oldest collection of cypresses in the Iberian peninsula. This is where the most important families in Lisbon hired architects, draughtsmen, sculptors and other artists to create their mausoleums and tombs. With the graves of countless famous figures and a variety of paths to follow, you can visit by yourself or join a guided tour. This 19th-century cemetery is impressive for its size and location, with an amazing view of Lisbon, the bridge and the river. Also visit the chapel, where you can see a collection of objects dedicated to the funeral ceremony, as well as an old autopsy room. Come and discover the quietest place in Lisbon.

title: The Regional Association of Hoteliers of the Estoril Coast, Sintra, Mafra and Oeiras
short_description: The Regional Association of Hoteliers of the Estoril Coast, Sintra, Mafra and Oeiras, is a business association that advocates the interests of hoteliers in the region.
full_description:
The association
The tourist legacy of the Estoril Coast, formerly called the Portuguese Riviera, allowed the hotel industry to ally, over the years, tradition to innovation, refinement and glamor to service of Excellency, where no detail is overlooked.
Resulting in a remarkable hospitality and responding exactly to what the visitor is looking for whether he is enjoying his vacation, alone or with his family, in his business trip, or even when organizing meetings.
The Regional Association of Hoteliers of the Estoril Coast, Sintra, Mafra and Oeiras, is a business association that for 30 years has been advocating the interests of hoteliers in the region and has called for a sustained and sustainable development of tourism in its area of influence.
Since 1985 that this Association has been fighting for the revitalization of tourism in the region, as well as the key player in the development of a recovery plan for the existing tourist infrastructures and in the creation of new centres of attraction for this area.
The uncompromising defence of the interests of all of those who invest the tourism in this region and the constant willingness to mediate the relationship between the private and the regional and national institutions continue to be our main goal.
We do this either through dialogue with the public authorities to establish, change or upgrade the infrastructures or proposing legislative measures and practices that address the protection of hotel companies and tourist interests of the municipalities of Cascais, Sintra, Mafra and Oeiras, or still want to support any relevant action of entertainment and event organization to the region.

title: Casino Estoril
short_description: Welcome to Casino Estoril. The largest complex of entertainment and leisure in Portugal.
full_description:
Casino Estoril is a great gaming and leisure complex distinguished by its ability to boost the arts and entertainment, and which no tourists fail to visit. Located in Estoril, 18 km from Lisbon and 20 km from its international airport, Casino Estoril is the largest in Europe, consolidating, year after year, a worldwide prestige.
Reference of Casino Estoril, Salão Preto e Prata is a prestigious show and diningroom. It´s equipped with state-of-the-art technology, with capacity to 1000 people. The most famous room in Casino Estoril is distinguished by the fact that it also hosts major international conferences. It is a true "show cathedral" in Portugal that selectively selects an artistic program of excellence.
Casino Estoril also has a modern auditorium with capacity for 350 people, which can host congresses, as well as jazz, theater, classical music or concerts.
The art gallery is one of the most prestigious in the country, with permanent painting and sculpture exhibitions.
Meanwhile, the Chinese restaurant Estoril Mandarim has been distinguished as one of the most elegant in Europe, offering its customers the authentic art of cooking in the region of Guangdong, made with imported products.

title: Pirescouxe Castle
short_description: Skip the usual tourist routes and come and discover another castle close to Lisbon. In an area little visited by tourists, this castle is a special find.
full_description:
Come and visit a 15th century castle in one of the least visited areas of Lisbon.
By the Tagus river, after passing Sacavém and before reaching Santa Iria da Azóia, you will find Pirescoxe and its castle.
In late Gothic style and adapted by Nuno Vasques de Castelo Branco for his residence.
This castle, on an elevation with a magnificent view of the river’s course, owes its name partly to a documentary reference mentioning Pêro Escouche or Pires, the Lame.
Choose the version that pleases you the most and be sure to visit this old fortified house that keeps the original towers and battlements.
Today, it is a cultural space with an art gallery and exhibition room.
Get out of the usual routes and come and visit this special castle so close to the centre of Lisbon.
location: Praça Viscondes de Castelo Branco, Santa Iria de Azóia, 2690-414, Loures

title: SUD Lisboa Hall
short_description: SUD Lisboa. Two venues of strong personality, united in perfect coexistence with the Tagus and their will of creating amazing sceneries.
full_description:
Here every view and every moment is unique. Come and discover the place where everything happens.
Based in the maritime area of Belém and the nearby 25th April Bridge, SUD Lisboa is located in the Brasília Avenue, about 5 km from the city center, with great accessibilities and proximity to the main access road to North and South of the country.
In this prime location you can find historical monuments, museums and cultural centers like the Jerónimos Monastery, Museu dos Coches, the Cultural Center of Belém, amongst others.
Belém has the greenery of the gardens, the freshness of the fountains and the breeze of the Tagus river that combined create the perfect environment for your Event.

title: National Music Museum
short_description: Museu Nacional da Música houses one of Europe’s richest collections of musical instruments. It develops a regular programme with concerts, theme tours and workshops.
full_description:
The Museu Nacional da Música houses one of Europe’s richest collections of musical instruments. Among them are National Treasures such as the Chevillard Stradivarius cello – King of Portugal, the Antunes harpsichord or the Pascal Taskin harpsichord.
The collection holds instruments made in Portugal and in other countries, dating from the 16th to the 21st centuries, of an erudite and popular tradition.
Apart from the instruments mentioned, the museum also houses a Documentation Centre, where visitors can find documents as well as phonograms and musical iconography.
Its vast cultural programme includes concerts, theme tours and workshops.

title: Mama Shelter Lisboa
short_description: Between the Avenida da Liberdade and Principe Real, Mama couldn't find a better place for you to discover Lisbon’s lifestyle. Just a stone’s throw of the Park Eduardo VII with a breathtaking view of the Lisbon bay from the rooftop.
full_description:
Between the Avenida da Liberdade and Principe Real, Mama couldn't find a better place for you to discover Lisbon’s lifestyle.
Just a stone’s throw of the Park Eduardo VII with a breathtaking view of the Lisbon bay from the rooftop, you’ll find this new setting next to the Botanical garden perfect for your city breaks.
Stay
In the city of 7 hills, Mama knows that you will need every comfort after a day of climbing in Lisbon. She offers you no less than 130 rooms with all the usual comforts and 5* bedding, open bathrooms and spacious showers.
There might even be some more surprises…
Restaurant, Bar & Rooftop
Lisbon is proud of its maritime heritage and Mama found a way to honor it in her restaurant with its distinctive decoration – a Portuguese reinterpretation of a French brasserie. Pizza, revisited tapas and many more delicious dishes always to share at Mama Lisboa. The rooftop will offer you a view of Lisbon and the Tagus that you’ll never forget.

title: O Quinto Esquerdo
short_description: Located in Lisbon, "O Quinto Esquerdo" is 1.5 km from Avenida da Liberdade and features free WiFi. All rooms are equipped with a flat-screen TV.
full_description:
Located in Lisbon, "O Quinto Esquerdo" is 1.5 km from Avenida da Liberdade and features free WiFi.
All rooms are equipped with a flat-screen TV.
Some rooms have a seating area where you can relax.
An in-room coffee machine is also available. Rooms have a private bathroom.
The "O Quinto Esquerdo" is 2 km from Amoreiras and 2.4 km from Rossio.
location: Av. Defensores de Chaves, 23 - 5º Esq e Dto, 1000-110, Lisboa

title: No. 12 Tram
short_description: Catch one of the trams that traverse the hills of Lisbon and visit the historic bairros where life goes on as usual and the clothes lines greet the countless sunny days.
full_description:
The no. 12 tram follows a circular route through the historic neighbourhoods, bairros, and the hills at the eastern end of Lisbon.
It sets off in Praça da Figueira, crosses Martim Moniz and Socorro and climbs up to the Miradouro das Portas do Sol.
It then descends towards the Miradouro de Santa Luzia, says hello to the cathedral and ends back at Praça da Figueira.
It’s the perfect form of transport if you don’t want to end up breathless or lost when trying to reach some of historic and panoramic Lisbon’s best tourist attractions.
Catch the no. 12 to discover another side of Lisbon where the streets, alleys and lanes are connected by steps and passageways in an intricate labyrinth that you can get out of – if you follow the tram’s bell.

title: VIP Inn Berna Hotel
short_description: Your strategic Hotel in Lisbon! When convenience is of higher importance, VIP Inn Berna Hotel is the perfect choice among Lisbon’s hotels.
full_description:
It is strategically located near the Financial and Business Centre, and only a few minutes away from the Historical Centre of the city, from Lisbon's International Airport and from the Subway station of Campo Pequeno.
This 3 star Hotel has 240 rooms, including 2 suites, to provide comfort in leisure and business travels.
The Hotel also has 5 conference rooms for congresses, conventions, meetings and banquets with the capacity up to 185 people.
The guests can also have access to Wireless Internet and Private Parking.
The Restaurant works exclusively for groups and offers a complete variety of Portuguese traditional cuisine.
