title: Casa do Marquês
short_description: Leading the catering and events sector for more than 25 years, with a premium service available for all kind of private and corporate events.
full_description: Created in 1989, "Casa do Marquês" is proud to lead the catering and events sector in Portugal. More than 25 years of success are a reflection of a premium service sustained by the quality, innovation and dedication of all its team. The company offers a complete service for events, from the exclusive production of catering, to the delivery of turnkey events to private and corporate clients, up to the production of major conferences and summits – taking charge of all its planning, production, logistics, decoration, and animation needed. In its exclusive venues, or in any location of Portugal and abroad, for 10 or 10.000 people, everything is (truly) possible to make your event a one of a kind.

title: Oriente Station
short_description: Discover this structure by Calatrava that marks Lisbon’s skyline. Built in the city’s newest area, Estação Oriente is a gateway to the capital and to architecture.
full_description: Estação Oriente is one of the most notable symbols of Parque das Nações. Even if you don’t arrive by train, this work of architecture is a must-see while in Lisbon. Created by the architect and engineer Santiago Calatrava, the elevated station has an impressive glass roof and an access tunnel that allows you to take unique photographs. It is one of Lisbon’s main links to the rest of the country, as well as the city within a city that was erected after the World Oceans Expo and gave the Tagus a new lease of life. Come and discover this unique arrival and departure point that marks the city’s most contemporary and futuristic side.

title: Cipreia Dive Club - Sesimbra
short_description: Dive Club Cipreia ... diving since 1992! We offer services related to maritime activities for leisure, from boating and snorkeling to diving experiences.
location: Edifício Clube Naval – Porto de Abrigo , 2970-263, Sesimbra

title: Café Paris
short_description: A “must-visit” in Sintra’s historical centre. This is a place to visit and reminisce about!
full_description: It has become a place for socializing, and is considered the most emblematic eatery in the historical centre. It is with great honor and pride that through the decades we have welcomed and served numerous members of the European Royal Houses, Heads of State, writers, artists and national and foreign intellectuals. In 1984 all the distinguishing features that maintain the refined and romantic atmosphere of Sintra were restored: the frescos on the ceiling, the marbling on the walls and the gilded mirror frames. In the dining room, the French palatial style of the eighteenth century has been maintained.

title: Hotel Palácio do Governador
short_description: The Palácio do Governador guarantees the best stay in a hotel that breathes history, refinement and luxury, in the historic centre of Belém.
full_description:
The Palácio do Governador guarantees the best stay in a hotel that breathes history, refinement and luxury, in the historic centre of Belém.
It’s not just another hotel in Lisbon. It is the Palácio do Governador (Governor’s Palace, in English), with 60 different rooms, near the Tagus river in the historic centre of Belém, one of the most beautiful and emblematic areas of the Portuguese capital.
Built on the site of the former house of the governor of the Tower of Belém, exquisitely restored, on top of roman ruins probably from the 1st to the 5th century, the Palácio do Governador is a palatial building with a reception that once was a chapel, a restaurant with original domes as ceilings and contemporary SPA that’s linked to the ancient thermal traditions of Mediterranean cultures.
Close to the Tagus and national historic monuments, Palácio do Governador is the natural choice for anyone who wants to be in Lisbon, near everything, from culture to leisure and far enough from the frenzy of the city centre.
If you are looking for a five star hotel in Belém, Lisbon with Spa and with an author’s cuisine restaurant, the Palácio do Governador guarantees the best stay in a hotel that breathes history, refinement and luxury, just 2 minutes walk to Belem Tower.

title: Setúbal Peninsula Regional Wine and Vine Commission
short_description: The Setúbal Peninsula Regional Wine and Vine Commission main objective is to guarantee the origin, quality and authenticity of the wines and promoting them.
full_description:
The Setúbal Peninsula Regional Wine and Vine Commission was formed as an inter professional association of private law in 1991.
Since then, it has had as its main objective to guarantee the origin, quality and authenticity of the wines, while promoting them along with the wine heritage of the region.
location: BlueBiz – Parque Empresarial da Península de Setúbal, Estrada Vale da Rosa, Edifício VII, R/C e Piso 1, Ala Nascente, 2910-845, Setúbal

title: Amoreiras Shopping Center
short_description: Amoreiras Shopping Center, a Lisbon’s icon. Placed in the center of the Portuguese capital became a meeting point for all, always demanding quality and attentive to the last fashion trends.
full_description:
Amoreiras Shopping Center is the flagship mall of Lisbon. Here you will find some of the most prestigious retailers both Portuguese and International. It is the ideal spot for those who seek serious retail therapy.
Ease of access, location in an upmarket and central area of the city makes this a perfect haven for shopping in the comfort of one of the most emblematic buildings in the entire city, recognised by all and proud winner of the Valmor and Municipal Prizes for architecture.
An extensive assembly of quality shops ensures that the Amoreiras Shopping Center is the perfect venue for those searching for that particular service, unusual object or special gift amongst a truly comprehensive choice of clothing, cosmetics, jewellery, communications and information services.
Non-resident EU visitors can also benefit from an immediate tax discount of up to 15% on purchases.In one of the towers, with shopping for access, is one of the main sights of the city.
Due to its location, the lookout provides visitors a unique 360 degree views over Lisbon.
See also:
Amoreiras 360º Panoramic View
location: Av. Eng. Duarte Pacheco, loja 2037, 1070-103, Lisboa

title: Atelier-Museum Júlio Pomar
short_description: Discover the life and work of one of Portugal’s greatest painters, an artist whose unique status within Portuguese art is well worth getting to know better.
full_description:
The Atelier-Museum Júlio Pomar is one place in Lisbon that lovers of art and architecture shouldn’t miss.
Created by Lisbon Council and the Fundação Júlio Pomar to preserve and divulge the work of this Portuguese modernist painter, it is a place of critical observation and freedom of thought with an eclectic collection of hundreds of artworks exploring a wide range of themes.
With paintings, sculptures, drawings, engravings, ceramics, collage and assemblages, on show is the career of an artist who created by absorbing and breaking rules and styles.
The building was designed by the architect Álvaro Siza Vieira in an austere and stripped down style that is discrete and inspiring.
Though it was never actually used as an Atelier/studio, this was the building’s initial function, thus maintaining the name of one of Lisbon’s most original cultural and museum spaces.

title: José Franco Museum Village
short_description: This miniature reconstruction of a village on the outskirts of Lisbon can be found on the road linking Mafra to Ericeira.
full_description: José France built a miniature replica of an outer Lisbon village at Sobreiro, on the road between Mafra and Ericeira. The miniature rural scenes and animated figures recreate the practices and customs of the region. Visitors can also travel back in time to an old-style schoolroom, a barber-dentist’s shop and Auntie Helena’s grocery store.

title: Campo Pequeno
short_description: Find out more about the history of a bullring that has been converted into a venue for cultural events where countless concerts and shows are staged.
full_description: The brick-built Campo Pequeno bullring was opened in 1892. Inspired by the bullring in Madrid and designed in a neo-Arabic revivalist style, the Praça de Touros was totally renovated in 2006. It’s history is integral to the development of bullfighting in Portugal, a history that the museum evokes. This tradition is worth discovering at the Campo Pequeno museum, which was responsible for developing the extraordinary Lusitano horse breed. The bullring is also a venue for markets, events, shows and concerts thanks to its flexibility. It also houses several galleries in a versatile and welcoming shopping centre. Come and discover a place where the traditional and the urban meet.
location: Centro de Lazer do Campo Pequeno, 1000-082, Lisboa

title: Museum of Lisbon - Casa dos Bicos
short_description: When visiting the Baixa, take the chance to pop into Casa dos Bicos to see the archaeological section of the Museu de Lisboa, with its remains of previous occupants of Lisbon.
full_description:
In one of the most original places you can visit in Lisbon – Casa dos Bicos – besides the remarkable history of a building inspired by the palaces of Bologna and the Fundação Saramago collection and exhibition you will find an archaeological section.
You’ll discover various remains of the various occupants of the city, including sections of the Late Roman and medieval walls.
Besides the everyday objects from the 16th to 18th century, you can also see the outline of several ancient tanks used at a fish processing factory. Discover the various forms that Lisbon has taken through the ages at this unique house.

title: Paula Rego House of Stories
short_description: This fascinating project in Cascais was set up by one of Portugal’s top artists.
full_description:
Paula Rego, who lives in London, is widely acknowledged to be one of Portugal’s leading artists. In 2006, she decided to build “her” House of Stories in Cascais.
The building was designed by the architect Eduardo Souto de Moura.
The collection includes a significant number of her painted and drawn works and some of those of her artist and art critic husband, Victor Willing, who died in 1988.
The Paula Rego House of Stories provides a first-class cultural service, through the rotating exhibition of its collection, its dynamically active education service and its wide-ranging complementary offer of cultural events.

Virtual tour

title: Sky Bar | Tivoli Liberdade
short_description: Visit one of the most impressive panoramic terraces in Lisbon. Climb up to the top floor of the Hotel Tivoli Avenida Liberdade Lisboa and enjoy the charms of the Sky Bar.
full_description:
There are many reasons to visit this luxury hotel in the city’s most prestigious avenue.
But it is the view from the amazing Sky Bar, open at the top of the building during the summer months that is responsible for attracting most locals and tourists.
The sophistication and stunning view take over, but while there remember to try one of the delicious tapas or signature cocktails.
With its different ambiances and three possible seating areas, the Sky Bar at the Tivoli  Avenida Liberdade hotel is definitely one of the most impressive panoramic terraces in Lisbon.

title: Onyria Marinha Boutique Hotel
short_description: Onyria Marinha Boutique Hotel is a boutique hotel with 68 deluxe rooms, 4 suites and 12 Deluxe Villas, all with balcony or terrace, overlooking nature....A heaven surrounded by nature and just a few steps from the sea!
full_description:
Onyria Marinha Boutique Hotel
Surrounded by nature and a few steps away from the sea, the Onyria Marinha Boutique Hotel, is a 5-star Boutique Hotel, located in Quinta da Marinha, Cascais.
Next to the golf course designed by Robert Trent Jones Sr., the Hotel has 68 deluxe rooms, 4 suites and 12 Deluxe Villas.
With an excellent service and facilities thought to detail to combine the best of leisure and business, Onyria Marinha Boutique Hotel offers facilities for meetings, meetings and conferences, as well as restaurants and bar, and spaces for dream events.

title: Inside Lisbon Tours
short_description: Walking tours and Day Trips from Lisbon to Sintra, Pena Palace, Óbidos, Fátima, and wine regions. Small-group and private tours for individuals and groups, with a maximum of 8 guests.
full_description:
Inside Lisbon Tours is dedicated to showing our clients the very best of Lisbon while creating unforgettable experiences.
We specialize in walking tours and full-day tours in and around the Lisbon region, designed for both individuals and groups.
We offer regular tours in English and Spanish, as well as private tours in English, Portuguese, Spanish, German, French, and Italian.
From classic highlights to fascinating experiences, you can choose the tour that suits you best, all led by passionate local guides committed to showing you the real Lisbon.
For those seeking something more adventurous, we also offer unique experiences such as jeep safaris, sailing tours, surfing, biking, Segway tours, and much more.
We are also experts in event management, particularly in the reception and coordination of groups throughout Portugal.
Across all our activities, quality, professionalism, and fun are always guaranteed.

title: SUD Lisboa Terrazza
short_description: Where Everything Happens - From the best food to the best events, allow yourself to be overwhelmed by the atmosphere and the perfect combination between location, style and service
full_description:
SUD Lisboa presents a side of the city that you still don’t know. Two venues of strong personality, united in perfect coexistence with the Tagus river and their will of creating amazing sceneries.
Here every view and every moment is unique. In this prime location you can find historical monuments, museums and cultural centers like the Jerónimos Monastery, Museu dos Coches, the Cultural Center of Belém, amongst others.
SUD LISBOA Terrazza offers the perfect moments with a great entertainment concept and a Mediterranean sharing menu perfect for every occasion, never forgetting a vast selection of wines and signature cocktails.
Unlike any other SUD LISBOA Hall is a reference for all events in Lisbon. A differentiating offer, a unique venue, the perfect location and great animation. Great both in size and state of the art technology, we can adapt to any type of event, decoration and entertainment. From private to social events here you'll create amazing memories.
Come and discover the place where everything happens.
location: Avenida Brasília - Pavilhão Poente, 1300-598, Belém | Lisboa

title: SAL Walking and Cultural Tours
short_description: SAL Sistemas de Ar Livre is a Tourist Entertainment Company and Tour Operator specialized in Walking Tours, River and Coastal Cruises, Environmental Education, Training, Motivation, Outdoor Entertainment and Event Organization.
full_description:
SAL Sistemas de Ar Livre is a Tourist Entertainment Company and Tour Operator, licensed by Turismo de Portugal IP, specialized in Walking Tours, River and Coastal Cruises, Environmental Education, Training, Motivation, Outdoor Entertainment and Event Organization.
Founded in April 1996, it maintains a permanent agenda of Walking Tours in Portugal, where, in addition to providing good walks in places of enormous beauty, it shows and explains all the values ​​of the natural and cultural heritage of the territories where the events take place.
For companies, institutions and private groups, SAL develops a wide range of Outdoor Animation, Motivation and Training events, in natural spaces or in places of enormous cultural interest. Examples of this are Team Building, Country Picnics, River Cruises, Rally Paper, Foot Paper, Traditional Games Tournament, Shooting Tournament, Treasure Hunt, Cultural and Heritage Tours, Nature Tours and Organization of Themed Parties.

title: Museum of the Lisbon Geographical Society
short_description: Be a 21st-century explorer and travel in time at this 19th-century society, with its museum full of objects from every country in Portugal’s former colonial empire.
full_description: The museum of the Lisbon Geographical Society is one of the best-kept secrets in Lisbon. It inherited its collection from what was once the “Colonial Museum” and assumed an ethnographic focus from early on. It grew through a campaign of acquisitions and the donations of collectors and travellers who accompanied the Portuguese outposts in the former empire. Discover ceramics, sculptures, furniture, paintings, fabrics, glass, tiles and even scientific instruments from the most disparate of sources: Angola, Guinea, Mozambique, São Tomé and Príncipe, Cape Verde, Timor and Macau. A unique exhibition, which brings together art and scientific/everyday objects and which shows the habits and depictions of distant lands. Be a 21st-century explorer and travel in time at this 19th-century society.
location: Rua das Portas de Santo Antão, 100, 1150-269, Lisboa

title: Eliseu dos Leitões - Duque de Palmela
short_description: We bring the best of Bairrada to the capital. Crispy skin, succulent meat and a sparkling wine to drink and cry over!

title: National Palace of Mafra
short_description: Built by order of King João V, consists of a Royal Palace, a basilica, a convent and a game reserve. Highlights include 2 carillons, 6 historic organs, a library and an 18th century infirmary.
full_description:
Virtual Tour
Church
|
Ground floor
|
1st floor
The National Palace of Mafra was proclaimed a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 2019.
The Royal Palace occupies the noble floor of the Mafra building and the 2 turrets.
The north turret was occupied by the king and the south turret by the queen, and both turrets are linked by a 232-metre gallery – making it Europe’s largest palatial corridor. This building, of about 40,000 m2, housed a Franciscan convent (300 fryers) – and features Campo Santo, the Infirmary, the Elliptical Hall (capitular), the Literary Acts Hall, the Stairway and the Dining Hall.
It boasts a large library thought to be one of the most beautiful in the world. The Royal Building has two carillons with 98 bells – the largest of its time. The Basilica’s 6 organs are pieces of unique heritage worldwide.
The Royal Convent and Palace of Mafra is the most important baroque monument in Portugal. The building covers an area of almost four hectares (37.790 m2), including 1.200 rooms, more than 4.700 doors and windows, 156 stairways and 29 inner yards and courtyards.
Such magnificence was only possible due to the Brazilian gold that poured into the country, allowing the King to carry out his patronage of the arts and the strengthening of royal authority.

title: Cervejaria Trindade
short_description: Great Portuguese cuisine served in a 13th century convent.
full_description: Great Portuguese cuisine served in a 13th century convent decorated with magnificente tile panels.

title: Monsanto Cycle Path
short_description: Discover a side of Lisbon that few know: the natural side. This leafy park of 900 hectares is Lisbon’s lung, giving a green hue to its white and blue.
full_description: The Monsanto cycle path is the best place to pedal in Lisbon if you’re looking for a natural environment. This forestry park has a network of paths and routes covering over 42 km for all types of bicycle, with stretches that are more suited to mountain biking. The whole green area has places for resting, hydrating and recharging your batteries to get ready for the next stage. With excellent access to the Green Corridor, designed by the famous landscape architect Gonçalo Ribeiro Telles, the centre of Lisbon is easily linked to the Monsanto cycle paths without needing to dismount. Come and explore nature and enjoy the more adventurous side of the city on these paths made for pedalling.

title: Entidade Regional de Turismo da Região de Lisboa (ERT-RL)
short_description: Tem como missão a valorização e desenvolvimento das potencialidades turísticas da Área Regional de Turismo de Lisboa.
full_description: A Entidade Regional de Turismo da Região de Lisboa, também designada sob a forma abreviada por ERT-RL, foi criada pela Lei n.º 33/2013, de 16 de maio, diploma legal que lhe conferiu a missão de valorização e desenvolvimento das potencialidades turísticas da Área Regional de Turismo de Lisboa, que é coincidente com a NUTII Lisboa, integrando a Área Metropolitana de Lisboa e a Península de Setúbal. Participam, assim, na ERT-RL, para além do Estado, através da Secretaria de Estado do Turismo, os 18 municípios da NUTII Lisboa: Alcochete, Almada, Amadora, Barreiro, Cascais, Lisboa, Loures, Mafra, Moita, Montijo, Odivelas, Oeiras, Palmela, Seixal, Sesimbra, Setúbal, Sintra e Vila Franca de Xira. Juntam-se às entidades públicas 18 entes privados, com interesse no desenvolvimento e valorização turística da região. A ERT-RL tem sede em Lisboa e uma delegação em Setúbal.

title: Fronteira Palace
short_description: Discover one of the great Lisbon palaces and its significant art collection spread magically around its halls and gardens. It’s worth getting off the regular tourist routes.
full_description:
One of Lisbon’s great palaces lies in a calm area away from the centre of the city.
The Palácio de Fronteira, in the Benfica area, is near one of the entrances to the city’s lungs – Monsanto – and deserves a visit.
You’ll love its baroque gardens full of fountains, lakes and statues; and its unique and well-preserved set of rocaille decorations and seventeenth century azulejo tiles.
The palace itself, built in the Mannerist style with Baroque decoration, perfectly illustrates the 17th-century palatial style.
In the Sala dos Painéis Holandeses (Dutch Panel Room), gallery of Liberal Arts and Sala das Batalhas (Battle Room) you can admire impressive artworks and learn more about the marquises of Fronteira, who played an influential role in the history of Portugal.
location: Largo de São Domingos de Benfica, 1, 1500-554, Lisboa

title: Yconic Travel Studio
short_description: Our focus is to provide authentic experiences and human interactions and we are passionate about Portugal and we love to showcase the best that our country has to offer.
full_description:
Yconic Travel Studio was founded by four partners: Ricardo, Miguel, Joana and Claudia. Long-time friends with several years of experience in the tourism and hospitality industry, they decided to open Yconic Travel Studio, a boutique DMC focused on the high-end segment, with the philosophy that luxury goes far beyond the sense of exclusivity.
Our focus is to provide authentic experiences and human interactions.
We are passionate about Portugal and we love to showcase the best our country has to offer. Despite being a small country, we are one of the few that offer so much cultural diversity in such a small geographical area. We value local culture, respect our people and local suppliers because they work the hardest to make Portugal a unique country in every way.
Our clients are equally unique, each with their own habits, tastes, desires, and passions. That's why we have our travel studio, where we design tailor-made itineraries according to each profile, to surprise them throughout their journey. As a boutique DMC, we provide a close and highly personalized service. We treat our clients individually with the aim of developing the best project for each of them.
Start planning your Yconic Travel Experience
location: Rua Carlos Calisto, Torres do Restelo 2, 1400-195 , Lisboa

title: Castro Rua Áurea
short_description: Atelier de Pastéis de Nata Castro is dedicated to making one of the greatest examples of Portuguese sweets, in a tribute to centuries of history and tradition.
full_description:
With three shops open in Portugal, two in Lisbon and one in Porto, Castro is opening the doors to a journey into the past, allowing the Portuguese to experience the flavour of their history and those who visit us to find in this recipe the true Portuguese identity, a delicious memory to keep from their trip.
Castro's Pastel de Nata preserves the authenticity of the recipe through handmade confectionery and the choice of high-quality ingredients. The perfect balance between the light, crunchy pastry and the slightly sweet filling with citrus notes is the result of hours of perfecting the recipe in search of the best pastel de nata.
More than just faithful representatives of Portuguese Conventual Sweets, Castro's Pastéis de Nata are true masterpieces ready to be enjoyed.
As tradition dictates, it should be sprinkled with cinnamon and icing sugar in moderate proportions and accompanied by coffee, tea, a glass of port or even a ginjinha.
Translated with DeepL.com (free version)

title: Estação Sul e Sueste / Doca da Marinha
short_description: With the rehabilitation of Sul e Sueste station, Lisboa thus gains new cultural and leisure spaces and new possibilities for connections between both banks of the Tejo.
full_description:
Eight boat companies have at your disposal different river tours to discover the Tejo and its margins from 9.00am to 8.00pm.
Integrated into the Sul e Sueste is the Centro Tejo, a multimedia presentation for interpretation, appreciation, and environmental awareness on both banks of the river where you can discover different views of Lisboa and know more about Tejo and is experience.
Sul e Sueste also has available a Tourist Office and a Lisboa Shop, open from 10am to 7pm, where visitors can get all information they need and buy a different souvenir.
Two cafes will be part of the Sul e Sueste station, one including an exterior terrace and the other only terrace. Both serving their guests with the best Lisboa flavors and overlooking the Tejo estuary on a unique ambiance.
The intervention at the Doca da Marinha gave rise to a wooded area, where the view over the city and the river invites passersby to stroll, in a space that houses an artistic installation by Julião Sarmento.
This area now becomes a space for catering and entertainment activities, where pedestrian and cycling routes are also guided.
Twenty-three traditional boats of different types, like “Catraios”, “Varinos” or “Muletas”, among other, will be able to take you along the Tejo for a unique journey. Open from 9am to 8pm.

title: Tivoli Avenida Liberdade Lisboa Hotel
short_description: Discover Tivoli Avenida Liberdade: The Grande Dame of hotels in central Lisbon, a landmark of timeless hospitality and distinctive service since 1933.
full_description:
Discover Lisbon's best kept secrets with our dedicated team, shop till you drop in Avenida da Liberdade and Chiado's luxury stores, or simply unwind by the oasis of the hotel's unique swimming pool amidst the fronds of its semi-tropical garden.
The wealth and variety of rooms and views at our luxury 5-star hotel in Lisbon, from Deluxe Rooms to the stunning Presidential Suite, make each stay an unforgettable experience.
Wining and dining are paramount in European tradition. Join in the thriving and bustling ambience of the Cervejaria Liberdade and indulge yourself with the best fish and seafood in Lisbon, sourced from the over 800 km of Portuguese coastline.
Relish breathtaking city views, contemporary flavours and exquisite cocktails at the trendiest spot in Lisbon: SEEN Lisboa.
For more relaxed moments, step into the luxurious world of Anantara Spa and immerse in exceptional pampering.
For unique, seamless meetings and conferences, step across the courtyard to our 19th century palatial Conference and Events Centre.

title: National Ancient Art Museum
short_description: It houses the most relevant public collection, from the 12th to the 19th century. Painting, sculpture, silver, gold and jewellery, decorative arts – Portuguese, European, African and Oriental.
full_description:
[
Closed for renovations from 29th September
]
The main museum in Portugal houses the most relevant public collection, from the 12th to the 19th century.
Painting, sculpture, silver, gold and jewellery, decorative arts – Portuguese, European, African and Oriental –, including the largest number of works classified as “national treasures”.
Among them, The Panels of Saint Vicent, by Nuno Gonçalves, and the Belém Monstrance, symbols of 15th and 16th centuries Portuguese art, and notable paintings by Bosch, Memling, Dürer, Raphael or Piero della Francesca.
The museum has a magnificent garden, with restaurant, over the River Tagus.

title: Church of Our Lady of the Purification
short_description: This Gothic, Renaissance and Mannerist church was built in the second half of the 16th Century.
full_description: This town centre church was built in the second half of the 16th Century. The architecture is a blend of Gothic, Renaissance and Mannerist. The chequered tiles and decoratively painted arches and domes of the interior are largely Mannerist. The altar carvings are Portuguese-style baroque and the paintings in the chancel are probably 17th Century. The tile-lined chancel has a carved Baroque reredos with figures of mermaids and sculptures of the evangelists. In a small outbuilding to the right of the entrance, there is a 1.7 m Gothic pediment carved with the Holy Trinity. This piece belonged to the Chapel of the Holy Spirit.
location: Largo do Espírito Santo, 2670-655, Bucelas | Loures

title: Vanellus
short_description: Tours | At Vanellus we go further, looking for places, stories, landscapes, characters and things that truly constitute our identity and our culture.
full_description:
At Vanellus we believe that the natural and cultural heritage has multiple strands and, in that sense, our proposals are true discovery experiences.
We flee from the obvious and from what is widely known and publicized. We go further, looking for places, stories, landscapes, characters and things that truly constitute our identity and our culture, shaped over the centuries by a multitude of diverse people who crossed our territory, leaving their marks and influences.
Our Team
At Vanellus we are a small multifaceted team, our origins, backgrounds and interests are varied, but we have been working as a team for a long time. We have already travelled almost everywhere in Portugal and established lasting local partnerships with people, companies and institutions. What moves us is the passion of discover and share of our heritage; we see our customers as partners in this journey.

title: JNcQUOI
short_description: The perfect hangout place for both day and night.
full_description:
Located on the first floor of the Tivoli theatre building and emboldened by its high ceilings and its hundred-year-old history, the JNcQUOI Restaurant has a capacity of 90 seats with views over the Avenida da Liberdade.
The open kitchen at the very centre of the dining room is where the finishing touches of each course are applied, courtesy of Chef António Bóia: Portuguese and international staples with the Jncquoi refinement and a special attention to the quality of ingredients, favouring seasonal and organic produce — some of each available for the first time in the Portuguese market — and a wine list complete with 500-plus references providing the best wine experience in the country.
The intermediate floor of the Tivoli Theatre building is home to the JNcQUOI Delibar. A counter-bar with 42 seats is surrounded by a wine and gourmet grocery shop with the finest selection of the best national and international flavours: cheese, charcuterie and delicatessen.
In the same floor, you will find an acclimatised room with a selection of rare wines and a wine-tasting table.
The perfect hangout place for both day and night, the unceremonious counter of the Delibar is the perfect setting for light — and not so light — meals as well as relaxing moments with a comprehensive and ever surprising drink and cocktail list.
In this floor you will also find two international brand corners: one by publisher Assouline featuring the best books on art, design, photography, fashion and travel; a broad selection of true cultural and leisure gems; the other by the renowned Parisian patisserie Ladurée.

title: Local by Clube Viajar
short_description: Local embodies the essence of proximity, authenticity, and deep-rooted knowledge. Our name reflects our unwavering commitment to delivering immersive experiences that reveal the heart and soul of Portugal.
full_description:
As part of the Clube Viajar Travel Group, founded in 1995 and a proud member of Lufthansa City Centre since 2020, we bring over 30 years of expertise to ensure your events and journeys in Portugal are seamless and unforgettable.
At Local, we transcend typical DMC services by offering insider knowledge and exclusive access to Portugal's unique traditions, exceptional products, and hidden gems.
Every journey and event is meticulously crafted with integrity and excellence, connecting you to the people, flavors, and culture of Portugal in a way that is both innovative and authentically Portuguese.
Our comprehensive event management services go across Portugal, including Porto, Lisbon, Madeira and the Azores. Whether you're organizing corporate meetings, incentive programs, or exclusive leisure experiences, each program is meticulously crafted to meet your objectives, seamlessly blending Portuguese culture with our local expertise in every detail.
From designing custom itineraries to overseeing on-site execution, we handle every aspect with precision and care.

full_description:
We are a team of four Portuguese artists who own and operate the Living Lounge, named the "world's best boutique Hostel" in The Times.
With help from friends and design specialists, we have combined our unique talents to create the Living Lounge, a new and beautiful home for hostel art and traveling life.
Each bedroom has been individually designed to reflect aspects of Lisbon's rich culture. The common rooms are spacious, stylised and incorporate every facility the traveler may need.
It is our mission to provide our guests with supreme comfort, security and style during their stay. We look forward to providing you with an unforgettable time in Lisbon.

full_description:
Located in the Saldanha area, just 200 meters from the metro station and a 15-minute metro ride from Rossio, the 4-star Hotel Zenit Lisboa is an excellent starting point to explore the city, whether you visit for leisure or business.
Built on the foundation of a historic building, the façade of which has been preserved, Hotel Zenit Lisboa offers 86 rooms of various types (single, double, triple, junior suite, and suite), parking facilities, a gym, meeting rooms, a restaurant, and a bar.

title: Barco Varino Liberdade
short_description: A boat trip on the typical boat Liberdade is an unique experience. A single visit affords nature, river and cultural tourism. The natural beauty only the Tagus can offer will remain in your memory.
full_description:
BOAT TRIPS ON THE TAGUS RIVER - TIPYCAL BOAT LIBERDADE A boat trip on the Tagus River aboard the typical boat Liberdade is an unique experience.
Its historical value makes it part of the Vila Franca de Xira Municipal Museum, and a single visit affords nature, river and cultural tourism. Whether downstream or upstream, the natural beauty only the Tagus can offer will remain forever etched in your memory.
The Tagus Estuary Nature Reserve, the small river islands or are just a phone call away.
From April to November, welcome aboard! Capacity: 40 places For booking and tickets: Municipal Tourist.

title: Pena National Palace
short_description: The Pena Palace and Park, in the Sintra mountains, are the product of the creative genius of King Fernando II and the epitome of 19th Century Romanticism in Portugal.
full_description:
The Pena Palace and Park, in the Sintra mountains, are the product of the creative genius of King Fernando II and the epitome of 19th Century Romanticism in Portugal.
Architecturally speaking, they are a mix of Manueline and Moorish architecture.
The palace was built so that it could be seen from any point in the park, forest or luxuriant gardens, with their five hundred plus species of trees from the four corners of the world.

title: Rock in Rio
short_description: Rock in Rio was created to give voice to a generation and promote unique and innovative experiences.
full_description:
In 1985, the event was responsible for putting Brazil on the international concert route. Breaking attendance records at every edition and generating positive impacts in the countries where it is held, it has established itself as the largest music and entertainment festival in the world. Aware of the disseminating power of the brand, today Rock in Rio strives to be an event with the purpose of building a better world for happier, more confident and empathetic people on a healthier planet.
The internationalisation of the brand began in Portugal, Lisboa, in 2004, where the event is still held today, followed by Spain (Madrid) and the United States (Las Vegas).
At Rock in Rio, the numbers keep growing. Millions of visitors have passed through the Cities of Rock and the festival has conquered the world and became a veritable playground of experiences, but far beyond that, it has grown and expanded its operations, always looking to the future.
By adopting and encouraging practices that support the collective, Rock in Rio strives to build a better world and joins forces with companies that have the same vision and guidelines. In 2013, it was recognised for its achievement by receiving the ISO 20121 - Sustainable Events certification. Since its first edition, it has generated millions of direct and indirect jobs and invested, together with its partners, in different projects, covering topics such as sustainability, education, music, forests, among others.
location: Edidicio LACS - Cais da Rocha do Conde D'Óbidos, 1350-352, Lisboa

title: Lisbon Marriott Hotel

full_description:
Find your home away from home in the heart of Lisbon. The Lisbon Marriott Hotel welcomes business and leisure guests with luxury accommodation and a prime city location near Lisbon Airport.
Enjoy splendid views of the hotel garden and the city from the balcony in your stylish room or suite, or stretch out on your luxury bedding.
High-speed Internet access keeps you productive, and our outdoor terraces help you to relax.
Sample flavorful cuisine from Portugal at Citrus Restaurant, or order room service and dine in your luxury accommodation.
Those in Lisbon for work will appreciate our 21,000 square feet of elegant, versatile meeting space, as well as an expert planning and catering staff to attend to every detail.
And with city attractions including the Lisbon Zoo just a short distance away, you'll have plenty to discover during your visit.
Experience the future of travel at the Lisbon Marriott Hotel.

title: Pestana Cidadela Cascais, Pousada & Art District
short_description: Situated in Cascais, at Fortaleza da Cidadela (Citadel's Fortress), is Europe's first hotel that welcomes and promotes an Art District on the inside. With an amazing location overlooking the bay of Cascais
full_description:
This luxury hotel in Cascais, located at the Citadel Fortress that defended the entrance of the Tagus River, is a unique project in Portugal and in Europe. Much more than a hotel, it is a real Art District where you can find a Project Room and 6 Ateliers or Studios, which house resident artists and completely distinct and original projects and artworks. In addition, it houses two completely disparate museums: the World Press Cartoon and the Museum of the Presidency. Formality side by side with irreverence in an inspiring and absolutely memorable symbiosis.
But do not think that the appeal of the Cascais Citadel is recent: after all, there must be a reason for this place to have been elected as a royal holiday destination since the reign of King D. Luís I. Its amazing location overlooking the bay of Cascais, sheltered from the wind by the imposing defensive walls and protected from prying eyes by its upper position, is irresistible.
Here, the past is combined with the most innovative and original design, as well as with the most stunning art installations and performances. When you enter, you are welcomed by the 600 swallows crafted by the irreverent Rafael Bordallo Pinheiro, announcing the importance of the Portuguese culture in this area and, simultaneously, the arrival of warm weather and happy days. As you wander along the corridors, you can come across both an installation by Bruno Pereira, the Hot L, evoking libido and fantasies commonly associated with these spaces; or a replica of the famous Bocca Sofa, originally designed by Salvador Dalí, the great surrealist master, inspired by the lips of the stunning Mae West. This piece, a true icon of modern design, invites you to spend a voluptuous hour reading the newspaper or stealing the show on your instagram feed.
The minimalist style that characterizes the outdoor spaces is replicated in the rooms, which give you all the light and space of experience, punctuated with unforgettable pieces of art. Incidentally, the Signature Rooms are an unique experience that gives a whole other dimension to your stay. This minimalism is essential to serve as a counterweight to the strength of the outside. The stunning beauty of the sea of Cascais, the deep blue that punctuates the view of the walls and the brightness of the Sun that gives life even to the most dormant stone in the terrace, makes you want to take advantage of the shadows, rest in the nooks and seek the serenity of the yards sheltered by the Sun. At the top of the wall, by the sophisticated and elegant restaurant Maris Stella, you'll find the fabulous indoor pool, whose doors and ceiling open to an even more symbiotic experience with the area. And if you go the beautiful Parapet Walk, you will find the Armory Garden and the Art Pool, the outdoor pool with its original installation with its lawn deck and its inviting sun beds. Under the scorching sun in the Solarium or by the shadow of history, your days will feel the appeal of the calmness and deep serenity of the blue.
And since you seek serenity, be sure to visit the Magic Spa, which has quiet massage and treatment rooms, and where you can choose to order a light lunch fresh from the sea to extend the feeling of lightness that follows any of your incursions into this space. Stroll, explore, lose yourself and find yourself again. Eat a snack at Taberna da Praça or a modern, authorial dish at Maris Stella. Visit Dejá Lu, the first solidarity bookstore in the country; or visit one of the ateliers of the resident artists. The truth is that, two steps away from the marina and from the cultural center of Cascais, and a five-minute walk from the town center, there is plenty to do outside the walls; but the truly unique experience that you can feel during your stay is lived within the fortress. And you can be sure that every minute of it will be memorable.

title: Cael - Consórcio de Automóveis Excelsior
short_description: Constituída em finais de 1958, a CAEL iniciou a sua atividade de aluguer de automóveis sem condutor no início de 1959, no seguimento da publicação do diploma que criou esta indústria.
full_description:
Em 1975, a CAEL foi pioneira no aluguer de automóveis a empresas, tendo mantido uma frota de 500 viaturas em aluguer destinadas apenas a esta vertente e conquistando uma posição prestigiada graças à qualidade do serviço prestado e ao progressivo alargamento do leque de clientes.
No final da década de 80, seguindo a tradição vanguardista que a caracteriza, a CAEL voltou a inovar com a criação de uma nova área de negócio, o aluguer de viaturas comerciais.
Presentemente, a CAEL dispõe de uma vasta frota direcionada para as mais diversas necessidades do mercado empresarial, assim como para o turismo mais exigente.
O know-how adquirido, a organização desenvolvida e o aperfeiçoamento de métodos, garantem um futuro próspero aos seus colaboradores, parceiros e, sobretudo, aos clientes, razão principal da existência da CAEL.

title: Casa-Museu Amália Rodrigues
short_description: Discover the house and life of the greatest fado singer in history, as well as some of the artworks she was presented with during her career.
full_description:
To get to know “how strange life can be”, visit the impressive Casa-Museu Amália Rodrigues.
This yellow house in Rua de São Bento is where the greatest fado icon lived for almost half a century.
Discovering and reliving the atmosphere that surrounded the world famous “Voice of Portugal”, gives you a better idea of who she was and what motivated this independent, determined and unique woman.
She was considered one of the greatest singers of the 20th century and had some of the best contemporary Portuguese poets writing for her.
With over 170 albums and 30 million records sold, Amália is buried at the National Pantheon – a tribute to her unmistakable importance.
Discover her house and the work of her foundation, on a unique and soulful journey through the history of 20th-century Portugal.

title: Noori Sushi
short_description: The best way to eat Sushi
full_description:
Noori started in 2009 with a brand new concept never seen in Portugal. Temakerias were famous in Brazil, but in Portugal eating Sushi in a cone was a new! We started with one store in Chiado and nowadays we count 9 stores in Portugal and 2 in Madrid.
Our Sushi is fresh, made in the moment in front of the client, fast, high quality and with an affordable price.
This is why we consider our brand as “The best way to eat sushi”.
What are we most proud of? People refer to Temakis as Nooris ;)
location: Armazéns do Chiado, Rua do Crucifixo, 87, 1100-100, Lisboa

title: Belas Clube de Campo
short_description: Belas is a Championship Golf Course. The layout is extraordinary, with several fairways and greens set in deep valleys, and it affords unforgettable panoramic scenery to golfers and visitors.
full_description:
Belas is a Championship Golf Course. The layout is extraordinary, with several fairways and greens set in deep valleys, and it affords unforgettable panoramic scenery to golfers and visitors.
With its excellent variety of holes, the course is a pleasure to play, whatever one’s handicap level.
It demands both skill and tactical play, and is acknowledged both locally and internationally as one of Portugal’s best layouts.
It has in operation an Environmental Management System (EMS) that enables its staff to monitor all matters relating to the environment.
The EMS holds an ISO 14001 certification, and is also certified to the European “Committed to Green” Programme.

title: Shaaré Tikvah Synagogue
short_description: If you want to learn more about Jewish culture and its impact on Lisbon, take a tour organised by the Jewish Community of Lisbon and visit the old Jewish quarters.
full_description: If you want to visit the Lisbon synagogue in the São Mamede quarter, you’ll need its exact address. Opened in 1904, the temple is indistinguishable from the street, built at a time when it was forbidden for any faith or religion other than the Catholic Church to be openly visible. Its construction combines Roman, Byzantine and Romantic elements and is austere in style, with the temple built facing in the direction of Jerusalem. The columns supporting the galleries highlight the bimah and desk, which identifies the place of celebration. See also the Ark, where the Torah scrolls are kept. If you want to learn more about Jewish culture and its impact on Lisbon, take a tour organised by the Jewish Community of Lisbon which visit the old Jewish quarters and the surviving traces of the old Alfama Synagogue. It’s well worth learning about the presence and influence of this community in Lisbon.

title: Palácio Biester
short_description: Biester Palace is located in the heart of Sintra, being a part of the lavish scenic landscape which enabled the village to become the famous world capital of Romanticism.
full_description:
Biester Palace is located in the heart of Sintra, being a part of the lavish scenic landscape which enabled the village to become the famous world capital of Romanticism.
Erected in the last decades of the 19th century, the building was designed by virtuous architect José Luiz Monteiro, and decorated by the best artists of the period, namely Luigi Manini and Leandro de Souza Braga, amongst others.
The wealth of stylistic influences it exhibits results in a whole of great historical and artistic importance, perfectly merging with the Romantic spirit of the surrounding landscape, whilst simultaneously asserting as a great expression of late century modernity, highlighted by  a leading functional approach, and by the audacity of its decorative programme.
The Palace is surrounded by the majestic Biester Park, a true Eden of rare and exuberant arboreal species, brilliantly conceived by French landscapist François Nogré, in which the dewy verdant areas and watercourses flourish, moreover favoured by the existence of two magnificent viewpoints from where it is possible to sight the Moors Castle, or enjoy a stunning view all the way through to the sea.
But Biester universe is much more than the eyes can reach, with a history full of secrets, and a mysterious, captivating spiritual dimension. All the events conducting to its edification, especially of the Chapel and the Library, are enclosed in intricate and profound mysticism, where art and religion meet in most peculiar and unexpected ways, constructing a path with themes as intriguing as the Knights Templar, Rome and Christendom, or even through esoteric connections to the spheres of Occultism.
More than an historical monument, Biester Palace is a mysterious adventure in a fascinating and little explored cultural labyrinth – let us discover it.

title: Pavilhão Carlos Lopes
short_description: The Pavilion Carlos Lopes was the Pavilion of Portuguese Industries in Brazil, in 1922. Located in the downtown of Lisbon it is now a unique venue for all events.
full_description:
The Pavilion Carlos Lopes was designed to be the Pavilion of Portuguese Industries in the 1922 during the International Exhibition of Rio de Janeiro, being built mostly with elements transported from Portugal to Brazil.
After the exhibition it was dismantled and transferred to Portugal, being implanted in Lisbon since 1932 in the Parque Eduardo VII, having been the site of the Great Exhibition of Portuguese Industry.
It was used during decades for performing sporting, cultural, artistic and political events.
With extensive refurbishment in 2016, the venue has an exceptional location in the downtown area and it is a unique venue in Lisbon with different rooms for all type of events: meetings, conferences, lunch and dinner, fairs and exhibitions, product presentations, etc.
location: Parque Eduardo VII - Avenida Sidónio Pais, 1050, Lisboa

We decided to create touristic tour programs which enable us to share some of this knowledge and, at the same time, allow a pleasant visit in a unique space with wine cellars, presses and destilary, perfect to accommodate different type of events.
It is the Pereira da Fonseca Family whom invites and welcomes you into Quinta do Sanguinhal.
Visits and wine tastings intend to show you a 100 years history within the context of Portuguese wine.
The goal is to stimulate the spirit of discovery and experimentation, providing a unique experience.
You may also visit:
Quinta das Cerejeiras |
Wine Shop - Museum - Wine Tourism

title: Restaurant Gambrinus
short_description: Located in the heart of Lisbon, Gambrinus is a reference point in Lisbon’s Cuisine with over 75 years of history, frequented by national and international personalities.
full_description:
SINCE 1936
The Restaurant has maintained the comfortable atmosphere of the rooms, high quality products and service of excellence, since the remodeling done in 1964 by the architect Maurício de Vasconcelos. The same artist was also responsible for the decoration.
Table Tradition
At the table you can enjoy specialties such as Partridge Pie, Turbot in Court- Bouillon, the Eisbein with Choucrute, the Fish Stew or the famous Crepes Suzette. There are also other options within the traditional Portuguese cuisine.
AT THE COUNTER
At the counter there is a relaxed ambience, ideal for a lighter meal, with the same quality service and a rich cuisine that meets the customer’s wishes.
Out of Time
In the Downtown of Lisbon and near Avenida da Liberdade, Gambrinus provides a social ambience even between meals, in extended dinners or at the end of the night.

title: Sé de Lisboa | Lisbon Cathedral
short_description: With more than 800 years of history, this is the most important and oldest church in Lisbon. It is worth discovering every corner of this building of undeniable historical, architectural, religious and spiritual value.
full_description:
Visiting the Lisbon Cathedral means traveling through 800 years of history.
From the high choir to the chancel, from the central nave to the ambulatory with its radiant chapels, passing through the Bartolomeu Joanes Chapel and the Patriarch's dressing room, without forgetting the Treasury Museum and the balcony with its magnificent view over the city and the river, it is worth discovering every corner of this building of undeniable historical, architectural, religious and spiritual value.
In this church, Saint Anthony was baptized, studied  and joined the choir.
One of the signs of his presence the Cathedral can be found on the wall of the staircase that leads to the high choir: the famous cross of Santo António.
Legend has it that, when he was a child and studying at the school of this Cathedral, Saint Anthony was tempted by the devil in this place and repelled him by drawing the sign of the cross that was engraved here.
This may have been the first of the many miracles that God performed through the saint born in Lisbon in 1190.
Also in the Lisbon Cathedral can be venerated the reliquary coffer containing the relics of the martyr São Vicente, patron saint of Lisbon.
The Patriarchal Monstrance, one of the most splendid monstrance’s in the world, and the most remarkable liturgical implement made in Portugal in the 18th century, can be seen in the Museum of the Treasury.
*
Closed on Sundays and Holy Days

title: Príncipe Real
short_description: Príncipe Real is filled with trendy restaurants, grand mansions, tiny antique shops, Moorish kiosks, and miniature gardens. This neighborhood is located between one of the best viewing platforms in the city, the Miradouro São Pedro de Alcântara, and the charming neighborhood of São Bento.

title: Convent Square Lisbon Hotel, Vignette Collection
short_description: The Convent Square Hotel Vignette Collection is located among the most historic and emblematic Lisbon neighborhoods, in a privileged position in the heart of Lisbon, next to Praça do Rossio and just a few steps from Baixa Pombalina.
full_description:
RETURN HERITAGE AND HISTORY TO LISBON
Born from the rehabilitation of the Convento de S. Domingos, a historic building, commissioned by King D. Sancho II and whose origins date back to 1242, this 121-room hotel celebrates Lisbon's historical and cultural heritage, inviting its guests to discover the mysteries of the city through a unique concept inspired by the influence of the Dominican Order.
Built around its majestic cloister, the Convent Square Hotel Vignette Collection returns this former Dominican Convent to the city, focusing on the reconstruction and preservation of the site's authenticity, focused on enhancing Lisbon's historical and cultural heritage.
PRIVILEGED LOCATION
Located in Praça do Rossio, a premium area in the center of Lisbon, favored by fantastic rehabilitation projects, the Convent Square Hotel Vignette Collection is the starting point for exploring Lisbon. In just a few steps, guests find themselves in the heart of Baixa Pombalina, discovering the famous Santa Justa Elevator – designed by one of Gustave Eiffel's disciples –, Praça do Comércio or the Tagus River.
Lisbon's cultural life is literally on our doorstep, with Teatro D. Maria just a few meters from the hotel, while crossing Rossio is all it takes to find yourself at Rossio Train Station. Strolling along Avenida da Liberdade and enjoying the most exclusive luxury stores doesn't take more than a minute and Lisbon's most trendy and sought-after neighborhoods are just a hill away: Príncipe Real, Bairro Alto, Chiado.

The hotel has 75 rooms, of which eight are suites, and it’s complemented by a restaurant, bar, ice cream parlor, massage and jacuzzi space, gym, business centre and a museological área.
More than just provide a stay, it constantly seeks to enhance the guest experience to make it unforgettable.

Our client portfolio includes highly prestigious groups with real estate operations in the Algarve, Lisbon and Porto, along with many other real estate developers and hotel chains, public entities, financial institutions, investment funds, liberal professionals, and both Portuguese and foreign individual, corporate and institutional buyers.

title: João de Deus Museum
short_description: A museum devoted to this 19th-century poet and educationalist which tells the story of this advocate of reading and education, and that of his son too.
full_description: At a time when illiteracy in Portugal had reached alarming levels, one man proposed setting up a system of education to combat it. This man was João de Deus and here you can learn about his life. A museum devoted to this 19th-century poet and educationalist which tells the story of this advocate of reading and education, and that of his son too, João de Deus Ramos. The museum collection consists of busts, paintings, drawings and even a historical library. You will also find a series of methods for teaching the first steps of reading and arithmetic. Between an ice cream and a stroll in the Jardim da Estrela, get to know a crucial part of the history of education in Portugal.

title: Monsanto Forest Park
short_description: See the various attractions and activities in Monsanto, which stretch from bird-watching to remote-controlled cars, just a few minutes from the city centre.
full_description: Discover “Lisbon’s lungs”, a huge area of forest next to the city which replenishes its oxygen and contributes to the health of the many inhabitants who play sport there – or take their children there at the weekend. This unique getaway in the Monsanto hills – a holy place in ancient times – is a kind of wild but controlled wood, dotted with eucalyptus, oak and pine trees. The 900 hectares of greenery is criss-crossed by cycle paths, endowed with viewing points to admire the Tagus estuary, and inhabited by a large variety of sheltering wildlife: squirrels, rabbits, owls, bats and others. See the various attractions and activities in Monsanto, which stretch from bird-watching to remote-controlled cars, just minutes from the city centre.

title: Graça Urban Handicrafts Fair
short_description: Climb up to Graça, with the help of the no. 28, for the Feira de Artesanato Urbano da Graça every last weekend of the month, the extra push you need to face the slopes.
full_description: It’s impossible to visit Lisbon and not go to Graça. The no. 28 tram helps, the view is the reward and, every last weekend of the month, the Feira de Artesanato Urbano da Graça gives the extra push you need to face the ups and downs of this typical part of Lisbon. You’ll find jewellery, fashion, ceramics, photos, illustrations and lots of other artistic objects which share an urban quality rather than a traditional one. Largo da Graça fills up with stalls full of original, inventive and different products that can be excellent souvenirs to remember Portugal by.

title: Coyo Taco Príncipe Real
short_description: Coyo Taco brings the most authentic Mexican vibes and flavours. These are the ideal spots for margaritas, burritos and tacos in Lisbon. And fiesta too, of course!
full_description:
There’s always a reason to celebrate! So hit the road and follow the blue light.
The wait for the most traditional tacos in Lisbon is over. The fever for Mexican food has arrived in town and is in full fury in Príncipe Real. Everyone has been caught up in it!
An electric blue door – the colour that characterises the restaurant – is the gateway into the most Mexican spot in the neighbourhood.
Coyo Taco is all about street culture. Good food, good music and a good bar. Burritos, margaritas, tequila and fiesta!

title: Evolutee Hotel
short_description: Evolutee Hotel, the 5-star boutique hotel at Royal Óbidos Spa & Golf Resort, offers a unique and unforgettable experience.
full_description:
Evolutee Hotel at Royal Óbidos Spa & Golf Resort offers a unique and unforgettable experience.
It is the perfect setting for a romantic break or for a relaxed family holiday and for those who seek discreet but detail oriented service.
The interiors of the amenities and 39 rooms are designed by yoo Studio, the design team at yoo, the brand founded by property entrepreneur John Hitchcox and renowned designer Philippe Starck.
In a truly idyllic location, Evolutee Hotel offers amazing views of the golf course, Atlantic Ocean and the Lagoa de Óbidos, Portugal’s largest lagoon.
Evolutee Hotel allows you to enhance your wellbeing with a visit to Elemental Spa, offering indulgent massages and treatments that will leave you in total balance and harmony.
Kobe Restaurant offers a dynamic steak and sushi menu in a refined environment, while the bars and the amazing swimming pool are perfect for a relaxing moment.

Smile you're at Lisbon's most playful and connected hotel
Lisbon is a city buzzing with energy, with a reputation for art, multiculturalism, gastronomy, and host of major international events.
Moxy Lisboa Oriente, located in the heart of Parque das Nações; Lisbon´s creative hub where the future is happening.
The stylish hotel, with an unbeatable location close to Lisbon´s most vibrant transportation hub and a few minutes away from Lisbon´s airport.
With 222 cozy rooms with modern TVs, lightning fast free Wi-Fi, world renown Marriott plush queen beds, bathrooms with fancy product dispensers, energy filled communal space, food, snacks and drinks available 24/7 and gym, Moxy Lisboa Oriente´ s presents an affordable experience.
At our lobby, bustling with music, you can chill out with friends, have a drink, or grab yummy snacks any time of the day or night, because our bar and our crew is always awaiting you.
Portugal´s eclectic capital city is on every traveler’s bucket list and Moxy is the starting point.

title: Olissippo Lapa Palace Hotel
short_description: Welcome to Lapa Palace! A Palace from the 19th century, a luxurious 5 star hotel, on one of Lisbon hilltops, overlooking the Tagus River, surrounded by a beautiful garden.
full_description:
Built in 1870 as private residence, the Lapa Palace is a quiet oasis of tropical gardens and private luxury offering the ultimate in modern service for the traveller.
Set on a hill overlooking the Tagus River, amid tranquil gardens with ornamental fountains and streams, surrounded by stately embassy buildings, it is just minutes away from the historic and cultural area, the pier, and shopping area.
Business facilities include 8 meeting rooms and a fully equipped auditorium.
An outdoor heated swimming pool and a SPA with its indoor pool, gymnasium, sauna, steam bath, massages and exclusive beauty treatments from Élèmis, offer a true resort feeling.
The Lapa Restaurant is specialized in Mediterranean cuisine and is considered one of the best eateries in Lisbon.
Make yourself at home at the Lapa Palace.

short_description: A modern 4-star hotel, in the centre of Lisbon! VIP Executive Picoas Hotel is the result of the conversion of an old court into a modern 4-star hotel.
full_description:
A modern 4-star hotel, in the centre of Lisbon
With 131 rooms, VIP Executive Picoas Hotel is the result of the conversion of an old court into a modern 4-star hotel, in the centre of Lisbon.
With a privileged location, the hotel is 2 minutes’ walk from the Picoas Metro Station, 4 from the Parque Metro Station, while the famous El Corte Ingles department store is 5 minutes away and Marquês de Pombal Square 10.
The hotel features high quality public areas, such as the bar and the restaurant, ideal for enjoying your stay in Lisbon.
The bar is located on the ground floor and is an ideal meeting place for a drink, a light meal or an informal meeting.
On the 5th floor there is a restaurant where you can enjoy a varied breakfast, according to our guests’ requirements, who come for work or leisure, while enjoying a superb view over the city of Lisbon.
There is also a meeting room, with a maximum capacity of 40 people, where various events such as work meetings, training or workshops can be organized.
Guests will have at their disposal free Wi-Fi and all the facilities of a modern 4-star hotel.

title: Oeiras Ocean Pool
short_description: Find all the benefits of the beach, without the sand, near Lisbon. Have a swim with all the taste of the sea, but none of the inconvenience.
full_description: Those in Lisbon who like the sea but aren’t fans of the sand also have a place that suits them. The Oeiras Ocean Pool is filled with water from the sea and is split into two sections: one for kids and the other for adults with four diving boards. To make the most of the sun and heat, all you need to bring are your beach shorts. There are sun loungers, changing rooms, umbrellas and a bar serving light meals. With parking and a fantastic view of the marina and mouth of the Tagus, this is undoubtedly one of the best options when you don’t feel like going to the beach but fancy a refreshing swim during your holidays in Lisbon.

title: Lisboa Shop
short_description: The Lisboa Shop is a the ideal place to choose that special gift of Lisboa
full_description:

short_description: Near the Castle of Palmela, surrounded by vineyards developed in a harmonious whole of Cork Trees, where streams and lakes makes the magical beauty of this Golf course.

It is just 30 minutes south of Lisboa, one of Europe’s most stylish and historic capitals.
In operation since 1992, the golf course of Montado needed an impulse that could put again in a prominent place that belongs.
Was then initiated a work of remodeling in 2005 by already well-known Arq. Jorge Santana da Silva, in which the whole layout has been redesigned by creating new Tees and Greens that require some consideration when choosing the right putt before the next putting... highlight the hole 18 where it plays for a green located on an island.

title: MACAM – Museu de Arte Contemporânea Armando Martins
short_description: MACAM – Museu de Arte Contemporânea Armando Martins is displaying the private collection of Armando Martins to the public. Modern and contemporary Portuguese and international art gathered over the last 50 years.
full_description:
MACAM, Museu de Arte Contemporânea Armando Martins, is exhibiting the collection of Portuguese modern art and contemporary art from Portugal and abroad that collector Armando Martins began in 1974, and which continues to grow.
In addition to the permanent exhibition of the collection, MACAM presents other temporary exhibitions of pieces from other private collections, alongside a parallel programme of conferences and meetings that promote reflection on the exhibitions.
This museum space takes on an innovative concept: The House of the Private Collections. A house where the private becomes public, to receive and present other private collections, but also to welcome visitors in every sense, including the most literal of the term.

title: Fado Vadio
short_description: Already on the urban art itinerary, it captivates all those who see it. The mural “Fado Vadio” is a tribute to fado and one of the areas that most helped to develop it.
full_description: In a corner of Lisbon, in the neighbourhood of Mouraria, you’ll find the escadinhas de São Cristóvão, where an artist collective has created the impressive mural “Fado Vadio”. This inspired and original tribute to fado, Lisbon’s iconic music and classified as intangible cultural heritage, is one of the city’s must-see places. The symbols and figures from the history of fado have been portrayed over various walls. Fernando Maurício and Maria Severa, lyrics, bread and water on the table: these are just a few of the elements decorating this unique corner of Lisbon. Come and discover it and bring a camera: urban art is ephemeral by nature after all. Take the chance to immortalise it.

title: Câmara Municipal de Lisboa
short_description: The Lisbon City Council is the executive body of the municipality and its mission is to define and implement policies that promote development
full_description:
The Lisbon City Council is the executive body of the municipality and its mission is to define and implement policies that promote development.
Lisbon is the capital city of Portugal, incorporated in a metropolitan region with 2.98 million people.
From its 571,000 residents, the number of people using the city’s transport system rises by around 70% every day, due to commuting between home and work and home and school.
The region concentrates a large number of companies, close to 482,000, with a high level of technology and research.
This is where over 40% of the country’s total expenditure on R&D is focused.
This city with an ancestral history receives over 7 million visitors every year, with over half coming from markets abroad, in particular United States, Brazil, Spain, the United Kingdom, France, Germany and Italy.

title: Brilhante
short_description: Brilhante is romantic and bold. We like to offer a classic service and flavours, reminiscent of the refinement of Lisbon's nineteenth-century cafés.
full_description:
We shine without dazzling, but we are not afraid to surprise.
Inspired by the Lisbon eateries of days gone by, we want to preserve memories and traditions, in a truly elegant setting.
From our kitchen, centre-staged in the middle of the restaurant, emerge such classics as
Foie Gras Torchon
,
Salmon Gravlax
and
Sole Meunière
.
The star of the menu is the irresistible Steak à la Brilhante, a tribute to Steak à la Marrare.
The original recipe is a century-old Lisbon favourite. It was brought here, at the end of the eighteenth century, by the Neapolitan cook António Marrare, who started work in the pantry of a wealthy family home but who then went on to make a name for himself and open several restaurants under the name Café Marrare - one of them in nearby Rua dos Remolares.
Here time runs differently. Enjoy the elegance of a French brasserie, in a treasure trove that is just waiting to be discovered.

title: Vasco da Gama Aquarium
short_description: The Vasco da Gama Aquarium, located at the gates of Lisbon, is not only an Aquarium, it is also a Museum. A Museum born of a prince's dream and his ambition to know the depths of the ocean.
full_description:
Visiting the Vasco da Gama Aquarium is to allow yourself to board the Yacht Amelia, belonging to the Portuguese royal family, sailing along the coast of Sesimbra in the company of the then King of Portugal, Carlos I. From the ship's logbook to the Vasco da Gama Aquarium, in a trip between the depths of the sea and the sea slopes where we can contemplate only by sea.
It´s an institution of international interest wich qualifies trainers and taxidermists from across the globe, and strives to continuously improve its important role as a Living Natural History Museum.
It´s possible to observe rare specimens and other biological specimens, mainly molluscs, freshwater fish, aquatic birds, turtles and marine mammals. Has also specimens of other species, especially with regards to native and tropical fauna marine fish and both live and stuffed malacological specimens.
The Vasco da Gama Aquarium is one of the oldest museum aquariums in the world and has been visited by successive generations of families since its opening on May 20th, 1898. It is a historical heritage that promotes Portuguese aquatic life and it has the private King Carlos I Oceanographic Collection.
After renovations, now has a cafeteria with exterior terrace and an auditorium wich functions as a multipurpose space.

title: Fx Roadlights
short_description: FX Roadlights is a creative company, exploring new concepts of lighting and video in shows, brand events and television shows, it has a variety of solutions that offers to host in any kind of show or event.
full_description:
FX Roadlights was born in 2002 in Portugal, as a Light Designers company to meet the demands of an expanding market: it was urgent to solve the gap that existed in lighting and effects equipment in shows / TV studios. Along the years, the company complemented the activity, expanding its area of ​​operation to the video as a visual complement their performances.
In 2006, FX signed a partnership with G-Lec brand extending its area of operation to video as a visual complement of their performances. In 2015 it is chosen by PRG Alliance to be their representative member in Portugal, exponentially increasing the scale of equipment available for hire.
In addition to the technical know-how for 17 years proved in its vast portfolio through an experienced team, FX Roadlights is today a creative company, exploring new concepts of lighting and video in shows, brand events and television shows.
Making the difference in the variety of solutions that offers to host in any kind of show, creating lighting projects that respond to customer premises: create and innovate with maximum quality is the mission of FX Roadlights.

title: Jewish Cultural Center Rua da Judiaria
short_description: The Jewish Cultural Center Rua da Judiaria preserves and promotes Sephardic heritage in Lisbon, offering guided tours, educational programs and memory initiatives in the former Alfama Jewish Quarter.
full_description:
The Jewish Cultural Center Rua da Judiaria is dedicated to preserving, promoting and sharing the Jewish-Portuguese heritage, with a special focus on the former Jewish Quarter of Alfama, where its headquarters are located. Founded to recover and strengthen the Sephardic memory of Lisbon, the Center develops cultural, educational and community programs that bring the public closer to Jewish history, from medieval times to the present.
Its main activities include specialized guided tours, historical research, public memory initiatives, exhibitions and intercultural programs. The Center is also responsible for projects such as Stolpersteine Portugal and for establishing the cultural corridor of Rua da Judiaria, integrating art, heritage and education.
Through a continuous commitment to safeguarding both the material and intangible legacy of Jewish life, the Center aims to inspire knowledge, historical awareness and a culture of tolerance by preserving and sharing this unique heritage.

With over 50 rooms, with its Italian bar and restaurant, Anarchia, and a Business Center for meetings or events, the Dos Reis offers an unforgettable experience in a unique environment.

title: Campo Pequeno Museum
short_description: The Campo Pequeno Museum is a space dedicated to the art of bullfighting in Portugal, focusing particularly in the history of Campo Pequeno's bullring, the building where the museum is located.
full_description:
This space aims to reflect upon this unique cultural practice, classified by the Portuguese state as part of folk Portuguese cultural heritage, with a strong impact on its communities.
This is a very beloved building by everyone who lives the
aficion
for bullfighting. The Campo Pequeno Bullring welcomes now a space that recalls the historic memory of various social and cultural events that were milestones in the century-old history of this iconic building of Lisbon and Portugal, which exudes culture for more than one hundred and twenty years.
The purposes of Campo Pequeno Museum are creating a memory space that recalls the history and life of Portuguese bullfighting and the history and existence of Campo Pequeno. It also aims to disseminate the bullfighting culture, by displaying it to the public in general with the revitalization of Campo Pequeno as a cultural and leisure space.

title: Praça do Comércio (Terreiro do Paço)
short_description: One of Lisbon’s iconic squares that you must see. This famous square is ready to welcome you with various attractions and a nobility that is clear to see.
full_description:
One of Lisbon’s most emblematic sqaures is Praça do Comércio. Better known by locals as Terreiro do Paço, since it was once where the paço, or palace, used to stand before the earthquake in 1755, it is one of the largest squares in Europe.
As a symbol of the city, it provides access to various attractions that mustn’t be missed: Pátio da Galé; Cais das Colunas; and the innovative Lisbon Story Centre.
You should also climb up the Arco da Rua Augusta to marvel at the symmetry of the streets, the calçada portuguesa and the view of the river.
Take a rest at the feet of King José I and the imposing horse Gentil, a statue sculpted by Machado de Castro which so well illustrates royal power and the nobility of the Lusitano breed.
Make Praça do Comércio your starting point for discovering the city.

Like Bairro Alto, Cais do Sodré is a cool neighbourhood to go to for a beer and catch up with friends.
