title: Albuquerque Foundation
short_description: Located in Sintra, Portugal, the Albuquerque Foundation is a cultural institution dedicated to the exhibition and research of both Chinese export ceramics and contemporary ceramics.
full_description:
The Albuquerque Foundation aims at promoting the debate and reflection on the artistic, cultural and historical meaning of ceramic production. Its exhibition programme includes a permanent presentation of the pieces in the Albuquerque Chinese Ceramic Collection along with solo shows by contemporary artists working in the expanded field of ceramics. Artists, scholars and curators will be regularly invited for short and long-term residencies, in which research work on the Collection may constitute the point of departure for new artistic productions, academic works and innovative exhibitions.
The former Quinta de São João was refurbished and expanded to host the extensive activities of the Albuquerque Foundation. It features a specialized library, the Albuquerque Collection, maintained under strict museum guidelines, a concept store showcasing and selling works by Portuguese ceramists, and a café with a terrace and views of the vast gardens and Sintra landscapes included in the UNESCO World Heritage List.

title: PGA Aroeira N.1
short_description: Only 25 km from Lisbon, located in a protected pine woodland and close to the coastal town of Caparica, Aroeira is a unique estate comprising of two golf courses.
full_description:
Only 25 km from Lisbon, located in a protected pine woodland and close to the coastal town of Caparica, Aroeira is a unique estate comprising of two golf courses and a host of impressive mansions.
Designed by Frank Pennink and opened in 1973, Aroeira I was affectionately christened the ‘Wentworth of Lisbon’ by the UK press and it is easy to see why.
Played through tall avenues of pine forest and surrounded by wildlife and nature, the golf course plays to 6,650 yards and demands accuracy and placement off the tee in order to maximise opportunities to hit the target greens in regulation.
The course was the venue for the Portuguese Open in 1996 and 1997 and, in addition to staging several Ladies’ European Tour events, played host to the prestigious European Men’s Club Trophy in 2013.

At Battuta, we are Explorers.
We want to radically transform tourism into a form of playful, conscious, and regenerative exploration — where people don’t just visit places, but connect with them, care for them, and temporarily belong to them.
We create experiences to be lived, felt, shared, and remembered.
Battuta was born not as a tour company but as a playground for explorers, a stage for encounters, a space for shared moments.
Our mission is to create interactive, social, and meaningful experiences in Portugal that bring people together, break cultural barriers, and build bridges between realities — always in a safe, respectful, and conscious way.

title: Armazéns do Chiado
short_description: Armazéns do Chiado is a current, comfortable and qualified shopping center with a strong cultural component, located in one of the most noble and charismatic areas of the city.
full_description:
ARMAZÉNS DO CHIADO Right in the historic heart of the capital, this shopping center holds the memory of a historic symbol of Lisbon. In this same place, at the end of the 19th century, the old “Grandes Armazéns do Chiado” were inaugurated, a classic department store of the Lisbon trade destroyed in the fire that haunted Chiado in 1988.
With a modern structure, the current Armazéns do Chiado and its building (Cultural Heritage) revive some of this past aura, making available to visitors more than 50 shops in various areas (from fashion to perfumery), 15 restaurants and a Hotel with a fantastic view.
Armazéns do Chiado is a current, comfortable and qualified shopping center with a strong cultural component, located in one of the most noble and charismatic areas of the city.

title: Christ the King Shrine and Monument
short_description: Cross the river and discover one of the most impressive views of Lisbon. The Santuário do Cristo-Rei is a place of peace and gives you a unique panoramic view of the entire Lisbon region.
full_description:
There is a monument in Lisbon that will make you wonder what side of the Atlantic you are actually on.
But don’t be deceived, you really are in Lisbon and the Christ the King monument on the south bank of the river is not the one in Rio de Janeiro.
The view from it of the city is equally impressive. The monument was built by means of the fundraising efforts of Portuguese children over a period of 19 years – and through the commitment of the episcopacy based on an idea proposed by Cardinal Cerejeira in 1934.
The monument, which was erected in thanks of the peace which Portugal was granted during the 20th century, was inaugurated on 17 May 1959 and provides one of the most beautiful views of Lisbon.
Come and see for yourself.

The Onyria Quinta da Marinha Hotel is located in the excellent area of Cascais and Sintra, just 25 kms away from Lisbon, and stands between the city and nature, the beach and the mountains, offering you the perfect setting to fulfill your wishes.
In 2014, the Hotel did an extensive ReStyling with improvements in all key areas, including: Reception, Lobby, Rooms, Rocca Restaurant, Five Pines Restaurant and Trent Jones Bar.
It is the ideal place to close a business, play golf and live unforgettable moments while discovering a new vision of luxury and comfort in the heart of nature. Here you will find everything that only a 5 star hotel can offer, always a new emotion.
The bucolic brings us to a quiet countryside, allowing the communion between man and nature. Break the routine and offer yourself unforgettable moments in the perfect setting of the Onyria Quinta da Marinha Hotel.
The scent of the sea inebriates who choose our Hotel for business meetings or just for leisure.
You can find all facilities, like swimming pools, restaurants and the most beautiful and prestigious golf courses in the region, and you also have Sintra nearby.
Welcome...

short_description: Chez Croissant is all about the flavors and good memories!
full_description:
Our mission is to bring back the memory of the good and classic croissant shops of the 80s and 90s.
We celebrate and evoke the authenticity when the time passed slowly and invite us to indulgence.
In the space, we find warm, natural tones that invoke memories, such as the handmade mosaic wainscoting and the terrazzo floor, which make us revisit a place of comfort and memory.
For all the occasions.
Produced daily in each location, our croissants are vegan, in a hybrid mix of puff pastry and brioche.
Baking takes place in two stages and guarantees a slightly caramelized and crunchy topping that appeals to the senses. With various filling possibilities, from sweet to savory,

title: Almaria Edifício Ex Libris
short_description: Ex-Libris redefines the concept of Premium accommodation and its excellent location allows to easily step out for a stroll in Chiado or Avenida da Liberdade.
full_description:
This building's excellent location places it in the heart of the action, where things are always happening. You can easily step out for a stroll in Chiado or venture out to Avenida da Liberdade, where you'll find the most exclusive luxury shops. You're also steps away from the trendiest bars and restaurants in the city.
With 18 apartments spread over 6 floors, Ex-Libris redefines the concept of Premium accommodation, offering 1 bedroom, 2 bedroom and 3 bedroom suites in a building that combines sophistication, tradition and contemporary design.
Perfect for vacation and at the same time being at home, our apartments can be rented in a short or medium period, to be alone, accompanied, with friends or the whole family.
Wifi, Bath and Bed linen, fully furnished and daily maid service.
These 18 apartments are theme-based, created "from the Books of" Portugal's most important authors, with each floor of the building dedicated to an author and each apartment named after a classic work by an author. The design of the interiors reflects a modern space with traditional elements, emphasising Portuguese design and young, local emerging artists.
These elements include paintings by Maisa Champalimaud, wood furniture designed by Cabanas and a lobby featuring Urban Art by the Portuguese graphic designer Add Fuel, who adds a contemporary touch to traditional Portuguese azulejo tiles.
Lisbon is the place to be. What could be more fitting than to stay in a meticulously designed and decorated suite right in the heart of the city, one that offers a truly unforgettable experience?

Unique and trendy, the Hotel Internacional offers an unrivalled blend of cosmopolitan comfort, personalized service and local insider knowledge that set’s it as a place apart.

title: Odivelas Memorial
short_description: Come and try to discover the truth hidden in this impressive stone memorial, the Memorial de Odivelas, one of Lisbon’s historical enigmas.
full_description:
Discover one of the oldest enigmas in the region of Lisbon. In the historic part of the city, the Odivelas Memorial – or Cruzeiro de Odivelas – is an arch that may have been built at some time in the 14th century, according to the most plausible theories about its style and significance.
But these theories are not certain, since not a written or engraved word exists which could indicate the monument’s purpose. The cross at the top might be in the style of the Order of Avis, but it is most likely just a marker delimiting the jurisdictional area of the convent of Odivelas.
The most popular theory, however, is that this Memorial was used to carry the body of King Dinis, who died in Santarém, to the mausoleum at the convent.
Try and discover the truth hidden behind this impressive stone memorial.
location: Rua Guilherme Gomes Fernandes, Largo da Memória, 2675, Odivelas

title: Haliotis - Centro, Escola e Loja de mergulho
short_description: Sesimbra is located in a huge bay facing south, the weather being predominantly north, it is very sheltered throughout the year allowing unique conditions for diving.
full_description:
The dives realized in this place are characterized by magnificent views of the Arrábida mountain range with its great cliffs, numerous bays and several cables. Here we can enjoy dives at different depths, between 5 and 10 meters for beginners and below 30 meters for more experienced divers and / or technicians.
The great diversity and complexity of the seabed allows the observation of numerous species and more than 1,100 species of marine macro-organisms are known today.
During almost all the year it´s also possible to perform fascinating night dives in complete safety.
location: Porto de Abrigo de Sesimbra, 2970-152, Sesimbra

title: Centro Cultural de Belém
short_description: Visit one of Lisbon’s most interesting cultural centres. With one of the best modern art collections in Portugal, the CCB is a celebration of culture, opening its intensive programme of activities to all kinds of cultural events.
full_description:
The Centro Cultural de Belém is one of modern Lisbon’s biggest icons.
Designed by Manuel Salgado and Vittorio Gregotti, it was built on a special site. Its modern architecture contrasts gently with some of the most iconic buildings of old Lisbon.
If you walk from Jerónimos to the CCB, you cross centuries of history in just a few steps.
The CCB is one of the most popular places amongst Lisbonites and visitors for its closeness to the river, its gardens and cafés offering some of the best public spaces in the city.
With auditoriums, museums, temporary shows and major events, the CCB is a cultural hub with something for everyone.
Come and visit the shops, restaurants and cafeterias and the various exhibitions and concerts which make it one of the liveliest venues in Lisbon.

title: Basílica da Estrela
short_description: One of the most impressive and attractive churches in Lisbon. Take some time to admire this impressive piece of architecture and discover the surrounding area.
full_description: The Basílica da Estrela is an obligatory port of call in Lisbon. It is also a stop on the no. 28 tram route, another must during your stay. Two of the things on your to-do list can be found here, in this elegant bairro with its lovely park. But the neoclassical 18th-century church deserves most of your attention. Its two bell towers peal charmingly, while its paintings by Pompeu Botoni and Pedro Alexandrino accentuate the baroque style of its interior. As a simplified replica of the Convento de Mafra, it is also the result of a vow made by the queen in the hope of producing a son and heir. Its white dome marks the skyline. And the interior, decorated with marble of every colour houses the queen’s majestic tomb, and a unique nativity scene made of 500 cork and terracotta pieces.

title: O Forcado Restaurant
short_description: The Restaurant "O Forcado" is situated in the heart of Lisbon in Bairro Alto. Opened in June 1967, the godmother is Amália Rodrigues.
full_description:
The Restaurant "O Forcado" is situated in the heart of Lisbon in Bairro Alto. Opened in June 1967, the godmother is Amália Rodrigues.
The "O Forcado" name means a member of a group of men that performs the “face catch" and take the bull on the final event in a typical Portuguese Bullfighting.
It is decorated with bullfighting motifs and very old tiles.
On the walls there are frameworks of Bullfighting made by the great painter Domingos Saraiva.
In almost half a century of existence where many great Fado artists like Filipe Pinto, Antonio Rocha, Tristão da Silva, Fernando Maurício, Francisco Martinho and for 28 years guitarist Jorge Fontes.
location: Rua da Rosa, 221, Bairro Alto, 1200-348, Lisboa

title: Lisboa Congress Centre (CCL)
short_description: Lisboa Congress Centre - CCL is the largest congress centre in Lisbon with a total area of 29.000sqm, including 8 auditoria, 5 pavilions and 35 meeting rooms.
full_description:
Lisboa Congress Centre - CCL is the largest congress centre in Lisbon with a total area of 29.000sqm., including 8 auditoria, 5 pavilions and 34 meeting rooms.
It has been designed to host National and International conventions, congresses, seminars, exhibitions and meeting of different sizes. It offers flexibility, maximum comfort and efficiency which allows the holding of several and different events simultaneously.
CCL, located in the historical area of Belém, by the Tagus River, has two parking lots with 1.100 spaces and a river-view restaurant for 400. The venue is featured with the latest in technology (audiovisual and IT equipments).
Since its Opening in 1989, we had two refurbishments and have been hosting International Congresses from 100 to 9980 people, including the Presidency of the European Union.
We provide all services needed for the event`s organization. CCL has national and international recognition and earned several certifications: ISO 9001, AIPC Quality Standards, and Meeting Matrix International PCMA Space Verified

title: Valle-Flôr Restaurant
short_description: In the Valle Flôr restaurant, enjoy the best of Portuguese cuisine with a palatial essence while enjoying the Regency style ... memorable for all your senses!
full_description:
In the Valle Flôr restaurant, enjoy the best of Portuguese cuisine with a palatial essence while enjoying the Regency style that dictates the decor of the walls and ceilings; and live an experience that will be memorable for all your senses.
The Regency style multiplies the feeling of luxury and well-being, contrasting the golden hues with the fresh green and blue tones of the decor and the wrought iron of the huge luminous windows.

At the Pestana Palace Music Room you will find the lively Allegro bar. Here you can taste soups, ceviches and carpaccios, but you can also share deliciously Portuguese snacks.

Established in 1994, PAGS (Portugal Algarve Golf Services) is Portugal’s premier specialist Golf destination management company. We work with the Golf Tour Operator trade only and represent 50+ Golf Tour Operators from 17 different countries.

title: Lisbon Zoo
short_description: A visit to the Zoo is a true journey around the world in a single day… without ever leaving Lisbon! A place where memories are made and nature finds its voice.
full_description:
With over 140 years of history and located in the heart of Lisbon, Lisbon Zoo is a place dedicated to biodiversity conservation, scientific research, and environmental education. Home to around 2,000 animals from approximately 300 different species, the Zoo’s mission goes far beyond Sete Rios, reaching all corners of the globe.
Be inspired by the flight of the birds in the Enchanted Forest Presentation and the energy of the Dolphin Bay. Discover the Zoo from a unique perspective aboard the cable car, and explore the fascinating world of reptiles and amphibians in the Reptile House. All of this included in just one ticket.
A visit to the Zoo is an unforgettable experience that combines leisure, discovery, and a commitment to wildlife conservation.
Highlights
: Dolphins Bay, Cable Car, Reptile House, Children’s Farm, Bird Presentation — all included in one ticket!

title: Miradouro do Torel
short_description: This is one of the best-kept secrets in Lisbon. Come and visit the miradouro which deserves a visit for its unique beauty.
full_description:
The Miradouro do Torel is one of the new stars of Lisbon.
Located in the Jardim do Torel (garden) once belonging to an old 18th-century farm, it has a broad view of the Avenida da Liberdade and São Roque hill – home to the Miradouro de São Pedro de Alcântara.
Surrounded by a garden and grand 18th- and 19th-century houses.
You can reach it via the Elevador do Lavra or by Rua do Telhal.
With lots of shade and few tourists – Campo Mártires da Pátria is not one of the busiest areas – this viewing spot can be almost yours alone during the winter months.

title: ADBP - Associação de Dinamização da Baixa Pombalina
short_description: This association emerged from a group of merchants with long traditions in downtown Lisbon, the group for the Dynamization of Baixa Commerce was born, which, since 1993, has been promoting commerce in the city's downtown.
full_description:
In 1993, a group of traders with long traditions in downtown Lisbon set up the Grupo de Dinamização do Comércio da Baixa (Baixa Trade Dynamisation Group).
Existing cyclically to promote downtown commerce, the businessmen soon realised the need to hold various initiatives and events to attract consumers to this shopping area.
Since associationism is a phenomenon of combining interests with long traditions in our country, generating synergies that induce the capacity to respond to external threats, the pre-Associação de Dinamização da Baixa Pombalina was born.
There have been advances and setbacks during this process. However, imbued with the conviction of the common interest that the creation of the future association represented, the Installation Committee held a Founders' General Assembly on 28 November 1996, attended by 300 traders.
Traders were informed of the objectives, strategies and campaigns that were to be developed until the end of the 20th century.
location: Rua da Assunção nº 42 – 1º Sala 7 , 1100-044 , LISBOA

title: DOMA Portugal
short_description: Located 30 minutes from Lisbon, DOMA is a forest estate where people come to stay, gather, and work on ideas together.
full_description:
The grounds include rooms and glamping, Bakery & Bistro, a coworking lounge, forest trails, and flexible event spaces.
The Steam Center offers guided Steam sessions and sensory rituals designed to slow down and reset.
Alongside a curated year-round program of workshops, talks, and weekend experiences, DOMA is also open to those hosting their own events, from intimate retreats to full-scale offsites.

title: Motoxplorers
short_description: Motorcycle rental with unlimited kilometers, helmets, GPS and insurance. BMW Motorrad Official partner.
full_description:
BMW MOTORRAD OFFICAL RENT & TRAVEL PARTNER PORTUGAL
Motoxplorers is the result of more than 25 years of motorcycle touring around the globe. We're a passionate and professional team, totally dedicated to offering a unique and exclusive travel experience.
In 2009 we were pioneers in the creation of the only professional structure specifically dedicated to Motorcycle Travelling in Portugal. All the tours we offer on this website were thoroughly scouted by our guides, which choose the best roads, the most exceptional accommodation, the concealed attractions and all the details that change a simple tour into a most memorable one.
Along the last 5 years, we have developed the project "Portugal Best Riding Roads" where we have scouted and cataloged the most beautiful and genuine corners of our country. The result is routes that show the best and most authentic Portugal, a country where the sun shines more than 220 days a year, full of magnificent and deserted roads where time still passes slowly.
Motoxplorers is a company created by motorcyclists to motorcyclists, a team that knows the specific needs of a motorcycle traveler and that can help you on your next tour, to Portugal or the World.
location: Rua Francisco Lyon de Castro, 14-C, 1600-311, Lisboa

title: InterContinental Cascais-Estoril
short_description: More than a hotel, the InterContinental Cascais-Estoril is an open window to the Atlantic Ocean and to modern luxury experiences.
full_description:
With 59 Ocean front guestrooms, the InterContinental Cascais-Estoril offers one of the best locations and views over Cascais Portugal’s coast. Modern and elegant, it´s perfect for romantic escapes or family getaways.
A contemporary Oceanfront Hotel in Cascais Portugal, the InterContinental Cascais-Estoril has a privileged location on the water front and boasts inspiring views over the Atlantic Ocean. This new InterContinental destination is inspired by Portuguese flavours and offers luxury and modern accommodation on the coastline of the Portuguese Riviera located in the region of Cascais.
Located on the site of the former “Atlântico Hotel”, a property which became a symbol of Estoril and an important tourism legacy in Cascais Portugal, the InterContinental Estoril respects the history and remains true to the essence of this charming place.
The hotel combines the heritage of the past with the modernity of the present and a service of excellence which will create memorable experiences for our guests. From the moment you arrive until we bid you farewell, our talented team will bring to life the quintessence of Estoril and Cascais.

title: 5 Oceanos
short_description: Located in one of the most privileged zones of the city, besides the sight on the Tagus river we are focused on the quality and freshness of the food that we made.
full_description:
Portuguese food, fish and seafood prepared with refinement and good taste.
The 5 Oceanos offers the best of oceans with special rigor in the freshness of food. Fish and seafood prepared with refinement and good taste and a special selection of the best portuguese dishes.
We are located in one of the most privileged areas of the city, Docas de Santo Amaro with an incredible view of the Tagus river and Cristo Rei.
Whether outdoors, on the terrace, or in our upper room, you can enjoy the best of national cuisine in a large space full of light.

title: Subserra Municipal a State
short_description: With a magnificent view over the Tagus, Quinta de Subserra retains the peace and quiet. The Palace halls and the historic garden are available for events.
full_description:
Down below, we can observe the fast pace typical of the urban setting. With a magnificent view over the Tagus River, Quinta de Subserra retains the peace and quiet of bygone days.
Founded in the 17th century, this set comprises the Palace and the Chapel of S. José, with 17th-century (ceramic tiles) and a main altar painting by the famous artist Bento Coelho da Silveira.
Ancestrally linked to farming, the Estate grows its own wine Encostas de Xira.
The Palace halls and the historic garden are available for holding events.
location: Largo 1º de Maio, 1, São João dos Montes, 2600-782, Vila Franca de Xira

title: Casa dos Bicos
short_description: Visit one of the most fascinating houses in Lisbon, returned to its past glory after the José Saramago Foundation took up residence.
full_description:
The Casa dos Bicos, which belongs to the Museum of Lisbon and is currently home to the José Saramago Foundation, is unique.
Behind its façade of diamond-shaped stones lies the personal library of the Nobel Prize-winner for Literature in 1998. It also houses an exhibition about his life and work.
Outside, under an olive tree, lie the ashes of one of Portugal’s greatest authors.
But the building’s history dates back further, to 1523 and the son of the second governor of Portuguese India. Inspired by the Italian Renaissance and the palaces of Bologna, he decided to build one for himself.
But the symmetry of the doors and windows was neglected. The end result is a house which looks like a door into an enchanted kingdom. Considering the vast library inside, the description is very apt.

title: Truly Amazing
short_description: By choosing to tour with Truly Amazing, you are embarking on a unique experience! Expect unforgettable moments with your family, in the waters of the wonderful and emblematic Tagus, overlooking Lisbon... shall we start our tour?
full_description:
Tours
in the Tagus River Unforgettable
By choosing to ride with Truly Amazing, you're embarking on a unique experience!
Expect unforgettable moments with your family, in the waters of the wonderful and emblematic Tagus, overlooking Lisbon... Taking advantage of the sun that enchants the city so much or in the late afternoon where the orange tones of the sunset paint the horizon.
Count on all the safety, commitment and professionalism of our team! Shall we start our tour?
Learn
more about us
The beginning of our and your incredible journey
The Truly Amazing team is small but wants to make all the difference! In your life, in the life of the Tagus River, always with the view dazzle over Lisbon to the other bank, of course!
We are a very recent project, we started in 2021, but we are prepared for the challenge and for all the fun we will have when sailing with our customers. We have in mind enjoying every day with sun (and other cloudy too, why not?), from the hints of spring to the days when winter does not bring weather. We love to walk, but yours and our safety comes first!
Our skipper is former Merchant Navy officer Carlos Alcobia! Breathe sea, live the river and bring in luggage many hours sailed and the challenge of many waves. It has had this design in the drawer for many decades and can now realize it.
Alongside the dream comes the reality of a well-trained team, coordinated, the concern with all logistics, maintenance and safety.
To look at Lisbon is to know the history, to see the modernity, to observe an unparalleled light and to let yourself be lulled by the waves.
Truly
Amazing is your hostess in this discovery!
location: Praceta João Villaret, 1 , 2825-446 , Costa de Caparica

title: Estufa Fria - Casa do Marquês
short_description: Delight with the exuberance of the Estufa Fria’s plants and elements, with 800 m2, the Nave da Estufa Fria de Lisboa is available for holding any type of event (parties, weddings, conferences, congresses, among others).
full_description:
Initially thought of as a shelter for plants, the Estufa Fria de Lisboa is a space where you can enjoy pleasant moments among lakes, waterfalls, statues, and hundreds of different plant species from all over the world.
With 800 m2, the Nave da Estufa Fria de Lisboa is available for holding any type of event (parties, weddings, conferences, congresses, among others), thus recovering the glamour, mystique and the power of attraction that have always accompanied it, since it was built in the 1950s.
It is a semi-buried building in reinforced concrete, consisting of ten vaults that follow the slope of the hill on which it is located, integrating, in the extreme North, a rocky outcrop of basalt. The interior is lined with small pebbles, which resemble washed marble.
Over the years, several events have been held at the Nave da Estufa Fria de Lisboa, such as concerts, exhibitions, congresses and publicity actions.
This could be the ideal space for your event!

title: Rocco
short_description: One restaurant, three distinct universes plus the sheer brilliance of the truly unforgettable moments that dazzles you whichever way you look. At Rocco, every experience is genuinely unique.
full_description:
A festive atmosphere and an aura of sophistication wander around Rua Ivens. The architecture and charm of Chiado nourish this ambience, but it is behind the doors that open onto the ground floor of The Ivens Hotel that you will find Rocco, the magnetic centre from which these vibes come from.
Here, exceptional culinary creations are unveiled in a scenic setting that is just perfect for any form of celebration.
Italian flavours, see flavours and the very best of Portuguese cuisine are the protagonists in a vibrantly bold show. We will challenge your senses and introduce you to new sensations.
Leave the world behind and enter the Rocco universe.
• A new concept
The red carpet that covers the stairs at our entrance whisks you on and up to a sensory experience that has no parallel. We reach our stage and the theatrical performance begins, guiding us on a journey through an experience that is as intimate as it is bold.
The light, the reflections and the textures combine to enhance each sensation, in settings created in partnership with the interior designer Lázaro Rosa-Violán. The versatility of the space and the breadth and depth of the menu ensure each visit is unique. Every time you come back to Rocco, it feels as invigorating as your very first visit.
• Gastrobar
The circular counter around the Gastrobar is crowned by a spectacularly suspended display brimming with drinks from across the globe. Here you will find the perfect company for the best dishes that Portugal and rest of the world have to offer.
The central bar serves meals at any time of the day and to suit any appetite: snacks, classic dishes, salads, sandwiches, cold cuts, cheeses and much more. For your toast, choose our special Negronis, a classic cocktail or a wine from one of the world’s four corners. Whether you sit at the counter or the high tables by the windows, the vinyl records played by our DJ will certainly liven up the occasion.
• Crudo Bar
Just as you can hear the sea by putting a shell to your ear, at Crudo you can breathe in the atmosphere of the Italian coast. At the top of this charming space, the counter, , where the fish andseafood are prepared, confidently affirms that the sea does indeed lap at this shore.
The menu is common to Gastrobar, combining land and sea with a gastronomic journey that goes perfectly with Sparking Wine and Prosecco.
• Ristorante
The home of Italy's most authentic flavours. In this vibrant room, the two kitchens on opposite sides complement each other, to ensure you experience traditional Italian cuisine just as it is meant to be.
As we watch the final touches being given to the Italian dishes, the wafting aromas simply add to the textures and colours of a space that takes us back in time. It is here that classic dishes such as Lobster Linguine, Crab Ravioli and Sea Bass alla Ligure parade before our eyes.
On the other side of the window, is the leafy Terrazza. The sensations from inside the restaurant bubble over into this al fresco dining area.

short_description: Your cruise, your choice! You can choose between a 2-hour, 4-hour or 8-hour private cruise, starting in the morning, afternoon, or around sunset hours for the best experience possible!
full_description:

title: Biovilla
short_description: The biovilla, as an agro-tourism project inserted in the Serra da Arrábida Natural Park, intends to develop a tourist activity that contributes to the economic, cultural and environmental development of the region in a sustainable way.
full_description:
Nature Tourism is one of the segments of tourism activity that aims to use the natural and cultural heritage in a responsible and sustainable way.
The biovilla, as an agro-tourism project inserted in the Serra da Arrábida Natural Park, intends to develop a tourist activity that contributes to the economic, cultural and environmental development of the region in a sustainable way.
In this sense, more than a tourist project, we intend to be a “living example” of sustainable practices, providing all our visitors with a unique and unforgettable experience.
location: Herdade do Pinhal Bastos - Vale de Barris, 2950-055, Palmela

title: Holiday Inn Lisbon-Continental
short_description: Holiday Inn Lisbon - Continental hotel is only 4 km from Airport Humberto Delgado and 10 minutes by metro from the Old Town.
full_description:
Hotel with meeting rooms, a fitness centre and free Wi-Fi.
Holiday Inn® Lisbon – Continental hotel is only 4 km from Airport Humberto Delgado and 10 minutes by metro from the Old Town.
Regular shuttles run to the airport at Republica Avenue, and Campo Pequeno metro station is close by.
Entrecampos train station, near the hotel, offers local and regional service.
You can host up to 200 delegates in the hotel’s seven meeting rooms and drive to the Feira Internacional de Lisboa convention centre in 15 minutes.
Museu Calouste Gulbenkian, a 10-minute walk, houses a rich collection of work by Portuguese and international artists.
Fifteen minutes away by metro, medieval São Jorge Castle offers panoramic hilltop views of the surrounding Old Town.
Warmly decorated, air-conditioned rooms have wooden floors, and pillow menus to help ensure a good night's sleep.
Suites have a separate living room with a sofa bed.
Wake up to a breakfast buffet in the Coffee Shop restaurant, and dine on seasonal Portuguese cuisine in the evening.
Kids stay and eat free program. After a busy day, you can meet friends to chat over cocktails in the sleek City Bar.

title: Tapisco
short_description: Tapas | Petiscos | Vermutes - If on the one hand unity makes strength, at Tapisco sharing makes the table. It is through this concept that Chef Henrique Sá Pessoa invites you to enjoy tapas and snacks from both sides of the border, Portugal and Spain.
full_description:
At number 81 Rua D. Pedro V, in Príncipe Real, Spanish
tapas
, Portuguese
petiscos
and
vermouth
are served, traditional flavours prepared by the Chef, who leaves the sophistication of fine dining at the door, but never the quality, attention to detail and taste.
For a year, Henrique Sá Pessoa and sous-chef João Gama chose the ingredients, tested the products, fine-tuned the flavours and created a varied menu, without borders, which brings together references from here and there, very familiar to all those who like to share good times at the table.
As for the
wines
, there are more than 65 references from Portugal and Spain.
However, the highlight is the vermouths, whites, reds and rojos.
With 32 seats, 10 of them at the counter, Tapisco is the perfect place for the curious.
You can see the team in action, the dishes being prepared and even ask the chef about a detail or curiosity about what is being prepared.
Bar
VERMUTE
Inspired by neighbouring Spain, we present the city's first vermouth bar, where vermouths take centre stage at mealtimes.
The restaurant's large window facing Príncipe Real's main street guarantees passers-by a privileged view of the bar team's work and the chance to order vermouths and cocktails from outside.

title: Sintra-Cascais Nature Park
short_description: Visiting the Parque Natural de Sintra-Cascais is one of the essential road trips you have to do when in Lisbon – whether by car, motorbike or bus.
full_description:
This is one of the essential road trips you have to do when in Lisbon – whether by car, motorbike or bus.
The Parque Natural de Sintra Cascais covers the area of dunes in Guincho and the Sintra hills, an area of 17, 000 hectares where rocky escarpments and steep cliffs coexist with the changing sands of the dunes and the pine trees of the valley.
What they share is the Atlantic ocean and its immense power, the winds of which shape the dunes and whistle through the trees in the hills.
Cape Roca shows you a part of the park, which must be visited with time and with various stops at the improvised viewing points along the road.
The sea caves are not easy to visit, but the oak forests and Mediterranean pine woods fill the air with the pure scent of nature, in contrast to the salty air of the sea.
Come and take this unmissable tour, so close to Lisbon.

full_description: The company Barraqueiro Transportes S.A. is a private passenger transport operator, by bus, in Portugal. short_description: Located deep in the heart of Lisbon at Av. da República, Jupiter Lisboa Hotel is the ideal choice for city breaks or business trips, as well as for those seeking to discover and explore the Portuguese Capital.
full_description:
Lisbon is history, is culture, is entertainment, is “saudade”, is voyage, is return… is Jupiter Lisboa Hotel.
Our source of inspiration?
Lisbon’s traditional neighborhoods, monuments, symbols and colors, celebrating it inside out and combining traditional and trendy to perfection, well reflected in the preserved historic facade designed by renowned architect Miguel Ventura Terra, dating from 1906.
Our commitment?
Three c’s summarize it perfectly: comfort, confidence and centrality. Located deep in the heart of Lisbon at Av. da República, Jupiter Lisboa Hotel is the ideal choice for city breaks, short breaks or business trips, as well as for those seeking to discover and explore the true magic of the Portuguese Capital.

title: Música no Coração
short_description: Música no Coração promotes the biggest and best festivals, with a special emphasis on the major summer events.
full_description:
Música no Coração vibrates with the applause, is moved by the tears and chants of the audience, and is attentive to the demands of the fiercest fans and to the requirements of whoever, at the end of the day, is the consumer.
Música no Coração believes that a show often becomes a remarkable memory – both individually and collectively – that time helps crystallise, sometimes as a myth.
Audiences are diverse, and Música no Coração knows that better than anyone. From the most famous names in pop rock to the most alternative forms of electronic music, and from world music to fado, Música no Coração makes sure to present the most comprehensive range of artists possible.
Whilst being the market leader, Música no Coração aims to increase the quality of its services and infrastructures with every show. One example of this is the summer festivals: year after year, the venues are improved, and more and better logistic conditions are created, for the crowds who make these festivals true pilgrimages.
For Música no Coração, work is a kind of play, where the heart beats in time with the heart of the audience. Música no Coração's audience.
Música no Coração promotes the biggest and best festivals, with a special emphasis on the major summer events, creating spaces for all audiences and sounds, from the most revered artists to showcasing the most exciting up-and-coming acts at any given moment.
Música no Coração takes pride in the smiles on the audience's faces when, tired but happy, they leave the venue.

title: Piedade Municipal a State
short_description: A former 14th century noble estate and an 18th century manor, it is a remarkable set of considerable asset value, featuring its ceramic tiles inside. This place is excellent for visiting, walking and holding events.
full_description:
A former 14th-century noble estate with farming links and an 18th-century manor, the Urban Park and its Palace are located in the heart of Póvoa de Santa Iria.
The Palace (a Property of Public Interest) is a remarkable set of considerable asset value, featuring its ceramic tiles inside, the Church and Hermitage of "N.ª Sr.ª da Piedade", Hermitage of "Senhor Morto" and the "S. Jerónimo" Oratory.
The Urban Park has various sports facilities, as well as children's and youth recreational areas and an Educational Farm.
Excellent for visits and walks, the park also allows holding events.
location: Rua Padre Manuel Duarte, Póvoa de Santa Iria, 2625-173, Vila Franca de Xira

title: Arpad Szenes - Vieira da Silva Foundation
short_description: The Arpad Szenes-Vieira da Silva Foundation was created to study and to diffuse the knowledge of Vieira da Silva work, one of the most outstanding plastic artist in the 20th century, as well as her husband work, Arpad Szenes.
full_description:
The Arpad Szenes-Vieira da Silva Foundation was created to study and to diffuse the knowledge of Vieira da Silva work, one of the most outstanding in the 20th century, as well as her husband work, Arpad Szenes.
Based in a way to give a time/ historical and esthetical frame to the art of these two artists, the Foundation presents, regularly, temporary exhibitions from other artists, national and international, who lived or shared with them artistics affinities.
The museum program also aims to give a place to Arpad Szenes and Vieira da Silva’s work in the panorama of national and international art.

title: Sacavém Ceramics Museum
short_description: The Sacavém ceramics factory was built in the mid-eighteen hundreds and became one of the most well-known producers of fine faience in Portugal and Europe.
full_description:
Although Portugal is known for its Vista Alegre china, it had a big competitor in the shape of the Sacavém ceramics factory.
This pottery, created in the mid-eighteen hundreds, became one of the best-known producers of fine faience in Portugal and Europe thanks to its kaolin-based pottery, a very pure form of clay.
Its manufacturing processes were modernised by an Englishman who raised production to such an extent that he was awarded the title of Baron of Howorth of Sacavém by King Luís I.
The collectivism and pioneering approach to caring for the factory’s employees turned Sacavém into one of the most important factories in the country – and a hotbed of anti-fascist feeling.
Closed after countless financial and management problems in 1994, the area beside kiln no. 18 was converted into a museum explaining the history of the factory – and Portugal’s development in the 20th century.
location: Urbanização Real Forte, Rua Álvaro Pedro Gomes, Sacavém, 2685-137, Loures

title: Altis Grand Hotel
short_description: The Altis Grand Hotel, five star Conference & Business Hotel, combines elegance, sophistication and comfort with the best location in Lisbon’s city centre.
full_description:
Recently renovated the hotel has 300 bedrooms and suites, including brand new deluxe rooms, with great views over the cultural and historical quarters.
A fully equipped business center and 20 multipurpose meeting spaces with capacity from 8 up to 750 people, suitable for the most demanding events.
There is also a new spa with a covered heated swimming pool, Sauna, Jacuzzi, fitness room, 5 treatment cabinets.
To enjoy the Portuguese excellent cuisine, there`s a panoramic restaurant on the top floor - the D. Fernando Grill, for a casual meal the coffee-shop in the lobby or for a private group meal the Rio de Janeiro restaurant and 3 bars giving guests the chance to unwind.

title: Praia D'el Rey Golf & Beach Resort
short_description: Arguably the most stunning golf course in Europe, is set among extensive pine forests and undulating dunes, with spectacular views over the Atlantic Ocean.
full_description:
Arguably the most stunning golf course in Europe, Praia d’El Rey is set among extensive pine forests and undulating dunes, with spectacular views over the Atlantic Ocean and the Berlenga Islands beyond.
Designed by the renowned American golf architect, Cabell B. Robinson and inaugurated on 14th June 1997, the courses 87 bunkers, generous greens and abundant natural vegetation contrast sharply with the lush fairways making it the complete golfing challenge.
Praia d’El Rey is rated amongst the top courses in Europe by Golf World magazine and is one of the major golfing destinations for both private and sponsored events, as well as international tournaments.
Praia d’El Rey offers a highly sought after and unique mixture of seaside links on the back-nine (including 4 holes stretching along the seafront) and 5 parkland holes on the front-nine.
A true test of golf for golfers of all levels.

title: Beaches of Ericeira
short_description: Head over to the World Surfing Reserve half an hour from Lisbon. You’ll find pretty beaches and a quaint fishing town called Ericeira, whose soul was forged by the sea.
full_description: Discover the World Surfing Reserve in Ericeira where even if you don’t have a board you’ll find the perfect beach. While Pedra Branca, the Coxos and Ribeira d’Ilhas are world famous for their perfect wave conditions, there are other beaches where the water is calmer. One of them is Praia dos Pescadores, one of the safest in the whole region thanks to the bay created by the pier. But if you’re looking for beaches further from the centre, with great bars, call in at Foz do Lizandro and Praia de São Julião. Wide and stunning, they are an ode to the Atlantic. The picturesque Praia do Matadouro and impressive Praia de São Lourenço also deserve a visit. And of course Praia do Sul, an Ericeira landmark. To recharge your batteries, the fine restaurants in Ericeira have the freshest fish possible.

short_description: At The Local Affair, we believe that every trip should be a deep, authentic connection. We specialize in creating unique, personalized experiences that immerse travelers in the true essence of Portugal.
full_description:
Rooted in local expertise and deep relationships, we unveil the country’s hidden gems, authentic traditions, and vibrant culture in a way only insiders can. Our name reflects our mission: to create a passionate “affair” between travelers and this remarkable country, through the eyes of those who know it best, Our Local heroes.
When you travel with us, you don’t just see Portugal, you feel it. As a Boutique Destination Management Company (DMC), we craft immersive experiences that go beyond sightseeing. From savoring local delicacies in family-run taverns to walking the cobblestone streets where history whispers, every experience is designed to connect you with the essence of this land.
Our secret lies in our Local Heroes: the people who breathe life into each experience. These are the chefs, artisans, historians, and adventurers who share their passion and craft, turning every moment into a story. They are the heartbeat of Portugal, and together with them, we offer more than just services, we offer real connections.
Through
The Local Affair
, you’ll fall in love with Portugal’s beauty, traditions, and vibrant soul, creating memories that stay with you forever. Let us, your dedicated Boutique DMC, guide you on a journey where every detail is infused with care, authenticity, and warmth.
Portugal is our Love story, let’s make it yours!

title: Fried Cuttlefish from Setúbal
short_description: A typical dish from the Lisbon region. Try fried cuttlefish on a trip to Setúbal, where this speciality was first invented and tastes best. It’s well worth the trip.
full_description:
The remains of a Neolithic settlement in Setúbal provide no clues as to whether the region’s most famous delicacy was enjoyed at the time.
Discover the traditional fried cuttlefish, served with chips and salad, to add even more flavour to days spent on the beach in Arrábida or in trips on the River Sado.
The truth, however, in this land of survivors of occupation and conflict is that the cuttlefish is often imported.
The local variety is too small and too scarce to satisfy the thousands of people who order this unique dish every day.
Fried in a thin batter, lemon juice should be squeezed onto the cuttlefish when it arrives at the table.
This is a regional speciality that must be tried when you visit this unique fishing town in Arrábida.

Positioned in 58th place in the prestigious Top 100 by Golf Magazine 2015, it ranks number one in Portugal.
The planning of the golf course involved the preservation of the delicate ecosystem of the dunes on which it sits.
The Oitavos Dunes is the first golf course in Europe and second in the world to get the Gold Signature Sanctuary status, quality certificate awarded by Audubon International, in recognition of the highest level of conservation.
All 18 holes of this field offer stunning views over the Atlantic Ocean and of Sintra.

title: National Pantheon
short_description: The National Pantheon – or church of Santa Engrácia – is a grand Baroque building housing the tombs of the country’s most distinguished figures.
full_description:
Come and discover the final resting place of Portugal’s most illustrious figures. Built in the 16th century as the church of Santa Engrácia by Princess Dona Maria, daughter of King Manuel I, the church never fulfilled its purpose.
It is thanks to her that the saying “like the construction of Santa Engrácia” came into being to denote something which is never completed. In fact, it was finished in the mid-20th century, 350 years later.
You’ll thus discover the legend of the secret love of Simão Pires and Violante, a novice from the neighbouring convent of Santa Clara.
It was the curse uttered by Simão that gave rise to the expression when unjustly burned alive for suspicion of stealing some relics when in fact he was just going to meet his lover.
Come and discover the grand stage for such a tragic love story, considered the most beautiful Baroque building in Lisbon.

Our team handles the full licensing process for public spaces and event permits, working directly with local authorities to ensure that every production complies with municipal and national regulations. Beyond permits and production, Picapps provides comprehensive ground support, supervising vendors, technical crews, and logistics partners to ensure smooth and efficient event delivery. To complement our operational services, we also offer digital tools for attendee registration and access control, integrating event apps that streamline participant management and communication.
Combining local know-how, regulatory expertise, and technology, Picapps delivers seamless event operations throughout Portugal — from licensing and planning to flawless on-site execution.

title: Hot Clube de Portugal
short_description: The oldest jazz club in Europe, working since 1948. DownBeat magazine, the renowned American jazz magazine, considers HOT CLUB to be one of the best 100 jazz clubs in the world.
full_description:
In 1945, jazz enthusiasts were already meeting at the Sangareau brothers' house, exchanging records, listening to what was new and playing. The group grew and organised, more or less informally, jazz sessions with Portuguese and foreign musicians who were passing through Lisbon and were invited to join in.
With the ground prepared to found a jazz club (since it had been gathering potential members for this purpose through a radio programme since 1948), this group, led by Luiz Villas-Boas, moved on to the next step: its formalisation. On 16 March 1950, the statutes of the Hot Clube de Portugal were approved with the slogan "Dissemination of Jazz Music".
From 1950 onwards, they organised Modern Music Festivals, concerts with famous musicians from Sidney Bechet to Count Basie, recordings and many Jam Sessions.
Today, the Hot Club is one of the oldest jazz clubs in Europe and is certainly the one that has been running uninterruptedly for the longest time.
DownBeat magazine, the renowned American jazz magazine, considers HOT CLUB to be one of the best 100 jazz clubs in the world.

title: Convent of Arrábida
short_description: Discover a unique convent in an amazing place close to Lisbon. In the middle of the Serra da Arrábida Nature Park, this convent could be taken from a fairytale.
full_description: When you visit Lisbon, set aside a day to explore the amazing Arrábida Nature Park. Besides the pretty hidden beaches and views of the stunning Sado estuary you’ll see as you climb the hills, the convent da Arrábida awaits, concealed by the vegetation, in a layout that’s unique anywhere in the country. The cells are spread across the slope and look like a village of hermits, a characteristic of this Franciscan convent founded in 1542 by Frei Martinho de Santa Maria. There are also cells dug into the rock at the top of the hill, beside the Hermitage of Memória, where the first four friars established themselves. Nowadays, the convent belongs to the Fundação do Oriente and you have to book a visit in advance, which are scheduled for Wednesdays, Saturdays and Sundays. Come and find pure spirituality in the midst of the Arrábida hills.

title: Santa Marta Lighthouse Museum
short_description: Discover Portugal’s lighthouses through this museum, which is both a museum and a working lighthouse.
full_description: The Santa Marta Lighthouse Museum, which is right next to Casa de Santa Maria, occupies the former fort and the lighthouse with the same name. The whole complex was converted into a leisure and cultural centre, although the lighthouse is still part of the coastal signalling system. The museum offers an overview of lighthouses in general, plus specific exhibits on such themes as lighthouses in Portugal and the work of the lighthouse keeper, amongst others. Visitors can also enjoy the documentary film: Lighthouses of Portugal. Five Centuries of History.
location: Rua do Farol de Santa Marta, 2750-341, Cascais

short_description: Dolphin watching center and nautical activities in Sesimbra, 30 minutes from Lisbon. Contact us for more information and explore the biodiverse waters and stunning landscapes of Sesimbra and Arrábida!
full_description:
For 15 years, Bolhas Tours has been the benchmark for adventure and nature connection in Sesimbra, providing unique experiences that bring together all the essential elements of this region.
Discovering the Earth, represented by the unique geology of the Arrábida coast with its caves, rock formations, and isolated bays.
The Air, felt in the gentle breezes that accompany the nautical expeditions for dolphin and whale watching, elevating the experience to new levels of serenity and admiration for marine life.
The Water, the central element of the activities, where participants have the opportunity to dive into the crystal-clear waters of the Professor Luiz Saldanha Marine Park and discover the marine biodiversity that inhabits the seas of Sesimbra.
And the Fire, represented by the enthusiasm and passion that our local guides dedicate to each adventure, turning simple outings into unforgettable memories.
Explore Bolhas Tours's programs to discover Sesimbra's secrets in comfort and safety with the best means, vessels, and guides in the region!

title: Town Museum - Cascais
short_description: This museum covers the history of the town from the Neolithic through to the early 20th Century.
full_description:
Cascais has a new museum that covers the history of the town and the wider municipality, from the Neolithic through to the early 20th Century.
The new Town Museum, which is divided into 5 display areas, boasts state-of-the-art visitor interaction technology.
The exhibits, which comprise some of the most iconic relics from the town’s past, illustrate the main events in the region’s history and prehistory.
The museum’s multimedia displays allow visitors to navigate interactively through Cascais’ story.

title: Roman Galleries
short_description: Come and discover the most ancient part of the city on a tour accompanied by highly knowledgeable guides able to explain Lisbon’s earlier lives.
full_description:
One of the most exclusive experiences you can have in Lisbon is a visit to the underground Roman galleries in the centre of the city.
Open for only one day a year, you have to book and reserve a place a long time in advance. These galleries are an impressive part of the life of the Roman city of Olisipo in the 1st century AD.
Although there are various explanations for their existence, it is now agreed that they were “cryptoporticos”, structures which the Romans used on uneven or difficult land to support large public buildings.
It is possible to infer that they were constructed during the era of the emperors Julius and Claudius and that these vaulted structures once supported a public monument.
Come and discover the most ancient part of the city on a tour accompanied by highly knowledgeable guides able to explain Lisbon’s earlier lives.
location: Rua da Prata / Rua da Conceição (nº 77), Lisboa

title: Fundação Inatel
short_description: The INATEL Foundation, a unique organization in Portuguese society, has the distinct mission to contribute to the integral well-being and personal development of each one, as well as for social inclusion of all citizens.
full_description:
The INATEL Foundation for its history and institutional nature, is a unique organization in Portuguese society that declares a distinct mission, vision and values, which aim to contribute to the integral well-being and personal development of each one, as well as for social inclusion of all citizens.
Created in 1935 as the Fundação Nacional para Alegria no Trabalho (FNAT), the INATEL Foundation, is currently under the supervision of the Ministry of Labor, Solidarity and Social Security, developing activities to enhance leisure time in the areas of social tourism, popular culture and amateur sport, with deep humanism concerns and high quality standards.
It is present throughout the country with a network of hotel units, the INATEL game park, several sports pavilions and the INATEL Teatro da Trindade, in Lisbon.
The INATEL Foundation's action involves an associative mass of around 250 thousand individual members and 3500 collective members (Culture and Sport Centers).
The INATEL Foundation is a reference organizer, throughout the national territory, in the activities of leisure and leisure activities for young people, workers, seniors, families and active support communities in all areas of its intervention.

title: D. Fernando II e Glória Frigate
short_description: The Frigate D. Fernando II e Glória is the last wooden sailing ship of the Portuguese Navy and the last “Nau” of the “Carreira da Índia”, the connection between Portugal and India.
full_description:
The Frigate D. Fernando II and Gloria was launched in the sea in 1843, being the last ship of the Portuguese Navy exclusively by sail and the last ship of the “Carreira da India” (the maritime connection between Lisbon and Goa, India).
Over 33 years, it has traveled more than 100,000 nautical miles, the equivalent of five rounds around the world. Its name is a tribute to the Queen of Portugal, D. Maria II, and her husband, D. Fernando II.
It set out on its maiden voyage in 1845 from Goa to Lisbon, arriving at the Tagus River on the 4th of July. It made its last voyage in 1878, when it turned into the Naval Artillery School until 1938. In 1963, the history of the Frigate was marked by a great fire, which left it almost submerged in the Tagus River until 1992. At that time, it began a huge restoration work, which ended in the opening to the public as a museum at EXPO 98.
Built entirely of wood, it is a unique example in the world. Climb aboard, take a trip through history and feel like a true sailor, facing the sea fearlessly.
Come discover what life in the 14th century aboard was like.

title: St. Pedro’s Church
short_description: This 16th Century church is lined with 18th Century Baroque tile panels depicting the life of St. Peter.
full_description: St. Pedro’s, the parish church of Palmela, was built in the 16th Century. Its three naves are flanked by Tuscan columns and the inside of the church is lined with 18th Century Baroque tile panels depicting scenes from the life of St. Peter. The church also houses a number of important sculptures and 18th Century paintings.

title: Montebelo Vista Alegre Lisboa Chiado Hotel
short_description: Welcome to Montebelo Vista Alegre Lisboa Chiado Hotel, Vista Alegre’s new hotel in the centre of Lisbon. The hotel is just a five-minute walk from the Tagus River and very close to some of the city’s main attractions.
full_description:
A meticulously restored historic building, the hotel’s reception area introduces the theme of design, with a large porcelain display complemented by two original Vista Alegre lamps.
The staircase invites everyone to carry on exploring and is topped off by a skylight that floods the interior with Lisbon’s famous light.
Inspired by the brand’s artistic and artisanal tradition, Vista Alegre’s artisans have hand-painted new original works from existing porcelain art pieces, creating a unique environment with a touch of fantasy.
The same design approach gives the 58 rooms and suites a personality all of their own. Each of the hotel’s three wings features a distinct theme. The rooms facing Rua das Flores, the rooms are decorated in floral motifs. On Rua do Alecrim, each room has a smell of rosemary, complemented with other natural elements. The rooms facing the larger Largo do Barão de Quintela are decorated with Vista Alegre biscuit porcelain pieces, with the suite retaining the original ornate stucco ceilings.
The Montebelo Vista Alegre Lisboa Chiado Hotel is a haven to enjoy the best that the city of Lisbon has to offer. Surrounded to art galleries, centenary cafés, museums, theatres, bars, restaurants and stores, its location is perfect to start your journey to Lisbon’s most iconic shopping and entertaining areas.
A place where art, design and hospitality come together to create a truly unique experience in Lisbon’s historic centre.

title: Loures Municipal Museum - Quinta do Conventinho
short_description: This little museum in Loures, just outside the gates of Lisbon, guards the memories, habits and tools of the region’s rural and rustic way of life.
full_description:
This little museum in Loures, just outside the gates of Lisbon, guards the memories, habits and tools of the region’s rural and rustic way of life.
In an eclectic collection mixing ethnography, archaeology and art, the museum shows what life used to be like in this part of Lisbon in the 19th century.
The building and grounds belonged to an order of monks until 1834, when all religious orders were abolished in Portugal.
It was bought by Costa Cabral, a minister to Queen Maria II, who preserved the original religious and farming aspect.
Visit the chapel of the Invocação do Espírito Santo with its unique canvas by Bento da Silveira entitled “Pentecostes”.
Also take the chance to discover the richly decorative azulejo tiles in the cloisters.
An alternative attraction very close to Lisbon.
location: Estrada Nacional 8, KM 4,3, 2660-346, Stº. António dos Cavaleiros | Loures

title: Castelo de Palmela
short_description: It’s your turn to conquer this monumental castle in the Lisbon region. Take a trip to Palmela to get to know the area and the Arrábida hills which surround it.
full_description: Palmela castle is one of the most impressive sights in the Lisbon region. Occupied, conquered, lost and regained, it is a faithful testament to the battles waged during the first royal dynasty (1143 to 1383) to establish the territory of Portugal. Erected at a uniquely strategic point, between the Tagus and Sado rivers, it was founded by the Romans, probably in 310 BC. But the first fortifications were built by the Moors from the 8th to 9th centuries. The castle was successively added to: the primitive round towers by the Romans; the square towers by the Moors; the entrance tower by the Master of Avis; and the cannon fortifications by Pedro II. It has a great view of the Arrábida hills and Sado estuary. It also houses two churches, one lined with 17th-century tiles and a set of 16th-century graves. Come and visit for its view and unique location.

title: Carcavelos Beach Hotel
short_description: The hotel is absolutely perfect if you want to stay near the city of Lisbon and simultaneously on a quiet beach with a wonderful panorama at the same time.
full_description:
Lisbon by the Sea...
Set between Lisbon and Cascais, and quietly located facing the largest sandy beach of the Lisbon Estoril shoreline, Carcavelos Beach Hotel, timeless and contemporary, is the first city hotel by the Atlantic Ocean.
The hotel is absolutely perfect if you want to stay near the city of Lisbon and simultaneously on a quiet beach with a wonderful panorama at the same time.
The interior design of this beach hotel, concept of the late 60s, offers the best that modern basic European interior design can achieve, and provides the ideal atmosphere for a leisurely stay, rest or work. Pure tranquility and seaside escape.
Comfortable and cozy rooms and suites, with a simple and basic decoration that fits well with the relaxing surroundings of the hotel.
75 rooms with private balconies providing lovely sea views, specially from the upper floors - as the beach is only around 100 yards away, 73 rooms to the back overlooking the Carcavelos and the Sintra hills, 3 junior suites with amazing views of the Lisbon Coast, 3 superior suites at the top of the building with private terraces providing a truly gorgeous and unforgettable panorama over the whole Lisbon Estoril Coast, from the Tagus Estuary with the old Lisbon Bridge, through the Caparica beaches into the Cascais Bay.
Contemporary furnishings and old wardrobe from the 60s. Jacuzzi in the superior suites. Connecting rooms available.

title: Hotel A. S. Lisboa
short_description: Hotel AS Lisboa invites you to visit Lisbon and its charms. We restyled some areas focusing in portugueses culture and tradition always thinking in our guests. We are waiting for you!
full_description:
Hotel AS Lisboa invites you to visit Lisbon and its charms. Immerse yourself in the mystique of the city in one of the most emblematic areas of the capital, Alameda D. Afonso Henriques.

Through room decor that centers on Portuguese culture and literature.
Topics such as the Popular Saints, Galo de Barcelos, Florbela Espanca or Fernando Pessoa evidence in some rooms.

short_description: Manzwine’s values are quality, recuperation, and revitalization. Manzwine is today a growing national brand in Portugal. Internationally, it's an example of what is best made here, exporting a good part of our production to more than 30 countries.
full_description:
Manzwine’s values are quality, recuperation, and revitalization.
All the history of past years, which conquered us and made us look at this place in a special way, led us to project onto the wines everything we were already doing, and everything we were learning.
Our concept is therefore close to a wine boutique, a premium quality product, delicate, careful, rich in aromas and velvety in flavor.
Today we have 14 different labels, with a modern image, with the marks of the history that influences us.
Manzwine is today a growing national brand in Portugal. Internationally, we are an example of what is best made here, exporting a good part of our production to more than 30 countries.

title: Jerónimos Monastery
short_description: The Jerónimos Monastery was proclaimed a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 1983. This notable 16th century work of architecture became part of Portuguese identity and culture.
full_description:
The Jerónimos Monastery is a National Monument and was classified a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 1983.
King Manuel I built a large monastery near the location where the Infante D. Henrique ordered a church to be built in the mid-15th century by invocation of St. Mary of Belém.
To immortalise the memory of the Infante, for his intense devotion to Our Lady and faith in St. Jerome, in 1496 King Manuel I decided to found the Monastery of St. Mary of Belém, near the city of Lisbon, next to the Tagus River.
Donated to the monks of the Order of St. Jerome, today it is commonly known as the Jerónimos Monastery.
A pantheon of the Avis-Beja Dynasty, in the 19th century the church became the sepulchre for heroes and poets: Vasco da Gama and Luís de Camões.
A notable work of architecture, it became part of Portuguese identity and culture.

title: Pestana Alfama
short_description: Welcome to the genuine heart of Lisbon! The Pousada Alfama came about from the restoration of a historic building that exudes authenticity while combining the traditional with the contemporary.
full_description:
The Pousada Alfama came about from the restoration of a historic building that exudes authenticity while combining the traditional with the contemporary. Right at the entrance you are welcomed by a work of urban art by the artist Bordallo II, ushering you into what will be a unique and innovative experience right in the heart of the Portuguese capital.
This hotel in Alfama is spread over 4 floors and has 43 rooms of different types, all of which offer you the comfort needed for spending unforgettable days in Lisbon. The hotel is located in a pedestrianised area dominated by Lisbon’s most typical and original delights, and from here, you can experience the city’s most breathtaking sunset.
The prominent location of this hotel in the historic centre of Lisbon puts it right opposite the viewing point of the Portas do Sol. This name comes from the old Porta do Sol, one of Lisbon’s medieval gates, which was part of the city’s Cerca Moura, destroyed in the 1755 earthquake.
The essence of Alfama is fado, popular saints and tradition and it’s also an excellent starting point for getting to know the authentic heart of Lisbon, its charming streets with distinctive Portuguese paving, the traditional courtyards and the famous number 28 tram. From the Pousada Alfama, you can easily reach, on foot, Lisbon’s Cathedral, St. George’s Castle, the Santa Justa lift and other famous Lisbon landmarks, such as the Praça do Comércio or the Baixa Pombalina.

title: Hyatt Regency Lisbon
short_description: Hyatt Regency Lisbon is the first brand hotel in Portugal. This 5-star superior hotel has 204 upscale accommodation units with a contemporary design, in a prime location with breathtaking views of the Tejo River.
full_description:
Hyatt Regency Lisbon is the first brand hotel in Portugal. This 5-star superior hotel has 204 upscale accommodation units with a contemporary design, in a prime location with breathtaking views of the Tejo River.
It is perfect for enjoying the magnificent city of Lisbon, its gastronomy, history and culture – it is surrounded by the most relevant museums and monuments of the city, such as the Hieronymites Monastery, Belém Tower, Belém Palace or Cordoaria National Factory.
The hotel has direct pedestrian access from the riverside via a bridge and offers a world-class wellbeing concept for the ultimate in luxury living, just 5-minute drive from Lisbon’s historic downtown, where day and night life merges.
The hotel has a 1000m2 spa managed by the award-winning brand
Serenity – The Art of Well Being
, a state-of-the-art gym and offers personalised health and wellness programs.
Guests and visitors can enjoy a stunning view of the Tejo River from the hotel’s rooftop and the delights of a top restaurant.
The hotel also features a luxury shopping area, meeting rooms and a car park.

title: Church of the Antigo Mosteiro de Jesus
short_description: Take a trip five centuries back in time 30 km outside Lisbon. The church of the Antigo Mosteiro de Jesus is an old and impressive building that you should visit.
full_description:
Discover one of the first churches built in the Manueline style in Lisbon that curiously isn’t in the city.
In Setúbal, just a short drive from the capital, you’ll find the most impressive building in the city where the Treaty of Tordesilhas was ratified.
Designed by Diogo Boitaca in 1494, it was completed in 1500 and later renovated and extended on various occasions.
The impressive Manueline vault in the choir, as well as the arches made of Arrábida stone, unique to Portugal, make a visit to this church, which is in excellent condition, worthwhile.
The highlights, however, are the eighteen azulejo panels of the 16th and 17th centuries, depicting scenes from the life of the Virgin, the gilded woodwork on the high altar, the Chapter House and the Sacristy.
Travel five centuries in time in under 30 km.
location: Convento de Jesus - Largo de Jesus, 2900-499, Setúbal

title: Famous Crows Guesthouse
short_description: The Famous Crows Guest House is a friendly and welcoming, ready to be sent to a relaxing environment. Most of the space is a central location that allows travelers to easily visit the city and surrounding área.
full_description:
The Famous Crows GuestHouse is located in Belém, a cultural and tourist site. It is close to the Jeronimos Monastery, National Museum of Cars, CCB, Maritime Museum, Berardo Collection Museum, Tropical Botanical Garden, Ajuda Palace, Discovery Monument, Belém Tower and also near the Tagus River where you can walk and see the 25th April Bridge and on the other side the Monument of Christ the King.
Public transport is excellent, with trams, big tour buses, buses and taxis. It is close to the Belém Ferry Station, where you can reach the beaches of the Costa da Caparica and also the train station of Belém, connecting Estoril and Cascais on one side and Lisbon on the other.

title: Intercontinental Lisbon
short_description: Discover InterContinental Lisbon, a 5-star luxury hotel by Parque Eduardo VII, offering elegant rooms, Tejo views, the Akla restaurant, Uptown Bar, and exclusive event spaces.
full_description:
InterContinental Lisbon
is a five-star luxury hotel that embodies contemporary elegance and the refined hospitality of Portugal’s capital. Ideally located next to Eduardo VII Park and just minutes from Avenida da Liberdade, it offers a privileged base from which to discover Lisbon’s cultural, leisure and shopping highlights.
The hotel features spacious and light-filled rooms and suites, many offering views over the city and the Tagus River, combining comfort, refined design and meticulous attention to detail for a truly serene and sophisticated stay.
The gastronomic experience stands out at Akla Restaurant, where Portuguese cuisine is creatively reinterpreted with international influences, while the Uptown Bar invites guests to relax in an elegant and welcoming atmosphere.
With versatile event spaces and state-of-the-art technology, InterContinental Lisbon is also a benchmark venue for meetings, conferences and exclusive celebrations, always delivering a highly personalised service.

We specialize in creating meaningful, authentic, and seamless events and retreats, carefully tailored to reflect your group or company culture and objectives. full_description:

Whether you’ve rented a house and are looking for the best food possible or just want to discover the taste of the local fruit, the Mercado Biológico Agrobio de Lisboa, in Campo Pequeno, sells various types of produce by different certified Portuguese farmers.
You’ll find vegetables, aromatic plants, fruit, legumes and even essential oils and cosmetics essential for a more sustainable and healthy life.
Every Saturday, in the garden of Campo Pequeno, you’ll find stalls full of the authentic, certified and organic flavours of Lisbon – and Portugal.
