title: Escape Hunt Lisboa
short_description: Feel like a real Sherlock Holmes. Do you have what it takes to get out? You have 60 minutes...the clock is ticking!
full_description: Imagine a seemingly normal room. Now imagine you and your friends locked inside that room. From the moment you walk in you sense there is something eerie in the air, drawers you can't open, weird objects lying around... that's until someone finds a key and you realise you can use it to open a box. This will reveal a code that will allow you to solve a riddle and open a drawer that you thought was irrelevant or unimportant. You will then be immersed in clues and puzzles that will make you feel like a real Sherlock Holmes. Do you have what it takes to get out? You have 60 minutes... the clock is ticking!

title: Lost Inn Lisbon Hostel
short_description: Lost Inn Lisbon Hostel is a cozy Hostel, located in the historical district of Chiado, five minutes walk from the Metro and Train Station of Cais do Sodré and Chiado.
full_description:
Lost Inn Lisbon Hostel is a cozy Hostel, located in the historical district of Chiado, five minutes walk from the Metro and Train Station of Cais do Sodré and Chiado, near the Pink Street and the best fashion bars, clubs and restaurants in town.
Lost Inn Lisbon Hostel is housed on a renewed eighteenth century building keeping its vaulted ceilings, époque tiles and wooden floors. It is full equipped and decorated in a casual chic decor, offering a comfortable and high quality stay.
Our staff will always welcome you with a smile and is available to give all the informations concerning Restaurants, Museums, Tours, Historical Places, Bars, Clubs and so on. We also offer free Lisbon maps and partnerships for city walking tours and pub crawls.

title: Hotel Mundial - Rooftop
short_description: Discover the castle, the river and the city’s rooftops with a glass in your hand. This is a pretty and lively place whose terrace has a special atmosphere.
full_description:
The Hotel Mundial is in one of the liveliest and most multicultural squares in the heart of Lisbon – Martim Moniz.
Whether you prefer to discover the Baixa Pombalina or climb up to the castle, this is the ideal place to start exploring.
But it also has one of the best secrets in the capital: the Rooftop Bar. This bar is at the top of the hotel and has an amazing view of the Castelo de São Jorge, the river and the rooftops and buildings in Baixa.
The panoramic lounge has a modern atmosphere and its sunset parties are famous amongst locals in the summer.
Besides snacks and drinks, enjoy the moment to admire the city skyline and do a bit of people watching.
After all, the view is incredible for many reasons at this special place.

title: The Lingerie Restaurant
short_description: The Lingerie Restaurant is the first erotic chain of restaurants in Portugal! The concept rests in a mix of high-quality aphrodisiac cuisine, glamorous environment, lively and tasteful entertainment.
full_description:
The Lingerie Restaurant is the first erotic chain of restaurants in Portugal! The Lingerie Restaurant open in 2004 brings a unique concept.
The concept rests in a mix of high-quality aphrodisiac cuisine, glamorous environment, lively and tasteful entertainment.
Additionally (and aptly explaining the restaurant chains' name) the restaurant's waiters and waitresses wear only the most sexy lingerie!
location: Avenida António Augusto de Aguiar, 88A, 1050-018, Lisboa

title: As Janelas Verdes
short_description: As Janelas Verdes is a small charming Boutique hotel in the historic center of Lisbon.
full_description:
This small 18th century palace is next to the National Art Museum in the romantic Rua das Janelas Verdes.
Eça de Queirós, the famous Portuguese novelist, is connected to the hotel by tradition and there are those who say that he lived and found inspiration here in this palace for another magical home - “O Ramalhete” in his novel Os Maias.
The memory of his presence is still very much alive in the atmosphere we find ourselves immersed in: small objets d’art, books, paintings and mementoes take us back to another era now being relived.
The house like the street of the same name, deserves to be discovered slowly, little by little, all the time savouring its gentle, romantic, welcoming atmosphere.
The rooms are sunny and cheerful. The Tagus is close by. One feels good here… When Spring arrives, the days begin with a wonderful breakfast served in the garden while you breathe in the calm of an older Lisbon where it still feels good to feel love.

title: Hotel Vila Galé Cascais
short_description: Set in the town’s historic center with a view over the sea.
full_description:
The Vila Galé Cascais hotel is located over the sea and a few meters from the renowned Boca do Inferno, the Cascais Marina and the town’s historic center. Having been fully renovated in 2009, this hotel in Cascais features 233 rooms and suites, all of them being spacious and many having breath-taking views of the Atlantic Ocean.
The hotel also offers a restaurant with a show cooking station, two bars, event rooms with natural sunlight, a Health Club where you can indulge in massages and aesthetic treatments, as well as free wi-fi access in all areas. In the outdoor area, make sure you enjoy the gardens, the swimming pool and, for the youngest, the playground.
We also recommend a visit to the Paula Rêgo Casa das Histórias, a walk along the marina or a visit to Boca do Inferno.
Make sure you don’t miss the Guincho beach either. And sports fans will have plenty to keep them busy as they can play golf or surf, bodyboard and kite surf nearby.

title: Interpretative Center of the History of Cod
short_description: Interpretative Center of the History of Cod - It is a space to pay homage to codfish, as a symbol of gastronomy, culture and the history of a Country.
full_description:
This Portuguese thing about traveling to the end of the World to get lunch every day is yet to be explained. It remains to be explained how countless generations of Portuguese people risked and lost their lives in the most inhospitable seas in the world just to make onion’s stew.
Fish from distant Newfoundland and Greenland has long been part of our cuisine but, above all, of our popular culture.
It is a space to pay homage to codfish, as a symbol of gastronomy, culture and the history of a Country that has long thought globally.
It will remind generations of sailors and fishermen and explain the way of being of the Portuguese people always ready to offer what they have and to travel to the end of the world to seek what they lack.
Throughout several exhibition rooms, visitors can watch the beginning of the odyssey of those who launched themselves in the 'seas of the end of the world', discover how the myth of the 'faithful friend' at the table was born, see the future of codfish, the consumption, fishing and to learn new ways of cooking it.
location: Terreiro do Paço - Torreão Nascente, 1100-148, Lisboa

Within walking distance of Lisbon's historic centre, close to Marques de Pombal Square, this hotel provides guests with a cosy base during their stay.

full_description:
Opened in 2000, designed by Donald Steel and built to exacting European Tour recommendations, Aroeira II compliments its older sister well.
Visitors will enjoy the distinct tranquil and serene Aroeira environment but will discover a course that possesses a few more thrills and spills along the way.
At more than 7,000 yards from the back tees,
PGA Aroeira N.2
bares its teeth with water in play on no fewer than nine holes and large, undulating greens testing the putter to the maximum.
It has hosted the Ladies European Tour Qualifying School and several ladies Portuguese Opens.

title: Solar do Castelo
short_description: Solar do Castelo is truly a Romantic Boutique hotel in the historic center of Lisbon.
full_description:
The only hotel within St. George's Castle walls, at the top of Alfama, Solar do Castelo is a small Mansion built on the site of the former Alcaçova Palace.
With two floors and a mansard, an inner courtyard and garden, most part of the furniture was specially designed for the hotel in a Medieval Contemporary style.
You will find welcoming rooms offering complete modern day comfort, such as free wireless access.
During the second half of the XVIII century a mansion was built within St. Jorge's Castle walls on the site of the former Alcáçova Palace kitchens. That's why it is still known as the "Palacete das Cozinhas" (Kitchen Mansion).
Along with two floors and a mansard, an inner courtyard and gardens, the Palacete das Cozinhas possesses some Medieval remains - such as the cistern - which are thought to have formed part of some of the original Palace's outbuildings.
The mansion combines elements of the rationalist Pombal style of building - the sober frames around the windows and the main gateway - with others that expressed a greater freedom, such as the elaborate design that surmounts the wall attached to the gateway in the main façade, on which we can read " 1765 " - the probable date of its construction.
The mansion, which is classified as an Historic Building, has now been transformed into a hotel under the name Solar do Castelo and has been brought back to life by a high-quality contemporary design.

title: Portugal Jewels
short_description: Portugal Jewels was born from the desire to promote Portuguese jewelry, recovering traditional models and designs, with a contemporary approach.
full_description:
It all began when our grandfather, José, established a traditional jewelry workshop. Little did we know that this beginning would shape the course of our family's history.
Years passed, and our mother, Rosa Amélia, became one of the few women in jewelry distribution. Her pioneering spirit deepened our family's love for jewelry-making, evolving it into a cherished legacy. In 2017, we, Joana and Alexandre, the third generation, embraced this legacy, bringing Portugal Jewels to life.
Our passion for preserving Portuguese traditions inspired us to infuse traditional jewelry design with a modern touch. At Portugal Jewels, our purpose is simple: to create jewelry that embodies the essence of Portugal. Each piece tells a story, from the symbolism it holds to the expert craftsmanship behind it.
We believe our jewelry allows you to wear history and make your own. Each piece captures moments and emotions, becoming a part of your unique story.
Join us on this journey to discover the essence of Portuguese jewelry, where each piece is a timeless treasure.

title: National Coach Museum
short_description: The museum houses an unique collection in the world consisting of vehicles from the 17th, 18th and 19th centuries including coaches, berlins, sedan chairs and carriages.
full_description:
Virtual Tour -
https://my.matterport.com/show/?m=crADZwGeEXF
The National Coach Museum opened at the old riding ring of the Palace of Belém on the 23rd of May, 1905.
It was created by Queen D. Amélia of Orleães and Bragança (the wife of king D. Carlos) as a way to preserve the important collection of vehicles belonging to the Royal House.
The museum houses a unique collection in the world consisting of vehicles from the 17th, 18th and 19th centuries including coaches, berlins, sedan chairs and carriages – all of which are decorated in a rich and profuse style.
This makes it the most visited museum in Lisbon, if not in the whole of Portugal.
To commemorate its 110th anniversary, the museum was transferred to a brand new building designed by the Brazilian architect Paulo Mendes da Rocha.
The New Coach Museum, inaugurated on the 23rd of May 2015, is yet another reason to visit this wonderful collection.

title: Lisbon Cruise Port
short_description: Lisbon Cruise Port, the entity that operates in two passengers terminals - Santa Apolónia and Jardim do Tabaco -, is held by Global Ports Holding, Grupo Sousa, Rotal Caribbean Cruises and Creuers del Port de Barcelona.
full_description:
Lisbon Cruise Port, held by Global Ports Holding, Grupo Sousa, Royal Caribbean Cruises and Creuers del Port de Barcelona, ​​operates in two Lisbon passenger terminals: Santa Apolónia and Jardim do Tabaco.
The new terminal, inaugurated in 2017, designed with flexible features, offers generous areas for boarding and arrivals, duty-free shops, spaces for indoor and outdoor events and a terrace with 360º panoramic view.
Passengers can find support, customer service and purchase products and services at the Guest Information Center. Those in transit and turnaround will find connections to diverse destinations and access to the numerous attractions of a welcoming city, within walking distance of the terminals.
Lisbon, a captivating port of call for cruise lines, attracts travelers who, upon stepping into its welcoming port area, are embraced by a tapestry of old-world charm and vibrant modernity.
The first impression is a charming mix of cobblestone streets, colorful facades and ornate architecture. With stunning views, Lisbon enchants visitors with its beauty and historicity, the perfect setting for a remarkable cultural experience.
The Port of Lisbon and Lisbon Cruise Port are actively committed to sustainability, implementing, in particular, innovative projects and methodologies that aim to reduce their environmental impact and enhance the positive passenger experience.
location: Doca Jardim do Tabaco Terminal de Cruzeiros de Lisboa, Av. Infante Dom Henrique, 1100-651, Lisboa

title: Air Museum
short_description: You can always count on the Portuguese to find new ways to leave their little rectangle beside the sea. Come and discover the history of Portuguese aviation.
full_description:
Discover the history of aviation in Portugal at the Museu do Ar, near Sintra. Long before Lisbon airport’s mythical landings with views of the river, bridge and city, Portuguese aviators were successfully crossing the Southern Atlantic by celestial navigation alone.
But the great adventure of Portuguese aviation is not just about Sacadura Cabral and Gago Coutinho.
The museum also contains historic planes, the history of TAP and the ANA collection, alongside helicopters, engines, simulators, propellers and much more.
The Pioneers Room is devoted to the great feats of Portugal’s aviators. If you have time and are an aficionado, the Alverca and Ovar sections are also worth seeing.
location: Granja do Marquês, 2715-021, Pêro Pinheiro | Sintra

title: Golf do Estoril
short_description: It's one of the oldest and most emblematic clubs in Portugal. It's a challenging golf course in terms of accuracy rather than distance, demanding excellent tactics.
full_description:
Estoril Golf Course is 25 minutes away from Lisbon and just 3 from restaurants, hotels, casino and Estoril beach.
The Estoril Championship Course started in 1929 and redesigned in 1936 by Mackenzie Ross. It is known as one of the oldest and most emblematic clubs in Portugal and where two of the Portuguese most important tournaments have been held frequently: the Portuguese Open 20 years and the Portuguese International Championship Amateur 63 years.
A long list of famous golfers have played Estoril including Ballesteros, Montgomerie, Sam Torrance, Canizares, Gene Sarazen, Peter Allis and Bernard Gallacher.
Since the opening of the Estoril Golf Course in 1936, a number of alterations and improvements have been made to its original layout.
Overlooking Estoril and the sea, the 5262 meter long course is set amongst eucalyptus, pine and mimosa trees, giving it a uniquely colourful touch.
The architect, Mackenzie Ross, and the successive green keepers have made excellent use of these various trees which, together with the uneven terrain, make the Estoril Golf Course challenging in terms of accuracy rather than distance. The Estoril Golf Course demands excellent tactics.

title: Museu Medeiros e Almeida
short_description: Discover this house museum off the main tourism routes in Lisbon and enjoy the impressive collection assembled by Medeiros e Almeida throughout his life.
full_description:
This unique museum in Lisbon contains a remarkable art collection assembled over many years.
António de Medeiros e Almeida was a businessman who found in art a passion which he would enjoy throughout his life.
He bought the house where his unique collection was stored, which was built in 1896 and once belonged to the Vatican, in 1943.
The collection was begun for the personal and social decoration of the house, but it grew exponentially with the explosion of unique artworks that came onto the market in the post-war period.
Over 2,000 works are displayed, from Chinese terracotta to porcelain, Portuguese and French furniture, Dutch and Flemish paintings, watches and jewellery.
With no heir, he created a foundation to maintain and preserve the house and collection.

title: Holiday Inn Express Lisbon - Av. Liberdade
short_description: The Holiday Inn Express Lisboa - Av. Liberdade is a comfortable accommodation choice set right on the Lisbon city centre.
full_description:
The Holiday Inn Express Lisboa - Av. Liberdade is a comfortable accommodation choice set right on the Lisbon city centre. Liberdade Avenue is a 4-minute walk and is home to some of the trendiest shops and fashion houses in the city.
All air-conditioned rooms are non-smoking and include free WiFi access, flat-screen cable TV, private bathroom and an electric kettle. Complementary coffee and tea is provided.
A varied breakfast buffet is available each day at the Holiday Inn Express. For more meal options, guests can stroll through the Liberdade Avenue, which features a variety of restaurants and cafés serving traditional Portuguese meals and international dishes.
Guests are welcome to relax in the lounge bar, where refreshing drinks are served.
The Marquês de Pombal Square is a 5-minute walk from the hotel. Lisbon’s nightlife in the Bairro Alto is a 5-minute drive away, with all its bars and cafés. The Chiado is a 7-minute drive and features plenty of local cafés and shops for guests to browse. Another nearby historical area is the Rossio, just a 4-minute drive away. The Marquês de Pombal Metro Station is 350 metres from the property and connects with most of the Lisbon area. Other nearby stations include the Rato, at 500 metres and Avenida, at 600 metres’ distance. The Lisbon International Airport is a 13-minute drive from the Holiday Inn Express Lisboa - Av. Liberdade.
An airport shuttle is available, at a surcharge.
Santo Antonio is a great choice for travellers interested in Gourmet Food, History and Monuments.
We speak your language!

short_description: Hotel Lisboa Plaza is a charming Boutique hotel in the historic center of Lisbon
full_description:
Located close to Avenida da Liberdade, the Plaza is a charmer.
Family owned and operated since its construction, the atmosphere of informal good taste and the constant updating of services provided making this charming hotel your best friend's home in Lisbon.
Opened at the beginning of the 1950's, this hotel is one in which the architect developed a concept of modernity through, as he himself said, "practical solutions, which promote a sense of well-being".
The family who own the hotel continued the architect's dream and over the years have created an atmosphere of informal good taste, which makes us feel well.
The beauty of the décor created by Graça Viterbo, one of the most famous Portuguese interior designers, the constant updating of services provided and the attention paid to even the smallest detail make this charming hotel one of the most character in Lisbon.

title: Beaches of Costa da Caparica
short_description: Visit Lisbon’s most popular beaches, whether in summer or winter, and you’ll find places to suit every taste and temperament along this sun and sand-filled coast.
full_description: Just a few minutes from Lisbon are the 15 km of beaches at Costa da Caparica. It’s very easy to get there by car, crossing the bridge or catching one of the buses from Praça de Espanha or Campo Grande. And when you get here, the best thing is probably to jump on the mini-train that stops at all the beaches from the centre going south, which vary in mood and crowd. The beaches in the centre are the busiest, while those further away are popular with those who want to spend the whole day on the sand or sun loungers. Going north, you’ll find the calmer S. João beach. All the beaches share the fact of having excellent bars and restaurants where you can order a jar of sangria, grilled fish and a plate of clams. See if the beach doesn’t taste better that way.

title: Quinta Real de Caxias
short_description: Elegantly Baroque, the Quinta Real de Caxias is a prime example of 18th and 19th-century aristocratic tastes. The house is stunning, but it’s the gardens that impress the most.
full_description: The Quinta Real de Caxias is one of the most important buildings in Oeiras. This wonderful palace was built in the 18th and 19th centuries by the Casa do Infantado. Baroque in design inspired by the French style of the era, it chiefly stands out for its unique gardens. Sophisticated, elegant and planned down to the last detail, the high point is the waterfall, with levels arranged like a throne. But the ponds, flowerbeds, viewing point and sculptures by Machado de Castro also contribute to the feeling of total geometry inspired by Versailles and show how the court entertained itself in the 18th century. Calm and splendorous, this is an obligatory port of call when visiting the area of Oeiras.

title: Miradouro da Graça
short_description: Discover one of the best views of Lisbon. Sitting at the top of one of the city’s famous hills, the view lets you write the history of Lisbon through the naked eye.
full_description: The ideal place to combine two of the things you can’t miss when in Lisbon: riding on the no. 28 tram to Graça and enjoying the view at the Miradouro da Graça. The shade of the church and its pine trees give it a certain air of spiritual refuge for those who take a rest at the café or on the walls which surround it. Located on the hill next to that of the castle, the view of Lisbon is quite simply stunning. The city falls away at your feet, the daily life of the historic neighbourhoods goes about its business, the Tagus flows in the background and the sound seems to get lost at this tranquil spot so popular with those who like to discover Lisbon on foot.

title: Darwin's Café
short_description: Darwin's Café offers a gastronomic experience with a fusion of Italian and Portuguese food.
full_description:
Darwin’s Café benefits from its stunning location on the riverfront and combines architecture and design with a diversified menu.
The design of Darwin’s Café takes all those who enter it to the world of the British naturalist and biologist to whom the Champalimaud Centre for the Unknown’s restaurant owes its name. It results from a partnership between the Foundation and the company Lanidor, and its location makes it one of the most attractive places in the city.
The furniture’s antique touch coexists with the space’s modernity, creating an original atmosphere. The windows, facing the river, draw guests into contemplation, and the menu, by chef António Runa, entices them to taste the dishes served here.
The terrace, with its spectacular view, completes the space, which has already become one of the country’s best restaurants.
location: Av. Brasília, Champalimaud Center for the Unknown, 1400-038 , Lisboa

title: Algés Seafront Walk
short_description: Enjoy Lisbon and the River Tagus in all their glory in one place. With a view of two stunning buildings and access to the pure sea air, this walk is ideal for... walking.
full_description: The Passeio Marítimo de Algés is the stage for festivals that bring tens of thousands of people to this spot. It is one of the best places in Lisbon for sport, mainly running, cycling and skateboarding. With the river by your side, this area offers a unique view of the estuary and city, as well as several remarkable buildings, such as the Port of Lisbon control tower. If you want to continue walking, you have access to the Jamor area, dedicated to professional and amateur sport, where you can find the high performance athletes centre. Come and explore this unique part of Lisbon.

title: Loures
short_description: LOURES – an incredible tourism area! The link between the saloia’s heritage, the landscape and built patrimony, festivities and leisure make Loures an irresistible destination.
full_description:
LOURES – an incredible tourism area!
Because of its essentially rural past and a present dominated by the economic development, demographic growth and the upgrading of the territory, Loures is nowadays a land of contrasts.
The eastern zone is characterized by urban and industrial landscapes outlining the modern road network.
While villages and rural towns, land and natural green areas paint, in other colours, the north part.
location: Estrada Nacional 8, Parque da Cidade, Pavilhão de Macau, Parque da Cidade de Loures, 2674-501, Loures

title: The Vintage Lisbon Hotel
short_description: With an excellent location, The Vintage Lisboa combines the comfort and the luxury of a 5 star hotel with Spa, only 300 meters from the famous Avenida da Liberdade
full_description:
The location of Vintage Lisboa is perfect for visitors travelling to Lisbon on leisure or business. This boutique hotel is in the center of all that is a must of the Lisbon city, a 300 meters from the Liberdade Avenue with monuments, theatres, stores, bars and restaurants.
With an excellent location, it combines the comfort and the luxury that only one five-star hotel can offer. The hotel features 56 rooms, a Spa, an indoor heated swimming pool, exercise room, event rooms, parking and free wi-fi throughout the hotel.
The hotel's rooftop lounge bar enjoys a breathtaking view for the Castle of S. Jorge and The Vintage Restaurant & Bar is one of the favorite spots of the Portuguese cuisine, with a range of traditional food and wine selection.

title: Hotel Real Oeiras
short_description: Located next to the biggest business centre in the country, Real Oeiras has everything you need for your trip: 100 rooms, 8 meeting rooms with capacity for 380 people, a restaurant, a Health Club and an outdoor pool.
full_description:
The Hotel Real Oeiras is a 4-star hotel with a privileged location on the Estoril Coast, between Lisbon, Oeiras and Cascais, close to the business centres of Quinta da Fonte, Tagus Park and Lagoas Park.
The Hotel has 97 rooms and 3 suites, 8 multifunctional meeting rooms with capacity for up to 380 people, free Wi-Fi internet connection, business center and a restaurant serving traditional Portuguese cuisine, the Real Restaurante-Bar.
In the Hotel you can enjoy the green spaces surrounding the outdoor pool and take care of your physical well-being in the Health Club and Turkish bath.
With a relaxed and welcoming atmosphere, the Hotel Real Oeiras is the ideal option for any business trip or leisure time with your family.

We’re present in the market with the following brands “Abreu Events - Meetings & Incentives”, “Abreu Events - Congresses” and “Abreu Events - Destination Management”.
Responding to the various business segments with specialized team and located in strategic points in Portugal.

title: VIP Executive Entrecampos Hotel & Conference

The Hotel is located in Entrecampos, near the financial and business centre and it is served by broad public transportation network.
his 4 star Hotel's architecture stands out from the rest and has the signature of two remarkable architects, Ricardo Boffil and Telmo Barata, offering great quality services.
VIP Executive Entrecampos Hotel & Conference has a total of 171 bedrooms, including 5 Suites, and all equiped with sound proof windows and sprinklers, 2 Restaurants, the “Jardim Buffet” next to the indoor garden, where the is served breakfast and "Ouro Preto" which offers a menu with Portuguese and international cuisine for weekends lunch or dinners and one coffee-shop - News Café.
Also available are a Piano Bar, a Newspaper Shop, private parking as well as an indoor swimming pool, turkish bath and sauna.
The Hotel has also available wireless Internet access in the rooms, public areas and meeting rooms.

title: Casa Santos Lima - Companhia das Vinhas
short_description: Casa Santos Lima is a family owned business for several generations, dedicated to the production, bottling and selling of Portuguese wines. Currently the largest producer of “Vinho Regional Lisboa” and “DOC Alenquer”, Casa Santos Lima is present, directly or indirectly, in the wine regions of Lisboa, Algarve, Alentejo, Douro and Vinhos Verdes.
full_description:
Casa Santos Lima (CSL) is a family owned business, managed by the family Santos Lima since the late 19th century that produces, bottles and sells Portuguese wines.
Currently the largest producer of “Vinho Regional Lisboa” and “DOC Alenquer”, CSL is present directly or indirectly in the wine regions of Lisboa, Algarve, Alentejo, Douro and Vinhos Verdes.
From around 390 hectares of vineyards, partially located about 45Km from Lisbon, Casa Santos Lima produces wines known for their excellent quality/price ratio and exports around 90% of its total production to close to 50 countries in all 5 continents.
In the past five years, CSL has been one of the most awarded Portuguese wineries with more than 900 medals in the most prestigious national and international competitions. In 2014, CSL achieved the 8th position in the “Wineries of the World” ranking from the “World Association of Wine Writers and Journalists” (WAWW) and is currently (July 2016) on the Top 3 of the ranking.
location: Quinta da Boavista, Aldeia Galega da Merceana, 2580-081, Alenquer

title: FIL - Lisbon Exhibition and Congress Centre
short_description: FIL – Lisbon Exhibition and Congress Centre is the biggest facility in Lisbon and in Portugal, in a total area of 100.000 sqm.
full_description: FIL – Lisbon Exhibition and Congress Centre is the biggest facility in Lisbon and in Portugal, in a total area of 100.000 sqm. It has 4 pavilions with 10.000 sqm. and 10 rooms each, 1 multipurpose wood pavilion (1.200 sqm.) with a modern architecture, being one of the outstanding and best equipped exhibition areas in Europe, prepared for both fairs and congresses. In FIL we also have FIL Meeting Centre, with 3 auditoria, 5 meeting rooms and 2 foyers, which offer the best conditions for organizing events up to 500 pax. and complementing bigger events held in FIL. Both venues are located in Parque das Nações area – only 7 minutes away from the international airport and 20 minutes from downtown, providing an involving of quality and modernity. The Oriente train/metro/bus station is only 2 minute away. The facilities include a restaurant and parking for 800 vehicles and are served with the latest in technology (audiovisual and IT equipments). FIL also provide all services needed for the event´s organization.
location: Edifício FIL, Rua do Bojador, Parque das Nações, 1998-010, Lisboa

title: Quinta de Sant’Ana
short_description: Just 30 minutes outside Lisbon, this estate nestles amongst vineyards, forests and orchards.
full_description:
Quinta de Sant’Ana, which is just 30 minutes from Lisbon, nestles amongst vineyards, forests and orchards.
The bucolic setting meshes harmoniously with the historic elegance of the estate.
With its fusion of English, German and Portuguese culture, Quinta de Sant’Ana offers an ambience of authenticity and charm that is suitable for any meeting or event.
The estate already produces a significant quantity of top quality wine and has garnered a number of international awards in recent years.
The wine tastings and wine tourism programmes are sufficiently special to ensure each and every person goes away with a lasting memory of their visit.

title: RTP - Radio and Television Museum
short_description: Visit some of the most iconic objects, which constitute the vast and valuable collection of Portuguese Radio and Television Museum.
full_description: The Museological Collection of radio and television displays some of the most iconic objects, which constitute the vast and valuable collection of RTP Museum. All the exhibition space invites to a journey through time, following the technological evolution of the Media, along a route where radio and television objects are organized in chronological order. Recreated spaces enable the visitor interaction with the past, through a radio studio from the ‘50s, and allow a contact with the present through two modern studios, of radio and television, where visitors can make broadcasting. The Museological Collection also offers an interactive multimedia area where one can recall some radio and television historical content, organized by decades.

title: Holiday Inn Express Lisbon Airport
short_description: Make the most of your stopover, business trip or weekend break at this inviting Lisbon hotel with breakfast and Wi-Fi included
full_description:
Make the most of your stopover, business trip or weekend break at this inviting Lisbon hotel with breakfast and Wi-Fi included
Enjoy easy access to Lisbon Airport (less than 2kms away), close proximity to Lisbon city center and Lisbon International Fair (FIL).
Guests love the convenient shuttle service running every 20 minutes from 5:00AM to midnight with a good value per person.
Just let our front desk staff know that you have landed in Lisbon to set up your Airport pickup.
The Holiday Inn Express Lisbon Airport is located just 10 minutes from downtown Lisbon with easy access to public transport, Hop On Hop Off buses and metro a mere 4kms from the hotel at Gare do Oriente, where numerous trains leave heading South and North.
Music lovers are a 5-minute drive from Meo Arena, where big shows are held throughout the year, and the Rock In Rio venue Parque da Bela Vista, and sports fans enjoy close proximity to catch the big games at Sporting stadium or Benfica Stadium.
Make your room your own with a choice of pillows and complimentary tea and coffee.
Our 24-hour lobby bar and our outside terrace are perfect to have a drink. Inclusive high-speed internet is available throughout the hotel and in all rooms.
Start your day off with our inclusive Express Start breakfast to ensure you are fully fueled for your day.
location: Rua da Guiné, 18, Prior Velho, 2685-334, Loures

title: Bedeteca at Amadora (Comic Strip Library)
short_description: A collection of comic strip publications that is well worth a visit.
full_description:
The comic strip library holds over 40, 000 publications, including albums, books, magazines and fanzines.
Throughout the year, it attracts comic strip aficionados from far and wide, particularly as it has invested in new technologies and digital comic strips.
In addition to the areas set aside for albums, books and magazines, the Bedeteca has two exhibition spaces and a Junior Bedeteca, with reading material for younger visitors.

title: Eventivos DMC
short_description: Eventivos is a dynamic and innovative company specializing in the planning, production and management of bespoke events.
full_description:
Eventivos is a young and innovative company specialising in the planning, production and management of bespoke events.
Working closely with both customers and suppliers, our team delivers creative, tailor-made solutions from start to finish.
We firmly believe in “One Client - One Project”. We know that each client is individual and that all their needs are different so the programmes that we create are unique and based on each client’s profile, objectives and budget.
We carefully select our suppliers for their ability to meet the customers’ service requirements within the budget set and to efficiently and effectively deliver a memorable experience for our customers.
location: Rua das Vigias, 2-0D, Parque das Naçoes, 1990-506, Lisboa

title: Portugal Guided Tours
short_description: Come and Discover Portugal with us! Daily Tours visiting the best areas and attractions
full_description:
Our Goal is to provide the guests the best authentic Lisbon Tours experience possible while in Portugal, sharing the love that we have for our country!
From group tours or private tours to more tailor made events everyone can enjoy a great day with us, visiting new places, trying amazing food and learning more about our history and culture!
Join Portugal Guided Tours- Lisbon Tours on an unforgettable journey through Portugal’s most iconic destinations. Whether you’re captivated by history, crave seaside tranquility, or seek natural beauty, our range of tours offers something for everyone.
Explore the magic of Sintra, unwind in the coastal charm of Cascais, experience the spiritual ambiance of Fátima, witness the awe-inspiring waves of Nazaré, and bask in the beauty of Arrábida’s landscapes.
Embark on a journey with us – where every tour is a personalized exploration of Portugal’s cultural richness and natural wonders. Book your adventure today and let us be your guide to the soul of this captivating country.
location: Av. 24 de Julho, 1200-109, Time Out Market, 1200-109, Lisboa

title: Medialivre
short_description: Medialivre - a specialist in the development of multimedia communication solutions, has the capacity and means to find the most appropriate solutions for effective and distinctive communication.
full_description:
Medialivre is a company specialising in the development of multimedia communication solutions, the leading group in Portugal in the press segment of the media sector.
It owns the following media outlets: Correio da Manhã and CMTV, Negócios, SÁBADO, Record, Máxima, Vogue, Flash, TV Guia, Destak and Metro. We have the capacity and the means to help our clients and partners identify and develop the most appropriate solutions for effective and distinctive communication.

1. Excellence in Organisation
We have the capacity and the means to help our clients and partners identify and develop the solutions best suited to their needs.
Medialivre offers a wide range of integrated services:
Graphic Identity, Advertising, Concepts, Activations, Sponsorship
2. Integrated Projects from A to Z
We manage everything from pre-event to post-event and follow-up
3. Multi-platform Media Amplification
Multi-platform editorial coverage of the event allows for a significantly broader impact than that produced simply by attendance at the event.

title: Hotel Vila Galé Collection Palácio Dos Arcos
short_description: This boutique 5-star hotel is set in the center of the town Paço de Arcos, 15 minutes from Lisbon and Cascais. It combines tradition with the comfort of a modern hotel.
full_description:
Built in the late 15th century and rebuilt three centuries later, it was Palácio dos Arcos that named the town Paço de Arcos.
This 5-star boutique hotel opened in 2013, combines the history and tradition of a century-old palace with the comfort and modernity of a current hotel.
It has a superb river view and a Spa Satsanga Collection with indoor pool. Inevitável is its gourmet restaurant with typical Portuguese cuisine. Magnificent gardens with century-old trees and an outdoor pool are also of note.
The hotel offers free Wi-Fi in all areas.
It is located in the center of the town Paço de Arcos, a 2 minutes’ walk from the train station line that connects Lisbon to Cascais in a single path alongside the sea and the river.

title: Centro Vasco da Gama
short_description: Boasting a prime location in Lisbon's Parque das Nações, and featuring 160 shops, a six-screen cinema and a health club, the Vasco da Gama Shopping Centre is a benchmark for anyone who values good shopping and leisure facilities.
full_description:
Strategically located in a prime area of Lisbon – the Parque das Nações – the Vasco da Gama Shopping Centre gears its offering towards a wide target public that includes not only tourists but also people who live and work in the surrounding area and the cities of Lisbon and Loures.
The centre opened its doors to the public in 2009 and since then has become a benchmark for everyone who values the option of combining shopping with leisure and free-time activities.
To accommodate its many visitors, the Vasco da Gama Centre has around 2,600 parking spaces and 160 shops covering a total gross area of around 50,000m2.
Apart from the huge selection of shops, the centre also features a six-screen cinema, a health club and luggage room.
Find out all about the Vasco da Gama's initiatives and news online at www.centrovascodagama.pt.

title: Happy Tours Portugal
short_description: My name is Nuno Baptista and I'm a local guide in Portugal. If you want to discover Lisbon and it's surroundings in a very passionate way, I'll be here to make your stay memorable!!! :)
full_description: With Happy Tours Portugal you'll keep your tour forever, with us you'll collect memories!

title: Fadoflats
short_description: Short term quality rental service since 2009
full_description: Our services are aimed for the medium/high end visitors to Lisbon and surrounding areas. Our mission is to provide comfortable, fully equipped, stylishly decorated flats, with our guests comfort in mind. The flats are located in desirable areas, within walking distances to all modes of transportation, shopping and entertainment venues to make your visit an enjoyable one. We also offer many additional services such as transfers, baby sitting, cooking classes and personalized tours, to make your visit enjoyable and unique.

title: Smartbus
short_description: SmartBus provides a wide range of mobility solutions including Urban Mobility, Transfers, Tours, VIP, Luggage Transfers & Storage, Reduced Mobility & Kids Transportation.
full_description:
SmartBus specialises in the transport of passengers and the provision of several related services. We are proud to enjoy distinguished trust and notoriety in our market, we have earned it over the transportation of more than 1,200,0000 passengers over our more than 15 years of operation in this sector.
With a current fleet of approximately 50 vehicles of varying capacities and configurations, specialized and adapted to each of our service areas, SmartBus provides a wide range of offers within our sector.
From our most established and ubiquitous services like School and Special Needs Transport to the more niche and diverse offers in Tourism, and Urban Transport, all the way to our Airport transfer, VIP, Tour and Luggage Transport services.
AIRPORT TRANSFERS
Personalised private transport in any direction between any destination and Lisbon's Portela Airport.
TOURISTIC TOURS
Private visitors' tours to take you through to all of the wondrous sites and historical marks of Portugal. Cultural, gastronomic and nature-filled trips in the company of our expert tour guides.
LUGGAGE TRANSFERS AND STORAGE
Enjoy our country from the first til the last minute! At arrival or departure, leave your luggage with us, enjoy the city and get it back comfortably at your accommodation our airport according to your request.
REDUCED MOBILITY
Fully equipped transportation services for transport of passengers with reduced mobility, SmartBus offers a universal solution which we pledge to be obstacle-free.
PRIME SERVICES
With SmartBus' Prime offering, your exclusive luxury vehicle will be driven by an expertly trained driver, a true concierge behind the wheel.
SCHOOL TRANSPORT
With over 15 years of experience in Childrens' transportation, SmartBus offers a specialised service of the utmost quality and safety. We are the oldest such service operating in Portugal.
location: Rua José Travassos nº 15 B Loja, 1600-410, Lisboa

title: BmyGuest - Short Term Rental
short_description: BmyGuest provides promotion and short term rental of private properties for tourists or business travellers who visit our country.
full_description: We provide a wide range of services. We will help and guide you to choose the perfect apartment fitting your requests taking in account multiple factors such as location, style and comfort so you will feel like home away from home and welcome in our country.

title: Imagine more - Meetings & Incentives
short_description: What makes imagine more different? Our original approach to the destination, our team, its courage, experience, freshness and mostly our commitment to make your event outstanding. imagine more…Portugal!
full_description:
imagine more is your DMC in Portugal. Created in 2010, we are four familiar faces with a renewed spirit who have decided to take a chance on what we think a DMC should be.
We propose to be your partner in Portugal working for and with you since we believe a DMC is fundamental when creating a professional incentive, meeting or conference.
imagine...

… and with you we make your incentive, meeting or conference unique imagine more Portugal with us!

title: Feiralegria
short_description: The FeirAlegria shows all the new trends and creations in urban handicrafts and design, besides having products and entertainment specifically for kids.
full_description: Praça da Alegria in Lisbon is better known for its lively nightlife and the excellent Hot Club than for the fair held there every fortnight. But FeirAlegria, which shows all the new trends and creations in urban handicrafts and design, besides having products and entertainment specifically for kids, can trace its roots back to the end of the 18th century and early 19th century when the Feira da Alegria was the forerunner of the Feira da Ladra, or flea market. But we are not talking about second-hand objects here, but items of fashion, sculptures, paintings, serigraphies and bijouterie. Get to know the Jardim Alfredo Keil and, if you have time, stay for a concert at the jazz club.

title: Hotel Smy Lisboa
short_description: A unique stay in the heart of Lisbon!
full_description:
Thanks to its convenient location in a residential area of Lisbon, the Smy Lisboa**** hotel brings its guests closer to the lively Portuguese capital while offering tranquillity due to its distance from the hustle and bustle of the city centre. Located between Parque Eduardo VII and Amoreiras, the hotel has excellent connections to the city and is close to the shopping and leisure areas.
Its 105 spacious rooms and suites are fully equipped to ensure a stay with maximum comfort. Designed to meet multiple needs, this hotel is ideal for both business trips and city breaks for two or with friends.
Its gastronomic offer comprises an à la carte restaurant and two bars, one of which has a magnificent view of the city. It also has a small gym open 24 hours a day and a meeting room for holding private events.
The hotel is conveniently located, close to underground stations, 1.1 kilometres from the elegant Avenida da Liberdade and 900 metres from Marquês de Pombal square. Furthermore, it is just a 5-minute walk from the first stop of the emblematic tram 24, which connects to the historic centre.

title: National Tile Museum
short_description: The museum is set in Madre de Deus Convent, founded in 1509 and its collections allow a journey through the history of tile, from 15th century till present days.
full_description:
Museu Nacional do Azulejo is one of the most important of the national museums by the singularity of its collection, Azulejo (tile), an artistic expression that differentiates Portuguese culture, and by the uniqueness of the building in which the Museum is set, former Madre de Deus Convent, founded in 1509 by Queen D. Leonor.
Its collections allow a journey through the history of tile, from 15th century till present days.
Belonging to the convent, the Madre de Deus church is decorated in full Portuguese baroque splendour, with gilded and carved wood, paintings and tile panels.

additional_sections.title: Additional Information
additional_sections.text: Schedule Tuesday to Sunday, from 10:00 to 18:00. Last entry at 17:15. Closed: Monday, January 1st, Easter Sunday, May 1st, June 13th, December 25th Library: Monday to Friday, from 14:00 to 17:30, by prior appointment.

location: Rua da Madre de Deus, 4, 1900-312, Lisboa

title: Palácio Ludovice Wine Experience Hotel
short_description: Located in the heart of Lisbon amidst the mythical Chiado, Bairro Alto and Príncipe Real neighbourhoods, at the former private residence of João Federico Ludovice, during the reign of of King João V, in the 18th century, its rooms and halls are brought back to life as 61 rooms and suites.
full_description:
Located in the heart of Lisbon amidst the mythical Chiado, Bairro Alto and Príncipe Real neighbourhoods, facing the breathtaking panoramic views of the São Pedro de Alcântara Viewpoint and the emblematic Glória Tram.
Former private residence of João Federico Ludovice, architect to King João V in the 18th century, its rooms and halls are brought back to life as 61 rooms and suites, completely transformed under a wine connoisseur’s eye.
ROOMS
Individually designed rooms, some offering multiple windows and balconies with unparalleled city views while others have gorgeous interior courtyard sights. Conceived by the architect and interior designer Miguel Câncio Martins who holds an impressive collection of projects around the globe.
Grand décor blends combining original XVIII century elements such as hand painted tiles or carved wood on ceilings and walls with a contemporary touch, will take you back in time to live as a distinguished Federico Ludovice guest in his private manor.
RESTAURANT & BAR
The courtyard at Palácio Ludovice is the rendezvous place for food lovers, wine connoisseurs, creative minds and bon vivants. The sophisticated atmosphere of FEDERICO restaurant, a vibrant bar located in an old cellar, and a top-notch service worthy of a palacete come together to offer a truly epicurean experience.
WELLNESS BOUTIQUE-SPA & GYM
In an exude of peaceful serenity, here you will find the world signature wellness treatments of CAUDALIE, a carefully curated boutique shop with a selection of the most exclusive products and a fully equipped gym.
location: Rua de São Pedro de Alcântara, 39-49, 1250-237 , Lisboa

title: EVOA – Tagus Estuary Birdwatching and Conservation Area
short_description: Come and watch birds in their natural habitat, explore the wild side of the Tagus, only at 30 minutes from Lisbon. Guided visits from 5€ to 12€ person.
full_description:
Come and watch birds in their natural habitat, explore the wild side of the Tagus, only at 30 minutes from Lisbon. EVOA is situated in the Natural Reserve of the Tagus Estuary, which is included in the Natura 2000 network since 1980 and is recognized as a wetland of international importance (Ramsar Site).
The visitor can enjoy the wild birds, mostly in three freshwater wetlands, with 70 ha, created by the project and managed for birds. The necessary tranquility and convenience for visitors are guaranteed through hidden trails and hides that are strategically located.
EVOA has programs of interpretive guided tours. The Visitor Centre offers a pleasant experience of visitation. It leads visitors to know the birds, the estuary and the lezíria and to participate in various interactive activities.
In addition to the exhibition area and a cafeteria where visitors can enjoy the space and the beautiful scenery.
location: Lezíria Sul, 2600, Vila Franca de Xira

title: Honorato | Parque das Nações
short_description: Ready for a delicious and crunchy experience? At Honorato, all our burgers are prepared with the utmost attention to detail to offer you the best experience! When are you coming for a visit?
full_description:
From our kitchen come the best burgers in the country.
And, our specialty goes beyond burgers. Visit us and explore our cocktail menu.
Honorato has the perfect recipe for group dinners: delicious burgers, divine cocktails, and tables where there’s always space for one more.
Or, take the flavors of Honorato wherever you go with our takeaway.

title: Palácio Chiado
short_description: Various gastronomic concepts share the same space, with a unique offer, due to its originality and diversity, the hard part will be to choose!
full_description:
Various gastronomic concepts share the same space, under the curatorship of Chef Manuel Bóia. With a unique offer, due to its originality and diversity, the hard part will be to choose!
The story of this elegant palace will now resume in 2016 as a place to escape from everyday routines, in an extravagant escape from the banal.
In the renewed Palácio Chiado, the historic rooms will now host alternatives for fine dining and other forms of entertainment for Lisbon residents and visitors. As an outstanding cosmopolitan venue in the city, it is a timeless blend of history and modern life.
In September of 2018, Palácio Chiado goes under a few refurbishment works and reopens in October, with new gastronomic concepts, albeit with the same diversity and originality it has always had. With exquisite gastronomic and artistic curatorships, this is the FOOD & ART spot of Lisbon.

title: Varanda de Lisboa | Panoramic Restaurant
short_description: A food experience with the best view over Lisbon!
full_description:
The emblematic Panoramic Restaurant Varanda de Lisboa boasts one of the most stunning views over the heart of Lisbon.
A symbol of the Portuguese gastronomy, Varanda de Lisboa has recently been through a major refurbishment in order to continue providing its customers with unique moments and “timeless” experiences, in accordance with Hotel Mundial’s mission: honoring its own history whilst reinventing the future, ensuring its guests rewarding experiences, exceeding their expectations.
In collaboration with the renowned Chef Vitor Sobral, the Chef who led the process of modernization of the Portuguese Traditional Gastronomy, Varanda de Lisboa presents an interesting Menu that celebrates the traditional portuguese cuisine, combining some of the most classic portuguese dishes with the contemporary trends and methods of such a dynamic industry. The main focus continues the same: the exceptional quality of the products and the service of excellence by which is recognized.
Lunch times are marked by Daily Suggestions, always inspired by traditional portuguese cuisine. Try our Pataniscas de Bacalhau on Tuesdays, or “Feijoada à Brasileira” on Saturdays.
The unique ambience is accompanied by piano music during dinner (except Sundays and month of August).

title: CCDRLVT
short_description: Commission for Regional Development and Coordination of Lisbon and Tagus Valley
full_description: CCDRLVT - Comissão de Coordenação e Desenvolvimento Regional de Lisboa e Vale do Tejo Regional Public Administration with greater powers to promote sustainable regional development, particularly in public investment management functions and coordination and cooperation of regional administrations, being itself a structure with highly decentralized services, working in the knowledge society environment, resources qualified and motivated human and relational culture and efficient service to the community and the "Clients".

title: Feitoria Restaurant & Wine Bar
short_description: The Feitoria Restaurant with signature cuisine bets in the best Portuguese cuisine with a creative twist that results in a sophisticated and perfect combination of flavors.
full_description: Feitoria Restaurant & Wine Bar is an exclusive gourmet restaurant specialising in signature dishes by the skillful hand of the head Chef João Rodrigues here traditional Portuguese gastronomy discovers and blends with the more exotic cuisines of the world, preserving the genuine essence of local traditional flavours. The journey kicks off with a sumptuous atmosphere of sophistication and is complemented by a generous selection of fine wines. The Feitoria Restaurant was awarded with 1 Michelin Star in the Michelin Guide of Spain and Portugal between 2012 and 2015. At the Feitoria Restaurant you will discover a full selection of creative dishes, tasting menus of Chef João Rodrigues: Land, Tradition and Journey . The Feitoria Restaurant also has a Wine Bar offering several hundreds of wine references ranging from the best local hand picked casts to those of carefully selected international wineries.

title: MFA Command Post
short_description: The MFA (Armed Forces Movement) command post was the hub of military operations against Portugal’s dictatorship.
full_description: Between 24 and 26 April 1974, the officers who led the 25 April revolution organised and directed operations from this command post inside the Pontinha Barracks. The command post has been fully renovated and refurbished and visitor support structures installed. The aim of this has not only been to make the most of the site but also, and mainly, to serve as an interpretation centre for school parties and all those interested in the landmark events in Portugal’s history.
location: Quartel do Regimento de Engenharia nº1 - Av. do Regimento de Engenharia, 1, Pontinha, 1675-103, Odivelas

title: Combatant's Museum in Forte do Bom Sucesso
short_description: Exhibitions from the Great War, Overseas War, Peace Operations, History of Aviation, The trench, in 3D, representing the life of Portuguese Soldier in the trenches of Flandres.
full_description:
Located at the Forte do Bom Sucesso (which was built in the 18th century in order to defend the port of Lisbon) the Museum of the Combatant houses one of the museological centres of the League of Combatants.
It is divided in three sections that focus on the Portuguese military interventions of the 20th century: World War I, the ‘Ultramar’ campaigns and Missions of Peace.
A memorial was built at the entrance to the building, which serves as an integral part of the museum and evokes the combatants who died defending the Old Portuguese Ultramar.

location: Av. Brasilia, no Forte do Bom Sucesso junto à Torre de Belém, 1400-038, Lisboa

title: Shrine to Nª Srª do Cabo
short_description: Come and see this intriguing and original Baroque monument at Cape Espichel, on an essential tour of the outskirts of Lisbon.
full_description: The shrine to Nossa Senhora do Cabo is one of the harshest, most primitive and impressive places you can visit near Lisbon. Assailed day and night by ceaseless winds, it stands on the most isolated plateau of Cape Espichel. Just a few kilometres from Lisbon, in a region rich in remains of the inhabitants of the earth many millions of years ago, you’ll find the only Portuguese shrine designed from the ground up. The sheer limestone cliffs provide an amazing natural backdrop. Come and discover this intriguing and original Baroque monument, on an essential tour of Lisbon’s outskirts.

title: My Lisbon Tours
short_description: My Lisbon Tours provides high quality Private and Small Group Tours in the Lisbon region. Our mission is to serve as hosts to the many travelers that choose Lisbon as an holiday destination.
full_description:
We have enhanced countless vacations through the friendly and informative experience we provide. Our guests come as customers and leave as friends, and it is for this reason that we have been consistently rated excellent on all our online platforms.
Our activities encompass all the major historical quarters of Lisbon, namely the "Baixa", Alfama and Belém, as well as Sintra, and are conducted by a professional local guide with deep insight into the culture and history of the city.
We also create and share content on our website so that our followers, who are unable to join us on our tours and activities, can still gain knowledge on Portugal's history and culture, as well as news and suggestions on things to do while vacationing in Lisbon.

title: Necropolis of Carenque
short_description: Come to Amadora, book a visit through the Museu de Arqueologia da Amadora and awaken the archaeologist within.
full_description:
Discover a virtually unknown national monument in Lisbon.
The Carenque Caves, or Carenque Necropolis, are a set of manmade caves dug out of the rock at the end of the Neolithic age – around the third millennium BC – that were used up until the Chalcolithic period (the Copper Age).
You can see several of the artefacts found beside the bodies at the National Archaeology Museum (MNA) in Lisbon.
In this circular mortuary chamber, limestone idols and crescent-shaped moons, slabs of schist and even depictions of utensils, such as an adze, were found.
The three collective graves excavated from the rock contained skeletons and ceramics and have maintained their characteristic architecture with a corridor and a skylight, both covered by limestone slabs.
Come to Amadora, book a visit through the Museu de Arqueologia da Amadora and awaken the archaeologist within.
location: Serra das Brancas, Av. Luis de Sá, Mina, Amadora

In the space, we find warm, natural tones that invoke memories, such as the handmade mosaic wainscoting and the terrazzo floor, which make us revisit a place of comfort and memory.

At breakfast time this integrated space is used for a good buffet, whilst in the afternoon it is transformed into a pleasant lounge with a small library, complimentary newspapers and magazines, plasma TV, quality background music, Internet wi-fi access free of charge and a drinks and snacks self-service. Furthermore the hotel includes a meeting room, laundry, garage and a 24-hour luggage service.
Its prestigious setting next to the Marques de Pombal Square, one of Lisbon’s most well know landmarks, offers a very special combination of charm and comfort. The Hotel is embellished by the small Camilo Castelo Branco garden situated in front of the hotel.

title: Calçada Portuguesa
short_description: Learn more about this art form that you can step on in Lisbon. Let your feet walk on – and your eyes savour – the unique patterns that can be found in the city.
full_description:
Portuguese paving, calçada portuguesa, is one of Lisbon’s trademarks.
Playing a huge role in creating the city’s special atmosphere, as it reflects all of the light that falls on it, it is also one of the oldest features of the city.
Made using limestone cubes shaped and placed by hand by skilful craftsmen, the designs can be geometric, figurative or specific depending on the particular location and the tastes of the time.
Enjoy the undulating pattern in Avenida da Liberdade or stand back and observe the symmetry of the design in Chiado.
It’s worth walking down the same street twice, once to look at the buildings and another to discover the impressive designs on the ground.

title: Jardim Botânico da Ajuda
short_description: This charming botanical garden was the first of its kind in Lisbon. It’s completely unique and has a charming view of the city and the river.
full_description: This was the first botanical garden ever created in Lisbon and Portugal, in 1768. It was built by the Marquis of Pombal and designed by the Italian botanist Domingos Vandelli, installed in the grounds of the Palácio da Ajuda and used to complement the education of the princes of Portugal. It assembled over 5, 000 species of plants and allowed the study of the varied vegetation found in Portugal and the overseas territories. Expeditions were sent to Angola, Brazil and Cape Verde to collect seeds and plants. The garden, laid out in the style of the era, flourished. On the upper level, you’ll find King José’s ponds and tree garden with myriad examples of tropical flora. On the lower, a baroque box garden with a central pond, nurseries and herbs. It provides a unique view of the Tagus amid species from all over the world.

full_description:
Paired with great wines and well poured beer, you can enjoy our delights in a friendly and relaxed environment.
More than seventy years after opening our doors in the most emblematic Avenue of Lisbon, we continue to serve truly spectacular meals, with dishes that go beyond just seafood and that will make your visit to Ribadouro a delicious and unforgettable gastronomic feast.

You only need to choose your favourite, and it will be prepared and served the way you desire.
Get ready: the wave of flavour from the sea will land at your table.
Ribadouro’s flavour goes beyond our Seafood. In our kitchen, specialities such as our Steak with Ribadouro Sauce, Beef fillet “Pica Pau” Style, Alentejo-Style Pork, or the baked Cod Loin complement our original and unique seafood dishes, like “Gambas à Brás”, or Marinheira Style Seafood.
Have you tried them?

full_description:

And, the Newly opened (2023) Cenozoic – Ice Age trail.
At Dino Parque in Lourinhã, you will learn the history of the fantastic world and period of the dinosaurs and prehistoric animals, not only of their passage through Portugal, but all over the world.

title: Adventure Park
short_description: Dare your family and friends and come have fun in a total safety environment, overcoming obstacles and breaking barriers.
full_description:
CANOPY is an activity that consists of sequentially passing through obstacles between platforms which are positioned on the tops of the trees – hanging bridges, ropes, nets, tunnels from tree to tree, Tarzan jumps – always ending with a slide.
To start your day of discovering Canopy, you’ll be equipped with an harness, which will allow you to go from tree to tree in all safety.
Next, for all the participants over 1,40 meters high, we have a safety briefing – about 15 minutes of explanations and training, in which all the participants will have their first canopy experience and be let loose on top of the trees, free to fly on the slide and swing amongst the trees. And, of course, instructors will always be at hand, regularly patrolling the forests.
The result is amazing. This outdoors adventure is full of adrenaline, takes people out of their comfort zone, and, above all (no pun intended), it’s a great outdoors activity for kids, families, birthday parties, farewell parties, groups, students, companies and team education.
For about 2 hours, venture in the middle of a dense forest and climb up to 12 meters high, wander around on the tops of the trees, crossing rope bridges, hanging tunnels, sliding in a 200 meter slide above the landscape or flying like Tarzan, in a thrill ride.
In our Parks, we organize additional activities such as: climbing walls installation, performing of orientation tests, inflatables installation and at Jamor we can enjoy an integrated offer of Canoeing, Football fields, Archery, Tennis.
Contact us we have lots of attractive programs.
location: Rua da Carreira de Tiro, Complexo Desportivo do Jamor, Cruz-Quebrada, 1495-751, Linda-a-Velha

title: Health and Pharmacy Museum Lisbon
short_description: The Health and Pharmacy Museum proposes an attractive and unusual experience through 5000 years of history of health.
full_description:
Its vast collection includes objects of rare artistic, anthropological, and scientific value, describing a journey, where each civilization is portrayed under a narrative of disease-fighting and the seeking of healing and pain relief, since the first traces of life on earth until today, passing through cultures and civilizations as distinct in time and place as Mesopotamia, Egypt, Greece, Rome, the Incas, the Aztecs, Islam, Africa, Tibet, China, Japan, among others.
From an Egyptian sarcophagus, where hopes for eternal life were deposit, to the black plague doctor suit, as a symbol of the struggle against one of the greatest pandemics in history, the objects that make up the collection of the Health and Pharmacy Museum in Lisbon are distinct and unique.
A journey that, although it started in prehistory, it is still being built today on the testimonies that now reach us from the “future”, such as portable pharmacies and first aid kits used by NASA astronauts aboard the Space Shuttle Endeavour.

In 2008 while the golf course was being refurbished a new Clubhouse was built.
Planned by the architect João Paciência, the Clubhouse offers you the chance to savour a delicious meal overlooking the lake in perfect harmony with the surrounding landscape.

title: Cape Roca
short_description: The point where the land ends and the sea begins. Immortalised in the famous words of Portugal’s greatest poet, Luís Vaz de Camões, Cape Roca impresses all who visit.
full_description: The grandest and most imposing of all of Lisbon’s viewing points isn’t in Lisbon. More specifically, the viewing point where nature reveals its true power isn’t in Lisbon. It lies between Cascais and Sintra and obliges visitors to travel along one of the prettiest roads in the region. The slow and winding climb doesn’t suggest the magnitude of the view from the most westerly point in continental Europe. Cape Roca, in the Sintra hills, combines calmness and inclement weather in one place; a wide, open area where the land drops abruptly into the sea from its 140-metre-high cliffs. You can buy souvenirs, but it’s the photos and pure sea air that will always remind you of the potency of the Atlantic. The 1772 lighthouse still safely guides the vessels that sail along the Portuguese coast.
location: Cabo da Roca - Azóia, Colares, 2705-001, Sintra

title: Palácio das Especiarias
short_description: A Palace with History
full_description:
Located in the historical center of Lisbon, the Palácio das Especiarias gives its name to an old building of high architectural beauty, whose construction dates back to the 16th century.
To date, spices, much coveted by the large European manor houses and the upper bourgeoisie, were extremely rare and expensive, and even for those who were willing to pay fortunes, they did not always have access to them.
It was then that, as a result of the discovery of the maritime route to India by the celebrated Portuguese navigator, Vasco da Gama, as well as new territories in Africa, Asia and Brazil, spices, gold, diamonds and other metals and precious stones, began to reach Lisbon in huge quantities and varieties, transforming the city into one of the main centers of world trade.
The name, Palácio das Especiarias, therefore invites a trip to this time when its foundations were built and also the foundations of the globalized world we live in today – the era of discoveries.
Within the walls of this ancient Palace, which has a decor alluding to the time, and was the birthplace of the Vice-Kings of the old Portuguese empire in India and Brazil, we can still hear murmurs of those times, and here you can find interpretations of historical figures from Portugal.
Through the unique themed events that we prepare, it is still possible to enjoy a dip to the origins of Portuguese culture, gastronomy, music and customs.
Its privileged location allows to visit the best that the city of Lisbon has to offer.
At your disposal there is a qualified team, where sympathy is the brand image.
location: Rua da Horta Seca, 11, Misericórdia, 1200-221, Lisboa

short_description: One of Lisbon’s gastronomic landmarks, its cuisine rooted in Portuguese tradition, built on the choice of ingredients, flawless execution and abundance of flavour.
full_description:
RESTAURANT Hailed as one of Lisbon’s leading restaurants, Pap’Açôrda was founded in 1981, in the heart of Bairro Alto, nerve centre of the city’s nightlife
In 2016, hot on the heels of Lisbon’s shifting dynamic, we accepted the invitation of Time Out Lisboa and moved to the first floor of the Ribeira Market.
In this lofty, riverfront space converted by architects Aires Mateus, we continue with our cuisine rooted in Portuguese tradition, built on the choice of ingredients, flawless execution and abundance of flavour.
Two spacious rooms, each with a bar, separated by a transparent wine cellar and by the kitchen, organise the wide space. We can welcome more clients but our homey feel and quality remains the same.
Besides the space, two other changes: the extensive snack menu and the extended timetable.
Now you can drop by at any time of the day for a snack and a drink.

Boasting a modern and harmonious style, TRYP Lisboa Caparica Mar features guest rooms fully equipped, of different types, scattered over 7 floors. The bathroom includes a hairdryer, as well as amenities in dispensers. Laundry service, room service, babysitting, and garage are available upon request. The Hotel has an outdoor swimming-pool, Spa, a fitness centre and a play area for children.
With a panoramic view over the Costa da Caparica beach, at Restaurante Horizonte, “à la carte”,  you may enjoy a meal in a refined atmosphere. Restaurante Paparica is the Hotel’s Buffet Restaurant where breakfast, lunch, and dinner are served with high-quality ingredients. The Hotel also boasts a roof top “Mateus Terraza”.
Bar Jardim is the ideal spot to taste an aperitif in the mid-afternoon or a late cocktail, where one can enjoy the sun and unwind. Red Dragon Beach Club is Hotel’s beach bar with a privileged location opposite the sea, on the Dragão Vermelho Beach, and an area reserved for Hotel guests.
TRYP Lisboa Caparica Mar is fitted with meeting rooms, ideal for organizing business meetings, civil ceremonies, cocktails or other events.
Guests have free access to the Portuguese and international press. The Hotel is pet-friendly, against payment of a fee, and an adorable welcome pet kit awaits your pet.

short_description: Amazing Venues, which benefit from a perfect historical environment, in the heart of the city.
full_description:
These venues emphasize the attractiveness of Lisbon as a premium touristic site, revealing themselves as the perfect venues to live and share the city.
Pátio da Galé, inaugurated in 2011, is an incomparable premium venue in Lisbon, located in Terreiro do Paço. It is the place where the Royal Palace and the India House were located, when the earthquake of 1755 took place.
A modern renovation, undertaken in the venue in 2010, paid attention to the maintenance of the architectural details and to the meaning of the square and the building to the history of the city.
If, on one hand, Pátio da Galé respects its patrimonial and historical reputation, is has become as well a very versatile venue, able to host a great variety of events..
The spacious cloisters and the neutral features offered by the Lioz (Portuguese limestone) floor, allow innumerous layouts and the use of various colours in the multiple corporate and private events.
Pátio da Galé with its trendy concept is given by the important events that they host every year, is indeed the perfect venue for your dinner, conference, wedding or cultural event!

title: Manueline Architecture
short_description: Get to know the main examples of Manueline architecture in Lisbon and the way this globally unique style first appeared in Portugal.
full_description:
An unique art form first seen in Lisbon at the end of the 15th century. In the transition from the Gothic era to the Renaissance, an architectural style influenced by Moorish elements began to come into use in the palaces in Lisbon and Sintra, as well as in the construction of the monastery of Jerónimos and Torre de Belém. During the reign of King Manuel I, countless monuments, windows and doorways were built that were more or less influenced by a Naturalist style responsible for more architectural structures than decorative motifs. The size and depth of the arches and twisted framing cornices, facades and vaults distinguish the pure Manueline style, which began to wane with the Renaissance from 1517 onwards. The style is part of the history, richness and glory of this unique period in Portuguese history.
See all the examples

Located on the lively Rua de São Paulo, one of Lisbon’s most vibrant and sought-after neighborhoods, Independente Bica is a boutique hotel that blends history, character, and contemporary design.
Housed in a building over 150 years old, this former bed-and-breakfast has been reimagined with a minimalist aesthetic inspired by Japanese design, offering a unique and refined hospitality experience.
More than just a place to stay, Independente Bica is a gateway to the city’s creative energy:
