title: Miradouro de Santa Catarina
short_description: Visit Adamastor to see the view of Lisbon, without fear of storms. See the prettiest and most unforgettable sunset in the city, with music, conversation and good company included.
full_description: Few locals know of the Miradouro de Santa Catarina, but almost all of them have heard of Adamastor. It’s popular amongst the younger generation for watching the sunset, with a unique atmosphere of music, conversation and beer in the evening and into the night. The view is stunning and catches all of the Tagus estuary, the bridge and the rooftops. It has a small garden, but it’s the vast terrace that’s the real attraction, with a huge statue of Adamastor, the mythical figure created by Camões to symbolise the Cape of Torment. If you were wondering, that was the original name of the Cape of Good Hope and the place where so many Portuguese ships were wrecked. Finally rounded by Bartolomeu Dias in 1488, Adamastor ceased to be so scary and today is fine company for watching the sun go down.

title: Sport Lisboa e Benfica
short_description: Come visit “Estádio da Luz”, the largest and most modern stadium in Portugal. Join ours tours, which take place every 20 minutes, and have a great experience.
full_description:
Come visit “Estádio da Luz”, the largest and most modern stadium in Portugal, stage of the Champions League final in 2014. Join ours tours, which take place every 20 minutes, and have a great experience.
You can go near the pitch, to the dressing room, visit the Press Conference Room, take a photo with “Vitória”, the eagle and much, much more! Relive Eusébio’s best moments, the greatest Benfica legend, while you also learn more about the Lisbon, Portugal and the World’s History at Benfica Cosme Damião Museum.
An innovative Museum with a contemporary approach, that resorts to the most modern technologies to emphasize knowledge, emotion and the experience.
We are waiting for you!

title: HF Fénix Lisboa
short_description: Your home away from home
full_description:
Here, your day starts with a wide smile, a sense of belonging, and a mouth-watering breakfast!
The HF Fénix Lisboa is one of the most emblematic hotels of the Portuguese capital and one that shows you, from your window, the rhythm and the romance of one of the most cosmopolitan and beautiful areas of the city, Praça Marquês de Pombal.
Located in the centre of Lisbon, with the metro just around the corner, you just need to leave the hotel to start exploring, whether it is Avenida da Liberdade, Baixa (downtown) or Parque Eduardo VII, and always finishing your day with a stroll along the banks of the Tagus river.

With a central span of over one kilometer and a total length of 2.3 km it is one of the longest suspended bridges in the world.

full_description:
Bco Congresos is the congress organizing division of Ávoris Corporación Empresarial, the travel company branch of the Barceló Group.
Over the last 30 years our team of over 100 employees has organised more than 2000 meetings for Spanish, European, Latin American and International Associations. Our sustained growth comes down to the continual trust and recommendations received from our loyal customers, regardless of the size, location or length of their congress.
We'll help you turn your vision into a reality. Together let's make your congress a resounding success.
Think professional, trust expertise!
Shall we meet?

title: Portucale Voice
short_description: Offer excellence and innovation in your customers' guided tours with Portucale Voice radio guides
full_description:
Our mission is to add value to all guided tours and events, ensuring that each participant receives all the information conveyed, with complete clarity and comfort.
We offer advanced, sustainable, and reliable technology, backed by over 15 years of experience, a dedicated team and constant positive feedback from our customers who have been with us on this journey since day one!
Our equipment is an asset for national and international tours, visits to museums, factories and farms, guided tours during cruise stopovers, corporate events, product presentations, and meetings in unusual environments.
In addition, our system can be used at events with simultaneous interpretation, allowing professional interpreters to use the equipment to convey the message in different languages in real time.
Reinforce your company's innovative positioning by differentiating your services and exceeding expectations. Contact us to find out how we can support you.
location: Rua Luis Braille, 6 - R/C Loja C, 1400-031, Lisboa

title: Mouraria
short_description: A traditional Lisbon quarter identified by some scholars as the possible cradle of fado; the place where Severa, a mythical figure in the world of fado, lived and died.
full_description:
Mouraria is where the Moors were allowed to live after the Christian reconquest of Lisbon in 1147.
It's a very authentically Lisbon neighbourhood, known as the birthplace of Fado. Legend says that It was here that the first great Fado singer lived in the 1800s, the legendary Severa.

title: APAVT
short_description: APAVT - Portuguese Association of Travel Agencies and Tourism is the only association of this class in Portugal and the oldest and representative of the national tourism.
full_description:
APAVT - Portuguese Association of Travel Agencies and Tourism is the only association of this class in Portugal and the oldest and representative of the national tourism, bringing together:
• Permanent Partners - Nacional Travel Agents
• Allies Associates - other companies related to tourism such as foreign travel agencies, airlines, hotels, restaurants, rent-a-car, official tourism bodies, campsites and road transport, among others.
APAVT was founded on May 30, 1950 by a group of Travel Agents who considered that, in the form of association, could better defend the rights and interests of their sector.
Over the last fifty seven years the Association has played a key role in the development of national tourism, promoting the exchange of ideas among its members, presenting proposals at all levels, anticipating market changes and preparing the sector for the challenges already placed.
Given the pivotal position of travel agents, which are the main link between tourism and consumers alike, the action of APAVT often beyond the scope of this class, contributes significantly to tourism as a whole.
As evidence of the importance of APAVT sector development, the Ministry of Tourism attributes the Gold Medal of Touristic Merit, on September 30th, 1982, published in the Diário da República No. 262 - Series II 1982.
Founded as Gremio and converted into Association after the 1974 revolution, in the early 2000 was also set up the company APAVT Service - SGPS, SA, a holding company wholly owned by APAVT aimed at the development of associational activity and professionalism of its services.

title: Real Sports Bar
short_description: Are you passionate about sports and food? Here’s the perfect combination! Real Sports Bar, the new spot in Lisbon to gather friends and enjoy sports!
full_description:
Are you passionate about sports and food? Here’s the perfect combination!
Real Sports Bar
, the new spot in
Lisbon
to gather friends and enjoy sports!
An amazing spot to see your favorite club in action, or just rock a hamburger and a cold beer. Join the club.
At Real Sports Bar, the
passion for sport
goes hand in hand with the
passion for food
. Can there be a more spectacular combination? An amazing place with vintage sports inspired decor, to watch Football, Rugby, Tennis, NFL, AFL, Basketball, NBA, Athletics, Golf, F1…? You name it!
And the menu? We have the classics you already know and the latest trends for those who like new challenges.
TO SMASH THEM: Prego em Bolo do Caco (veal steak in a regional bun), a Veal Burger with cheese and bacon, a Classic Bitoque (veal steak) with egg, a Club Sandwich, or a Special Toast? It’s hard to choose.
FAIRPLAY: And if the idea is to share with friends, even if they are from different clubs, there is Veal Pica Pau, Nachos with guacamole or Cheese Boards.
And the perfect score, you know? Croquettes 4 – 4 Lambretas. A set of four homemade veal croquettes with mustard + four 10 cl draught beer.
OVER TIME: And if there is an extra time, there is also Almond Tart, Chocolate Brownie or Fruit Salad, to finish it all off at the end.
And don’t worry, you won’t get thirsty… There are classic Portuguese beers, craft beers, international beers and special sangrias. For the more conservative, there is a selection of wine, red and rosé whites, sparkling wines and champagne, and a variety of spirit drinks to forget about the result or celebrate big!
Gather your friends, gear up and live an epic sporting experience.
location: Rua São Sebastião da Pedreira, 234 C, 1050-210 , Lisboa

title: Seaview Nautical Events
short_description: Welcome aboard! Boat Tours, Corporate and Exclusive groups, Teambuilding Activities. The best speedboat experience in Lisbon!
full_description:
SEAVIEW is a nautical events company in Portugal, with a wide range of boats in its portfolio, in order to meet the specific needs of each event, in the most diverse areas.
We make circuits between Lisbon and Cascais along the coast, transfers, regattas, trips on sailboats, catamarans and more extreme activities, in order to use all the resources available in the Lisbon Region.
We have boats prepared for business events (meetings on board, charm actions, cocktails, among others).
Sesimbra, Troia, Porto and Algarve are other regions where we also operate.
location: Pateo Alfacinha-Rua do Guarda Jóias, 44, 1300-294 , Lisboa

title: Panoramic Living
short_description: Fully-equipped luxury flats with superb river views, offering a wide range of services designed for a memorable and 100% hassle free stay.
full_description:
Located in the heart of the Lisbon Business District, at the Lisbon Expo site, Panoramic Living offers you the comfort and convenience of gathering business and leisure with quality, making you feel like you never left home.
Designed with luxury and comfort in mind, Panoramic Living offers 28 fully-equipped panoramic flats, from studios to 1,2 and 3 bedroom apartments. Our flats are decorated with clean and sober lines, independent and fully-equipped kitchens, spacious living areas and rooms with amazing views over the River Tagus and Lisbon Expo CBD.
Panoramic Living also provides a wide range of services such as complimentary wifi internet, daily housekeeping service and access to breakout rooms and to our exclusive Executive Lounge.
Panoramic Living will provide you a peaceful atmosphere, perfect to relax, work and enjoy your time-out with serenity.
location: Avenida D. João II, 53 – Loja A, Parque das Nações, 1990-085, Lisboa

title: Rota de Vinhos da Península de Setúbal
short_description: Experiences to live with family and friends among cellars and vineyards that have been keeping stories for generations. Find us in Palmela, at Casa Mãe da Rota dos Vinhos.
full_description:
Having Arrábida as our witness and inspired by winegrowing, the Península de Setúbal Wine Route designed tailor-made programmes for you to enjoy with your family and friends.
Unique experiences lived among cellars and vineyards that have been keeping secrets and stories for generations.
Wine tastings, wine courses, cruises on the Sado Estuary, foot treading, picnics in the vineyards.
In September, find out the harvest activities, with the treading in the cellars or the traditional Adiafa.
The year does not end without the "Sons do Vinho", the concerts in the cellars.
Unique flavours and unforgettable landscapes just a step away from Lisbon.
Find us in the heart of Palmela village, at Casa Mãe da Rota dos Vinhos (wine route house), an old cellar and wine and regional product shop, where you can taste the best of the region and that invites you for a visit.
location: Casa Mãe da Rota de Vinhos - Largo de S. João Baptista, 2950-248, Palmela

title: Cascais Visitors & Convention Bureau
short_description: Cascais Visitors & Convention Bureau is the Destination Marketing Organization for promoting internationally the diversity of tourism products featuring our offer
full_description:
Cascais – The Charm of the Atlantic Coast
Located on the west coast of Portugal and just 15 minutes from Lisbon and it’s international airport, is a must-see destination full of glamour, tradition and scenic beauty.
With a natural park, sandy beaches and warm local people, be prepared to discover for yourself our fascinating stories of Kings and spies.
Experience the calm and tranquility of seaside terraces, a choice of world-class golf courses as well as year-round surfing and sailing.
There are so many reasons to come to Cascais.
But there are lot more reasons to stay.

title: André Tours DMC
short_description: André Tours is an incoming agency DMC, working throughout Portugal and islands. We cater for all your needs, always bearing in mind comfort and quality, allowing you to discover the genuine Portugal.
full_description:
André Tours was born in 1995 as an agency turned to Inbound Tourism, result of a 20 year experience in this business by some of its board members.
This was our work philosophy until the beginning of 2002. In March 2002 the company broadens its activities to the Outbound Tourism sector, by opening a second office in the Algarve.
André Tours becomes then IATA member and also increases its work in Inbound Tourism in the Algarve, which as a matter of fact was always a primary objective of the agency’s settlement in this region, by definition very important in Tourism.
The idea that presided the creation of André Tours and which prevails until today, is keeping with our clients and/or business partners a close relationship, meaning we try hard to handle all issues in a personal way, putting ours efforts into everything we do. It is this idea that transports us to the universe of services by measure «Taylor Made». This is the base of our Work.

title: Meliá Lisboa Aeroporto
short_description: Is a landmark 4-star hotel in the Portuguese capital, located just within 200 metres of the International Airport of Lisbon. It has access to 2nd Circular / A1 and is just a few minutes walk from public transport.
full_description:
It offers a perfect combination of comfort and elegance in a sophisticated atmosphere, serving as the starting point for exploring the main cultural attractions. The Hotel features fully equipped guest rooms with different types, a modern and cosy décor, and a bathroom with hairdryer, cosmetic mirror and amenities in dispensers. Laundry service, room service, babysitting and electric vehicle chargers (one specific for TESLA) are available.
El Spa offers a whole experience to unwind body and mind with an indoor swimming-pool, jacuzzi, hydro massage, water jets, sauna and Turkish bath, massage and treatments rooms. The Hotel also has a gym and outdoor swimming-pool.
Restaurante Colinas de Lisboa is open daily for buffet breakfast. At lunch (buffet during the week) and dinner it is possible to taste the best delicacies and to experience the “open kitchen” concept. This is the right spot for those who look for quality, good gastronomy, and comfort. Terminal Bar boasts a wide array of beverages and snacks, with a view of the pool, and is ideal for a meeting, a cocktail in the late afternoon or a romantic date.
From meetings to formal parties or commemorative dinners, Meliá Lisboa Aeroporto has the perfect conditions (1500m2 and audiovisual services) to carry out various types of events, up to 350 people.
Guests have free access to the transfer service, at scheduled times, and to the global press. The Hotel is pet-friendly, and an adorable welcome pet kit awaits your pet.

Portugal Dive is a brand for those who like to dive and would like to give it a try in Portugal. If you are coming to Portugal, or just thinking about it, and you want to know all about diving in Portugal, this is the place to start.
If you're in for recreational diving or technical, here you will find all the information you need to choose the best dive spot or diving program.

title: Tasting Fado
short_description: TASTING FADO offers the best of Lisbon: a visit to the theatre, a Fado show accompanied by Port and custard pastry. A unique experience with a scent of glamour!
full_description:
TASTING FADO Evoking the Portuguese soul, Fado is melancholic with mourning voices expressing longing, accompanied by an acoustic and a Portuguese guitar.
A national symbol and included in UNESCO’s list of World’s Intangible Cultural Heritage, beyond the music, FADO is a sensory experience, a journey through history, art and the gastronomy of Portugal.
TASTING FADO offers the best of Lisbon: a visit to the theatre, a national monument considered one of the most beautiful buildings in the city, whilst being taken on a journey by Portuguese musicians, feeling the essence of Fado accompanied by the traditional flavours of Port and the infamous custard pastry.
A unique experience in exquisite surroundings with a scent of glamour!

title: Maxime Hotel
short_description: Located in Lisbon city centre, the former Cabaret Maxime became a Hotel, bringing back the soul of the icon of Lisbon's bohemian life dating back to the mid-20th century.
full_description:
Surrounded by Praça da Alegria, parallel to the cosmopolitan Avenida da Liberdade, Maxime Hotel is the starting point to discover Lisbon through its 7 hills.
A boutique hotel with 75 rooms that invites us to travel back in time and have fun in a cabaret-flavored restaurant, where gastronomy and music are a single show.
Overlooking the Praça da Alegria garden, the burlesque courtyard or the city of Lisbon, this boutique hotel's bedrooms have been recreated to resemble the old cabaret.
The setting, which was intended to be burlesque, promises a stay in Lisbon that will certainly remain in your memory.
At Maxime Hotel there is always something happening with its versatile, surprising and addictive shows, made to remember the old iconic cabaret.

title: MAC/CCB Museum of Contemporary Art and Architecture Centre
short_description: MAC/CCB defines itself as the great museum of modern and contemporary art in Portugal. Here, the visitor can enjoy works by artists from the most diverse cultural contexts and varied expressions.
full_description:
The Centro Cultural de Belém Foundation hosts the MAC / CCB Museum of Contemporary Art and Architecture Centre.
The Museum receives in deposit works from the State Contemporary Art Collection (CACE), the Teixeira de Freitas Collection, the Holma/Ellipse Collection, and the Berardo Collection.
The new MAC/CCB affirms the mission of the Centro Cultural de Belém, enhancing the dialogue between visual arts, architecture, and performing arts with the presentation of temporary exhibitions by contemporary artists.
A varied programme covering educational activities, conferences and guided tours is also available to everyone visiting the museum.
location: Centro Cultural de Belém - Praça do Império, 1449-003 , Lisboa

title: Benfica Handicrafts Market
short_description: Urban handicrafts meet residential neighbourhood in this Lisbon market, where it’s worth spending some time. Discover a different bairro from those in the tourist guides.
full_description:
In one of Lisbon’s oldest residential neighbourhoods, you’ll find the Benfica handicrafts market. Because it’s not just home to a world famous football club; it’s also a neighbourhood full of life and commerce.
On the second Saturday of every month
, it gets more interesting, with the holding of this special market.
Near Nilo patisserie on the Estrada de Benfica you’ll find artisans, artists and small producers selling all kinds of things connected to their areas.
Patchwork, crochet, rag bags and ceramics are just some of the examples of what you can find in this calm and tree-lined area of Benfica beside the church.
Discover how people live outside the centre of the city and take a unique souvenir of Lisbon home with you.

title: Delmira Vineyard House
short_description: Delmira Vineyard House is a project born out of a passion for nature, a connection with the land and a taste for growing grapes.
full_description:
It is the project of a family dedicated to wine. It respects the cycles of nature and the vineyard.
Here you can enjoy ‘the best of both worlds’. Situated on a plain in the Palmela region and near the mouth of the river Sado, here you will slow down your pace and feel at home while enjoying the dynamics of working on a vineyard.
Experience the rhythm of country life and have good experiences.
Located 60 km from Lisbon, Delmira Vineyard House is in Fernando Pó, in the municipality of Palmela.
The Journey on Delmira
The accommodation is in a privileged location that combines city life with country life.
With a swimming pool overlooking the olive grove and vineyard, you can enjoy beautiful moments of leisure until sunset. Let the fleeting aromas of the vineyard and olive grove envelop you as you walk to your room.
In the comfortable rooms you can entertain yourself, take a look at the landscape or simply read a book in peace.
Delmira Vineyard House is an accommodation consisting of a large outdoor parking area, four bedrooms, a living room, a dining area, a large terrace and a swimming pool with fantastic views of the olive trees and vineyards.
WINE TOURISM
Come and taste the true tradition of Fernando Pó wines, with wine tastings and workshops. Experience a day with the winemaker at the harvest or come and add a special touch to your events, meetings or gatherings in our excellent spaces in the winery.
location: Estrada de Fernando Pó, CCI 2402, 2965 – 2965, Águas de Moura

title: FRS Portugal
short_description: Welcome aboard our modern convertible ship! The panoramic roof can be flexibly moved if necessary. Spoiled by the Portuguese sun, the Sunset Cruise will thus be the highlight of your Lisbon vacation.
full_description:
Discover the monuments of Lisbon from the Tagus River, one of the most beautiful in Portugal. On our
boat cruises
, you'll have the unique opportunity to enjoy breathtaking views of the promenade and the most famous sights in Lisbon.
You can choose between the
boat ride at sunset with welcome drink included
and the
boat ride on the Tagus River
.
For the
sunset boat trip with welcome drink included
, we begin our tour of the river at
Praça do Comércio
right at sunset. Over the next hour and a half we pass spectacular points of interest like
Ponte 25 de Abril, the Museum of Art, Architecture and Technology (MAAT), Padrão dos Descobrimentos, the Belém lighthouse, the Belém Tower and finally the Santuário de Cristo Rei
. The city has spectacular views and brilliant monuments. To help you relax on the Sunset Cruise, we'll serve you a welcome drink included. The chill-out music playing in the background completes the experience.
On our
Boat ride on the Tagus River
, you can also enjoy the monuments of Lisbon at other time of the day. Get on board in one
of the most comfortable and quietest
boats and take amazing pictures of the monuments you will see. Perfect even for families. It lasts for 1h 30 minutes.
Book now
location: Estação Fluvial Sul e Sueste, Pontão 2, Terreiro do Paço, 1100-038, Lisboa

title: Museum of Communication
short_description: Visit Lisbon’s Museu das Comunicações that examines how the Portuguese have communicated with each other over the last 5 centuries.
full_description:
The Communications Museum is located between Santos and Cais do Sodré in Lisbon. It’s a space that reveals how the Portuguese have communicated for over five centuries.
Take a trip back in time at the "
Overcoming Distance - Five Centuries of Telecommunications in Portugal
” exhibition, where you can see the evolution and improvement of the techniques that have enabled Humanity to communicate more rapidly and efficiently.
To understand how the internet became global, you can visit the "Submarine Cables" exhibition. It showcases a timeline of 150 years of history marked by moments that have critically influenced and are essential to our daily lives. These include the development of fibre optic cables that support almost all global multimedia communication and are responsible for bringing individuals, societies and nations closer together. The exhibition also highlights the decisive role that Portugal played in these developments.
Finally, don't miss the
mala-posta
(postal service) exhibition centre. It recreates the moulting station where the mail carriages would stop for change-overs and rest. With life-size figures, this exhibition centre showcases situations and episodes associated with the transportation of mail and people in the 18th century. In the exhibition, employees and passengers seem to have been immobilised in a moment where time has stopped, seeming like they will return to their everyday lives in the next instant.
A visit to the Communications Museum is interesting for families with children because it possesses a vital educational component. Together, they’ll be able to visualise and get an accurate perception of a different time in history, one when it was impossible to communicate with a simple click.

title: True Odysseys
short_description: Plan Less. Experience More. At True Odysseys, we’re redefining the travel experience by harnessing the power of technology to offer spontaneous, immersive, and authentic local experiences.
full_description:
We believe the best memories are created when travel is effortless, allowing more room for discovery and delight.
Innovative Tourism Solutions: We're not just a tour operator; we're innovators in the tourism sector. Our tech-driven platform connects travelers with in-destination activities, empowering them to plan less and experience more, right from their hotel lobby.
Empowering Travelers & Partners: Our mission extends beyond tours; we facilitate seamless, real-time interactions between adventurers and a diverse range of experiences. By providing a direct gateway to Portugal’s treasures, we ensure guests and local businesses alike thrive through enhanced connectivity.
A New Chapter as True Odysseys: While we expand our scope, Odyssey Tours continues to operate, ensuring the legacy of curated travel endures. We remain committed to showcasing Portugal's splendor through both traditional guided tours and innovative tech solutions.
Join us on this journey of innovation and exploration. Discover Portugal through True Odysseys—where every step is part of a greater adventure.
location: Rua Abel Manta, 14 - 1 O B, Algueirão, 2725-604, Mem Martins

title: Directorate-General for Cultural Heritage (DGPC)
short_description: The Directorate-General for Cultural Heritage (DGPC) is responsible for studying, conserving and enhancing the cultural heritage; it manages 23 museums, palaces and monuments and coordinates the Portuguese Museums Network.
full_description:
The Directorate-General for Cultural Heritage (DGPC) is responsible for managing the cultural heritage in mainland Portugal.
Amongst other responsibilities, it conducts studies and research, disseminates information about immovable, moveable and intangible property; it manages the architectural and archaeological built heritage and implements conservation works in key monuments; it undertakes conservation and restoration of moveable property and its fixed fixtures; manages 23 museums, palaces and monuments and coordinates the Portuguese Museums Network; it also promotes documentation and the inventory of intangible heritage.
The DGPC is responsible for:
• Knowledge, inventory, safeguarding, conservation, enhancement and dissemination of the architectural, archaeological, movable and intangible heritage, as well as development and implementation of the national museum policy.
• Management of the main Portuguese museums and all the monuments inscribed on UNESCO’s World Heritage List.

title: Elohim Spa
short_description: At ELOHIM SPA, you will have an unforgettable experience, with face and body therapies.
full_description: At ELOHIM SPA, you will have an unforgettable experience, with face and body therapies that are combined with practices of revitalization, detoxification, and stress release. elohim offers massages, reflexology, chinese medicine, osteopathy, body scrub, facial and reiki.

title: Casas da Baixa Planet
short_description: It is the perfect place to stay if you want to enjoy Lisbon to the fullest.
full_description:
These apartments offer an intimate and welcoming environment in alternative to large luxury hotels.
In these self-service apartments, be close to every transportation with the comfort and beauty of a Portuguese-styled building.

title: Fun Happens - Food Events Network
short_description: With a team passionate about the art of service, we transform each order into a unique experience, where quality, creativity and attention to detail come together to surprise.
full_description:
We are passionate about food and the power to has to transform moments into unforgettable memories.
It is this passion that drives us to innovate, surprise and elevate every detail, because we know that the right gastronomy can make a difference and make every occasion truly special.
With a team passionate about the art of service, we transform each order into a unique experience, where quality, creativity and attention to detail come together to surprise.
Specializing in large events, we offer personalized services ranging from innovative buffets to interactive live cooking stations, always with the commitment to exceed expectations.

title: Garden of the Gulbenkian Foundation
short_description: Grab a book or newspaper and take a seat in the Gulbenkian garden, a unique place with various ambiances that you should wander until finding the ideal spot for a rest.
full_description: The Gulbenkian, as you already know, is one of Lisbon’s main cultural venues and a place you visit for excellent exhibitions, concerts and activities. But you may not know that the Foundation’s fantastic gardens, designed in 1957, have a pond and a rivulet, various paths between the various plant species, a magnificent open-air amphitheatre where you can see plays and concerts, and spots just to sit and read a book. It’s worth discovering this unique place in Lisbon where various species coexist calmly in an atmosphere that’s both natural and manmade. Come and experience the tranquillity in the middle of the city.

title: Marina Parque das Nações
short_description: Marina Parque das Nações The perfect place to, safely, leave your boat the entire year and to enjoy activities such as Sailing, Canoeing, Windsurfing and bike riding.
full_description:
An urban, central and cosmopolitan marina. Situated in Parque das Nações, the former exhibition site, it offers major attractions such as the Oceanarium, Lisbon’s Casino, MEO Arena, relaxing gardens and riverside walks.
Integrated in the Tagus Estuary Nature Reserve, considered to be one of the ten most important estuary areas in Europe and excellent for bird watching, this marina provides you some refreshing and welcoming terraces.
With plenty activities for you and for your family, such as Canoeing, Sailing, Windsurfing and Bike riding, this is, for sure, one of the most charming Portuguese marinas.

title: Lisbon Five Stars 8 Building
short_description: 8 Building - Studios, one, two & three bedroom apartments
full_description:
Located in Lisbon’s historical centre, 8 Building is one of Lisbon’s reference buildings from 1950’s.
Right next to the famous “pink street”, Cais do Sodré train and tube stations it is one minute away from the lively Mercado da Ribeira.
Its façade reveals a building with character and its clock tower provides breathtaking views towards the river Tagus.
With high-quality finishes and complying with the highest building standards, this building includes studios, one, two and three bedroom apartments in an unique location.

title: Mother Church of Póvoa de Santo Adrião
short_description: Discover this 16th-century church, a bastion of sacred art in Lisbon, with particular mention for the paintings by Pedro Alexandrino in the high chapel.
full_description: If you want to visit the most important 16th-century churches while in Lisbon, don’t forget the small and impressive mother church of Póvoa de Santo Adrião. Hidden by the buildings that have since been erected but marking the historic centre of this town on the outskirts of Lisbon, its oldest foundation stone dates back to 1546. Though its exact date is unknown and records only exist from 1564 onwards, there is no doubt that it is a unique building. Its front portico and a Manueline-style stoup deserve a visit, as well as the paintings by Pedro Alexandrino in the high chapel and the gilded wooden altarpiece. Also discover the chapel of Santo António lined with 17th-century polychrome azulejo tiles at this bastion of sacred art in Lisbon.
location: Largo Maj. Rosa Bastos, Póvoa de Santo Adrião, 2620-118, Odivelas

title: Hospital da Luz Lisboa
short_description: Hospital da Luz Lisboa, the largest and most advanced private hospital in Portugal, provides the full spectrum of care from wellness visits to complex surgeries.
full_description: The Hospital da Luz Lisboa is part of an integrated medical complex consisting of an acute care hospital and a residential hospital. The quality and experience of the clinical staff and the technological innovations make the Hospital da Luz Lisboa a model of clinical excellence and innovation. Also, its unique architecture combines safety with comfort and privacy of patients. Hospital da Luz Lisboa offers all medical and surgical specialities, with emphasis in areas organized in multidisciplinary centers of excellence, contributing to a comprehensive and integrated approach to patient and is decisive for the quality of health care. Hospital da Luz Lisbon became, since its opening in 2006, a reference in the health sector in Portugal.

title: São Julião da Barra Fort
short_description: This imposing and formal site, between two of the most popular beaches on this part of the coast, is one of Portugal’s largest maritime military structures.
full_description: Take a break from swimming at Torre Beach in Oeiras, or Carcavelos Beach, and come and see the old “Shield of the Realm”. São Julião da Barra Fort is one of Portugal’s largest maritime fortifications and is impressive for its size and power. Besides being the official residence of the Portuguese Ministry of Defence, it is the largest Vauban-style military complex in the country. On foggy mornings and afternoons, the line of its walls looks like the bow of a ship ploughing along the Tagus. It was built to protect the sandbar and access to the port of Lisbon. Construction began in the 16th century under King João III and its walls have been witness to some of Portugal’s major military confrontations. It’s well worth discovering its history protected by the ditches and bastions at the entrance to the city.

title: No. 25 Tram
short_description: A journey back in time to another Lisbon, through calm bairros and shopping streets, lets you discover a city where everything moves at a slower pace.
full_description:
This is a different way to explore the other traditional neighbourhoods of Lisbon.
The no. 25 tram travels the route between Prazeres and Campo das Cebolas, crossing the elegant bairro of Campo de Ourique, passing through Estrela, with its basilica and park, descending (or climbing) down to Lapa and running parallel to the river from Santos to São Paulo, Cais do Sodré and Praça do Comércio until coming to rest in Campo das Cebolas in front of the Casa dos Bicos.
Less popular with tourists than the no. 28, it is an original way of travelling in Lisbon and reaching some of the city’s biggest attractions.
It is also very popular with locals who are not in a hurry.
With its wooden carriages from the early 20th century, the no. 25 takes you on a journey back in time that’s worth experiencing.

title: Almada's Murals in the Maritime Stations Interpretative Centre
short_description: This Interpretative Centre is an interactive experience of the history and content of Almada Negreiros' murals at the Alcântara and Rocha do Conde de Óbidos Maritime Stations, as well as learning about the artist's life and Almada's artistic footprint in Lisbon.
full_description:
The Interpretive Centre “
Murais de Almada nas Gares Marítimas
” (
Murals of Almada in the Maritime Stations
), is located at the Alcântara and Conde d'Óbidos stations in Lisbon.
On a tour of nine rooms, the Centre tells us the story about the mural paintings and the context in which they were created; the artist and the artist’s work throughout the city of Lisbon, and of those who passed through these stations during the second half of the 20th century.
A visit to this new space in Lisbon allows us to find out about the life and work of Almada Negreiros, a renowned Portuguese multidisciplinary artist and his artistic footprint, which is still visible in various areas of the city today,
The route between the two stations is covered by a vehicle based on an original design brought to life by the studio of Architect Pardal Monteiro in the 1940s to transport passengers' luggage.
This transport is exclusively for visitors of the Interpretive Centre and operates every day between 10am and 6pm.
Maritime Stations
Business hours: 10am/7pm (last entry: 1 hour before closing time)

title: Tapada Nacional de Mafra
short_description: Natural heritage with unique characteristics and home to deer, fallow deer, wild boar, foxes, birds of prey alongside the many other species.
full_description:
King João V, “the Magnanimous” (1689-1750), commissioned the construction of the Palace-Convent in the town of Mafra to meet a promise he made prior to the Queen making him heir to the throne.
This grandiose monument, built at a time of great royal prosperity resulting from the exploration of gold and diamonds in Brazil, represents a high-water mark in the Portuguese Baroque.
The
National Hunting Grounds of Mafra
were established in 1747 with the objective of providing surroundings appropriate to the Monument along with providing a recreational hunting ground for the King and his Court and in addition to supplying firewood and other products to the Convent.
Covering an area of 1,187 hectares, the Royal Hunting Grounds of Mafra were entirely surrounded by a worked limestone wall across a length of 16 Km.

Small town house hotel with the warmth of a private club.
Dating from the 1940’s and designed by a famous portuguese modernist architect Cassiano Branco the Hotel Britania is the only hotel of this period that survived unscathed.
The original Art Deco ambience was recently restored and the hotel was classified as an historic building.
Tradition and modernity go hand in hand in this small family run hotel.

full_description:
The statue of the Santuário do Cristo-Rei, one of the most emblematic monuments of the 20th century, can be found in Almada. Built in 1959, it is 110 metres high and offers a unique view over the capital and the Tagus estuary. Inspired by the Cristo Redentor monument in Rio de Janeiro, the figure of Christ opens its arms to Lisbon and receives hundreds of tourists, as well as pilgrims. The Costa de Caparica, one of the most popular beach destinations in the region, can be found on what is known by the people of Lisbon as "the other side" or the "south bank".
More than 13 kilometres of excellent beaches wait to receive those who love the sun and the sea. In Caparica you can also find the Convento dos Capuchos, a 16th century Franciscan monastery that has several interesting and admirable details, and is an excellent spot for a walk.

title: Bairro Alto
short_description: In some of Lisbon’s oldest and most traditional neighbourhoods, you’ll find a nightlife and festive spirit that you’ll find hard to believe in the centre of the city. The streets fill with people of all ages who come to catch up, have a drink, watch the people passing by and discover all the many new things there are to be found.
full_description:
In some of Lisbon’s oldest and most traditional neighbourhoods, you’ll find a nightlife and festive spirit that you’ll find hard to believe in the centre of the city. The streets fill with people of all ages who come to catch up, have a drink, watch the people passing by and discover all the many new things there are to be found.
There’s little point asking a Lisboeta where the best place is to go out at night. The whole city meets at the centre of its nightlife, where there are places to suit every taste and music for every genre. The more experienced guarantee that Lisbon’s nightlife consists of a steady downhill motion – and the more poetic of them swear that every conversation is spoken in italics. Why? Because it always begins in Bairro Alto with its many and varied bars and restaurants, then heads down to Bica with a glass in hand and always ends in Cais do Sodré, where the most popular nightclubs intermingle with the old sailors’ bars, combining an original mix of cultures and ambiances. Whether you like 80s classics, hard rock or the freshest of musical sounds, you’re bound to find something that’s tailored to suit your tastes.
During the day, Bairro Alto is home to various alternative shops, from records to art and hairdressers.

title: Palaphita Cascais

On the one hand, the Palaphita Galeão, at Rio de Janeiro Airport. On the other, the Palaphita Cascais, in Cascais, Portugal. In the center, the Amazonian roots.
ENJOY DURING
BREAKFAST & DINNER
We have everything for a perfect gastronomic experience. The view to the sea, the companies and, of course, the Amazonian spices.
OUR DRINKS
An environment conducive to cultivate friendships and enjoy life as nature asks. Whether night or day, the experience is perfect at Palaphita Cascais

title: Maria Matos Municipal Theatre
short_description: Come and see one of the most multipurpose theatres in Lisbon. A cultural hub and activity centre, the Maria Matos Theatre is an alternative sight to see in the city.
full_description: Much more than just a theatre, the Teatro Municipal Maria Matos is one of the most interesting places in Lisbon. A well of creativity in every area, from theatre to dance, concerts and children’s activities, the curtain rises on a varied and eclectic programme for a variety of audiences. Come to the centre of Lisbon to discover this unique place where the arts transcend the city and take part in nationwide programmes. The contemporary nature of its performances is one of the aims of its current artistic directors. Designed by Barros da Fonseca, the theatre was opened in 1969. Refurbished and reopened in 2006, it is now one of the city’s principal cultural centres. Come and discover this original and interesting project during your stay in Lisbon.

title: Fort of St. Jorge de Oitavos
short_description: This fort, which was built after Portugal’s independence, was designed to prevent hostile landings along the coast.
full_description: Oitavos Fort, which is rectangular in layout and sits atop the cliff, was built between 1642 and 1648, following the restoration of Portugal’s independence. It was part of the defensive system for the entrance to the Tejo River. The fort was garrisoned by a small detachment, which had the task of stopping hostile forces from landing between Guincho and Guia. The architectural restoration work was closely guided by a 1796 plan of the fort, to ensure the original features were retained. This project has resulted in the recreation of an authentic 17th Century ambience in many of the rooms.

title: Gamma Expeditions
short_description: Gamma Expeditions is a tour operator based in Lisbon, Portugal, specializing in private transportation and guided sightseeing tours
full_description: We offer spectacular tours, full passion and history - give us the opportunity to enrich your visit to our city.

title: Lumen Hotel & The Lisbon Light Show
short_description: Your hotel in the heart of Lisbon! Located in the heart of the city, the Lumen Hotel promises to honor the famous Light of Lisbon, invading those who visit it with sensations.
full_description:
Your hotel in the heart of Lisbon!
Located in the heart of the city, the Lumen Hotel promises to honor the famous Light of Lisbon, invading those who visit it with sensations.
Inaugurated in 2021, this 4-star superior is the first hotel in Portugal to offer an immersive experience of light, through a video mapping show that introduces Lisbon to all the guests.
All rooms are equipped with the latest technology (such as tablets and USB sockets) and extra wide beds to provide guests with the greatest comfort.
The Clorofila restaurant, the rooftop pool, the gym, meeting rooms and co-working spaces complete the offer.
Exclusive light and color show for guests
Every day, the interior garden of Lumen Lisboa Hotel envelops you in an unmissable immersive experience of light and color. Join this show and get to know the charms of Lisbon's inspiring light!
Rooms
How are you feeling today?
Enthusiastic and energetic like a golden dawn?
Passionate and warm as a copper nightfall?
Calm and relaxed as pure light?
We want your bedroom to be a mirror of your state of mind!
Get inspired by these colors and sensations and choose your mood for an unforgettable stay!
At Lumen Hotel, your room is your creation!

title: Castro Rua Garrett
short_description: Atelier de Pastéis de Nata Castro is dedicated to making one of the greatest examples of Portuguese sweets, in a tribute to centuries of history and tradition.
full_description:
With three shops open in Portugal, two in Lisbon and one in Porto, Castro is opening the doors to a journey into the past, allowing the Portuguese to experience the flavour of their history and those who visit us to find in this recipe the true Portuguese identity, a delicious memory to keep from their trip.
Castro's Pastel de Nata preserves the authenticity of the recipe through handmade confectionery and the choice of high-quality ingredients. The perfect balance between the light, crunchy pastry and the slightly sweet filling with citrus notes is the result of hours of perfecting the recipe in search of the best pastel de nata.
More than just faithful representatives of Portuguese Conventual Sweets, Castro's Pastéis de Nata are true masterpieces ready to be enjoyed.
As tradition dictates, it should be sprinkled with cinnamon and icing sugar in moderate proportions and accompanied by coffee, tea, a glass of port or even a ginjinha.
Translated with DeepL.com (free version)

title: Portugália Cervejaria Almirante Reis
short_description: Portugália is the most famous beer hall in Portugal.
full_description: Portugália is the most famous beer hall in Portugal. With almost a century of history, it is hard to find a true Portuguese who does not know of our existence. Our steaks and sauces are unique. Our seafood is delicious. Our beer is special. Our ambience is relaxed. Our service is welcoming. That is why generations of customers look for us and always return. title: Cityrama Grayline Portugal
short_description: An excellent host offering a variety of ways to discover Portugal in a unique and unforgettable way: regular tours, hop on hop off, incoming and outgoing.
full_description:
Cityrama Gray Line Portugal is one of Portugal’s main tour operators. Évora, Sintra, Fátima and Óbidos are some of the regular tours, starting in Lisbon, with guides who are fluent in six languages.
Our hop-on-hop-off tour includes over 55 stops and leaves every 20 minutes giving visitors the ability to choose how to visit Lisbon. The tour is done on a panoramic bus and we provide information in 14 languages through audio-guides and maps of Lisbon with all the information needed.
Let yourself be charmed by our country: Cityrama Gray Line Portugal will take care of everything else so that you can have a unique and unforgettable experience.
location: Rua Forças Armadas Lote 21 , Camarate, 2680-128, Lisboa

title: MAAT - Museum of Art, Architecture and Technology
short_description: MAAT – Museum of Art, Architecture and Technology is the new cultural centre for Lisbon. It’s a museum where these three areas intertwine within a space of debate, discovery, critical thinking and international dialogue.
full_description:
MAAT – Museum of Art, Architecture and Technology is the new cultural centre for Lisbon.
It’s a museum where these three areas intertwine within a space of debate, discovery, critical thinking and international dialogue. It’s an innovative project which establishes a connection between the new building, designed by Amanda Levete Architects’ studio, and Central Tejo Power Station, one of Portugal’s most prominent examples of industrial architecture from the first half of the 20th century, and one of the most visited museums in the country.
MAAT’s ambition is to present national and international exhibitions by contemporary artists, architects and thinkers. The programme will also include various curatorial perspectives on EDP Foundation’s private Art Collection, reflecting current subject matters and trends.

title: Upon Angels
short_description: We welcome you to Upon Angels, where we awaken the most daring and sensorial side of your stay. Located on Lisbon's vibrant Rua dos Anjos, we seek to question the rules, stimulate the senses and challenge our guests to do the same.
full_description:
The invitation is out. Dare to accept it.
A BOLD APPROACH TO HOSPITALITY
At 10 Rua dos Anjos, we unlock the secret of Upon Angels as a collision point to the unexpected.
We break the barriers of the couple routine and our own rules, without fear. We go beyond minimalism because being extra makes a difference. The influence of sassy glamor, burlesque rhythm, and angelic delicacy inspire the decor and attitude of our spaces and events.
This is the stay of the bold and curious, the restless souls who crave authentic ties, vibrant culture, and a touch of spontaneity. Indulge in this incomparable experience, where Cupid guides the encounters and we witness exchanges of glances, experiences, and interests.
Under the wings of angels, no one meets by accident.
